# QTOM pilot graph — 14517 entities, 11943 relations (cognee structural nodes/edges removed)

## Node types (our ontology)
untyped=4519, concept=1830, physicalquantity=1530, person=1254, technique=931, setup=916, phenomenon=738, publication=724, finding=690, material=456, sample=296, researchquestion=160, measurementtype=147, experiment=147, project=140, subteam=25, run=14

## Entities by type


### concept
- **"mode" column** — Column on the Main page indicating the connection and control status of an axis.
- **"position" column** — Column on the Main page showing current position and allowing adaptation in closed loop mode.
- **"preset" column** — Column on the Main page to select preset combinations of amplitude, frequency, and target range.
- **/pro upgrade** — Software/firmware feature that must be activated on all controllers to select and use axes of various devices.
- **14r connector** — 14‑pole round connector, male.
- **14‑pin binder connector** — Connector used for positioners with integrated optoelectronic encoders.
- **15v connector** — 15‑pin Sub‑D connector, female.
- **15‑pin peek connector** — Connector used for positioners with integrated optoelectronic encoders.
- **15 mev excitation** — A spectroscopic feature at ±15 meV observed only in flat‑band regions.
- **25‑pin subd connector** — TTL triggering connector with 25 pins on the back panel.
- **26‑pin d‑sub connector** — Connector on the AMC300 for RES sensors.
- **2d magnetism** — Magnetic order and excitations confined to atomically thin layers.
- **2s connector** — 2‑pole plug standard, male.
- **2v connector** — 2‑pole plug vacuum, male.
- **2‑pin connector** — Connector on positioners coded by a red chamfer for positive polarity.
- **3.3 v supply** — 3.3 V power rail provided on the SubD connector (max 300 mA).
- **3d fermi surface** — Three‑dimensional shape of the Fermi surface probed by angular magneto‑transport.
- **3×1 reconstruction of au(110)** — Another atomic arrangement of the Au(110) surface examined in the theory.
- **3‑d positioning unit** — Three‑dimensional positioning arrangement formed by stacking ANPx101 and ANPz101 positioners.
- **3‑pin connector** — Connector for the resistive encoder on positioners.
- **5r connector** — 5‑pole round connector, male.
- **5s connector** — 2+3‑pole plug standard, male.
- **5v connector** — 2+3‑pole plug vacuum, male.
- **60‑degree periodicity** — Observed periodicity in conductance related to crystallographic alignment.
- **a and b sublattices of graphene** — Two sublattice degrees of freedom entering the tunneling amplitude.
- **a/b channels** — Two complementary signal lines used in quadrature encoding.
- **aa stacking** — High‑symmetry stacking where the two lattices are perfectly aligned.
- **aa stacking region** — Local stacking configuration in TBG where the two layers are directly on top of each other, hosting concentrated electric fields.
- **aa stacking regions** — Regions of the moiré superlattice where the two graphene layers are directly on top of each other.
- **aa stacking site (carbon above both boron and nitrogen)** — High‑symmetry stacking configuration where carbon atoms are aligned with both sublattices of hBN.
- **aa‑localized states** — Electronic states that are spatially confined to the AA stacking regions of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **absolute position information** — Exact knowledge of the axis location, absent in open‑loop mode.
- **absolute position measurement** — Technique to measure absolute nanopositioner position with high accuracy.
- **acoustic isolation** — Use of acoustic isolation tubing to reduce noise transmission.
- **acoustic phonon** — Low‑energy phonon with linear dispersion.
- **acoustic phonon** — Low‑energy phonon mode whose energy is smaller than optical phonons.
- **acoustic phonon modes** — Low‑energy phonon modes (e.g., ZA, TA, LA).
- **acoustic phonons** — Low‑energy lattice vibrations with atoms moving in phase.
- **acoustic phonons** — Low‑energy phonon modes with linear dispersion.
- **acoustic ta mode** — Transverse acoustic phonon mode in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **ac‑in / mod bnc connector** — BNC connector for inputting AC voltages.
- **ac‑in input** — Alternating‑current input channel of the ANC300.
- **adapter cable** — Cable used to connect the BNC output port of an axis to a positioner.
- **adapter plate** — Adapter plate used to mount rotators or scanners on other positioners.
- **addition‑energy spectrum** — Spectrum of energies required to add successive single electrons to a quantum dot.
- **adhesion enhancement** — Improved sticking between cantilever and sample caused by heating.
- **adiabatic local density approximation (alda)** — Time‑dependent DFT kernel that treats exchange‑correlation adiabatically.
- **adjustable actuator parameters** — Set of parameters that can be tuned for the positioning actuator.
- **adjustable sensor parameters** — Set of parameters that can be tuned for the sensor attached to an axis.
- **alignment path** — Defined optical trajectory used for aligning components in the setups.
- **altermagnet** — Class of materials with compensated antiferromagnetic order and non‑relativistic spin splitting.
- **altermagnetic interactions** — Interactions in CrSb that generate chiral spin splitting and the six‑fold spectral‑weight signature.
- **altermagnetic magnon waveguide** — Stray‑field‑free waveguide for magnon packets enabled by altermagnets.
- **altermagnetism** — Altermagnetism, a magnetic order with compensated antiferromagnetic sublattices and spin‑split bands.
- **altermagnetism** — Magnetic order with alternating spin polarization without net magnetization.
- **ambient environment (rt)** — Room temperature ambient environment.
- **amc/ids** — Sensor types that require a closed‑loop feature.
- **amp dial** — Dial used to change the stepping or offset amplitude.
- **amplitude and phase matching** — Condition where tip and sample react with identical amplitude and phase to disturbances.
- **amplitude matching** — Design goal where tip and sample respond with equal amplitude to disturbances.
- **analytic behavior of i(φ)** — Smooth, analytic dependence of the tunneling current on band offset when v₀ > v_D.
- **angle** — Twist angle controlled by the rotation stage.
- **angle correction** — Adjustment of the rotation angle of the writing coordinate system using marker corners.
- **angle dependence** — Dependence of measured signals on twist angle.
- **angle‑dependent transport** — Transport properties that vary with the orientation of the sample relative to the magnetic field.
- **angular distribution** — Dependence of RPED intensity on emission angle.
- **angular‑momentum‑polarized auger electrons** — Auger electrons emitted with a defined orbital angular momentum.
- **anharmonicity** — Deviation of lattice vibrations from a purely harmonic potential.
- **anisotropic gap function** — Superconducting gap that varies with momentum direction.
- **anisotropic g‑factor correction** — Direction‑dependent contribution to the g‑factor.
- **anisotropic kitaev quantum spin liquid** — A Kitaev spin liquid with direction‑dependent exchange couplings.
- **anisotropic superconducting gap** — Superconducting gap whose magnitude varies with momentum.
- **anisotropy** — Direction‑dependent variation of a physical quantity, here Bc₂(θ).
- **annihilation operator br,q** — Operator that annihilates a phonon of mode r and momentum Q.
- **antiferromagnetic (afm) order** — Compensated magnetic ordering underlying altermagnetism.
- **antiferromagnetic spintronics** — Spintronic applications using antiferromagnets.
- **antiunitary symmetry r** — Symmetry operator (R = σ_y K) that protects the Kramers degeneracy in the tip Hamiltonian.
- **aperiodic effects** — Effects arising from lack of periodicity in quantum systems
- **approximate c6 symmetry** — Six‑fold rotational symmetry observed in the measured electrostatic potential of the G/hBN interface.
- **aquadb protocol** — Communication protocol where signals on lines A and B designate the increment and direction of a position change.
- **assumption o1** — Assumption that the coefficients a_k(t) remain very small for a short time after tunneling begins.
- **assumption o1** — Assumption concerning the accuracy of the first‑order approximation in Bardeen's theory.
- **assumption o2** — Assumption that inner products ⟨φ_j|ψ⟩ are very small, linking |a_j(t)|² to transition probabilities.
- **assumptions o1 and o2** — Conditions required for Bardeen’s formulas to remain valid at small tip‑sample distances.
- **atom superposition approximation** — Method approximating charge density as a superposition of atomic charge densities.
- **atomic magnetic moments** — Magnetic moments of individual atoms that can be extracted from XMCD or MCD data.
- **atomistic strain and relaxation** — Microscopic strain and lattice relaxation effects in twisted van‑der‑Waals stacks.
- **attocube positioner** — Attocube piezoelectric positioner controlled by the ANC300.
- **atto‑cube coarse motor** — Coarse approach mechanism used in STM design model 1.
- **automatic optimization** — Software‑driven routine that adjusts laser parameters without user intervention.
- **auto‑ground selector** — Selector that must be set to On for the trigger input to be effective.
- **auto‑ground timer setting** — Setting controlling automatic grounding interval.
- **axis activation** — State where an axis is enabled for motion.
- **axis deactivation** — State where an axis is disabled.
- **axis direction** — Upward or downward motion direction for an axis step.
- **axis down trigger** — Trigger line that commands an axis to step down.
- **axis mode** — Operational mode of an axis (gnd, inp, cap, stp, off, stp+, stp-).
- **axis module** — Axis module containing electronics that can be removed for service.
- **axis up trigger** — Trigger line that commands an axis to step up.
- **backplate** — Backplate to which axis boards are connected inside the ANC300.
- **backscattering geometry** — Raman configuration where incident and scattered light travel along the same axis.
- **backward motion** — Direction of motion where the step size is usually smaller.
- **back‑action from defect** — Influence of a defect on the local electron population.
- **band alignment** — Relative energy positions of electronic bands across a heterostructure interface.
- **band asymmetry** — Difference between conduction and valence band structures leading to nonreciprocal effects.
- **band curvature regularization** — Introducing curvature to otherwise linear bands to remove the singularity in the tunneling current.
- **band dispersion** — Energy-momentum relationship of electronic bands.
- **band distortions** — Modifications of electronic band dispersion due to external perturbations.
- **band gap between flat and dispersive bands** — Energy region used to set μ_T for selective probing of empty or occupied flat‑band states.
- **band intersections** — Points where the Dirac cone of the tip meets a probed band of the sample, determining tunneling.
- **band minimum ξ_cm** — Energy of the bottom of the upper remote conduction band (~70 meV).
- **band renormalization** — Strong modification of electronic bands due to interactions.
- **band structure** — Energy dispersion of TBG, including central flat bands and remote dispersive bands.
- **band structure** — Energy‑momentum relation of electronic states in a crystal.
- **band structure** — Electronic band structure of the material.
- **band‑off‑diagonal pairing** — Superconducting pairing between different electronic bands.
- **bardeens current formula** — Formula for tunneling current derived by Bardeen, often approximated from the NEGF current formula.
- **bardeens theory** — General tunneling theory (Bardeen’s formalism) that can be applied at both low and large bias if the tip and sample wavefunctions are correctly accounted for.
- **bardeens tunneling theory** — Theory of tunneling current based on Bardeen's formula and associated assumptions.
- **bardeens tunneling theory** — Theoretical framework for tunneling currents used to model STM images.
- **bare coulomb interaction vq** — Unscreened Coulomb potential used as the interaction line in Feynman diagrams.
- **batch number** — Identifier printed on each graphene piece.
- **bcs mean‑field theory** — Standard weak‑coupling theory of superconductivity.
- **bcs pairing** — Conventional s‑wave pairing mechanism between time‑reversed electronic states.
- **bcs theory** — Bardeen–Cooper–Schrieffer theory of superconductivity.
- **beam divergence** — Spread of the THz beam that the telescope aims to minimise.
- **beam focusing** — Adjustment of the ion beam to a tight spot before milling.
- **beam on** — State indicating that the electron beam is active.
- **bending motion** — Transverse motion of components that is neglected in the simplified model.
- **bending motion** — Flexural deformation of the STM tip‑sample assembly that contributes to signal response.
- **bernal-stacked configuration** — Reference configuration where two graphene layers are shifted relative to each other by the bond vector e1.
- **bernal‑stacked configuration** — AB stacking of graphene layers before twisting.
- **berry curvature** — Geometric property of Bloch bands influencing anomalous transport.
- **bethe‑salpeter equation (bse)** — Many‑body formalism for excitonic effects.
- **bevel** — Physical notch on connector pins that indicates the positive side.
- **bias shift** — Energy shift of the graphene band caused by the tip‑side bias.
- **bias voltage** — Applied voltage bias across the tunnelling junction.
- **bias voltage (ev)** — Voltage applied between tip and sample that sets the tunneling energy.
- **biaxial strain (0.5 %)** — Small isotropic strain applied to the graphene tip, shifting its Dirac point.
- **binder connector** — Connector whose housing is made of Binder material.
- **binding energy tuning** — Control of excitonic or electronic binding energies via external parameters.
- **bipolar piezo** — Piezo actuator driven with both polarities, used in ANSx150.
- **birefringent domains** — Regions with distinct optical axis orientation in NiI2.
- **bistritzer-macdonald (bm) hamiltonian** — Effective low-energy Hamiltonian for TBG.
- **bistritzer‑macdonald (bm) model** — Continuum model describing the band structure of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **bistritzer‑macdonald (bm) model** — Microscopic model of MATBG that features c‑like and f‑like orbitals.
- **bistritzer‑macdonald hamiltonian (h₀)** — Single‑particle Hamiltonian for twisted bilayer graphene.
- **bloch bands** — Electronic bands of the moiré superlattice; the ten closest to charge neutrality were kept.
- **bloch flat bands** — Flat electronic bands described by Bloch theory, investigated in polar twisted bilayers.
- **bloch hamiltonian** — Hamiltonian describing electrons in a periodic lattice, used as the basis for the model.
- **bloch spinors** — Momentum eigenstates (spinors) in graphene.
- **bloch wave function** — ψn(k), the periodic part of electron wavefunction in a crystal.
- **blue‑shift of quasi‑particles** — Slight increase of quasi‑particle energies in 3D systems.
- **bnc connector** — Coaxial connector type used on ANC300 outputs and on the AAC300 cable.
- **bode plot** — Frequency‑domain plot used to display magnitude of transfer functions.
- **bode plot** — Log‑log plot of magnitude (dB) versus frequency used to visualise the transfer function.
- **bogoliubov angle** — Angle characterizing the particle‑hole mixing in superconducting quasiparticles.
- **bogoliubov coherence factors** — Momentum‑dependent amplitudes that weight electron‑ and hole‑like excitations in a superconductor.
- **bogoliubov excitation** — Quasiparticle excitation in superconducting state.
- **bogoliubov excitations** — Quasiparticle excitations in a superconductor described by Bogoliubov‑de Gennes theory.
- **bogoliubov quasiparticle** — Excitations in a superconductor described by energy E_k.
- **bond length changes** — Changes in the bond lengths of graphene layers caused by atomic displacements.
- **bond vectors e_j** — Vectors connecting nearest‑neighbor carbon atoms.
- **bonding lines** — Electrical bonding lines on the PCB that need to be handled well in the layout.
- **bose‑einstein distribution n_b(ν)** — Thermal occupation factor for bosonic excitations appearing in the self‑energy expression.
- **bosonic collective mode** — General class of bosonic excitations, of which plasmons are an example.
- **bosonic mode** — A generic collective excitation (e.g., plasmon) whose density of states D(ω) enters Eq. (12).
- **bosonic modes** — Quantized collective excitations such as plasmons that couple to electrons.
- **bottom‑layer dirac point (κ)** — Dirac point of the bottom graphene layer in TBG.
- **bragg scattering processes** — Three momentum‑conserving scattering channels in the graphene superlattice.
- **brillouin zone** — Reciprocal‑space unit cell of graphene and TMD used to map k‑vectors.
- **brillouin zone** — Reciprocal‑space region over which phonon momentum Q is summed.
- **brillouin zone** — Reciprocal‑space unit cell of the graphene lattice.
- **broadband source** — Broad spectral illumination used in the FTIR‑QTOM setup.
- **broken-symmetry state** — A state in which a symmetry of the system is spontaneously broken.
- **bulk photovoltaic effect** — Generation of a dc photocurrent in a non‑centrosymmetric material without external bias.
- **bulk photovoltaic effect (bpve)** — Photocurrent generation without p‑n junctions.
- **b 2pz orbital** — Boron 2pz orbital in hBN.
- **c-like electrons** — Electrons that behave like Dirac (light) c‑electrons, coupled to f‑like electrons via Coulomb interaction.
- **c2x symmetry** — Twofold rotation about the x‑axis.
- **c2z symmetry** — Two‑fold rotation symmetry about the z‑axis exploited to simplify calculations.
- **c2zt symmetry** — Combined twofold rotation and time‑reversal symmetry.
- **c3 component of moiré lattice symmetry** — Minor three‑fold symmetric contribution observed in the moiré potential.
- **c3 symmetry** — Three‑fold rotational symmetry of the lattice that yields a factor of three in the sum over \(j\).
- **c3 symmetry** — Three‑fold rotational symmetry observed around the CN stacking site in the calculated potentials.
- **c3z symmetry** — Three‑fold rotation symmetry about the z‑axis used in the response‑function symmetry relations.
- **c6z symmetry** — Six‑fold rotational symmetry of the graphene lattice.
- **cabling restrictions** — Guidelines on cable resistance, length, shielding, and grounding for optimal performance.
- **calibration file** — File matching nanopositioner serial number used to calibrate each channel.
- **cap mode** — Mode that must be selected before capacitance can be read.
- **capacitance mode (cap)** — Mode constant CAP with numerical value 2.
- **capacitance mode (cap)** — Mode that enables capacitance measurement on the selected axis.
- **carrier‑density control** — The ability to tune nT and nB independently using VTG, VBG and Vb.
- **cascade phenomenology** — Series of abrupt changes in the electronic spectrum of MATBG as carrier density is varied.
- **cascades between light and heavy fermions** — Sequence of electronic reconstructions linking light‑mass and heavy‑mass quasiparticles.
- **cascades without symmetry breaking** — Electronic cascades that occur without an accompanying order parameter.
- **cascading behaviour of heavy electrons** — Periodic appearance of heavy‑electron spectral weight with filling (Δν≈1).
- **cb stacking** — One of the high‑symmetry stacking configurations in the moiré lattice.
- **cb stacking site (carbon above boron)** — High‑symmetry stacking configuration where a carbon atom sits on top of a boron atom in hBN.
- **cc – custom configuration** — Preset designation for user-defined configuration.
- **ce label** — Label containing serial number and manufacturer address.
- **center of rotation** — Geometric centre about which the sample rotates; required for accurate alignment.
- **central bands** — The two low‑energy bands near charge neutrality in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **central bands** — Low‑energy bands around the Dirac point.
- **central conduction band** — Low‑energy conduction band of TBG where the Van Hove singularity resides.
- **channel** — Channel identifier (1 to 3) for motion control.
- **characteristic voltage v*** — Voltage at which elastic tunneling onsets, defined by eV* = ħ v_D q_0.
- **characteristic voltage v**** — Voltage at which nesting occurs, defined by eφ(V**) = ħ v_D q_0.
- **characteristic voltage v**_b** — Higher bias voltage where nesting of Dirac cones occurs; shows a square‑root dependence on the twist angle.
- **characteristic voltage v*_b** — Bias voltage at which the tunneling current onsets, proportional to \(\sin\theta\).
- **charge carrier density** — Density of charge carriers determined from transport measurements.
- **charge gap** — Energy required to add or remove an electron from the system.
- **charge neutral tbg** — Twisted bilayer graphene tuned to the charge neutrality point.
- **charge neutrality** — Condition n_T + n_S = 0 (overall tip‑sample neutrality) imposed in the model.
- **charge neutrality** — Condition of zero net carrier density in the system.
- **charge neutrality point** — Gate voltage where carrier density changes sign in a graphene layer.
- **charge neutrality point (cnp)** — Gate voltage at which the carrier density in graphene changes sign.
- **charge order** — Spatial modulation of electronic charge density that can be imaged with the atomic SET.
- **charge polarization perpendicular to layers** — Redistribution of charge normal to the graphene/hBN interface that gives rise to electrostatic potentials.
- **charge reshuffling between light and heavy states** — Redistribution of carriers that underlies Dirac revivals.
- **charge separation** — Result of plasmon decay into an electron in the high‑potential layer and a hole in the low‑potential layer.
- **charge susceptibility χ^{(0)}(iωₙ)** — Linear response function governing the elastic tunneling contribution.
- **charge-neutral system** — Condition where carrier densities in top and bottom layers are equal in magnitude and opposite in sign.
- **charge‑density‑wave (cdw)** — Collective electronic ordering leading to periodic modulation of charge density.
- **chassis ground connection** — Ground connection from the NP‑Amp chassis to the surrounding environment.
- **chemical potential (µ)** — Energy needed to add an electron to a system.
- **chemical potential (μ)** — Fermi‑level energy of each graphene layer, controllable via gate and bias voltages.
- **chemical potential inside flat bands** — Condition where the chemical potential lies within the flat‑band energy range.
- **chemical potential outside flat bands** — Condition where the chemical potential lies above the flat‑band energy range.
- **chern bands** — Energy bands characterized by non‑zero Chern numbers, leading to topological properties.
- **chern insulator** — Topological insulating state associated with a quantized Hall conductance.
- **chern mosaic** — Spatial pattern of domains with different Chern numbers observed by scanning SQUID.
- **chern‑insulator phase** — Topological phase with quantized Hall conductance without external magnetic field.
- **chiral limit of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Simplified lattice model focusing on the chiral symmetry of magic‑angle tBG.
- **chiral magnetic domains** — Magnetic domains distinguished by the handedness of their spin helicity.
- **chiral magnetic domains** — Magnetic domains with a handedness (chirality).
- **chiral quantum spin liquid** — U(1) chiral spin liquid with a gapped spinon spectrum.
- **chiral symmetry** — Symmetry assumption used to simplify the HF band‑structure calculations.
- **chirality** — Handedness of a structure or excitation, lacking mirror symmetry.
- **circular dichroism (cd)** — Difference in absorption of left‑ and right‑circularly polarized light, observed in RPED.
- **circular dichroism in altermagnets** — Circular dichroism effect observed in altermagnetic materials.
- **circular injection current** — Circularly polarized light induced photocurrent.
- **circular photogalvanic effect** — Photocurrent that changes sign with the helicity of incident light.
- **circular photogalvanic effect (cpge)** — Helicity‑dependent photocurrent observed in NiI2 domains.
- **circular photogalvanic effect (cpge)** — Photocurrent generated by circularly polarized light.
- **circularly polarized light** — Light whose electric field vector rotates circularly in time.
- **class iv laser** — Classification of the FIRL100 laser according to EN60825 and 21‑CFR‑1040.
- **closed loop control** — Concept of closed loop control used for positioning motions.
- **closed loop mode** — Control mode where the controller uses sensor feedback to regulate the positioner.
- **closed loop positioner** — Positioner with feedback, whose parameters are stored in a LUT file.
- **closed‑loop mode** — Closed‑loop operation mode for approaching targets.
- **cluster‑model approach** — Theoretical model treating a finite group of atoms as a scattering cluster.
- **cn stacking** — Another high‑symmetry stacking configuration in the moiré lattice.
- **cn stacking site (carbon above nitrogen)** — High‑symmetry stacking configuration where a carbon atom sits on top of a nitrogen atom in hBN.
- **coarse approach mechanism** — Mechanical system that brings the STM tip close to the sample before fine scanning.
- **coarse motion control** — Step‑wise voltage‑driven motion for larger displacements.
- **coarse stepper** — Coarse stepping mode of the positioner.
- **collective bosonic excitations** — Bosonic modes (e.g., plasmons) that can mediate inelastic tunneling.
- **collective electronic excitations** — Many‑body electronic modes such as plasmons.
- **collective excitations** — Bosonic modes of the sample (e.g., plasmons, phonons) that can be emitted or absorbed during inelastic scattering.
- **collective mode** — Hypothetical intrinsic excitation possibly responsible for the 15 meV feature.
- **collective mode dispersion** — Dispersion of collective excitations such as phonons.
- **collinear antiferromagnetic ordering** — Antiferromagnetic order where spins are aligned collinearly.
- **collinear magnets** — Magnetic systems where all spins are parallel or antiparallel to a common axis.
- **collinear magnets** — Magnetic systems where spins are aligned parallel or antiparallel.
- **colour scale (rainbow)** — Visual mapping of Raman intensity values.
- **command string** — String generated by VIs that is sent to the ANC300 for command execution.
- **commensurate stacking** — Real‑space registry where the two lattices form a 7 × 7 supercell.
- **commensurate twist angle** — Specific twist angles at which the two lattices become commensurate in real and momentum space.
- **compensated magnet** — Magnet with fully compensated magnetic moments (zero net magnetization).
- **compensated spin structure** — Two equivalent magnetic sublattices with opposite spin.
- **computer‑aided design (cad)** — Software‑based design workflow that provides geometry for FEM simulations.
- **conduction band** — Lowest unoccupied electronic band of a semiconductor.
- **conduction band of hbn** — Electronic conduction band of hexagonal boron nitride.
- **conservation of in‑plane momentum during tunnelling** — Requirement that tunnelling processes conserve the lateral momentum of carriers.
- **cont switch** — Switch that triggers continuous stepping while held down.
- **contact resistance** — Large resistance at the electrical contact that dominates the voltage drop for |Vb| < Vth.
- **continuum description of tip‑sample tunneling** — Theoretical framework treating tunneling in a continuous model.
- **continuum interlayer tunneling hamiltonian** — Model describing electron tunneling between the two graphene layers.
- **cooper pairing** — Formation of bound electron pairs leading to superconductivity.
- **correlated insulating state** — Interaction‑driven insulating phase seen in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **correlated insulator states** — Insulating phases arising from strong electron correlations in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **correlation potential** — Part of the exchange‑correlation potential that LDA does not describe correctly at large distances.
- **corrugation** — Geometric corrugation at the tip‑sample interface that is neglected in the model.
- **corrugation effects** — Out‑of‑plane deformations influencing interlayer coupling.
- **coulomb cutoff** — Technique to improve calculations for low‑dimensional materials by truncating the Coulomb interaction.
- **coulomb diamond** — Characteristic diamond‑shaped region in a Vgate–Vbias map of a quantum dot.
- **coulomb diamond doubling** — Observation of two overlapping Coulomb diamonds in transport data.
- **coulomb hamiltonian** — Hamiltonian describing electron‑electron Coulomb interactions.
- **coulomb hamiltonians** — Hamiltonians describing electron‑electron Coulomb interactions.
- **coulomb interaction** — Long‑range electron‑electron repulsion.
- **coulomb interaction renormalization** — Modification of electron‑electron interaction strength due to many‑body effects.
- **coulomb interactions** — Electron‑electron repulsion in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **coulomb screening** — Reduction of Coulomb interaction strength by external screening.
- **coulomb staircase** — Stepwise increase of charge transport due to Coulomb blockade
- **counterterm h_c** — Term added to regularize the divergent self‑energy from the Dirac sea.
- **crystal field multiplet model** — Theoretical model used to interpret the RPES spectra.
- **crystal momentum conservation** — Selection rule for tunneling that requires the in‑plane momentum to be conserved modulo a reciprocal lattice vector.
- **crystallographically aligned graphene sheets** — Assumption that the two graphene layers share the same lattice orientation.
- **current limiter** — Protection feature limiting current to prevent damage.
- **current signal between tip and sample** — Electrical current measured during IV spectroscopy.
- **current spectral density (γ_ph)** — Spectral distribution of the photon‑assisted tunneling current.
- **cutoff energy (e v_b)** — Energy below which photon‑assisted tunneling current vanishes, set by the applied bias.
- **cutouts for non‑rotating cables** — Circular cutouts required to let five rotator cables and a few capacitive read‑out cables pass.
- **cycloidal component** — Pure cycloidal rotation of spins in the x–y plane used in monolayer DFT simulations.
- **c‑electron states** — Light‑electron band states near the Γ point with weaker overlap with the Hartree potential.
- **c‑f coulomb repulsion** — Inter‑orbital Coulomb interaction driving Dirac revivals.
- **c‑like electrons** — Electrons associated with the light‑electron (dispersive) bands.
- **c‑like orbitals** — Orbitals in the BM model that give rise to light‑electron behaviour.
- **c–f hybridisation** — Mixing between c‑like and f‑like states that is neglected in the toy model.
- **c–f hybridization** — Hybridization between heavy (f) and light (c) electron sectors.
- **c–f hybridization** — Strong hybridization between conduction (c) and f electrons considered in the THF model.
- **c 2pz orbital** — Carbon 2pz orbital contributing to graphene’s π band.
- **c₂x p symmetry** — Combined two‑fold rotation and particle‑hole symmetry of the model.
- **c₃ symmetry‑broken semimetal** — Strain‑induced or spontaneous symmetry‑broken semimetal.
- **c₃z symmetry breaking** — Loss of three‑fold rotational symmetry about the out‑of‑plane axis, detectable in tunneling spectra.
- **c₆z rotation** — Six‑fold rotational symmetry of the monolayer‑graphene tip.
- **damped driven harmonic oscillator** — Model describing the tip motion including damping and driving forces.
- **damping force** — Viscous force proportional to velocity, characterized by damping factor b.
- **damping matrix d_j** — Matrix containing the damping coefficients for model j.
- **dark current** — Current flowing through a device in the absence of illumination.
- **dc-in functionality** — Enabling DC‑IN amplification only when voltage risk is absent.
- **dc‑in bnc connector** — BNC connector for inputting DC voltages.
- **dc‑in input** — Direct‑current input channel of the ANC300.
- **dead region** — The area on the PCB that must be positioned correctly relative to the pocket.
- **decoherence** — Loss of quantum coherence caused by inelastic processes such as electron‑electron or electron‑phonon scattering.
- **deep‑ultraviolet optical conductivity** — Optical response of freestanding 2D hBN in the deep‑UV spectral range.
- **defect** — Localized imperfections in the sample that provide extra tunnelling channels.
- **defect level resonance** — Condition where the defect’s energy aligns with the chemical potential of the top or bottom electrode.
- **defect‑assisted elastic tunnelling** — Unwanted elastic channel that can be activated by defects, mitigated by high graphene quality.
- **defect‑assisted resonant tunnelling** — Tunnelling process that involves localized defect states in the barrier.
- **defect‑assisted tunneling** — Tunneling process mediated by localized defect states.
- **defect‑assisted tunnelling** — Additional tunnelling channel opened when the tip overlaps an atomic defect.
- **defect‑bound excitons** — Excitons localized at nitrogen vacancies or carbon impurities in hBN (2.1‑2.5 eV).
- **deformation potential** — Potential arising from lattice deformations that contributes to electron‑phonon coupling.
- **deformation potential** — Shift of the Dirac point energy caused by strain‑induced changes in the carbon‑carbon bond length.
- **deformation potential coupling** — Contribution to electron-phonon coupling arising from the lattice deformation potential V(r) = -D ∇·u.
- **deformation pseudopotential** — Potential contribution due to lattice deformations within the moiré unit cell.
- **degeneracy‑lifted landau levels** — Landau levels whose spin/valley degeneracy is broken.
- **demagnetized sample** — Sample with zero net Néel vector, used as a reference for extracting the CD of a single domain.
- **density of states** — Number of electronic states per energy interval.
- **density of states (dos)** — DOS per unit area of graphene, denoted ρGr(E), used to convert discrete states to a quasi‑continuum.
- **density of states ν_c(ε) (conduction)** — The DOS of an empty dispersive band, entering Eq. (10).
- **density of states ν_v(ε) (valence)** — The DOS of a fully occupied valence band, entering Eq. (11).
- **density-density response function χ0** — Non‑interacting density‑density response function employed for twisted bilayer graphene.
- **design** — Pattern to be written on the chip.
- **detection bandwidth** — Frequency range over which the measurement signal is recorded.
- **determination of effective mass from sdh oscillations** — Procedure to obtain the effective mass of charge carriers by analyzing SdH oscillation amplitude.
- **device active area** — Overlap region of the two graphene electrodes that emits light under bias.
- **dhcp** — Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for automatic IP assignment.
- **dielectric function** — Frequency‑dependent response describing screening in a material.
- **dielectric thickness (d_g)** — Thickness of the hBN gate dielectric separating the graphene layers from the gate electrode.
- **differential capacitance** — Measurement method using differential capacitance to obtain absolute position.
- **differential conductance g(v,θ)** — Measured quantity dI/dV as a function of bias and twist angle, encoding spectroscopic information.
- **differential high voltage waveforms** — Differential high‑voltage waveforms used to drive piezoelectric elements.
- **diffraction‑limited focusing** — Optical performance of the Schwarzschild objective across the visible‑THz range.
- **dilute electron gas** — Low‑density electron system.
- **dirac band** — The conical electronic band structure of monolayer graphene around the K points.
- **dirac bands** — Linear dispersion bands representing monolayer graphene in the toy model.
- **dirac cone** — Linear band crossing point in graphene’s electronic structure.
- **dirac cone** — Conical band structure of graphene around the K (or K′) points where conduction and valence bands meet.
- **dirac cone overlap** — Alignment of Dirac cones from two graphene layers.
- **dirac cones** — Linear band crossings in graphene’s electronic structure.
- **dirac delta function** — Mathematical function enforcing energy conservation in the tunneling transition rates.
- **dirac delta function** — Mathematical function used to enforce energy and momentum conservation in tunneling integrals.
- **dirac description** — Low‑energy Dirac model for graphene electrons.
- **dirac dispersion** — Linear energy‑momentum relation near a Dirac point.
- **dirac electrons** — Light c‑like electrons with linear dispersion forming a Dirac point.
- **dirac fermions** — Massless relativistic‑like carriers in graphene.
- **dirac level splitting** — Possible splitting of graphene Dirac point influencing tunneling.
- **dirac matching features** — Features in the tunneling spectra that arise when the Dirac point of one layer aligns with the band structure of the other layer.
- **dirac point** — Linear crossing point of the light‑electron dispersion.
- **dirac point** — Linear band crossing point of graphene’s low‑energy spectrum.
- **dirac point in the tip** — The linear band crossing point of the tip material that participates in momentum‑conserving tunneling.
- **dirac point of the qtm probe** — The Dirac point of the probe that moves along a circular trajectory during measurement.
- **dirac points** — Linear band crossings characteristic of the C₃‑broken semimetal.
- **dirac points kb and kt of ma tbg** — Two inequivalent Dirac points in the moiré Brillouin zone of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **dirac quantum spin liquid** — Gapless U(1) quantum spin liquid with massless Dirac spinon dispersion.
- **dirac revivals** — Re‑emergence of Dirac‑like dispersions at specific twist angles.
- **dirac velocity (v_d)** — Slope of the linear graphene dispersion.
- **dirac‑delta spectral function** — Model for the tip’s spectral function assuming a delta peak in both conduction and valence bands.
- **dirac‑like µ(n)** — The characteristic chemical‑potential versus density relation of graphene near the Dirac point.
- **dirac‑point tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic technique that probes the energy dispersion by aligning the Dirac point of a graphene tip with sample bands.
- **disable sensor** — Checkbox to deactivate position feedback (requires RES positioner).
- **dispersive band** — Energy band with significant dispersion, used as a reference for plasmon‑assisted tunneling.
- **dispersive bands** — Electronic bands with significant energy-momentum dispersion.
- **dispersive c band** — Dispersive electronic band with c-electron character in MA TBG.
- **dispersive c‑electron states** — Dirac‑like itinerant bands that retain a finite velocity in the HF model.
- **displacement field (v_d)** — Electric field generated by the gate configuration, entering the electrostatic equations.
- **disturbance concept** — Base‑plate motion treated as an external force exciting the system.
- **doping** — Intentional introduction of charge carriers into a material.
- **double x structure** — Distinct conductance pattern observed near 21.8°.
- **down constant** — Constant DOWN representing numerical value 0.
- **drain (in qtm setup)** — Electrode acting as electron sink in tunneling measurements.
- **dresselhaus term** — Spin‑orbit interaction term arising from bulk inversion asymmetry.
- **driving force** — External sinusoidal force applied to the cantilever.
- **dual‑gate‑screened coulomb potential** — Electrostatic model where two metallic gates (or high‑κ dielectrics) screen the Coulomb interaction in the sample.
- **dynamic moiré configurations** — Continuously varying moiré patterns generated by rotating two vdW layers relative to each other.
- **dynamic power limit** — Power limit that depends on the measured capacitance of the positioner.
- **dynamical correlations** — Time‑dependent electronic correlations influencing spectral properties.
- **dynamical polarization** — Frequency‑dependent response of the electron density to an external perturbation.
- **dynamical screening** — Frequency‑dependent reduction of Coulomb interaction.
- **dynamical screening potential w** — Frequency‑dependent screened interaction appearing in the self‑energy diagrams.
- **dynamical screening potential w(q, iωn)** — Screened interaction appearing in the self‑energy Feynman diagrams.
- **dyson equation** — Equation relating the full Green’s function to the non‑interacting one and the self‑energy.
- **dyson equation** — Integral equation relating the Green’s function to the self‑energy.
- **dzyaloshinskii‑moriya interaction** — An antisymmetric exchange interaction that favors canted spin arrangements.
- **dzyaloshinskii‑moriya interaction** — Antisymmetric exchange interaction that favors chiral spin textures.
- **d₆ point group** — Six‑fold dihedral symmetry group relevant for MATBG.
- **effective dielectric function ε_eff** — Dielectric function describing screening in the presence of the tip.
- **effective dirac hamiltonian** — Low‑energy effective Dirac Hamiltonian (ħv_F k̄·σ) derived for TBG.
- **effective electro‑chemical potential** — Electro‑chemical potential felt by a site after accounting for repulsion from other electrons.
- **effective interaction v(r₁,r₂)** — Interaction kernel used to define the gap function.
- **effective interaction v_eff** — Frequency‑dependent interaction that includes tip screening.
- **effective mass** — Curvature‑related mass parameter m_T and m_S for tip and sample bands.
- **effective permittivity ε = εx εz** — Combined dielectric constant of the anisotropic spacer used in the screened interaction formula.
- **effective tip size** — Finite spatial extent of the STM tip influencing tunneling.
- **eigenfrequency (f₀)** — Fundamental resonance frequency of the cantilever or tuning fork.
- **elastic current density (j_el)** — Current density contribution from momentum‑conserving elastic tunneling, given by Eq. (S12).
- **elastic scattering cross‑section** — Measure of how strongly a nanostructure scatters light without energy loss.
- **elastic tunneling** — Tunneling process that conserves electron energy.
- **elastic tunneling features near γ point** — Strong conductance peaks arising from momentum‑conserving tunneling at the Brillouin‑zone center.
- **electric control of spin helicity** — Using an electric field to switch the helicity of a spin texture.
- **electric field control** — Manipulation of material properties using an external electric field.
- **electrical detection of plasmons** — Reading out plasmons by converting their energy into a tunnelling electron and a hole, creating charge separation.
- **electrically switchable p-wave magnet** — A p‑wave magnet whose magnetic order can be switched electrically.
- **electric‑field tuning** — Modification of electronic energies (e.g., exciton binding) by an applied electric field.
- **electrochemical potential (v)** — Sum of electrostatic and chemical potentials.
- **electron density (n)** — 2D carrier density on tip (n_T) or sample (n_S) layers, linked to gate and bias voltages.
- **electron self-energy σ** — Complex self‑energy Σ(k,ε) describing interaction‑induced renormalization and broadening of electronic states.
- **electron self‑energy (σ)** — Complex quantity describing many‑body corrections to the electron propagator.
- **electron spill‑out** — Extension of graphene electron wavefunctions into the vacuum influencing overlap.
- **electron spin relaxation mechanism** — Theoretical process governing spin relaxation in quantum dots, experimentally demonstrated in 2018.
- **electron transport** — Movement of electrons through a material or molecule
- **electron tunneling** — Quantum‑mechanical transfer of electrons across a thin barrier.
- **electron tunneling one at a time** — Regime where individual electrons tunnel sequentially due to low current.
- **electron-boson coupling** — Interaction between electrons and bosonic modes such as plasmons.
- **electron-electron interaction** — Coulomb interaction between electrons, assumed weak compared with band widths in the analysis.
- **electronic band structure** — Energy‑momentum relationship of electrons in a vdW heterostructure.
- **electronic bands of two‑dimensional materials** — Energy‑momentum structure of 2D crystals probed by QTM.
- **electronic properties** — Band structure, conductivity and related characteristics of a material.
- **electronic properties of graphene** — Fundamental band structure, carrier mobility, and Dirac physics of graphene.
- **electron‑boson model** — Model describing electrons coupled to bosonic excitations.
- **electron‑doped** — State of the tip where extra electrons raise the chemical potential.
- **electron‑electron interactions** — Many‑body effects that modify the band shape beyond the non‑interacting BM prediction.
- **electron‑electron scattering** — Coulomb interaction between quasiparticles that determines their lifetime.
- **electron‑k‑phonon interaction** — Momentum‑dependent electron‑phonon coupling.
- **electron‑phonon coupling** — Interaction between electrons and lattice vibrations
- **electron‑phonon coupling (epc)** — Interaction strength between electrons and lattice vibrations, central to the study.
- **electron‑phonon coupling (epc)** — Interaction between electrons and lattice vibrations.
- **electron‑plasmon coupling (g_c,f)** — Matrix element describing interaction between electrons and plasmons.
- **electron‑plasmon interaction** — Coupling between electrons and plasmon modes, described by a matrix element proportional to the plasmon zero‑point potential.
- **electron‑plasmon interaction hamiltonian (he‑pl)** — Part of the sample Hamiltonian describing coupling between electrons and plasmons.
- **electron–electron interactions** — Coulomb interactions between charge carriers that modify the graphene band structure.
- **electron–optical coupling** — Coupling between electrons and optical phonon modes (TO/LO) in graphene.
- **electrostatic effects** — Screening and charge redistribution phenomena influencing electronic structure.
- **electrostatic force** — Long‑range force that can be eliminated by equalizing tip‑sample potential.
- **electrostatic model** — Capacitive model that relates VTG, VBG and Vb to the carrier densities nT and nB in the two graphene layers.
- **electrostatic potential** — Potential landscape probed by QTM.
- **electrostatics** — Self‑consistent electrostatic potential calculation of the QTM junction.
- **electrostatics of graphene‑graphene junction** — Charge distribution and potential in a graphene junction.
- **electrostatics of qtm contact** — Electrical configuration of bias and gate voltages in the Quantum Twisting Microscope contact.
- **electro‑static sensitivity** — The property of a device being sensitive to electrostatic discharge.
- **elliptic regime (v₀ < v_d)** — Parameter regime where the δ‑function contours in the integrals are ellipses.
- **elliptical equal‑energy contours** — Shape of constant‑energy lines near φ_n when v_D > v_n.
- **elliptical intersections** — Intersection geometry (ellipses) occurring when tip velocity v_D > band velocity v_n.
- **emc en61326-1:2021** — European electromagnetic compatibility standard applicable to the ANC300 equipment.
- **emergent gauge field** — Effective gauge field arising in fractionalized phases such as quantum spin liquids.
- **emv housings** — Electromagnetic compatibility housings used as enclosure for D‑Sub connectors.
- **end‑of‑life unit** — Self‑contained operational electrical/electronic product at the end of its service life.
- **energy and momentum conservation** — Condition that restricts the tunnelling current to the region defined by eVb/ħ ± vF k∥.
- **energy denominator (ħv_d q₀)** — Energy scale appearing in perturbative expressions for electron‑phonon coupling.
- **energy gaps** — Bandgap openings induced by external perturbations such as strain.
- **energy resolution** — Upper bound on the energy resolution of the QTM measurements, δE≈10 meV.
- **energy‑ and momentum‑resolved measurements** — Capability of the QTM to resolve electronic states as a function of energy and momentum.
- **enhancement ratio** — Metric used to evaluate the Raman signal amplification in TERS.
- **equal‑energy line (ε=0) along px axis** — For v₀ = v_D the ε=0 line coincides with the entire px axis.
- **equation (5)** — Equation whose solution is to be approximated by the solution of Equation (6).
- **equation (6)** — Equation used to approximate the solution of Equation (5).
- **equations of motion** — Differential equations describing the dynamics of the STM models.
- **equipotential lines** — Lines of constant electrostatic potential that appear as rings in the conductance maps.
- **error in terminal** — Input terminal of a VI that receives error information.
- **error out terminal** — Output terminal of a VI that forwards error information.
- **esaki diode** — Tunnel diode exhibiting interband tunnelling.
- **ethernet 10/100 port** — LAN port for network connectivity.
- **eucentric double rotator** — Geometry where two rotation axes intersect at the sample centre, used in the atto3DR.
- **eucentricity** — Alignment condition where the sample rotation axis coincides with the beam.
- **exact symmetries** — Symmetries that hold rigorously for the interacting model.
- **exchange position** — Stage position used to move the sample out of the chamber.
- **excitations independent of filling (~±15 mev)** — Spectral features at ±15 meV that do not vary with carrier filling.
- **exciton** — Bound electron‑hole pair in a semiconductor.
- **exciton dispersion** — Momentum‑dependent energy of excitons in 2D hBN.
- **exciton polarons** — Hybrid quasiparticles consisting of an exciton coupled to phonons.
- **exciton resonance** — Energy level of a bound electron–hole pair in a TMD.
- **excitonic weights at k point** — Distribution of exciton oscillator strength concentrated at the K point of the Brillouin zone.
- **exciton‑assisted charge transfer** — Charge transfer mediated by excitonic states.
- **exciton‑assisted current peaks** — Tunneling current enhancements due to resonant exciton participation.
- **exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling** — Resonant tunnelling process in which an exciton in a TMD provides the energy for electron transfer.
- **exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling** — Electron tunnelling process that is facilitated by creation of excitons in the TMD layer.
- **exciton‑polaron coupling** — Interaction between excitons and lattice polarons within moiré wells.
- **exhaust line for gas handling** — Vent line that must remain unrestricted and be routed to a safe location outside the building.
- **exotic order parameter** — Non‑standard superconducting order parameter inferred from rotational symmetry breaking.
- **extended brillouin zone (third bz)** — Higher‑order Brillouin zone where the Fermi surfaces of the two graphene layers overlap at 21.8°.
- **external mechanical disturbances** — Vibrations or forces from the environment that affect STM stability.
- **external potential** — Additional electrostatic potential applied to the system, e.g., via gates.
- **extremal orbits** — Closed trajectories on the Fermi surface perpendicular to the magnetic field that dominate quantum oscillations.
- **extreme environmental conditions** — Operating regimes such as cryogenic temperatures, high magnetic fields, and ultra‑high vacuum.
- **extreme strain on tip** — Mechanical deformation of the tip that may modify electronic structure.
- **extremum in the sample band** — A band edge (minimum or maximum) in the sample that defines the momentum‑matching condition.
- **extrinsic (fizeau drag) nonreciprocity** — Breaking of reciprocity in plasmon propagation by a dc current flow.
- **e⁴ law** — Approximate relation I_SERS ∝ I_Ex × I_Em describing the Raman enhancement dependence on excitation and emission field intensities.
- **f-like electrons** — Electrons that behave like heavy f‑electrons in the model, carrying light/heavy aspects and distinct Hartree couplings.
- **fabry‑perot‑type resonance** — Interference effect caused by pn‑junctions that form near the contact region, leading to standing‑wave patterns in the sample.
- **fabry–pérot oscillations** — Interference pattern arising from the finite size of the tunnelling tip.
- **fano resonance** — Interference between a continuum and a discrete state causing asymmetric spikes.
- **fast magnetic switching** — Rapid reversal of magnetic order, desirable for spintronic devices.
- **feature code** — Code that activates a specific feature on the ANC300.
- **feature codes** — Codes that enable additional features of the ANC300.
- **feature codes** — Feature codes displayed in the ANC300 GUI.
- **feature trajectory** — The path traced by a selected sample feature during rotation, used to locate the centre of rotation.
- **feedback loop** — Control loop that maintains constant tunneling current during STM operation.
- **feedback loop (off during mfm)** — Control loop kept disabled during MFM to allow direct mapping of force gradient.
- **fermi circle** — Approximate circular Fermi contour of the tip used in the triangulation analysis.
- **fermi circles** — Constant‑energy contours of graphene’s Dirac cones at a given carrier density.
- **fermi edge** — The edge of the occupied electronic states in the sample.
- **fermi line** — Contour of constant energy at the Fermi level in momentum space.
- **fermi surface** — Surface in momentum space separating occupied from empty electronic states.
- **fermi wavelength (λ_f)** — Characteristic length scale of electrons, λ_F = 2π/κ_F.
- **fermi-edge singularity** — Singular feature in dI/dV arising from sample's Fermi edge.
- **fermiology of tbg** — Study of the Fermi surface and related properties in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **fermis golden rule** — Perturbative formula used to compute the inelastic tunneling current.
- **fermi‑dirac distribution** — Occupancy function f(E) = {1+exp[(E−E_F)/(k_B T)]}⁻¹ for electrons in graphene electrodes.
- **fermi‑dirac distribution (n_f)** — Thermal occupation function appearing in the tunneling current expression.
- **fermi‑liquid expectation τ ∝ 1/(e‑ef)²** — Standard scaling of quasiparticle lifetime in a Fermi liquid.
- **ferroelectric polarization** — Spontaneous electric polarization that can be switched by an external field.
- **ferroelectric polarization** — Electric polarization along the crystallographic [100] direction in NiI2.
- **ferroelectric properties** — Ferroelectric nature of the piezo material contributing to hysteresis.
- **ferroelectricity** — Spontaneous electric polarization that can be reversed by an external electric field.
- **ferromagnetic phase diagram** — Phase diagram describing ferromagnetic order.
- **fft‑resolved photocurrent** — Fourier‑transform analysis of the photocurrent signal in an interferometric measurement.
- **fieldpositionlist** — List that stores different writing field entries for multiple designs.
- **field‑effect transistor** — Electronic device where current is modulated by an electric field.
- **field‑induced quantum spin liquid** — Quantum spin‑liquid state stabilized by an external magnetic field.
- **field‑stabilized ground state** — A possible ground state that only appears when a magnetic field is applied.
- **filling of flat bands** — Carrier‑density occupation of the flat bands in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **filter setting anm200** — Filter configuration ANM200 with selectable frequency values (0 Hz, 160 Hz, 16 Hz, 1.6 Hz, etc.).
- **filter setting anm300** — Filter configuration ANM300 with selectable frequency values (0 Hz, 1600 Hz, 160 Hz, 16 Hz, off).
- **filter settings** — Configuration parameters such as OFF, 16 Hz, 160 Hz for the ANC300 filter.
- **filtering** — Low‑pass filtering of the output voltage (16 Hz or 160 Hz).
- **fine motion control** — Continuous voltage‑driven motion for sub‑nanometer positioning.
- **finite electron lifetime** — Finite scattering time of electrons that regularizes the divergence of the tunneling current.
- **finite element method (fem)** — Numerical technique used to compute the mechanical response of the STM.
- **fir laser frequencies** — Discrete frequencies across the FIR spectrum generated by rotational transitions of polar gases.
- **first‑order approximation** — First‑order perturbative approximation employed in Bardeen's tunneling theory.
- **flat angle sample** — Sample orientation where the surface is flat for optimal focus.
- **flat band** — Energy band with negligible dispersion, providing a narrow density‑of‑states peak.
- **flat band** — Energy band with very low dispersion, characteristic of magic‑angle TBG.
- **flat bands** — Energy bands with very low dispersion.
- **flat bands** — Low‑dispersion electronic bands near the magic angle.
- **flat bands of twisted bilayer graphene** — Low‑dispersion electronic bands that appear near the magic twist angle.
- **flat f band** — Flat electronic band with f-electron character in MA TBG.
- **flat f‑electron states** — Nearly dispersionless bands originating from localized f orbitals in the HF model.
- **flat heavy‑electron bands** — Momentum‑independent bands that host heavy electrons in MATBG.
- **flat part of the plasmon spectrum** — Region of the plasmon dispersion that is nearly constant in frequency across momentum.
- **flat tbg bands** — Nearly dispersionless bands that appear in twisted bilayer graphene at small twist angles.
- **flat‑band dispersion** — Electronic bands with very low velocity that appear near the magic angle in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **flat‑band manifolds** — Nearly dispersionless electronic bands in magic‑angle TBG.
- **flat‑band region** — Region in twist‑angle space where the electronic bands are flat, highlighted in the measurements.
- **flat‑band system** — A material system with nearly dispersionless electronic bands, studied in the paper.
- **flat‑band systems** — Electronic systems with nearly dispersionless bands.
- **flat‑band wave functions** — Electronic states in TBG with negligible dispersion, leading to strong interlayer hybridization.
- **flavour phase diagram** — Phase diagram describing symmetry‑broken states in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **flexural mode** — Out‑of‑plane flexural phonon mode with quadratic dispersion, cut off at a non‑zero frequency.
- **flexural phonon** — Out‑of‑plane (bending) phonon mode of a graphene layer.
- **flexure scanner** — Scanner based on flexure mechanics.
- **floquet band inversion** — Light‑induced reversal of band ordering creating Floquet topological states.
- **fluence** — Fluence for 350 fs pulses ranging from 5 fJ m⁻¹ to 40 pJ m⁻¹.
- **focal length** — Long focal length design for the terahertz optical system.
- **focus** — Concept of optical focus in microscopy.
- **force calculation** — Conversion from deflection voltage to force (e.g., 0.3 V → 0.5 µm → 20 µN).
- **force gradient** — Spatial derivative of the tip‑sample force, very large for short‑range forces.
- **force microscopy** — Microscopy technique based on measuring forces
- **force sensor (quartz tuning fork)** — Concept of using a quartz tuning fork as the AFM force transducer.
- **form factor g_{ss}(k,q,n,l)** — Overlap factor of spinon eigenstates entering the spin structure factor.
- **forward focusing** — Direction in diffraction patterns where intensity is enhanced due to atomic rows.
- **forward motion** — Direction of motion where the step size is usually larger.
- **fourier transform** — Spectral analysis of tunneling current and z‑piezo signal.
- **fractional chern insulator** — Topologically non‑trivial state with fractionalized Hall conductance.
- **fractional quantum anomalous hall effect (fqahe)** — A topological state of matter proposed to emerge in moiré pentalayer graphene.
- **fractional spinon excitations** — Spin‑½ quasiparticles emerging in quantum antiferromagnets.
- **fractionalized quasiparticles** — Excitations carrying a fraction of the electron charge, relevant to fractional quantum Hall physics.
- **free‑electron gas** — Model used for the electronic states of the metallic tip.
- **frequency** — Frequency of the rotation drive (e.g., 203 Hz, 47 Hz when in contact).
- **frequency response** — Behaviour of the STM transfer function as a function of frequency.
- **frequency shift** — Change in cantilever resonance frequency caused by tip‑sample interactions.
- **friction‑driven actuation** — Operating principle of the positioner based on friction.
- **front cover** — Front protective cover of the ANC300 controller.
- **frq dial** — Dial used to change the frequency value for the respective axis.
- **frustration** — Geometric inhibition of simple magnetic ordering due to the triangular lattice geometry.
- **function a(p)** — Smooth function entering the tunneling matrix element.
- **function b(p)** — Smooth function entering the tunneling matrix element.
- **fuses** — Fuses protecting the controller circuits.
- **f‑electron states** — Heavy‑electron band states near Γ with strong overlap with the Hartree potential.
- **f‑like electrons** — Electrons associated with the flat heavy‑electron bands.
- **f‑like orbitals** — Orbitals in the BM model that give rise to heavy‑electron flat bands.
- **g/hbn moiré potential** — Periodic electrostatic modulation arising from the lattice mismatch between graphene and hBN.
- **gap function** — Momentum‑dependent superconducting order parameter.
- **gapped chiral quantum spin liquid** — A chiral quantum spin liquid with a finite spinon gap.
- **gap‑opening broken‑symmetry state** — A hypothesised state that would open a gap at the Fermi level at integer fillings.
- **gate screening** — Reduction of the Coulomb interaction by nearby metallic gates or high‑κ dielectric layers.
- **gate voltage (v_g)** — External voltage applied to the back‑gate that controls overall carrier density.
- **gate‑defined josephson junctions** — Superconducting weak links created by electrostatic gating in magic‑angle tBG.
- **gauge coupling** — Electron‑phonon coupling term related to changes in bond length, comparable for transverse and longitudinal acoustic phonons.
- **gauge field** — Effective gauge field appearing in the Dirac description of intralayer electron‑phonon coupling at long wavelengths.
- **gauge field a_l** — Effective vector potential induced by strain.
- **gauge fluctuations** — Fluctuations of emergent gauge fields associated with spin representations.
- **gauge transformation** — A transformation that eliminates the Peierls‑like phase introduced by phonon displacements in the interlayer tunneling Hamiltonian.
- **gaussian beam** — Standard Gaussian beam model referenced from Wikipedia.
- **general n2 bypass valve** — Valve closed before venting the plasma chamber.
- **generalized gradient approximation (gga)** — Exchange‑correlation functional used in the DFT calculations.
- **generalized wigner crystal** — Electron solid formed at low density in two‑dimensional systems.
- **geometric capacitance (concept)** — Capacitance used in theoretical modelling of the device.
- **geometric triangulation of nodal momenta** — Method to locate nodal points using zero-bias resonances.
- **ginzburg‑landau theory** — Phenomenological theory describing superconducting order parameters.
- **gnd** — Ground reference pin on the SubD connector.
- **gnd functionality** — Button that grounds the outputs, disables all other functions and connects the outputs to chassis mass to reduce electro‑mechanical noise.
- **gnd mode** — Ground mode to protect the piezo from uncontrolled voltages.
- **goldstone modes** — Gapless excitations associated with spontaneous symmetry breaking.
- **gorkov equations** — Formalism allowing position‑dependent superconducting gap.
- **gpio port** — Trigger‑signal connection on the AMC300.
- **graphene fermi circles** — Fermi surface contours of graphene determining momentum matching.
- **graphene plasmonics** — Study of surface plasmons confined in graphene.
- **graphene plasmons** — Collective charge oscillations in doped graphene with sub‑wavelength confinement.
- **graphene quantum dots** — Zero‑dimensional graphene fragments exhibiting quantum confinement and photoluminescence.
- **graphene‑electron spill‑out toward the gap** — Phenomenon where electrons from graphene extend into the spacer region, expected to depend on band‑structure details.
- **graphene‑probe fermi surfaces** — Fermi surfaces of the graphene probe that rotate with the QTM tip.
- **green flag** — Reference marker displayed on the screen for alignment.
- **greens function** — Two‑particle propagator calculated for f‑ and c‑electrons within Hubbard‑I.
- **gross‑neveu quantum criticality** — Quantum critical behavior described by the Gross‑Neveu field theory.
- **ground connection to piezo- pin** — Prohibition of connecting the Piezo‑ pin to ground.
- **ground mode (gnd)** — Mode constant GND with numerical value 0.
- **ground mode (gnd)** — Mode that grounds the selected axis.
- **ground on target** — Checkbox to activate grounding of the positioner when it is within the target range (requires RES positioner).
- **ground state** — Lowest‑energy many‑body state of the system.
- **grounding** — Zero voltage applied after reaching a position to avoid noise and drift.
- **grounding plane** — Ground plane layer used in PCBs for electrical stability.
- **ground‑state charge inhomogeneity** — Spatial variation of charge density in the electronic ground state.
- **gw approximation** — Many‑body perturbation method used to compute electron self‑energy and spectral functions.
- **gw quasi‑particle correction (gw‑qp)** — Self‑energy correction that yields quasi‑particle band energies.
- **g‑factor** — Zeeman splitting factor that determines the energy of a spin in a magnetic field.
- **g‑wave altermagnetism** — Higher‑order altermagnetic symmetry leading to inequivalent J11 and J12 couplings.
- **g‑wave antiferromagnetic order** — A magnetic ground state where spins are aligned purely out‑of‑plane along the c‑axis.
- **half-filling** — Electronic filling of exactly one‑half of a moiré miniband.
- **hamiltonian h = h0 + ht + hintra + hinter** — Total Hamiltonian describing uncoupled layers, interlayer tunneling, and electron‑phonon couplings.
- **hard gap** — Energy region with vanishing density of states.
- **hartree potential** — Electrostatic potential that couples differently to light and heavy electrons.
- **hartree terms** — Mean‑field electrostatic contributions that bend light‑electron bands.
- **hartree‑driven band stretching** — Theoretical and experimental effect where the Hartree potential caused by electron filling stretches the flat bands in momentum space.
- **hartree‑fock mean‑field potential** — Mean‑field potential generated by the non‑interacting ground state.
- **heavy electron bands** — Electronic bands with high effective mass (heavy‑fermion‑like) in twisted graphene systems.
- **heavy electrons** — Quasiparticles with large effective mass observed in the Hubbard bands.
- **heavy electrons (f‑like)** — Electrons forming flat bands, described as heavy in the toy model.
- **heavy f‑electron** — Localized, heavy‑mass electronic component of MATBG’s flat bands.
- **heavy-fermion model** — Theoretical model describing MA TBG with heavy fermion behavior.
- **heavy‑fermion representation** — Theoretical description of TBG in terms of heavy‑fermion physics
- **helical magnetic order** — Magnetic order with spins rotating in a helix, characterized by propagation vector Q.
- **helical magnets** — Magnetic materials with a helical spin texture.
- **helical quantum hall edge states** — Topological edge channels in graphene/STO under quantum Hall conditions.
- **helical spin structure** — One‑dimensional chain spin arrangement with two‑fold rotational symmetries aligned with the local spin.
- **helicity** — The handedness (+ or -) of circularly polarized light.
- **helicity‑dependent photocurrent** — Photocurrent whose magnitude differs for left‑ and right‑circularly polarized light.
- **helimagnetic** — Describing a material with a helical magnetic order.
- **helmholtz equation** — Fundamental wave equation referenced for free‑space propagation.
- **hexagonal brillouin zone** — Reciprocal‑space region for graphene.
- **hidden symmetry** — Symmetry not apparent in the bare Hamiltonian but revealed by RG analysis.
- **high temperature superconductivity** — Superconductivity with a high critical temperature, a central problem in condensed‑matter physics.
- **high vacuum (hv)** — High vacuum environment.
- **high-energy band** — A faint band that appears near each integer filling and moves toward the Fermi level with increasing filling.
- **higher‑order umklapp processes** — Multi‑phonon scattering events that can affect elastic scattering at specific angles.
- **high‑spin mn²⁺ ion** — Electronic configuration that yields a large XMCD signal.
- **high‑symmetry points of the moiré potential** — Central maximum and two minima separated by 60° within a single moiré unit cell.
- **high‑symmetry sites** — Specific stacking registries (AA, CB, CN) within the moiré unit cell that possess distinct local potentials.
- **high‑tc cuprates** — Family of unconventional superconductors with strong momentum‑dependent pairing.
- **hofstadter spectrum** — Fractal energy spectrum of electrons in a lattice under magnetic field.
- **hold position option** — Option that enables periodic small steps to maintain position during thermal settling.
- **hole polarons** — Polaronic states associated with holes in hBN.
- **honeycomb lattice** — Hexagonal arrangement of carbon atoms in graphene with two sublattices A and B.
- **hooke’s law** — Linear relation between force and displacement for the cantilever.
- **horizontal position stability** — Requirement that the tip remains laterally fixed with respect to the sample.
- **horizontal rotational axis** — Rotational axis orientation achieved with horizontal mounting using M2.5 screws.
- **hot‑electron injection** — Injection of high‑energy electrons that can disturb the ground state.
- **hubbard‑i propagator** — Approximate Green’s function used for the interacting f‑site.
- **hubbard‑like bands** — Flat heavy‑electron bands split by an effective charging energy U.
- **hybridization** — Mixing between c‑ and f‑like electrons; strong hybridization defined by γ/U > 1.
- **hybridization suppression** — Reduction of electronic coupling due to a tunneling barrier.
- **hyperbola intersection of fermi surfaces** — Geometric situation occurring when the Fermi velocity of the probe is smaller than that of the sample, giving a hyperbolic intersection of the two Fermi surfaces.
- **hyperbolic equal‑energy contours** — Two‑branch hyperbolic constant‑energy lines that appear for v₀ > v_D.
- **hyperbolic intersections** — Intersection geometry (hyperbolas) occurring when tip velocity v_D < band velocity v_n.
- **hyperbolic regime (v₀ > v_d)** — Parameter regime where the δ‑function contours are hyperbolas.
- **hypermagic regime** — Parameter region with multiple magic angles.
- **idle mode** — State of the plasma asher after completing a run.
- **image potential** — Potential experienced by an electron near a surface due to its image charge.
- **imbalanced fermi level** — Different chemical potentials on source and drain leading to asymmetric transport.
- **impedance** — Electrical impedance, a measure of opposition to AC current.
- **improper ferroelectric (purely electronic origin)** — Ferroelectricity driven by electronic rather than ionic displacements, offering high endurance.
- **improper ferroelectrics of purely electronic origin** — Ferroelectrics whose polarization is driven solely by electronic rearrangements, not lattice distortions.
- **in-layer contributions** — Electron‑plasmon interactions occurring within the same layer.
- **in-layer rpa screening potential** — Random-phase‑approximation calculated potential used to probe plasmon excitations.
- **incoherent band** — Diffuse spectral feature with width comparable to its energy, observed at large negative or positive filling.
- **incoherent high-energy band** — A new band that emerges after the previous high‑energy band becomes fully occupied.
- **independent particle approximation (ipa)** — Approximation that neglects local field effects.
- **indirect exciton** — Exciton formed with electron and hole residing in different momentum valleys.
- **indirect transitions** — Tunnelling processes involving momentum change.
- **individual per axis measurement mode** — Mode where each axis is measured on a different frequency, reducing noise coupling.
- **industrial environment (en 61326-1 intended environment)** — The intended operating environment for the ANC300 as defined by EN 61326-1.
- **inelastic current density (j_inel)** — Current density contribution from inelastic tunneling, given by Eq. (S8)–(S9).
- **inelastic momentum‑conserving resonance** — Inelastic tunneling process in which a photon is emitted while momentum is conserved; maximized for zero twist angle.
- **inelastic scattering** — Process where tunneling electrons emit or absorb a collective excitation to bridge momentum mismatch.
- **inelastic tunneling** — Tunneling process accompanied by emission or absorption of a boson (e.g., plasmon).
- **infrared (ir) absorption** — Spectroscopic method that measures absorption of infrared light.
- **input mode (inp)** — Mode constant INP with numerical value 1.
- **input mode (inp)** — Mode that enables input sensing on the selected axis.
- **integer filling** — Filling factor corresponding to an integer number of electrons per moiré unit cell.
- **interacting energy bands** — Energy bands of MA‑TBG modified by electron‑electron interactions, revealed by QTM.
- **interacting hamiltonian** — Hamiltonian that includes electron‑electron interaction terms.
- **interaction effects** — Modifications of electronic properties due to electron‑electron interactions.
- **interaction matrix element g(k,q)** — Amplitude for an electron with momentum k to emit/absorb a plasmon with momentum q.
- **interactions** — General electron‑electron interaction effects.
- **interband electron transitions** — High‑bias inelastic channel competing with plasmon generation.
- **interband transitions** — Transitions between valence and conduction bands, causing Landau damping.
- **interferometric experiment** — Measurement scheme that interferes two optical paths to resolve photocurrent phase.
- **interlayer bias (vb)** — Voltage applied between the two graphene layers, shifting their chemical potentials.
- **interlayer coupling** — Coupling between layers in twisted van der Waals materials that strongly affects low‑energy collective electronic excitations and plasmon dispersions.
- **interlayer coupling of infrared‑active phonons to excitons** — Strong interaction between infrared‑active phonon modes and excitons across layers.
- **interlayer coupling phase factors** — Complex phase factors that arise from the relative stacking and twist between the two layers.
- **interlayer distance modulation** — Changing the spacing between graphene layers to tune optical transitions.
- **interlayer electron-phonon coupling** — Coupling between electrons and phonons across two stacked layers.
- **interlayer electron‑phonon coupling** — Coupling between electrons and phonons that involves tunneling between the two graphene layers, includes a phase shift of π/2 and a strong dependence on the twist angle.
- **interlayer electron‑phonon coupling** — Electron‑phonon interaction involving atoms in different layers of the junction.
- **interlayer electron‑phonon coupling** — EPC that modifies the interlayer tunnelling amplitude.
- **interlayer electron‑phonon coupling** — EPC contribution that involves phonons coupling electrons across the two graphene layers.
- **interlayer electron‑phonon interaction** — Electron‑phonon coupling that involves vibrations between adjacent layers.
- **interlayer excitons** — Excitons formed across graphene‑hBN interface with very short lifetimes.
- **interlayer hopping amplitude t_⊥(r)** — Spatially dependent tunneling amplitude between layers.
- **interlayer tunneling asymmetry** — Unequal tunneling amplitudes between layers in a bilayer system.
- **interlayer tunneling matrices (˜tₙ)** — Momentum‑independent matrices describing hopping between the two layers.
- **interlayer tunneling matrix \hat{t}_τ^n** — Matrix that couples the two graphene layers in the BM model, containing parameters w₀ and w₁.
- **interlayer tunneling strength (w)** — Amplitude that opens gaps at Dirac cone crossing points.
- **interlayer tunnelling matrix t_jαβ** — Matrix describing electron tunnelling between top and bottom layers.
- **interlocked laser head** — Safety interlock that prevents exposure to the high‑voltage laser head.
- **internal (res)** — Default sensor type when no external sensor is selected.
- **internal bus** — Internal bus used for communication between CPU and axis modules.
- **internal circuitry** — Internal circuitry of NP-Amp preamp that connects selected channel and sensor.
- **internal magnetization** — Possible magnetic ordering within a defect.
- **intersublattice tunneling parameter** — Parameter describing tunneling between opposite sublattices in the effective low‑energy model of MA TBG.
- **intervalley coherent state** — Possible ordered state at integer fillings that the QTM tip cannot directly detect.
- **intervalley optical modes** — Optical phonons near the K point of the phonon Brillouin zone that connect opposite valleys.
- **intervalley optical to/lo electron‑phonon coupling (epc)** — Electron‑phonon coupling for intervalley optical transverse/longitudinal phonons.
- **intervalley pairing** — Pairing between electrons residing in different valleys.
- **intervalley scattering** — Scattering processes that mix the K and K′ valleys in graphene.
- **inter‑layer interaction** — Coulomb interaction between electrons in different layers.
- **intraband pairing** — Superconducting pairing within the same electronic band.
- **intralayer coupling** — Coupling term within a single layer.
- **intralayer electron-phonon coupling** — Coupling between electrons and phonons within a single layer.
- **intralayer electron‑phonon coupling** — Electron‑phonon coupling that occurs within a single graphene layer, used as a reference for the interlayer coupling.
- **intralayer electron‑phonon coupling** — Electron‑phonon interaction occurring within a single graphene layer.
- **intralayer electron‑phonon coupling** — EPC contribution that involves phonons coupling electrons within the same graphene layer.
- **intralayer electron‑phonon interaction** — Electron‑phonon coupling occurring within a single graphene layer.
- **intralayer hamiltonian (hintra)** — Hamiltonian describing electron‑phonon coupling within a single graphene layer.
