- **intrasublattice tunneling parameter** — Parameter describing tunneling between identical sublattices in the effective low‑energy model of MA TBG.
- **intravalley spin‑polarized superconductivity** — Superconductivity where Cooper pairs are formed within a single valley and have a net spin polarization.
- **intravalley tunneling** — Tunneling within the same valley of adjacent layers.
- **intra‑layer interaction** — Coulomb interaction between electrons within the same layer.
- **intrinsic nonlinear conductivity** — Conductivity arising from intrinsic band‑structure effects without scattering.
- **inverse decay length (a)** — Parameter governing exponential decay of wave functions in the vacuum barrier.
- **inverse electron lifetime (γ)** — Parameter γ that determines the Lorentzian broadening of the spectral functions.
- **inversion symmetry** — Symmetry of graphene that makes polarization vectors real and equal for ±Q.
- **inversion symmetry p** — Spatial inversion operation that flips spin chirality.
- **inversion‑symmetry breaking** — Loss of inversion symmetry in the magnetic order, enabling cross‑control of spin and electric degrees of freedom.
- **invert drive direction** — Setting that inverts the drive waveform, changing the forward travel direction of the nanopositioner.
- **invert position sensor** — Setting that multiplies the sensed position by –1 before it is displayed.
- **in‑layer epc mechanism** — EPC arising from phonon‑modulated hopping within a single graphene layer.
- **in‑plane momentum conservation** — Selection rule that forces tunneling electrons to retain their in‑plane momentum.
- **in‑plane phonon** — Phonon mode polarized within the graphene plane.
- **in‑plane relaxation of graphene lattice** — Stretching and compression of C–C bonds that occur to minimize strain in the heterostructure.
- **isospin competitions** — Competing ordering tendencies in the isospin degree of freedom of twisted graphene.
- **isospin‑extended hubbard model** — Theoretical model used to simulate tunable quantum criticalities in moiré systems.
- **isotropic g‑factor correction** — Direction‑independent contribution to the g‑factor.
- **isotropic superconducting pairing** — Superconducting order parameter that is uniform in momentum space.
- **iso‑spin in fermi surface** — Valley degree of freedom considered in analysis.
- **iso‑spin in the fermi surface** — Possible mixing of valley (iso‑spin) flavors that could explain the CD doubling.
- **itinerant electron systems** — Materials where electrons are delocalized and contribute to conduction.
- **i₁(ε)** — Integral representing the joint density of states for a given energy offset ε.
- **i₂(ε)** — Integral representing the interference term in the tunneling current, weighted by a phase factor.
- **joint density of states** — Combined density of states of tip and sample spectra that enters the tunneling current as I₁(ε).
- **jump to contact** — Instability where the tip snaps into contact with the surface.
- **jump‑to‑contact** — Instability where the tip snaps to the surface when attractive forces exceed cantilever restoring force.
- **k point** — Corner of the graphene Brillouin zone where Dirac cones are located.
- **k points** — High‑symmetry points at the corners of the graphene Brillouin zone.
- **k valley** — One of the inequivalent valleys in graphene's Brillouin zone.
- **k_t momentum point (kt)** — A high‑symmetry momentum in the flat‑band Brillouin zone where spectroscopy is performed.
- **kekulé orders** — Bond‑density wave patterns competing with superconductivity in graphene.
- **kekulé spiral state** — Another possible ordered state at integer fillings beyond the tip’s sensitivity.
- **kinetomagnetism** — Phenomenon linking moving magnetic textures to electronic spin polarization.
- **kink in di/dv** — Weak feature in the differential conductance indicating onset of plasmon‑assisted tunneling.
- **kitaev quantum spin liquid** — A theoretical spin‑liquid state arising from bond‑dependent Kitaev interactions.
- **kondo coupling** — Magnetic exchange interaction that does not affect the spin splitting in NiI₂.
- **kondo interaction** — Interaction between localized magnetic moments and conduction electrons, neglected in the model.
- **kramers degeneracy** — Time‑reversal symmetry protected two‑fold degeneracy at zero momentum.
- **kramers intervalley coherent (kivc) state** — Correlated insulating state at ν = −2 in magic‑angle TBG.
- **kt momentum (flat‑band region)** — Momentum point in twisted bilayer graphene where flat bands are located.
- **kt point** — Momentum point within the flat part of the bands where tunnelling spectroscopy is performed.
- **k·p approximation** — k·p approximation of the tip and sample Hamiltonians in the τ K valley.
- **köhler illumination principle** — Microscopy illumination principle guiding the collector lens design.
- **k‑dependent interlayer tunneling amplitude (tₖ)** — Matrix element describing coupling of wavefunctions between two layers; decays exponentially with in‑plane momentum k.
- **k‑resolved onset features** — Features in tunneling spectra that depend on in‑plane momentum.
- **k‑valley dirac hamiltonian** — Low‑energy Hamiltonian for a graphene layer rotated by an angle θ.
- **k′ point** — Time‑reversed counterpart of the K point.
- **k′ valley** — Time‑reversed counterpart of the K valley.
- **k⊥σ⊥ relation** — Linear coupling between transverse momentum and spin operators (h ⟂σ⊥ ∝ k⊥σ⊥).
- **laguerre‑gauss analytics** — Analytical description of Laguerre‑Gauss modes applied to the IR beam.
- **landau damping** — Decay of collective modes into particle‑hole excitations.
- **landau levels** — Quantized energy levels of electrons in a magnetic field.
- **laplace transform** — Mathematical tool used to obtain transfer functions from differential equations.
- **large area scanning** — Capability to scan a large surface area using the 1‑inch piezo tube scanners.
- **large fermi extremum (belly - b)** — Large extremum of the Fermi surface that gives rise to a fast SdH oscillation.
- **large flavor limit** — Theoretical limit N_f → ∞ with fixed N_f v_q.
- **laser transmission** — Transmission of the THz laser through the sapphire substrate.
- **lattice collective modes** — Collective excitations arising from the moiré lattice degrees of freedom.
- **lattice defects** — Imperfections in the crystal lattice that provide momentum kicks and break momentum conservation.
- **lattice reconstruction** — Structural relaxation and stacking rearrangement at small twist angles.
- **lattice relaxation** — Structural relaxation that makes the interlayer tunneling amplitude w₁ larger than w₀.
- **layer index l** — Label (0 or 1) distinguishing the TBG and MLG layers.
- **layer polarization** — A quantity describing the distribution of electronic weight between layers.
- **layer‑antisymmetric phonon** — Phonon mode antisymmetric with respect to the two layers.
- **layer‑antisymmetric phonon mode** — Phonon mode antisymmetric between top and bottom layers.
- **layer‑symmetric phonon** — Phonon mode symmetric with respect to the two layers.
- **layer‑symmetric phonon mode** — Phonon mode symmetric between top and bottom layers.
- **left chiral helix** — Specific left‑handed spin helix whose spin polarization reverses when chirality switches.
- **left chiral spin helix** — Spin‑helix state with left handed chirality selected by field‑cooling.
- **left chiral spin helix** — Spin helix with left‑handed chirality.
- **left chiral spin helix** — Spin helix in NiI2 with left‑handed chirality.
- **left‑chiral domain** — One of the two chiral variants of a spin‑helix domain with a specific ferroelectric polarity.
- **left‑circular polarization (lcp)** — One handedness of circularly polarized light.
- **left‑handed helix** — Helical spin texture with left chirality used in the calculations.
- **lifetime broadening** — Phenomenological broadening that gives a finite width to otherwise flat energy bands.
- **lifetime‑broadening picture** — Model where the spectral‑function peak width is inversely proportional to the quasiparticle lifetime.
- **ligand‑field multiplet parameters** — Parameters describing crystal‑field splitting and multiplet effects in transition‑metal spectra.
- **light c‑electron** — Delocalized, light‑mass electronic component of MATBG’s flat bands.
- **light electron bands** — Electronic bands with low effective mass in twisted graphene systems.
- **light electrons** — Quasiparticles with small effective mass that coexist with heavy electrons.
- **light electrons (c‑like)** — Electrons with a Dirac‑like dispersion near Γ, described as light in the toy model.
- **light‑assisted tunneling** — Tunneling processes modulated by external laser illumination.
- **light‑electron bands** — Dispersive bands (c‑like) that host lighter carriers.
- **light‑matter interaction hamiltonian** — Interaction term H_I = –e/m A·p describing coupling between the electromagnetic field and electrons.
- **light‑modulated exciton peaks** — Exciton spectral features that shift or change intensity under light.
- **light‑modulated interlayer tunneling resonances** — Resonant tunneling features whose amplitude or energy shift under illumination.
- **linear dependence** — Observation that 1/Apeak varies linearly with (E‑EF) providing a better fit than quadratic dependence.
- **linear dependence of δg on vbg** — Observed linear relationship between ΔG and Vbg.
- **linear dirac spectrum** — Ideal graphene band structure with linear dispersion near the Dirac point.
- **linear dispersion (dirac)** — Ideal linear graphene band structure without interactions.
- **linear electrostatic relation** — Simplified linear relation between the chemical potentials of the layers and the applied bias voltage.
- **linear graphene dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relation E = ħ v_D κ for Dirac electrons.
- **linear graphene dispersion** — Approximation that the graphene energy–momentum relation is linear near the Dirac point for the relevant carrier densities.
- **linear intersection of dirac cones** — At V_B ≈ V_peak the two Dirac cones intersect along a straight line, enabling elastic transitions over the whole bias window.
- **linear polarization** — Electric field oscillates along a fixed direction; basis for generating circular polarization.
- **linear response theory** — Theoretical framework employed to derive the inelastic contribution to the tunneling current.
- **linear shift current** — Second‑order photocurrent mechanism.
- **linear‑in‑t resistivity** — Electrical resistivity that varies linearly with temperature.
- **liquid immersion** — Technique to cancel long‑range attractive forces by submerging tip and sample.
- **local c2 symmetry** — Two‑fold rotational symmetry that governs the spin texture within a single domain.
- **local c₂ₓ symmetry** — Symmetry that protects the antisymmetric spin‑polarization along the Q_y direction.
- **local density of optical states (ldos)** — Spectral quantity that controls the strength of inelastic photon‑assisted tunneling.
- **local density of states (ldos)** — Electronic density of states at a specific position on the surface, governing the tunneling current.
- **local field effects (lfe)** — Corrections to the dielectric response arising from microscopic field variations.
- **local magnetic moments** — Magnetic moments on individual Mn sites probed by MCD.
- **local mass term (hz)** — Sublattice‑dependent term that opens a gap in the Dirac spectrum.
- **local thermodynamic properties** — Spatially resolved quantities such as electrostatic potential and temperature.
- **longitudinal acoustic (la) phonon mode** — Acoustic phonon branch whose EPC is compared with the TA mode.
- **longitudinal acoustic mode** — Longitudinal acoustic phonon branch at long wavelengths.
- **longitudinal acoustic modes** — Phonon polarization that couples only to the deformation potential.
- **longitudinal acoustic phonon (la)** — In‑plane acoustic phonon mode polarized along propagation.
- **longitudinal motion** — Motion along the axis of the tip‑sample line, considered in the model.
- **longitudinal motion** — Vibration mode along the tip‑sample axis.
- **longitudinal optical (lo) mode** — The other intervalley optical phonon mode near the K point.
- **longitudinal optical (lo) phonon mode** — In‑plane longitudinal optical phonon.
- **longitudinal optical (lo) phonon mode** — Optical phonon branch near the K point, involved in avoided crossing with TO.
- **longitudinal optical mode** — Longitudinal optical phonon branch.
- **longitudinal optical phonon (lo)** — In‑plane optical phonon mode polarized along propagation.
- **longitudinal phonon mode** — Phonon mode polarized along its propagation direction.
- **longitudinal phonons** — Phonon modes polarized along the propagation direction, subdominant in the interlayer coupling.
- **long‑range correction approximation (lrc)** — Semi‑empirical correction that adds a long‑range screening parameter.
- **long‑range coulomb interactions** — Coulomb forces extending over many lattice spacings.
- **long‑range forces** — Forces (e.g., van der Waals, electrostatic) that affect AFM operation at larger tip‑sample separations.
- **long‑range forces** — Electrostatic, magnetic and van der Waals forces acting between tip and sample.
- **loop mode** — Selection between open loop and closed loop operation.
- **lorentzian broadening parameter η** — Broadening parameter η = 0.4 meV employed in density‑of‑states calculations.
- **lorentzian spectral function** — Spectral function with Lorentzian shape used for the sample.
- **lorentzian spectral peak** — Shape of the spectral feature in the tunneling integrand, centered at ξ ≈ ω + ε.
- **low carrier density tip** — Operating condition where the graphene tip has a small electron density, giving a small Fermi wave vector.
- **low doping** — Regime where carrier density in the sample is small.
- **low frequency version** — A lower‑frequency variant of the measurement modes, used when cables are long.
- **low temperature (lt)** — Low temperature environment.
- **low temperature limit set** — Limit configuration preset for low‑temperature operation.
- **low voltage operation** — Use of low to moderate voltages (max 60 V or 150 V) to drive the positioners.
- **low‑energy excitation** — A newly observed low‑energy excitation in magic‑angle graphene not captured by existing models.
- **low‑temperature operation** — Cooling the AFM to reduce noise and improve resolution.
- **lt – low temperature use** — Preset designation for low temperature applications.
- **lua table** — Data structure in Lua created with table.insert and used for pattern storage.
- **lut‑file** — Standardized positioner configuration file used by the Configuration upload technique.
- **lvds levels** — Differential voltage levels used for A/B channel encoding.
- **lvds mode** — Low‑voltage differential signaling mode supported by the AquadB protocol.
- **lvttl levels** — Single‑ended voltage levels used for A/B channel encoding.
- **lvttl mode** — Low‑voltage TTL mode supported by the AquadB protocol.
- **magic angle (~1.1°)** — Twist angle at which TBG hosts nearly flat bands.
- **magic twist angle** — Twist angle around 1.1° where flat bands emerge in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **magic-angle graphene superlattices** — Graphene bilayers twisted near 1.1° that host flat electronic bands.
- **magic‑angle graphene superlattices** — Periodically twisted graphene layers at a specific angle that produce flat electronic bands.
- **magnetic atoms chain** — A linear arrangement of magnetic atoms on a substrate.
- **magnetic cd pattern** — RPED pattern obtained as the difference between CD spectra for opposite Néel vector directions.
- **magnetic characterization** — Characterization of magnetic properties of surfaces and thin films.
- **magnetic field safety** — Guidelines for magnetic field safety.
- **magnetic force** — Long‑range force that can be removed by using non‑magnetic tips.
- **magnetic heterostructures** — Heterostructures that exhibit magnetic order.
- **magnetic mount** — Mount that uses magnets to hold optical components; its placement influences the visible path.
- **magnetic nonreciprocity** — Reciprocity breaking caused by an out‑of‑plane magnetic field in a non‑centrosymmetric system via the quantum metric.
- **magnetic surfaces** — Surfaces possessing a net magnetic moment.
- **magnetization** — Net magnetic moment per unit volume.
- **magnonic device** — Device concept that exploits magnon transport in altermagnets.
- **magnons** — Spin wave excitations mentioned in the THz motivation.
- **magnon‑plasmon interactions** — Coupling between magnetic excitations and charge plasmons.
- **main power supply connector** — IEC connector for the mains supply (100 / 115 / 230 V, 50/60 Hz, max 100 VA).
- **main power switch** — Switch that turns the whole electronics unit on or off.
- **maintenance** — Routine check and verification of FIRL100 performance.
- **manual ip setting** — User‑defined static IP configuration for the ANC300.
- **manual optimization** — User‑adjustable parameters, especially for the crystal, that must be handled with care.
- **manual writing field** — User‑defined writing field (e.g., 250 µm WF – Manual ALWF 50 µm).
- **many‑body correlations** — Correlated electronic phenomena in strongly interacting systems, observed in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **many‑body excitations** — Excited states of an interacting electron system beyond single‑particle picture.
- **many‑particle tunnelling** — Tunnelling theory that accounts for interactions among many electrons.
- **mask size** — Size parameter of the lithography mask.
- **mass matrix m_j** — Matrix containing the masses of the rigid bodies for model j.
- **massless spring model** — Assumption that piezo elements behave as springs without mass.
- **mass‑spring model** — Simplified mechanical representation used to derive the transfer functions.
- **matrix element m** — Electronic matrix element assumed constant in the tunnelling analysis.
- **matrix element of tunneling** — Integral over the gap region that couples tip and surface wave functions.
- **matrix elements** — Overlap integrals governing tunneling probability between tip and sample states.
- **matsubara frequencies** — Imaginary‑frequency formalism used to evaluate finite‑temperature self‑energy expressions.
- **mean-field approximation** — Approximation that neglects gauge fluctuations and enforces constraints on average.
- **mean-field hartree-fock** — Mean-field approximation used for MA TBG.
- **measurement dimension** — Three dimensions of measurement: bias, angle, and wavelength (FTIR).
- **mechanical friction** — Friction arising from commensurate stacking of graphene layers.
- **mechanical interference** — Unwanted contact between the cantilever clip (or other AFM parts) and the sample during rotation.
- **mechanical relays** — Relays used to select the active channel.
- **memory of domain distribution** — Retention of domain pattern after thermal cycling.
- **metal boundary condition** — Solution for 10 µm light assuming metallic boundaries in the waveguide.
- **metal‑insulator‑semiconductor (mis) diode** — Electronic device architecture built from 2D nanomaterials.
- **microscopic origin of superconductivity** — Underlying electronic mechanism that generates Cooper pairing.
- **micro‑positioning** — Fine adjustment of optical components using micrometric screws or micro‑positioners.
- **mid-infrared generation** — The process of generating radiation in the mid‑infrared spectral region (and partly terahertz).
- **mid‑ir crystals** — Crystal materials whose transparency extends far into the infrared and are used for mid‑IR generation.
- **mini brillouin zone (mbz)** — Reduced Brillouin zone of the moiré superlattice.
- **mini brillouin zone (mini‑bz)** — Reduced Brillouin zone of the moiré superlattice where K and K′ valleys are folded.
- **mini brillouin zone (mini‑bz)** — Reduced Brillouin zone of the moiré superlattice in TBG.
- **minimum spot size** — Theoretical minimum spot size for 10 µm light at L = 1 m.
- **mirror symmetry** — Symmetry of conductance features around the twist angle.
- **mirrored appearance** — Mirrored appearance of features in forward and backward scans indicating external vibrations.
- **misalignment angle** — Deviation from the target twist angle, often expressed as θ > 5° for intentionally misaligned devices.
- **missing‑row geometry** — Structural motif where rows of surface atoms are missing, characteristic of Au(110) reconstructions.
- **mlg probe dispersion** — Intrinsic energy‑momentum relation of the monolayer graphene probe, used for calibration.
- **mn site a** — One of the two equivalent Mn sites in the α‑MnTe unit cell.
- **mn site b** — Second Mn site equivalent to site A in α‑MnTe.
- **modal analysis** — Analysis of the vibrational modes of the STM structure.
- **mode** — Operational mode of an axis (e.g., ground, input, capacitance, offset, step).
- **mode constants** — Predefined mode constants (GND, INP, CAP, OSV, STP, STPP, STPM) that map to numerical values for device operation.
- **mode velocity** — Propagation speed of acoustic phonon modes.
- **modular platform concept** — Design principle of the Zeiss Crossbeam allowing upgrades such as the LaserFIB.
- **modular platform concept** — Design allowing upgrades such as LaserFIB.
- **modular positioning unit** — Stackable combination of positioners and scanners forming a multidimensional positioning system.
- **moiré bands** — Electronic bands that arise from the moiré superlattice formed by twisted layers.
- **moiré brillouin zone** — Brillouin zone of the moiré superlattice used for the q‑grid.
- **moiré brillouin zone (mbz)** — Reduced Brillouin zone arising from the moiré superlattice of twisted layers.
- **moiré cell** — Periodic superlattice formed by the lattice mismatch and twist between graphene and hBN.
- **moiré centre** — Central point of the moiré superlattice around which symmetry is evaluated.
- **moiré excitons** — Excitonic states confined in moiré superlattice potentials of twisted TMD bilayers.
- **moiré exciton‑photon‑tunneling trihybridization** — Hybridization among moiré excitons, photons, and tunneling electrons.
- **moiré filling ν≈0** — Electron density corresponding to charge neutrality of the moiré superlattice.
- **moiré flat band** — Nearly dispersionless electronic band arising from moiré superlattice.
- **moiré hamiltonian** — Effective low‑energy Hamiltonian describing electrons in the graphene/hBN moiré superlattice.
- **moiré heterostrain** — Strain variation across a moiré pattern that can modify its periodicity.
- **moiré heterostructure** — Stacked 2D materials with a moiré superlattice.
- **moiré heterostructures** — Stacked 2D materials with a twist‑induced moiré pattern.
- **moiré lattice** — Superlattice formed by the twist between graphene layers, visible in AFM images.
- **moiré minibands** — Electronic miniband structure arising from moiré superlattice in twisted layers.
- **moiré pattern** — Interference pattern arising from a twist between two lattices.
- **moiré patterns** — Long-wavelength interference patterns arising from lattice mismatch or twist.
- **moiré period** — Length scale of the superlattice formed by twisting two graphene layers.
- **moiré periodicity** — Spatial period of the superlattice formed by the graphene/hBN lattice mismatch.
- **moiré phonons** — Phonon modes emerging from the quasiperiodic moiré superlattice of twisted interfaces.
- **moiré potential** — The periodic electrostatic potential arising from the moiré superlattice in a vdW interface such as G/hBN.
- **moiré potential** — Electrostatic potential modulation arising from the moiré superlattice of graphene/hBN.
- **moiré potential** — Spatially varying electronic potential arising from lattice mismatch or twist.
- **moiré potentials** — Periodic potential landscape generated by twist‑induced moiré pattern.
- **moiré reciprocal lattice vectors** — Reciprocal vectors (ḡ₁, ḡ₂, ḡ₃) defining the moiré superlattice in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **moiré reciprocal vectors q_n** — Reciprocal lattice vectors of the moiré superlattice in TBG.
- **moiré structure** — Long‑wavelength superlattice formed by the interference of two rotated graphene lattices.
- **moiré superlattice** — Long‑wavelength interference pattern arising from a small twist between two layers.
- **moiré superlattice** — Long‑wavelength periodic modulation that arises from the lattice mismatch and relative twist between graphene and hBN.
- **moiré superlattice** — Long‑wavelength periodic structure that emerges at specific twist angles between two graphene layers.
- **moiré superlattice** — Long‑period lattice formed by a small twist between graphene and hBN.
- **moiré system** — Superlattice formed by stacking 2D materials with a twist angle.
- **moiré twisting** — Geometric manipulation of the twist angle that controls tip radius and shape.
- **moiré‑controlled charge transfer dynamics** — Charge transfer rates modulated by the moiré superlattice.
- **moiré‑induced electronic structure** — Electronic band reconstruction caused by moiré patterns.
- **momentum conservation (ħδk)** — Condition k_B – k_T = ħΔK that must be satisfied for elastic tunneling.
- **momentum cut in bz** — Line in the Brillouin zone accessed by a given twist angle.
- **momentum k_m** — Direction of the emitted plasmon wave vector in the illustrated inelastic process.
- **momentum kθ** — Reciprocal‑lattice vector of the twisted bilayer graphene.
- **momentum kθ_tbg** — Reciprocal‑lattice vector of the untwisted bilayer graphene reference.
- **momentum m** — Another high‑symmetry point in the mini‑Brillouin zone used for measurements.
- **momentum mismatch** — Mismatch of parallel wave‑vectors between Dirac cones that forbids direct tunneling.
- **momentum mismatch** — Difference in momentum between electronic bands of twisted layers that requires phonon emission.
- **momentum separation (q_0)** — Offset in momentum space between tip and sample Dirac cones set by the twist angle.
- **momentum space** — Reciprocal‑space representation used to explain how phonon emission supplies missing momentum.
- **momentum κ′** — Specific point in the mini‑Brillouin zone where tunneling matrix elements are suppressed.
- **momentum-dependent spin splitting** — Spin splitting that varies with electron momentum in the Brillouin zone.
- **momentum-resolved electronic spectrum** — Electronic spectrum resolved in momentum space examined in the measurements.
- **momentum-resolved one-particle spectral function** — Spectral function measured along the tip Dirac point trajectory Kθ.
- **momentum-resolved one-particle spectral function** — The one‑particle spectral function of the sample resolved in momentum along the tip Dirac point trajectory.
- **momentum-resolved superconducting gap** — Superconducting gap extracted as a function of momentum along the Fermi line.
- **momentum‑conserving phonon emission** — Phonon emission that conserves in‑plane momentum.
- **momentum‑dependent dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relationship that varies with momentum, probed by the QTM.
- **momentum‑dependent spin splitting** — Spin splitting that depends on momentum arising from collinear antiferromagnetic ordering.
- **momentum‑dependent wavefunctions** — Wavefunctions of the flat bands that vary strongly with crystal momentum, reflecting the non‑trivial topology of MATBG.
- **momentum‑independent pairing** — Superconducting pairing potential that does not vary with momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relation of excitations accessed by varying twist angle and bias.
- **momentum‑resolved elastic tunnelling** — Elastic tunnelling process resolved in momentum space.
- **momentum‑resolved electron‑phonon couplings** — Coupling strengths as a function of momentum that can be extracted from QTM spectra.
- **momentum‑resolved energy dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relationship extracted from tunneling spectra.
- **momentum‑resolved energy dispersion** — The relationship E(k) of electronic bands resolved at specific crystal momenta.
- **momentum‑resolved epc** — EPC measured as a function of electron momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved spectra** — Spectral information that retains the electron momentum, accessible via QTM tunneling.
- **momentum‑resolved spectroscopy** — Measurement that resolves electronic states as a function of crystal momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling** — Tunneling process whose amplitude depends on the momentum mismatch between electronic states of the two layers.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling** — Tunnelling process conserving in‑plane momentum between tip and TBG.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling** — Spectroscopic technique that resolves electronic states as a function of in‑plane momentum.
- **momentum‑space matching** — Alignment of electronic states in k‑space between two layers.
- **momentum‑space spectral function** — Spectral function evaluated as a function of momentum and filling for both f‑ and c‑sectors.
- **momentum‑space spin polarization** — Spin expectation values ⟨σ(k)⟩ resolved in momentum space, calculated for the helimagnetic state.
- **momentum‑square‑dependent zeeman term (g₄₃)** — Zeeman contribution proportional to the square of the electron momentum, identified in the GaAs quantum‑dot experiment.
- **monolayer graphene dirac cones** — The Dirac cones of each graphene layer whose separation encodes the local twist angle.
- **motion aspects** — Parameters such as speed, step size, amplitude and frequency that define axis motion.
- **mott insulating state** — Electron correlation driven insulating phase.
- **move arrow** — UI arrow that initiates a move command when tapped.
- **move command** — Command sent via UI, remote interface, or trigger input to move the nanopositioner.
- **movement direction** — Direction of piezo actuator motion resulting from drive signals.
- **moving sample mechanism** — Coarse approach realized by translating the sample (model 2).
- **moving steel** — Moving steel objects near the magnet.
- **moving tip mechanism** — Coarse approach realized by translating the tip (model 3).
- **multidomain sample** — Sample containing many altermagnetic domains with different Néel vector orientations.
- **multiferroic** — Materials that simultaneously exhibit two or more ferroic orders, e.g., ferroelectricity and magnetism.
- **multiferroic phase (type ii)** — Phase of NiI2 below ~60 K where magnetic order and ferroelectricity coexist.
- **multiferroicity** — Coexistence of two or more primary ferroic orders (e.g., ferroelectricity and magnetism).
- **multiferroics** — Materials exhibiting coupled ferroelectric and magnetic order.
- **multimodal propagation** — Propagation of many waveguide modes (1, 10, 100, 1000) simultaneously.
- **multiplexor** — Multiplexor used by NP-Amp preamp to select channels.
- **multispinon continua** — Continuum of excitations formed by multiple spinons in a 1D magnet.
- **multi‑axis operation** — Capability to move in more than one degree of freedom simultaneously.
- **multi‑band moiré systems** — Materials with several electronic bands arising from a moiré superlattice.
- **n2 valve** — Valve opened after venting to control nitrogen flow.
- **naive layer polarization (pt)** — Simplified model of layer polarization used for comparison with experiment.
- **nanometer precision** — Ability to achieve positioning accuracy on the nanometre scale.
- **nanopositioning** — Nanometer‑scale positioning capability offered by attocube piezo‑driven positioners.
- **narrow electron bands** — Electronic bands with small bandwidth, enhancing interaction effects.
- **natural optical activity** — Rotation of the polarization plane of light in a chiral medium without external fields.
- **nd attenuation** — Attenuation of the laser beam controlled by the neutral density filter.
- **nearly orthogonal tip‑sample states** — Assumption that tip and sample wavefunctions are almost orthogonal.
- **negf current formula** — Exact tunneling current expression derived from nonequilibrium Green functions.
- **nematic pairing** — Pairing state that breaks rotational symmetry while preserving translational symmetry.
- **nematic topological semimetal** — Phase combining nematic order with topologically protected band crossings.
- **nesting condition** — Condition where Fermi surfaces of two layers nest, enhancing tunneling.
- **nesting line** — Condition in (θ, V_b) space where the tip and sample bands become parallel, giving rise to tunneling resonances.
- **nesting lines** — Curved‑X features in the twist‑angle vs bias‑voltage plane corresponding to momentum‑resolved nesting.
- **nesting of dirac dispersions** — Approximate alignment of tip and sample Dirac cones caused by an electrostatic shift eφ.
- **network settings** — Configuration parameters stored in attoIPconfig.json.
- **neutral collective modes** — Collective excitations without net charge, such as phonons, magnons, spinons, etc.
- **newton’s second law** — Relation between force, mass and acceleration.
- **no contact sensing** — Sensing method without physical contact, eliminating hysteresis and wear.
- **no sample preparation** — Raman measurement can be performed directly on the sample without preprocessing.
- **nodal band‑off‑diagonal superconductivity** — Superconducting order that couples different bands and features nodes in the gap.
- **nodal line ky = kz = 0** — Momentum‑space line where spin polarization changes sign.
- **nodal points** — Momentum locations where the superconducting gap vanishes.
- **nonanalytic behavior of i(φ)** — Kinked, non‑analytic dependence of the tunneling current on band offset when v₀ < v_D (sharp Dirac cone).
- **noncollinear magnetism** — Magnetic order where spins are not parallel or antiparallel.
- **nonlinear dispersion** — Deviation of the graphene Dirac bands from a perfectly linear relationship, inferred from the splitting of nesting lines.
- **nonlinear electron‑phonon coupling** — Higher‑order coupling between electrons and phonons beyond the linear regime.
- **nonlocal moments** — Magnetic moments arising from nonlocal electronic correlations.
- **nonmonotonic force curve** — Challenge in AFM where the force–distance curve is not monotonic.
- **nonrelativistic spin‑momentum coupling** — Coupling between electron spin and crystal momentum without relativistic effects.
- **non‑abelian kitaev spin liquids** — Kitaev spin liquids supporting non‑Abelian anyonic excitations.
- **non‑collinear dft** — Density‑functional theory formalism allowing spin directions to vary spatially.
- **non‑collinear spins** — Spin configurations where magnetic moments are oriented at arbitrary angles relative to each other.
- **non‑collinear spins** — Spin arrangement where neighboring spins are not parallel.
- **non‑hybridized f‑electron propagator** — Propagator G(ω) for f‑electrons before inclusion of hybridization.
- **non‑local effects** — Dependence of conductivity on both frequency and wave‑vector.
- **non‑magnetic version made from titanium** — Design concept for positioners that are non‑magnetic by using titanium construction.
- **non‑relativistic band structure** — Band structure calculated without spin‑orbit coupling, showing identical spin polarization for cycloidal and helical components.
- **non‑relativistic spin** — Spin degree of freedom that originates from non‑relativistic (exchange) interactions.
- **non‑relativistic spin splitting (nrss)** — Spin splitting in electronic bands that does not rely on relativistic effects.
- **non‑trivial topology** — Topological character of the MATBG flat bands that leads to momentum‑dependent wavefunctions.
- **non‑vacuum condition** — Experimental condition where air is present and its refractive index must be compensated.
- **np‑amp channel** — Channel that can be wired to a nanopositioner.
- **n 2pz orbital** — Nitrogen 2pz orbital in hBN.
- **octupole‑driven effect** — Electronic or transport phenomena governed by octupolar symmetry of the magnetic order.
- **od attenuation** — Attenuation of the laser beam controlled by the optical density filter.
- **odd‑parity character** — Parity property of the non‑relativistic spin polarization that is preserved without SOC.
- **odd‑parity magnet** — Magnet whose spin polarization has odd parity, leading to large non‑relativistic spin splitting.
- **odd‑parity non‑relativistic spin polarization** — Spin polarization with odd parity that couples directly to spin chirality.
- **odd‑parity spin polarization** — Spin texture lacking inversion symmetry, probed by CPGE.
- **off-shell self-energy** — Self‑energy evaluated at energies far from the Bloch band energies.
- **offset + step mode (stpp)** — Mode constant STPP with numerical value 5.
- **offset + step mode (stpp)** — Mode that combines a positive offset with stepping.
- **offset - step mode (stpm)** — Mode that combines a negative offset with stepping.
- **offset mode (osv)** — Mode constant OSV with numerical value 3.
- **offset mode (osv)** — Mode that applies a static voltage offset to the selected axis.
- **offset – step mode (stpm)** — Mode constant STPM with numerical value 6.
- **on/off** — Binary state used to set or get input values.
- **one-dimensional model** — Simplified mechanical model used to predict STM response.
- **one-step indirect tmd exciton creation** — Exciton creation in transition‑metal dichalcogenides (TMD) via a single indirect process.
- **one‑dimensional model** — Simple model used to derive STM design optimization criteria.
- **one‑particle spectral function** — Energy‑ and momentum‑resolved electronic density of states probed by dI/dV.
- **onset bias** — Minimum bias voltage at which inelastic tunneling into remote bands becomes possible.
- **onset line** — Linear feature in the (θ, V_b) map defined by V_b = ±ħ v_F K_D θ, used to extract the effective Fermi velocity.
- **open loop mode** — Control mode where the controller drives a positioner without feedback.
- **open loop motion** — Open loop motion mode of the nanopositioner.
- **open loop positioner** — Positioner operated without feedback encoder.
- **open loop positioning mode** — Mode of operation where the axis moves without position feedback.
- **openingdata_x response** — Response data returned by the ANC300 after an Open command (available only via Ethernet).
- **open‑loop command** — Motion command that runs without position feedback, e.g. step or continuous moves until stopped.
- **open‑loop control** — Control mode without feedback used by ANS scanners.
- **oppenheimers perturbation theory** — First-order perturbation theory used to approximate transition probabilities between tip and sample states.
- **optical absorption** — Interaction of light with electronic states.
- **optical and plasmonic properties** — Response of a material to optical and plasmonic excitations.
- **optical extinction** — Loss of incident light intensity due to absorption and scattering by plasmons.
- **optical lo mode** — Longitudinal optical phonon near the K point.
- **optical phonon** — Higher‑energy phonon with relatively flat dispersion.
- **optical phonon** — High‑energy lattice vibration mode.
- **optical phonon modes** — Higher‑energy phonon modes (e.g., ZO).
- **optical phonons** — High‑energy lattice vibrations where neighboring atoms move out of phase.
- **optical phonons** — Higher‑energy phonon modes with relatively flat dispersion.
- **optical plasmons** — Higher-energy collective charge oscillations in the double-layer graphene system.
- **optically pumped gas laser** — Laser that relies on absorption of pump radiation to create population inversion in a gaseous medium.
- **optics‑free devices** — Devices that operate using plasmons without mediation of photons.
- **optimal amplitude** — Amplitude that minimizes vertical noise in frequency‑modulation AFM.
- **optimal positioning and detecting performance** — Desired performance achieved by obeying cabling restrictions.
- **optimization criteria** — Conditions relating resonance frequencies to achieve reduced response.
- **optimization criterion** — Mathematical condition used to improve the performance of an STM design.
- **optoelectronic properties** — Electronic and optical response of van der Waals stacks measured by RT‑QTOM.
- **orange face** — Side of the stack where the PMMA layer is exposed.
- **orbital basis** — Original basis of spinon operators before diagonalisation.
- **orbital‑resolved bands** — Band structure plotted with contributions from specific atomic orbitals (C 2pz, B 2pz, N 2pz).
- **order** — Spontaneous symmetry‑breaking patterns emerging in the ground state.
- **order‑parameter symmetry** — Symmetry properties of the superconducting order parameter in two‑dimensional superconductors.
- **origin** — Reference coordinate (0,0) for writing designs.
- **origin correction** — Adjustment of the (U,V) coordinate origin using fiducial markers.
- **oscillation amplitude (a)** — Peak displacement of the oscillating cantilever; large A reduces sensitivity to short‑range forces, small A enhances it.
- **out trigger** — General purpose output trigger lines (Out 1‑4).
- **out-of-plane phonons (za, zo)** — Flexural acoustic (ZA) and optical (ZO) phonon modes perpendicular to the graphene plane.
- **output bncs** — BNC outputs capable of up to 160 V DC and 4.5 A peak.
- **output trigger high/low** — Digital level (high or low) of the output trigger.
- **output value** — Measured output voltage or current from the ANC300.
- **output voltage limit** — User‑set maximum for the sum of offset and stepping voltage on the ANM300 outputs.
- **out‑of‑plane phonon modes** — Phonon modes with atomic motion perpendicular to the graphene plane (ZA and ZO).
- **overlapping fermi surfaces** — Overlap of Fermi contours in the extended Brillouin zone.
- **p_y‑wave pairing** — Anisotropic pairing with p_y symmetry.
- **pairing glue** — Bosonic excitation that mediates electron pairing in a superconductor, hypothesized to be plasmons in MATBG.
- **pairing magnitude** — Absolute value of the superconducting order parameter as a function of momentum.
- **pairing mechanism** — Microscopic origin of Cooper pair formation.
- **pairing potential** — Momentum‑dependent superconducting order parameter.
- **pairing symmetry** — Symmetry classification of the superconducting order parameter (e.g., s‑wave, d‑wave).
- **parabolic band** — A 2D band with quadratic dispersion used to illustrate step features in dI/dV.
- **parabolic bands** — Two quadratic dispersion bands used in the toy model to mimic the conduction and valence flat bands of TBG.
- **parabolic regime (v₀ = v_d)** — Parameter regime where the δ‑function contours become parabolas.
- **parallel geometry (q ∥ x)** — Experimental geometry where the photon momentum vector is parallel to the x axis.
- **parameter** — Identifier used in ANC300_Preferences.vi to select which quantity to read or write.
- **partial moiré filling** — Filling factor ν ranging between –1 and +1 that controls the occupation of moiré superlattice states.
- **particle‑hole continuum** — Region of momentum‑energy space where interband particle‑hole excitations exist.
- **particle‑hole continuum** — Region of the excitation spectrum where single particle‑hole excitations exist, delimiting undamped plasmon regimes.
- **particle‑hole interaction vertex** — Vertex describing scattering between particle‑hole pairs in the self‑energy diagrams.
- **particle‑hole mixture** — Superposition of electron‑like and hole‑like components in a superconducting state.
- **particle‑hole spectral function** — Spectral function used as an analogy for Dirac QSL calculations.
- **particle‑hole symmetry** — Symmetry relating electron and hole contributions, used to simplify the current expression.
- **particle‑hole vertex** — Interaction vertex restricted to the screening potential generated by charge fluctuations.
- **passively stable design** — Mechanical and optical design that ensures long‑term stability without active feedback.
- **pattern down table** — Table of 256 values defining the downward voltage pattern for an axis.
- **pattern table** — Lua table used to store a sequence of pattern values (e.g., up‑pattern).
- **pattern up table** — Table of 256 values defining the upward voltage pattern for an axis.
- **pauli exclusion principle** — Principle stating that at most one electron may occupy a given quantum state.
- **pauli matrices σ_i** — Pauli matrices used to represent sublattice pseudospin.
- **pauli principle** — Requirement that the tunneling electron originates from an occupied state and ends in an empty state.
- **pause button** — Button that disconnects the sensors from the NP‑Amp preamp, pausing the position readout.
- **pause position readout** — State in which the position measurement is disabled.
- **peak‑valley ratio** — Ratio of peak current to valley current in the NDR region, used as a performance metric.
- **peek connector** — Connector whose housing is made of PEEK.
- **peek connector** — Connector made of polyether‑ether‑ketone, used for newer positioners.
- **peierls phase** — Phase factor acquired by electrons tunneling between layers due to phonon‑induced lattice displacements.
- **penetration zeeman term (g_p)** — Smaller Zeeman contribution originating from wave‑function penetration into the AlGaAs barrier.
- **perdew‑burke‑ernzerhof (pbe) functional** — Specific GGA functional employed in the first‑principles modelling.
- **performance improvement** — Enhanced operational characteristics of the STM after design changes.
- **periodic electrostatic potential v(r)** — Electrostatic modulation imposed by the moiré superlattice on the tip.
- **perpendicular geometry (q ∥ z)** — Experimental geometry where the photon momentum vector is parallel to the z axis.
- **perpendicular orientation** — Mounting configuration where one component is placed perpendicular to another, e.g., goniometer stacking.
- **persistent spin helix** — A spin texture protected against certain types of scattering.
- **perturbative calculation** — Theoretical expansion used to evaluate tunneling currents order by order in the electron‑phonon coupling.
- **perturbative theory** — Low‑order expansion approach to interlayer coupling.
- **phase matching** — Design goal where tip and sample respond in phase to disturbances.
- **phase shift π/2** — A constant π/2 phase factor introduced by the unitary transformation in the interlayer electron‑phonon coupling.
- **phase‑locked loop** — Electronic feedback system used to lock onto a signal phase.
- **phason** — Collective mode associated with moiré lattice reconstruction.
- **phason electron‑phonon coupling (epc)** — The electron‑phonon coupling associated with the phason collective mode in twisted van‑der‑Waals heterostructures.
- **phason mode** — Collective mode associated with sliding of layers.
- **phonon** — Quantized lattice vibrations.
- **phonon dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relationship of lattice vibrational modes.
- **phonon energy (ω_ph)** — Energy of the emitted phonon; the turn‑on bias satisfies Vb = ω_ph.
- **phonon injection** — Generation of lattice vibrations during tunnelling.
- **phonon mini‑brillouin zone** — Reduced Brillouin zone for phonons in the moiré superlattice.
- **phonon modes** — Vibrational excitations of the lattice.
- **phonon spectroscopy** — Technique to probe phonon dispersions and electron‑phonon couplings.
- **phonon wave vector q** — Wave vector of the emitted phonon, approximately equal to one of the qj vectors.
- **phonons** — Lattice vibrations discussed as THz excitations.
- **phonon‑assisted resonant tunnelling** — Tunnelling process where acoustic phonons mediate momentum mismatch between graphene and gold states.
- **phonon‑assisted tunnelling** — Tunnelling channel where a phonon is emitted before exciton creation.
- **phonon‑phonon interactions** — Interactions between phonons affecting their lifetimes.
- **photon‑assisted interlayer tunneling** — Interlayer tunneling processes assisted by photon absorption.
- **photon‑assisted tunneling** — Tunneling events accompanied by absorption or emission of photons, creating sidebands.
- **photovoltaic devices** — Solar cells that convert light into electricity.
- **photovoltaic materials** — Materials capable of converting light into electricity.
- **photo‑tunneling** — Tunneling of electrons induced by optical excitation across the hBN barrier.
- **piezo drive signal** — Piezo drive signal used to actuate the nanopositioner.
- **piezo positioning** — Concept of positioning using piezoelectric actuation.
- **piezo scanning tube** — Piezoelectric tube used for scanning motion.
- **pin assignment** — Pin assignment table for connector pins.
- **pipette tool** — Software cursor used to pick coordinates from the image of the sample.
- **plaid‑like spin splitting** — A spin‑splitting pattern resembling a plaid (checkerboard) in momentum space.
- **plasmon density of states** — Momentum‑unresolved density of plasmon states that can be related to the differential conductance.
- **plasmon dispersion** — Relation between plasmon energy ħω_Qθ and its wave vector Qθ.
- **plasmon frequency (ω)** — Energy of a plasmon mode at a given wave vector.
- **plasmon mode** — Collective charge oscillation mode in the doped monolayer graphene tip.
- **plasmon polaritons** — Hybrid light‑matter excitations involving collective charge oscillations.
- **plasmon satellite** — Feature in the tunneling spectrum arising from electron‑plasmon coupling.
- **plasmon spectrum** — The energy‑momentum dispersion of plasmons, predicted in Refs. [8,11,18] and expected to appear in differential conductance.
- **plasmon umklapp** — Momentum‑nonconserving plasmon scattering processes.
- **plasmon zero‑point motion** — Quantum fluctuations of the plasmon field that enter the electron‑plasmon coupling.
- **plasmon zero‑point potential (φ_q)** — Potential associated with the zero‑point motion of a plasmon mode, entering the electron‑plasmon interaction matrix element.
- **plasmons** — Collective charge oscillations in 2D electron systems.
- **plasmon‑assisted inelastic tunneling** — Tunneling process in which a plasmon is absorbed or emitted during electron transfer.
- **plasmon‑assisted resonant tunnelling** — Tunnelling process mediated by collective electron oscillations (plasmons).
- **plasmon‑assisted tunneling** — Inelastic tunneling where a plasmon is emitted during the transition.
- **plasmon‑induced superconductivity** — Superconductivity mediated by plasmon exchange.
- **plasmon‑phonon hybridization** — Hybridization between plasmons and phonons in the system.
- **plasmon‑phonon‑polariton** — Hybrid excitation of plasmons, phonons and polaritons in van der Waals heterostructures.
- **plasmon‑phonon‑polariton coupling** — Hybridization of graphene plasmons with STO phonons.
- **pn junction** — Electrostatic region where electron‑type (n) and hole‑type (p) carrier densities meet, induced by the contact‑region electrostatics.
- **point contact** — Regime where tip makes physical contact with sample
- **point group s₆** — Point‑group symmetry that does not contain the rotation of the space‑group operation.
- **point probe approximation** — Idealized limit where the STM tip is treated as a mathematically point‑like probe.
- **polar gas molecule** — Molecule with a permanent dipole moment, enabling rotational transitions in FIR lasers.
- **polar rank‑3 tensor mij** — Antisymmetric part of a polar rank‑3 tensor coupling spins to electric dipole.
- **polarization of electron states towards bottom layer** — Strong layer polarization of states at κ′ reducing tunneling matrix elements.
- **polarization vectors** — Vectors εα r,Q describing the displacement pattern of sublattice α for a phonon mode.
- **polarons** — Quasiparticles formed by electrons dressed with lattice polarization.
- **pole holder screw** — Screw that holds the pole; it became loose after contact with the purging box.
- **pollution degree 2** — Compliance classification indicating the level of environmental pollution tolerance for the equipment.
- **population inversion** — Transfer of molecular population from a lower to an upper energy level enabling stimulated emission.
- **position** — Physical concept of position measured in meters.
- **position encoder** — Device that provides feedback on position, can be resistive or optoelectronic.
- **position encoding capability** — Capability to encode position using capacitive sensing.
- **position feedback** — Information about the current position of the axis used in closed‑loop operation.
- **position list** — List of exposure jobs (lines) that define where the beam will write.
- **position sensor** — Position sensor signals from the nanopositioner.
- **positioner axis** — Individual axis of the positioner that can be activated and measured.
- **positioner control cable** — Cable set delivered with attocube positioner systems.
- **positioner grounding** — Behaviour where the axis is electrically grounded when it lies within the target range.
- **positioner obstacle stop** — Behaviour where the positioner halts motion when encountering a physical obstacle.
- **positioner type** — Field in the configuration view to select the type of positioner (default: ANPx101).
- **positive feedback control** — Control method that ensures the QTF always oscillates at its resonance frequency.
- **potential amplitude** — Peak‑to‑peak variation of the electrostatic potential (≈60 mV) measured at the moiré interface.
- **potential difference (φ)** — Electrostatic potential difference φ_T – φ_S between tip and sample.
- **potential ϕq** — Electrostatic potential associated with the plasmon zero‑point motion.
- **power** — THz beam power range 0.1–100 mW.
- **power density** — Power density range 0.015–110 W m⁻¹.
- **powermeter scanning** — Technique of moving a powermeter along the beam to map power distribution.
- **preconditioning** — 20 min pre‑etch step before the RIE graphene etching.
- **preventive maintenance (concept)** — Concept of scheduled upkeep to preserve performance.
- **probe dirac points** — Dirac points of the probe that trace a circular trajectory as the tip rotates.
- **profile (yellow/red)** — Configuration profile that can be edited independently of the active profile.
- **projected density of states (pdos)** — Energy‑resolved density of surface states projected onto the tunnelling direction.
- **propagation vector** — Vector defining the direction and period of the spin helix, taken along y∥[120] in the model.
- **propagation vector qy** — Direction of the LD domain propagation along the crystallographic [120] direction.
- **proper‑screw helical part** — Component of the spin helix where spins rotate around the propagation vector.
- **proper‑screw spin helix** — Incommensurate screw‑type spin helix observed in NiI₂ with a specific propagation vector.
- **proper‑screw spiral in x–z plane** — Alternative spiral orientation showing a spin‑polarization axis parallel to the y direction.
- **proper‑screw type magnetic order** — A helical magnetic structure that can induce ferroelectric polarization.
- **proximity-induced spin-orbit coupling** — Spin-orbit coupling induced in tip.
- **pseudoelectric potential (h0)** — Effective scalar potential arising from spatial variations of the graphene/hBN stacking and lattice relaxation.
- **pseudomagnetic field (hxy)** — Effective magnetic field generated by strain‑induced gauge fields in the graphene lattice.
- **pt symmetry** — Combined parity and time‑reversal symmetry broken by the spin helix.
- **p‑wave character** — Momentum‑dependent antisymmetric character of the spin expectation along Y–Γ–Y′.
- **p‑wave magnet** — Odd‑parity magnetic state with zero net magnetization but sizable spin splitting.
- **p‑wave magnets** — Magnetic systems with p‑wave symmetry of the order parameter.
- **p‑wave spin polarization** — Spin polarization with odd parity (p‑wave) associated with the spin‑helix texture.
- **p‑wave‑like symmetry** — Momentum‑odd symmetry of the odd‑parity spin polarization.
- **pₓ‑wave pairing** — Anisotropic pairing with pₓ symmetry.
- **q factor** — Quality factor of the resonator; high Q improves scan speed and sensitivity.
- **q_j vector** — Equal‑length displacement vectors between the K points of tip and sample after twisting.
- **qtm trajectory** — Arc traced by the MLG Fermi surface in momentum space as the tip is rotated.
- **quadratic increase of ε with py** — Energy ε grows quadratically with py on both sides of the px axis.
- **quadrature signal** — Two‑channel (A/B) signal encoding displacement and direction.
- **quantum anomalous hall ferromagnets** — Ferromagnetic states exhibiting quantized Hall conductance without external magnetic field.
- **quantum capacitance** — Capacitance arising from the finite density of states in low‑dimensional systems.
- **quantum coherence** — Phase‑coherent tunnelling where electron paths interfere.
- **quantum crystals** — Ordered electronic states with crystalline order emerging from strong correlations.
- **quantum dot charge states (n, n+1)** — Discrete charge configurations of the defect acting as a quantum dot.
- **quantum electrodynamics** — Theory of interaction between light and matter
- **quantum geometry** — Geometric properties of Bloch wavefunctions relevant for twisted multilayers.
- **quantum hall states** — Landau‑quantized electronic states observed at high magnetic field.
- **quantum mechanics** — Fundamental theory of physics at atomic scales
- **quantum nonlocal effects** — Nonlocal response of electrons influencing plasmonic behavior at the nanoscale.
- **quantum nonlocal effects** — Nonlocal response of electrons in graphene influencing plasmonic behavior.
- **quantum phenomena** — Quantum effects observable in Van der Waals heterostructures.
- **quantum transport** — Transport phenomena at the quantum scale in nanodevices.
- **quantum tunneling** — Electron tunnelling between two layers.
- **quarter metals** — Metallic phases where only a quarter of the spin‑valley degrees of freedom are occupied.
- **quasiparticle** — Elementary excitation that behaves like a particle with renormalized properties.
- **quasiparticle dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relation of Bogoliubov quasiparticles observed in the spectra.
- **quasiparticle lifetime (τ)** — Finite lifetime introduced for numerical stability (τ⁻¹ = 1 meV).
- **quasiparticle peak** — Sharp conductance peak associated with elastic tunneling into a band.
- **quasiparticle peaks** — Sharp spectral features corresponding to coherent electron excitations near the Fermi level.
- **quasiparticle residue z(e‑ef)** — Weight of the quasiparticle pole in the spectral function, dependent on energy relative to the Fermi level.
- **quasistatic behavior** — Regime where the structure thickness is much smaller than the wavelength, making s‑polarization negligible.
- **quasi‑particle** — Effective particle description used for excitations in metals and superconductors.
- **quasi‑particle states** — Excitations of a superconductor described by Bogoliubov quasiparticles.
- **quasi‑phase matching** — A technique that enables efficient nonlinear frequency conversion by periodically reversing the crystal polarity.
- **radial dependence** — Variation of the wave‑function amplitude with distance from the nucleus.
- **radially symmetric tip wavefunction** — Radially symmetric wavefunction model for tip states.
- **random phase approximation (rpa)** — Many‑body approximation that includes local field effects.
- **random phase approximation (rpa)** — Theoretical method used to compute the screening potential and plasmons in TBG.
- **random phase approximation (rpa)** — Theoretical framework used to compute density‑density response functions.
- **rashba and weyl systems** — Material systems where strong spin‑orbit coupling leads to Rashba or Weyl band structures.
- **rashba system** — Material system where Rashba‑type spin splitting occurs.
- **rashba term** — Spin‑orbit interaction term arising from structural inversion asymmetry.
- **rashba‑like mechanism** — Mechanism where ferroelectric polarization generates odd‑parity spin polarization.
- **ratio w₀/w₁ of tunneling parameters** — Relative strength of intra‑sublattice (w₀) to inter‑sublattice (w₁) tunneling in MA‑TBG.
- **real space** — Physical coordinate space where conventional STM images electron density.
- **real-time inputs** — Incoming communication signals processed in real time, requiring specific voltage and timing constraints.
- **realistic model** — A theoretical model incorporating material‑specific parameters.
- **real‑space registry** — Geometric alignment of atoms in real space between two layers.
- **recipe 11** — Editable recipe number 11 used for plasma asher runs.
- **reciprocal lattice vector (gₙ)** — Primitive reciprocal lattice vectors of the top graphene layer of TBG.
- **reciprocal lattice vectors** — Shortest reciprocal lattice vectors (19) used to construct the q‑grid.
- **reciprocal lattice vectors q1, q2** — Vectors spanning the reciprocal space of graphene.
- **rectangular contact** — A contact geometry that yields independent Lorentzian broadenings for x and y momentum components.
- **red‑shift due to screening** — Lowering of excitation energies caused by dielectric screening.
- **reflection mx (y–z plane)** — Mirror operation with respect to the y–z plane.
- **refresh button** — Button that repeats the last closed‑loop command.
- **region r** — Small region around p = 0 where the sample dispersion is linearized.
- **relative fermi velocity** — Ratio of the probe Fermi velocity to the system Fermi velocity (v_F^p / v_F^s).
- **relative permittivity (ε_r)** — Dielectric constant of the surrounding environment, taken as 6.6 in the calculations.
- **relative position of flake on optical microscope** — Physical coordinate of the flake used as reference when writing without markers.
- **relativistic effects** — SOC‑induced modifications of the band structure that depend on the spin‑spiral plane orientation.
- **remote band** — Higher‑energy bands of twisted bilayer graphene away from the flat‑band region.
- **remote bands** — Higher‑energy dispersive conduction and valence bands of TBG that lie away from the flat bands.
- **remote bands** — Electronic bands located away from the Dirac point, accessed at higher bias.
- **renormalized dispersion** — Modification of quasiparticle energy‑momentum relation by interactions.
- **repeat last command** — Action that re‑executes the most recent closed‑loop motion.
- **res sensor type** — Default sensor type for the AMC300 when no external IDS is connected.
- **resolution dot** — Resolution setting for mask design.
- **resonance condition** — Bias condition at which elastic tunnelling produces a negative‑differential‑conductance (NDC) peak.
- **resonance frequencies** — Natural frequencies of STM components relevant for vibration response.
- **resonance frequency** — Natural frequency of a mechanical mode of the STM that appears in the transfer function.
- **resonant condition** — Momentum‑conserving alignment of the two Dirac cones that yields a sharp tunnelling peak.
- **resonant photoemission diffraction (rped)** — Momentum‑resolved photoemission technique performed at a core‑level resonance.
- **res‑position read‑out** — RES‑position sensor read‑out used on three axes.
- **right chiral spin helix** — Spin‑helix state with right handed chirality, opposite to the left chiral state.
- **right chiral spin helix** — Spin helix with right‑handed chirality.
- **right chiral spin helix** — Spin helix in NiI2 with right‑handed chirality.
- **right‑chiral domain** — The opposite chiral variant of a spin‑helix domain.
- **right‑circular polarization (rcp)** — Opposite handedness of circularly polarized light.
- **rigid body assumption** — Tip, sample, ramp and other components are treated as rigid bodies with mass.
- **rohs directive** — EU directive aiming to reduce hazardous substances in electronic products.
- **room temperature limit set** — Limit configuration preset for room‑temperature operation.
- **room temperature operation** — Operating the microscope at ambient temperature.
- **rotation compensation upgrade** — Hardware/software upgrade required to enable rotation compensation.
- **rotation matrix d(θ)** — Matrix that rotates coordinates by the twist angle.
- **rotational motion of electrons** — Circular trajectory of electrons observed in diffraction experiments.
- **rotational states** — Energy levels associated with molecular rotation.
- **rotational symmetry breaking** — Spontaneous loss of C3z symmetry in the superconducting state.
- **rotational transitions** — Quantum transitions between rotational energy levels of a polar gas molecule.
- **rounding** — The suggestion that rounding does not make sense for the CNC mill used.
- **rpa screening potential** — Random phase approximation screening potential used in the calculations.
- **rs232 connector** — Serial connector for PC communication.
- **rt – room temperature use** — Preset designation for room temperature applications.
- **s-wave tip wave function** — Simplified tip wave function with spherical symmetry used in the spherical‑tip approximation.
- **s6 point symmetry** — Sixfold improper rotation symmetry observed in the MCD pattern of MnTe.
- **safety en61010-1:2010** — European safety standard applicable to the ANC300 equipment.
- **safety precautions** — Guidelines for safe operation of the NP‑Drive UM100.
- **safety warning** — Warnings about risk of electric shock and damage associated with the ANC300.
- **sample band structure** — Electronic dispersion of the flat‑band sample probed by tunneling.
- **sample doping** — Carrier concentration in a semiconductor that influences STM imaging.
- **sample hamiltonian (2)** — Hamiltonian governing the sample alone.
- **sample reciprocal lattice** — Reciprocal space lattice of the TBG sample.
- **sample reciprocal lattice vector g** — Reciprocal lattice vectors of the sample layer that keep k+g in the ±K valleys.
- **sample states** — Electronic states of the sample under investigation.
- **saturation effects** — Saturation of the piezo that leads to hysteresis.
- **sawtooth wave** — Default output waveform for stepping, linear rise then steep decrease.
- **sawtooth waveform** — Sawtooth waveform with a rapid slip part used in the NP‑Drive UM100.
- **scalar potential** — Potential term in the Dirac equation associated with the deformation potential.
- **scalar product (l·q)** — The dot product between the Néel vector L and the light helicity vector q, governing the magnetic CD signal.
- **scan piezo** — Piezoelectric actuator that moves the tip (or sample) during scanning.
- **scan speed** — Rate at which the tip can be rastered across the sample.
- **schwinger bosons** — Bosonic representation of spins, alternative to Abrikosov fermions.
- **screened coulomb interactions** — Coulomb forces reduced by dielectric screening.
- **screened coulomb potential** — Effective Coulomb interaction after screening.
- **screening** — Reduction of the electrostatic potential due to mobile charge carriers.
- **screening gate** — External gate used to screen Coulomb interactions.
- **screening parameter** — Semi‑empirical factor used in LRC to model long‑range screening.
- **screening potential** — Dynamical potential mediated by plasmons that enters the tunneling matrix elements.
- **screening potential ŵ** — Effective interaction mediating electron‑plasmon coupling.
- **screw axis 6₃** — Specific screw axis present in the MnTe space group.
- **second landau level** — The first excited Landau level (N=1) where tilt‑induced localization was studied.
- **second-order processes** — Second-order perturbative processes contributing to inelastic tunneling.
- **self‑consistent calculation** — More accurate calculation of charge density used for comparison with atom superposition.
- **self‑consistent electrostatic potential** — Electrostatic potential obtained by solving the Hartree equation with carrier screening.
- **self‑energy** — Quantity describing the interaction‑induced modification of electron propagators.
- **self‑energy σ** — Self‑energy computed from the screened interaction and used to renormalize quasiparticle energies.
- **sem/fib coincidence** — Alignment of the SEM and FIB beams at the same point on the sample.
- **semimetallic phase** — Phase observed at charge neutrality with states at Γ at the Fermi level.
- **sensitivity** — Ability to detect small forces or force gradients.
- **sensitivity (v / pm)** — Ratio of measured output voltage (rms V) to QTF vibration amplitude (pm).
- **sensor** — Sensor that can be turned on/off via NP-Amp circuitry.
- **sensor drive connector** — Connector on the NP‑Amp for sensor‑drive signals.
- **sensor in socket** — Interferometer input connection on the AMC300.
- **sensor pickup connector** — Connector on the NP‑Amp for sensor‑pickup signals (CH1SMP).
- **sensor type** — Field to select the used sensor (AMC/IDS – Closed loop feature required, default: Internal (RES)).
- **serial port** — Serial communication via USB/serial adapter for ANC300.
- **set‑point force** — Force set for the AFM feedback loop during approach (10–20 nN).
- **shape of atoms** — Concept concerning the spatial distribution of atomic electron density.
- **sharp dirac cone of tip** — Tip band structure with a steep linear dispersion (v₀ < v_D) leading to non‑analytic I(φ).
- **shear mode** — Low‑frequency interlayer vibrational mode in multilayer graphene.
- **short‑circuit protection** — Feature protecting outputs against short circuits.
- **short‑range forces** — Chemical forces with strong force gradients that dominate at small tip‑sample separations.
- **short‑range interactions** — Interactions acting over a limited spatial range.
- **sidebands** — Replica spectral features displaced by integer multiples of photon energy.
- **signal‑to‑noise ratio (snr)** — Metric quantifying the quality of a measured signal relative to background noise.
- **single electron state** — Additional single‑electron states populated at specific voltages (≈15 mV, 42 mV, 90 mV).
- **single particle splitting** — Hypothesized origin of the ΔE = ‑15 meV feature based on a simple particle‑level splitting.
- **single stepping** — Mode where stepping advances one step per command.
- **single-layer dirac hamiltonian (h)** — Effective Hamiltonian for a strained graphene layer.
- **single-particle spectral function** — Spectral function describing the density of single‑particle states in the sample.
- **single‑electron transistor (set)** — Device enabling charge transport of individual electrons, realized in graphene.
- **single‑particle spectral function** — Probability density for adding or removing an electron with given momentum and energy.
- **single‑particle spectral function** — Quantity describing the energy and momentum distribution of electronic excitations.
- **single‑particle spectral functions** — Quantities describing the probability of adding or removing an electron with given energy and momentum.
- **single‑particle tunnelling** — Tunnelling of individual electrons without many‑body effects.
- **slab geometry** — Finite layered model of the surface used in the calculations.
- **slip part of waveform** — Rapid voltage‑reversal portion of the sawtooth waveform with slip time < 1 µs.
- **slip‑stick drive** — The slip‑stick drive mechanism used for coarse positioning.
- **slip‑stick inertial motion** — Inertial motion principle based on slip‑stick actuation.
- **small fermi extremum (neck - n)** — Small extremum of the Fermi surface that gives rise to a slow SdH oscillation.
- **small quantum capacitance** — Regime where the quantum capacitance of graphene layers is much smaller than the geometric capacitance (q_TF·d ≪ 1).
- **smp** — Cable configuration using SMP connectors.
- **soft modes** — Low‑energy collective excitations that become soft near a phase transition.
- **space group** — Symmetry classification of a crystal lattice.
- **space group operation (rotation by π around z)** — Symmetry operation relating the two magnetic sublattices in MnTe.
- **spatial degree of freedom** — Ability to address different positions on a sample.
- **spatial degrees of freedom** — Position‑dependent measurement capability of the RT‑QTOM.
- **spatial resolution** — Ability to distinguish features on the nanometre scale.
- **spectral function** — Density of electronic states as a function of energy and momentum.
- **spectral function** — Function giving the density of electronic states after including self‑energy effects.
- **spectral functions of tip and sample** — Functions A_T,λ(k,ω) and A_S,λ(k,ω) describing the density of states of tip and sample Bloch states.
- **spectral properties** — Energy‑resolved electronic structure probed by spectroscopy.
- **spectral weight at the fermi energy** — Intensity of electronic states at the Fermi level as seen in dI/dV.
- **spectral weight transfer** — Redistribution of electronic spectral intensity due to correlations.
- **spectral‑function peak** — Peak in the measured density of states representing a quasiparticle excitation.
- **spherically symmetric tip form φ_j(r) ≈ a_j e^{-κ_j r}/r** — Spherically symmetric form for tip wavefunctions used in the Tersoff-Hamann derivation.
- **spin and valley degeneracy (n_f)** — Factor N_f = 4 accounting for spin and valley degrees of freedom.
- **spin chirality** — Alternating pattern of spin orientation in reciprocal space characteristic of altermagnets.
- **spin expectation value** — ⟨σ(k)⟩ = ⟨ψn(k)|σ|ψn(k)⟩, momentum‑resolved spin expectation.
- **spin group symmetry** — Spin group symmetry analysis.
- **spin helices** — Spiral magnetic ordering predicted for NiI₂ monolayer.
- **spin helicity** — Sense of rotation of spin texture along a propagation direction.
- **spin helix (chiral spin helix)** — A helical arrangement of spins in NiI2, with left‑ or right‑handed chirality.
- **spin helix (cycloidal spin spiral)** — Cycloidal spin spiral propagating along Qy∥[120] in NiI2, inducing ferroelectric polarization.
- **spin helix domain** — A region of NiI2 where the magnetic moments form a helical arrangement.
- **spin operator σ** — Pauli spin operator used to compute spin expectation.
- **spin polarization axis** — Direction of the net spin expectation value, orthogonal to the spiral plane.
- **spin space groups** — Symmetry groups that combine spatial operations with spin rotations.
- **spin space groups** — Symmetry groups that combine spatial and spin rotations.
- **spin spiral order** — A magnetic ordering where the spin direction rotates periodically in space.
- **spin splitting** — Energy separation of spin‑up and spin‑down bands.
- **spin structure factor** — Correlation function Gₛ(q,iωₙ) expressed in spinon operators.
- **spin su(2) symmetry** — Assumed symmetry of the electronic system in the tunneling calculation.
- **spin texture** — Spin texture of electronic states.
- **spinon** — Fractionalized fermionic excitation of a quantum spin liquid.
- **spinon dispersion** — Energy–momentum relation of spinons, e.g. ξ_k = ħ²k²/(2m_s) ± Δ₁s/2.
- **spinon fermi surface** — Quantum spin liquid where spinons form a Fermi surface.
- **spinon hamiltonian** — Effective low‑energy Hamiltonian describing spinon dynamics.
- **spinon mean‑field hamiltonian** — Hamiltonian whose eigenvectors define the band basis.
- **spinon particle‑hole excitation** — Low‑energy excitation of a quantum spin liquid involving a spinon particle and a spinon hole.
- **spinors** — Two‑component wavefunctions for graphene sublattices.
- **spintronics** — Field exploiting electron spin for information processing.
- **spin‑current mechanism** — Mechanism generating electric dipole from non‑collinear spins.
- **spin‑dependent currents** — Currents whose magnitude depends on electron spin.
- **spin‑group classification** — Systematic classification of magnetic symmetries that predicts spin‑splitting phenomena.
- **spin‑group symmetries** — Symmetries that leave the magnetic configuration invariant and can allow spin splitting without SOC.
- **spin‑helix domains** — Three pairs of equivalent domains with propagation vectors related by C₃ symmetry.
- **spin‑orbit coupling (soc)** — Relativistic interaction that adds additional spin splitting but does not dominate the effect.
- **spin‑orbit coupling (soc)** — Interaction linking spin and orbital degrees of freedom, required for symmetry operations.
- **spin‑orbit interaction** — Coupling between a particle's spin and its orbital motion.
- **spin‑polarized leads** — Leads with net spin polarization that enable probing individual components of S(q,ω).
- **spin‑polarized phases** — Phases where electron spins are aligned, observed in twisted bilayer‑bilayer graphene.
- **spin‑resolved** — Measurement that distinguishes electron spin orientation.
- **spin‑spiral plane** — Geometric plane containing the rotating spins; perpendicular to the propagation vector for the cycloidal‑type spiral.
- **spin‑spiral type ii multiferroic** — Multiferroic material where an inversion‑symmetry‑breaking spin helix induces ferroelectric polarization.
- **spin‑valley coupling** — Interaction linking electron spin and valley degrees of freedom.
- **spin‑valley flavor (nf = 4)** — Four spin‑valley flavors that contribute equally to the tunneling current.
- **spin‑valley symmetric phase** — Phase of TBG in which spin and valley degrees of freedom are symmetric.
- **spiral magnetic field** — A magnetic field whose direction rotates along a spatial coordinate.
- **spiral spin structures** — A magnetic ordering where spins rotate in a helical fashion.
- **spiral spin structures** — Helical arrangement of spins in magnetic materials.
- **spontaneously broken symmetries** — Phases where a symmetry is broken without external fields.
- **spot size** — Resulting spot size of 0.5–1 mm at the sample plane.
- **spring connector network** — Network of spring connectors linking PCB modules.
- **spring constant (k)** — Mechanical stiffness of the cantilever or tuning fork.
- **stability** — Passive stability of the laser output over time.
- **stacking potential** — Potential energy variation caused by local changes in the relative alignment of graphene and hBN within the moiré cell.
- **stacking pseudopotential** — Potential contribution arising from the local stacking configuration of graphene on hBN.
- **standard requirements** — Standard specifications required by supplier for PCBs.
- **stark effect** — Electric‑field induced splitting of defect levels in hBN.
- **static steel** — Static steel objects near the magnet.
- **steady‑state solution** — Part of the tip motion solution determined by the driving force.
- **step mode (stp)** — Mode constant STP with numerical value 4, used for stepping actions.
- **step mode (stp)** — Mode that enables stepping motion on the selected axis.
- **step switch** — Switch that triggers a single step up or down when pressed.
- **step voltage limit** — User‑set maximum for the stepping voltage alone on the ANM300.
- **stepping functionality** — Stepping output signal mode of the ANM300 module, default sawtooth wave.
- **stepping functionality** — Generates discrete voltage steps; disabled when low‑pass filters are enabled.
