- **helimagnet (multiferroic)** — A multiferroic material with a helical magnetic order.
- **helimagnetic semimetal** — A semimetallic material with helimagnetic order, e.g., α‑EuP₃.
- **helium gas** — Helium gas specified as part of the compressed gases.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn)** — Insulating crystal used as substrate and encapsulation.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn)** — Layered insulating material used as a barrier.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn)** — Layered insulating crystal employed as a substrate for graphene.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn)** — Dielectric material used as top and bottom gate insulators in the QTM.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn)** — Layered insulating material used as barrier and substrate.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn) flake** — Top encapsulation layer providing an atomically clean surface.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn) layer** — Thin hBN dielectric layer used both in the tip and in the sensor stack of the QTM junction.

- **high‑κ dielectric** — Dielectric material with a large permittivity used to screen electric fields.
- **homogeneous electron gas** — Idealized model of electrons with uniform density, used as a reference system.
- **honeycomb lattice** — Lattice geometry underlying graphene and related theoretical models.

- **iec60320 c19/20 connector** — Connector type for the device power cable.
- **inner macor tube** — Macor (machinable ceramic) tube forming the movable part of the coarse z motor.
- **insulating barrier** — Dielectric layer separating the defect quantum dot from the graphene electrode.

- **lithium fluoride (lif)** — Material discussed in exciton‑polaron context.

- **macor tube** — Macor tube that houses the coarse Z motor and the x/y/z piezo scanner.
- **magic-angle graphene** — Twisted bilayer graphene tuned to the magic angle (~1.1°) where flat bands appear.
- **magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Twisted bilayer graphene near the magic angle exhibiting flat bands.
- **magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (matbg)** — Two‑dimensional graphene bilayer with a twist angle near 1.1° that hosts flat electronic bands.
- **magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (matbg)** — The two‑dimensional material whose electronic structure is investigated.
- **magic‑angle bilayer graphene** — Twisted bilayer graphene tuned to the magic angle (~1.1°) where flat bands appear.
- **magic‑angle graphene superlattice** — Twisted bilayer graphene at ~1.1° twist angle where flat bands emerge.

- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Twisted bilayer graphene at ~1.1° where flat bands emerge.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (ma tbg)** — Magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (matbg)** — TBG tuned to the magic twist angle (~1.1°) where flat bands appear and superconductivity emerges.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (tblg)** — Bilayer graphene with a twist angle near 1.1°, exhibiting flat bands and correlated phenomena.
- **magic‑angle twisted trilayer graphene** — Twisted trilayer graphene exhibiting tunable strongly coupled superconductivity.
- **magnesium diboride (mgb₂)** — A superconducting compound with strong electron‑phonon coupling.
- **manganese (mn)** — Element constituting MnTe.
- **mesoscopic structure** — Structure with dimensions between microscopic and macroscopic.
- **metal bottom gate** — Metallic gate placed beneath the sensor stack to control carrier density.
- **metal stick** — Metallic tool used to pull in/out the sample and to check the stage.
- **metal surfaces** — General class of metallic surfaces studied by STM
- **metallic back gate** — Bottom electrode of the QTM junction.
- **metallic gate** — Conductive electrode placed close to the 2D sample to provide electrostatic screening.
- **mir crystal** — Non‑linear crystal used in the MIR module; fragile and power‑limited.
- **mlg layer (multilayer graphene) on qtm tip** — A multilayer graphene layer deposited on the QTM tip.
- **mn++** — Manganese ion in the +2 oxidation state, compared with Ni++ in charge‑distribution behavior.
- **mno (manganese oxide)** — A conventional antiferromagnetic material used as a reference in RPED studies.
- **mnte** — Local‑moment insulating altermagnet where spin‑split magnons have been reported.
- **moiré graphene** — Graphene with a moiré superlattice created by twisting.
- **moiré heterostructure** — Van‑der‑Waals stack in which a moiré superlattice is created by a twist or lattice mismatch.
- **moiré heterostructures** — Stacked 2D materials with a twist angle creating moiré patterns.
- **moiré superconductor** — A superconducting phase that emerges in a moiré graphene system.
- **molybdenum diselenide (mose₂)** — TMD used in heterobilayer with WSe₂.
- **molybdenum disulfide (mos₂)** — Stable, easily exfoliable TMD used in twisted bilayer benchmark studies.
- **molybdenum ditelluride (mote₂)** — TMD hosting topological and superconducting phases.
- **monochrome cmos cs165mu/m** — Monochrome CMOS sensor used for detection.
- **monolayer graphene** — Single‑layer graphene forming the probe tip.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg)** — Single layer of graphene used as the tip in the tunnel junction.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg)** — Single layer of graphene used as active layers.
- **monolayer nii2** — A single‑layer nickel iodide crystal exhibiting multiferroic behavior.
- **multiferroic materials** — Materials that simultaneously exhibit ferroelectric and magnetic order.
- **multiferroic perovskites** — Perovskite oxides that exhibit coupled ferroelectric and magnetic orders.
- **multilayer graphene** — Two or more stacked graphene layers.
- **nanographene** — Bottom‑up synthesized graphene fragments of a few nanometres lateral size.
- **nbse₂** — Niobium diselenide, a layered superconductor.
- **neodymium‑iron‑boron magnets** — Permanent magnets (0.3 T) used for eddy‑current damping of the AFM head.
- **ni wire (250 µm diameter)** — Nickel wire used as the precursor for electrochemical tip etching.
- **ni++** — Nickel ion in the +2 oxidation state, subject of form‑factor and charge‑distribution studies.
- **nickel (ni)** — Transition metal studied across its Curie temperature.
- **nickel iodide (nii2)** — Layered magnetic semiconductor NiI2 used as sample material.
- **nickel iodide (nii2)** — Transition metal halide studied for its magnetic ordering and spin‑splitting phenomena.
- **nif₂** — Nickel fluoride, a candidate altermagnet.
- **nii2** — Nickel iodide, the 2D material studied.
- **nii2** — Nickel iodide, a layered transition‑metal dihalide.
- **nii2** — Nickel diiodide, the layered material studied for its ferroelectric and spin‑polarized properties.
- **nii₂ crystals** — Magnetic van‑der‑Waals NiI₂ crystals.
- **nii₂ monolayer** — Single atomic layer of NiI₂ modeled in first‑principles calculations.
- **nii₂ single crystal** — Bulk NiI₂ crystal (5 mm × 5 mm × 0.1 mm) used for pyroelectric and ferroelectric measurements.
- **nio** — Nickel(II) oxide, an antiferromagnetic oxide.
- **nitrogen (n2)** — Nitrogen gas used for venting the chamber.
- **nonchiral, nonmagnetic material** — A material lacking chirality and intrinsic magnetism used in a dichroism experiment.
- **noncoplanar antiferromagnet** — An antiferromagnet whose spin configuration is not confined to a single plane.
- **nonmagic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (tbg)** — Twisted bilayer graphene with a twist angle away from the magic angle, exhibiting flat electronic bands.
- **non‑conductive epoxy** — Epoxy applied to the edges of the piezo slabs to prevent arcing.
- **non‑conductive epoxy (h77)** — Epoxy used to glue the quartz tuning fork base to the stainless‑steel rod.
- **non‑magnetic components** — Components chosen to allow operation in high magnetic fields.
- **non‑magnetic materials** — Materials with low magnetic susceptibility used in rotators to avoid interference.
- **n‑type doped inas buffer layer** — First deposited layer in the RITD stack.
- **n⁺ inas(001) substrate** — Heavily doped indium arsenide substrate used as the foundation for the RITD.
- **n₂ gas** — Nitrogen gas used for chamber venting and pressure control.
- **o2** — Oxygen gas for plasma cleaning.
- **objective lens** — Objective lens with limited 10 mm diameter and focal length, central to the QTM.
- **one‑dimensional wire** — Quasi‑one‑dimensional electronic conductor.
- **opa602 operational amplifier** — Operational amplifier used in the second stage of the transimpedance amplifier.
- **optoelectronic encoder** — Optoelectronic encoder integrated in some Premium Line positioners for closed‑loop feedback.
- **organic antiferromagnet** — Antiferromagnetic material based on organic molecules.
- **orientation‑patterned gaas** — A form of gallium arsenide engineered with periodic orientation domains to allow quasi‑phase matching.
- **oxide two‑dimensional electron gas** — 2DEG formed at oxide interfaces.
- **oxidized aluminum films** — Aluminum films with surface oxidation used to study surface‑plasmon dispersion.
- **oxygen (o2)** — Oxygen gas used via valve.
- **pair of mesfets** — Pair of MESFET transistors that determine the white noise level of the amplifier.
- **pb-ca sealed battery** — Bateria de plom‑càlcic segellada sense manteniment, utilitzada com a font d’energia en el dispositiu.
- **pcb d** — Printed circuit board D used in Project 3.
- **pcb e** — Printed circuit board E used in Project 3.
- **pcx 42 k 10 ak** — Component used on PCB 2.
- **peek** — Polymer material used for many connector housings.
- **perovskite oxides** — Oxide compounds with the perovskite crystal structure.
- **pico-coax line** — Coaxial cable used to connect PCBs, length specified as X cm.
- **pico‑coax wire** — Coaxial wire (≈50 cm) used for wiring PCBs.
- **piezo ceramic** — Piezoelectric ceramic elements that drive the motion of attocube positioners.
- **piezo ceramic tube** — 1‑inch long piezo ceramic tubes used as x, y and z scanners.
- **piezo material** — Material of the piezo element that determines capacitance and loss angle.
- **piezo slab (ebl #2 shear mode)** — Individual shear‑mode piezo slab (4 mm × 4 mm × 1 mm) used in the stack.
- **piezoelectric actuator** — The mechanical element driven by the ANM300, whose leads can be grounded or measured.
- **piezoelectric actuator** — Piezoelectric ceramic stack element used in positioners and scanners.
- **piezoelectric actuator** — Piezo elements modeled as mass‑less springs with viscous damping.
- **piezoelectric actuator** — Material that converts voltage into precise mechanical displacement.
- **piezoelectric actuators** — Actuators in NP nanopositioners driven by high‑voltage waveforms.
- **piezoelectric ceramic** — Piezoelectric ceramic stack elements used as actuators in ANS scanners.
- **piezoelectric ceramics** — Ceramic material that forms the actuators inside the positioners; behaves like a perfect capacitor at very low temperatures.
- **piezoelectric material** — Material with piezoelectric properties used in the actuator.
- **platinum** — Metal deposited by focused‑ion‑beam to form the tip pyramid.
- **platinum (pt)** — Pt layer in the heterostructure.
- **platinum pyramid** — FIB‑deposited platinum structure (1.2–1.6 µm tall) forming the apex of the QTM tip.
- **platinum pyramid (height 1.2–1.6 µm, base 2 µm square, apex ≈50 nm)** — Metallic tip grown by FIB deposition that serves as the support for the vdW device.
- **polar twisted bilayers** — Twisted bilayer structures possessing a polar character, studied without requiring magic angles.
- **poly(methyl methacrylate) (pmma)** — Coating on PDMS used during the flip‑transfer step.
- **poly(methylmethacrylate) (pmma) resist** — 200 nm PMMA layer of the bilayer electron‑beam resist.
- **poly(methylmethacrylate‑co‑methacrylic acid) (p(mma/maa)) resist** — 500 nm P(MMA/MAA) layer of the bilayer electron‑beam resist.
- **polycarbonate (pc) polymer stamp** — Polymer stamp used in deterministic dry‑transfer of vdW layers.
- **polydimethylsiloxane (pdms)** — Elastomer used as a stamp in the dry transfer process.
- **polymethylpentene (tpx)** — Low‑loss THz‑transparent polymer used for a THz lens.
- **polypropylene carbonate (ppc)** — Polymer film spin‑coated on the wafer to pick up vdW crystals.
- **polyvinyl alcohol (pva)** — PVA polymer film used in the transfer stack.
- **precursor gas** — Gases supplied to the GIS for deposition or etching.
- **printed circuit board (pcb)** — The PCB that is placed inside the pocket; its outer diameter and margins are considered.
- **ptfe** — Polytetrafluoroethylene insulator (ASTM D1710) used in the connector.
- **ptx(100) surface** — Platinum binary alloy surface
- **pvc** — Polyvinyl chloride tape used in stamp preparation.
- **pvc/pmma polymer stamp** — Alternative polymer stamp for dry‑transfer of vdW layers.
- **pzt ceramics** — Piezoelectric ceramic used as the actuator in the scanner.
- **pzt ceramics** — Piezoelectric ceramic material of the actuator.
- **pzt piezo ceramics** — Piezoelectric ceramic material used as the actuation element in attocube positioners.
- **quantum dots** — Nanoscale semiconductor particles that can interact with plasmons.
- **quantum spin‑liquid material (qsl barrier)** — A candidate two‑dimensional quantum spin‑liquid placed between the graphene leads to act as the tunneling barrier.
- **quartz** — Silicon dioxide glass used for tubes.
- **quartz tuning fork (ecs 3×8 model)** — Commercial quartz tuning fork used as the force sensor in the FM‑AFM setup.
- **relative humidity (rh)** — Atmospheric condition used as the absorbing medium in the measurement.
- **resistive sensor** — Sensing element of the position encoder.
- **resistive wires** — Resistive wiring (≥10 Ω) sometimes considered for connecting positioners.
- **ruo₂** — Ruthenium dioxide, a candidate altermagnet.
- **samarium tip atom** — Tip made of samarium used in STM measurements.
- **sapphire ball** — Sapphire ball acting as a contact element in the coarse z motor.
- **sapphire plate** — Sapphire plates glued to the macor tube to provide rigid support.
- **sapphire substrate** — Rigid substrate onto which vdW stacks can be transferred.
- **saturated nacl/h₂o solution** — Electrolyte used as the etchant in the first step of Pt/Ir tip etching.
- **sb25 male a** — SB25 male connector type A used in Project 1.
- **sb9 male b** — SB9 male connector B used in Project 2.
- **sb9 male c** — SB9 male connector C used in Project 2.
- **scotch tape** — Scotch tape used for hBN exfoliation.
- **sei-105-02-g-s-ab** — Surface‑mount component used on PCB 3 (10‑leg connector).
- **semiconductor nanodevices** — Nanodevices fabricated from semiconductor materials.
- **semiconductor surfaces** — Surfaces of semiconductor materials studied by STM
- **sharp tips** — Nanotips used in STM
- **shear‑mode piezo stack** — Six shear‑mode piezoelectric stacks that drive the coarse Z motor.
- **shielded twisted pair wires** — Extra shielded twisted pair wires used for the piezo voltage supply.
- **si(111) surface** — Silicon (111) surface with 2×1 reconstruction
- **si(111) surface** — Silicon (111) surface studied with STM.
- **si(111)-(7×7) surface** — Silicon (111) surface reconstruction used as a benchmark sample for STM and AFM.
- **si(111) 7×7 surface** — Silicon (111) surface with a 7×7 reconstruction, mentioned as an example of a large unit cell.
- **si/sio2 substrate** — Silicon wafer with silicon dioxide used as flat substrate.
- **si/sio₂ chip** — Silicon substrate with silicon dioxide on which the graphene/h‑BN stack is assembled.
- **si/sio₂ pillar** — Silicon/silicon‑dioxide pillar supporting the heterostructure.
- **si/sio₂ substrate (100 nm)** — Substrate on which graphene and h‑BN flakes are exfoliated.
- **silicon** — Common material for AFM tips mentioned as a reference.
- **silicon** — Semiconductor used for electrically driven surface‑plasmon sources and detectors.
- **silicon** — Alternative material for terahertz lenses.
- **silicon cantilever** — Silicon‑based cantilever studied for thermal‑noise effects.
- **silicon dioxide (sio₂)** — Glass substrate on which the vertical stack is fabricated.
- **silicon elastomer base** — Base component of silicone elastomer, stored in a big cylindrical container.
- **silicon elastomer curing agent** — Curing agent component of silicone elastomer, stored in a small glass bottle.
- **silicon surface** — Silicon substrate imaged by the scanning tunneling microscope.
- **silicon wafer (50 μm thick)** — Ultrathin silicon wafer patterned by deep reactive ion etching.
- **silicon‑on‑insulator wafer** — Wafer type from which integrated‑tip cantilevers were later machined.
- **silver** — Metal coating used on tips for tip‑enhanced Raman spectroscopy.
- **silver gallium selenide (aggase2)** — A mid‑IR nonlinear crystal.
- **single atomic defect** — Atomic‑scale defect acting as a quantum dot whose energy level shifts with local potential.
- **single-layer graphene** — One atomic layer of graphene.
- **single‑layer van der waals multiferroic** — A two‑dimensional crystal that simultaneously hosts ferroelectric and magnetic order.
- **sio2/si substrate** — Silicon substrate with 300 nm SiO2 layer used for growth and device fabrication.
- **sio₂** — Silicon dioxide used in the first micro‑machined cantilevers.
- **sio₂ substrate** — Silicon dioxide substrate used for some vdW stack transfers.
- **sio₂/si** — Silicon substrate with 300 nm SiO₂ dielectric.
- **si₃n₄** — Silicon nitride used in the first micro‑machined cantilevers.
- **smfeas(o,f)** — Layered iron‑based superconductor.
- **smp female b** — SMP female connector B used in Project 2.
- **smp female c** — SMP female connector C used in Project 2.
- **springs** — Mechanical springs (spring constant 18 kg m⁻¹) suspending the AFM head.
- **srtio3** — Strontium titanate substrate.
- **srₓbi₂se₃** — Layered topological superconductor material.
- **ss 1.4435** — Material listed for the beam_high entry.
- **stainless steel** — Metallic material commonly used for coaxial shields with relatively poor electrical conductivity at low temperature.
- **stainless steel** — Metal material comprising part of the coaxial wires.
- **stainless‑steel l‑shaped rod (1 mm diameter)** — Rod that holds the quartz tuning fork and positions the tip over the sample.
- **steel** — Magnetic material (e.g., reinforcement bars, columns) that can disturb cryo‑magnet operation.
- **steel** — Steel material mentioned in safety guidelines.
- **strontium titanate (srtio₃)** — High‑κ dielectric employed as an environment in some measurements.
- **strontium titanate (sto)** — Perovskite oxide substrate forming heterostructures with graphene.
- **substrate** — Generic supporting material on which 2D flakes are placed.
- **superconducting wires** — Superconducting wiring offering low resistance and reduced heat load.
- **superconductor** — Metallic material in the superconducting state, used in tunnelling experiments to reveal a gap.
- **suspended graphene** — Graphene membrane free from substrate interactions.
- **swagelok brass adapters b-810-1-6** — Brass adapters for water inlet and outlet connections on the compressor.
- **swagelok brass adapters b-810-1-6** — Brass adapters used for water inlet and outlet connections on the compressor.
- **tb₂ti₂o₇** — Pyrochlore magnet investigated for spin‑liquid properties.
- **tellurium (te)** — Element constituting MnTe.
- **thin film** — Two‑dimensional layered material.
- **thin tmd layer** — Thin transition metal dichalcogenide layer embedding the atomic defect.
- **ti primer** — Titanium primer used before applying resist.
- **ti/au (10 nm/30 nm) circular disks and markers** — Metal mask defined by evaporation and lift‑off for subsequent chemical etching.
- **ti\au** — Titanium‑gold alloy used for contacts.
- **titanium** — Metal used together with beryllium copper in the body.
- **titanium (ti)** — Thin adhesion layer (5 nm) deposited for electrodes.
- **titanium alloy 6al4v (grade 5, din 912 m2x4)** — Titanium alloy grade 5 (6Al4V) specified as DIN 912 M2x4 fastener, listed in the budget document.
- **titanium frame** — High‑purity titanium structure that amplifies piezo motion in ANS scanners.
- **titanium frame** — Titanium housing that secures the integrated piezo ceramics of the positioners.
- **tpx** — Polymer used for terahertz lenses.
- **transition metal dichalcogenide (tmd) layer** — General class of 2D semiconductors; here represented by WSe₂.
- **transition metal dichalcogenide (tmd) monolayers** — Family of 2D semiconductors such as WS₂, MoS₂, WSe₂, MoSe₂.
- **transition metal dichalcogenides (tmdcs)** — Group‑VIB monolayer semiconductors modeled with a three‑band tight‑binding approach.
- **transition metal dichalcogenides (tmds)** — Layered semiconductors with small Fermi surfaces at K or Q points.
- **transition‑metal dichalcogenide bilayer** — Bilayer of a TMD such as MoSe₂, WS₂, etc.
- **transition‑metal oxides** — Oxides containing transition‑metal cations (e.g., MnO, FeO, CoO, NiO).
- **trichloromethane** — Solvent for PC solution.
- **trilayer wse2** — Three‑layer tungsten diselenide acting as the tunnelling barrier that suppresses hybridisation between the graphene layers.
- **tube lens** — Optical tube lens component used in the QTM.
- **tungsten boat** — Metallic tungsten container that holds the source material (e.g., gold pellets) for thermal evaporation.
- **tungsten diselenide (wse₂)** — Air‑stable transition metal dichalcogenide used in twisted homobilayer structures.
- **tungsten disulfide (ws₂)** — TMD with larger bandgap used in twisted bilayer.
- **tungsten wire** — Etched tungsten wires used for early cantilevers.
- **tunneling barrier** — Barrier through which electrons tunnel between graphene layers.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (tbg)** — The 2D material whose plasmon excitations are studied.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (tbg)** — Graphene bilayer with a finite twist angle, used as a reference system.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (tblg)** — Two graphene layers with a small twist angle forming a moiré superlattice.
- **twisted graphene layers** — Two graphene sheets rotated relative to each other.
- **twisted graphene multilayers** — Stacks of graphene layers rotated relative to each other, creating moiré superlattices.
- **twisted trilayer graphene** — Three graphene layers with a small relative twist, forming a moiré pattern.
- **two-dimensional materials** — Atomically thin crystals such as graphene, hBN, MoO3, etc.
- **two-dimensional superconductors** — Superconducting systems confined to a single atomic layer.
- **two‑dimensional electron system** — Electron gas confined to a plane.
- **ucoge** — Uranium‑Cobalt‑Germanium, a ferromagnetic superconductor material.
- **usb led mount ledmt1e** — Mount for the LED in the QTM.
- **vacuum** — Dielectric environment with ε≈1.
- **vacuum environment** — The low‑pressure environment in which the high‑Q cantilever operates.
- **vacuum grease** — Lubricant sometimes used in vacuum environments, but prohibited on Attocube positioners.
- **van der waals (vdw) materials** — Layered materials held together by van der Waals forces; the broader class of systems to which TBG belongs.
- **van‑der‑waals ferroelectric halide perovskite** — Layered halide perovskite exhibiting ferroelectricity.
- **w tip** — Tungsten tip, mentioned as having stronger oxidation than Pt/Ir.
- **water (h2o)** — Deionized water used as the final rinse bath.
- **wireless ds4 remote controller** — Wireless DS4 remote controller for the AMC300.
- **wse₂** — Transition‑metal dichalcogenide used as a bilayer tunnelling barrier.
- **w‑based tmds** — TMDs containing tungsten whose indirect‑exciton energies are close to A‑exciton energies.
- **yttrium (y)** — Element used as an example of an anisotropic Fermi surface (Figure 1b).
- **zinc germanium diphosphide (zgp, zngep2)** — A mid‑IR nonlinear crystal widely used for infrared generation.
- **zinc oxide (zno)** — Semiconductor material forming heterostructures with gold.
- **zinc selenide (znse)** — Infrared‑transparent crystal used for an IR lens in the QTM optics.
- **zinc selenide (znse)** — Material for infrared lenses.
- **α‑mnte** — Manganese telluride, an altermagnetic material.
- **α‑rucl₃** — Layered magnetic insulator combined with graphene.
- **κ‑(bedt‑ttf)₂cu₂(cn)₃** — Frustrated triangular‑lattice organic Mott insulator, candidate quantum spin liquid.

### measurementtype
- **amplitude measurement** — Measurement of the cantilever vibration amplitude, which contains steady‑state, transient decay and beat components.
- **angle‑bias measurement** — Classical QTM measurement varying twist angle and bias voltage.
- **angle‑ftir measurement** — Measurement that combines twist angle control with FTIR spectroscopy.
- **angular dependence of photocurrent** — Photocurrent measured as a function of quarter‑wave plate angle.
- **angular‑dependent linear dichroism** — Measurement of linear dichroism as a function of the sample rotation angle.
- **arrhenius plot analysis** — Extraction of activation energy from temperature dependence of resistance.
- **beam diameter measurement** — Measurement of beam diameter using the adjustable slit.
- **bode‑plot measurement** — Technique for recording magnitude (dB) versus frequency.
- **conductance derivative with respect to twist angle (gθ) measurement** — Derivative of conductance versus twist angle.
- **conductance measurement** — Measurement of electrical conductance as a function of twist angle.
- **conductance measurement** — Measurement of current I under bias voltage Vb to obtain dI/dV.
- **conductance measurement (g)** — Direct measurement of dI/dV used to identify step‑like features.
- **conductivity measurement** — Lock‑in based measurement of sample conductivity.
- **coulomb blockade peak monitoring** — Tracking the shift of the defect quantum dot’s Coulomb blockade peak to infer local potential.
- **coulomb diamond measurement** — Transport spectroscopy showing charge stability diagram.
- **cross‑polarized imaging measurement** — Imaging of birefringent domains using crossed polarizers.
- **current imaging** — Imaging of tunnelling current as a function of lateral (X,Y) coordinates.
- **current measurement** — Measurement of the electron beam current using the Faraday cup.
- **current‑to‑voltage (i‑v) measurement** — Acquisition of the current response while sweeping the bias voltage.
- **current‑voltage (i‑v) curve** — Measurement of current as a function of applied voltage.
- **d2i/dv2 (second derivative of conductance)** — Second derivative of the tunneling current with respect to bias.
- **dc current measurement through nanostructures** — Technique enabled by the conductive tip to record steady‑state current.
- **di(θ, kf)/dv spectrum** — Differential conductance measured while varying tip twist angle and Fermi wave vector.
- **di/dv** — Differential conductance measured as a function of bias voltage.
- **di/dv** — Differential conductance measurement used to probe tunneling spectra.
- **di/dv differential conductance** — Differential tunnelling conductance measured as a function of top‑gate voltage, source‑drain bias, temperature and magnetic field.
- **di/dv intensity** — Differential conductance signal used to map the density of states.
- **di/dv map** — Spatial map of differential conductance as a function of bias and angle.
- **di/dv measurement** — Experiment recording the differential conductance as a function of bias, twist angle and filling.
- **di/dv measurement** — Differential conductance spectroscopy used to probe tunnelling spectra.
- **di/dv measurement** — Measurement of the differential conductance (dI/dV) as a function of bias voltage.
- **di/dv spectroscopy** — Differential conductance measurement used in QTM experiments.
- **di/dvnorm** — Normalized differential conductance used to map the electronic band structure.
- **differential conductance di/dv** — Observable tunneling conductance that receives contributions from inelastic processes.
- **differential conductance di/dv** — Raw tunnelling spectra recorded versus bias voltage and QTM angle.
- **differential conductance di/dvb** — QTM tunnelling conductance measured as a function of bias.
- **differential conductance measurement** — The specific measurement of dI/dV used as the spectroscopic observable.
- **displacement measurement** — Measurement of relative displacement enabled after optics alignment is stopped.
- **distance measurement** — Start/stop control for distance measurement in the IDS.
- **dose measurement** — Reading of the exposure dose from the software interface.
- **drive busy status** — Boolean indicator returned by getStatusDriveBusy showing whether an open‑loop command is in progress.
- **drive overload status** — Boolean indicator returned by getStatusDriveOverload showing whether the drive is overloaded.
- **dynamic stm image** — Dynamic STM image generated by a samarium tip over a silicon surface in a magnetic field.
- **d²i/dv²** — Second derivative of the tunneling current, used to reveal inelastic features.
- **d²i/dv² (second derivative of conductance)** — Second derivative of the tunnelling current, used to highlight inelastic features.
- **d²i/dv² map** — Spatially resolved measurement of the second derivative of the tunneling current.
- **d²i/dv² map** — Spatially resolved map of the second derivative of the tunneling current.
- **d²i/dv² maps** — Second derivative of tunneling current with respect to bias voltage, used to reveal spectral features.
- **efm image** — Image showing the electrostatic potential distribution across biased and unbiased conductive strips.
- **electron spin lifetime measurement** — Time‑resolved measurement of spin relaxation in a quantum dot.
- **fast y signal** — Lock-in output proportional to the phase of the QTF signal, used for scanning.
- **ferroelectric polarization measurement** — Measurement of the in‑plane ferroelectric polarization along the C2 axis.
- **fft‑resolved photocurrent** — Fourier‑transformed photocurrent signal used to resolve spectral features.
- **frequency shift measurement** — Measurement of the cantilever frequency shift as a function of tip‑sample separation.
- **high‑resolution current map i(x, y)** — Spatial map of the tunneling current recorded at Vb = –0.35 V and T = 0.2 K.
- **humidity measurement** — Measurement of relative humidity in the beam path.
- **hysteresis measurement** — Measurement of hysteresis curves of ANS scanners.
- **i00 spectra** — Spectra of tunneling current measured by QTM.
- **inelastic conductance step** — Discrete increase in conductance associated with phonon emission.
- **inelastic conductance steps** — Discrete increases in differential conductance occurring when the bias matches a phonon energy.
- **interferometric distance measurement (ids)** — Distance measurement performed with an interferometric sensor (IDS).
- **iv measurement** — Current‑voltage measurement performed with THz QTM.
- **i₀₀ spectrum** — Measurement of the second derivative of current versus bias voltage.
- **junction conductance vs vb and vbg** — Differential conductance recorded while sweeping bias and back‑gate voltages.
- **linear dichroism measurement** — Measurement of linear dichroism signal.
- **linecut a** — Spectroscopic linecut taken at filling factor ν = 1.5 (chemical potential inside flat bands).
- **linecut b** — Spectroscopic linecut taken at filling factor ν = 4.3 (chemical potential outside flat bands).
- **linecut measurement through moiré centre** — Spatial cut used to plot stacking and deformation potentials across the moiré unit cell.
- **local interference measurement** — Measurement performed with the QTM in the interferometer mode, exploiting multiple coherent tunnelling paths.
- **lock‑in detection of gate excitation** — Technique to detect small conductance changes by modulating gate voltage.
- **longitudinal resistance** — Resistance measured along the current direction (C3‑C1).
- **longitudinal resistance measurement (c3‑c1)** — Voltage drop measured between contacts C3 and C1.
- **longitudinal resistance minimum measurement** — Transport measurement of the resistance minimum in quantum Hall regime.
- **low temperature measurement (0.2 k)** — Measurements performed at 0.2 K to reduce thermal broadening.
- **magnetic field sweep** — Applying magnetic field (e.g., B = 5 T) during measurements.
- **magneto‑transport angle measurement** — Transport measurement versus sample rotation angle.
- **measurement of conductance steps δg** — Quantitative determination of conductance changes associated with phonon‑assisted tunnelling.
- **measuring the positioner performance** — Procedure to assess the positioner performance.
- **momentum‑dependent expectation ⟨σ_z(k)⟩** — Calculated expectation value of the σ_z spin component as a function of momentum.
- **normalized differential conductance (di/dv_norm)** — Differential conductance where the valence flat‑band peak is normalized to unity.
- **normalized differential conductance di/dv_norm** — dI/dV data normalized to the valence flat‑band peak intensity.
- **on-defect measurement at b = 5 t⊥** — Differential conductance measurement performed on the defect while applying a perpendicular magnetic field of 5 tesla.
- **opa602 output signal noise spectrum** — Noise spectrum measured at the OPA602 output when the feedback loop hasn't been established.
- **optical and photocurrent measurements** — Optical and photocurrent measurements on NiI₂ devices.
- **optical phonon energy** — Measured energy of intervalley TO/LO phonons as a function of twist angle.
- **output power measurements** — Measurement of laser output power for CO2 and FIR operating modes.
- **output voltage measurement** — Measurement that returns the axis output voltage via the geto command.
- **overall efficiency η_tot** — Integral of the spectral efficiency over the full photon‑energy range, representing total photons per tunnelling electron.
- **peak intensity i_peak** — Integrated area under a dI/dV peak (∫ dI/dV·dVb) used to quantify spectral weight.
- **phase measurement** — Measurement of the phase response of a positioner during resonance testing.
- **phonon spectrum** — Energy‑resolved tunnelling signal revealing phonon modes of bulk graphite.
- **photocurrent** — Current generated by illumination, measured in FTIR‑QTOM experiments.
- **polarization‑dependent photocurrent** — Photocurrent measured while varying the light polarization.
- **position measurement** — Measurement of channel displacement returned by getPosition.
- **positioner performance measurement** — Measurement of positioner axis performance using diagnostic tools.
- **positioner performance test** — Performance tests of the positioner initiated from the operation view.
- **positioner property display** — UI element that shows the measured values of a positioner.
- **positioner property measurement** — Measurement of positioner properties such as capacitance, resistance and temperature.
- **positioner serial number** — Serial number read out from the positioner configuration.
- **power measurement** — Measurement of the laser output power; installed for Oscillator and ALPHA HP but not for MIR.
- **power measurement (before/after tube)** — Measurement of THz power upstream and downstream of the nitrogen‑filled tube.
- **qtm tunneling spectra** — Differential tunneling spectra measured with the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **raman spectrum acquisition** — Recording of Raman intensity versus wavenumber.
- **raw di/dv signal** — The unprocessed differential conductance signal recorded during spectroscopy.
- **reading ac‑in status** — Operation that prints the current state of the AC‑IN input port.
- **reading pattern** — Operation that iterates over m2.patu and prints index/value pairs.
- **res‑position read‑out** — Read‑out of the RES position using shared lines.
- **room temperature measurement** — Measurements performed at ambient temperature (≈300 K).
- **rped measurement** — Measurement of resonant photoelectron diffraction patterns at a fixed photon energy.
- **rpes measurement** — Measurement of resonant photoemission spectra as a function of photon and binding energy.
- **scan** — The action of starting a measurement scan from the AFM software UI.
- **second derivative (d²i/dv²) measurement** — Numerical second derivative of the I‑V characteristic.
- **second derivative d²i/dv²** — Second derivative of current, sensitive to inelastic tunneling processes.
- **second derivative of tunneling current (d²i/dv²)** — Quantity plotted as a function of bias voltage and twist angle to reveal singularities.
- **second derivative tunneling spectroscopy (d²i/dv²)** — Measurement of the second derivative of tunneling current with respect to bias voltage.
- **second‑derivative d²i/dv² measurement** — The measured quantity used to reveal flat‑band and remote‑band features in the tunnelling spectra.
- **second‑harmonic generation measurements** — Second‑harmonic generation optical measurements.
- **spatial current measurement** — Mapping of current as a function of tip position using the AFM.
- **spectrally resolved inelastic tunneling probability** — Probability density J(k∥,ω) as a function of bias voltage and spacing.
- **spin chirality measurement** — Determination of spin chirality via electric dipole or related probes.
- **step‑size repeatability measurement** — Measurement of step‑size repeatability at a given frequency and applied voltage.
- **temperature‑dependent cpge** — CPGE measured as a function of temperature.
- **thickness measurement** — Measurement of resist thickness using a profilometer.
- **thickness measurement** — Determination of flake thickness by AFM.
- **tip contact area measurement** — Extraction of the tip’s contact area from defect‑induced current images.
- **total inelastic tunneling current density** — Integrated tunneling current density over k∥ and ω.
- **transconductance di/dvbg** — Derivative of current with respect to gate voltage.
- **transfer‑function measurement** — Method to obtain the frequency response of the tip‑sample distance.
- **transverse resistance** — Resistance measured perpendicular to the current (C3‑C4).
- **transverse resistance measurement (c3‑c4)** — Hall voltage measured between contacts C3 and C4.
- **tunneling conductance** — Differential conductance dI/dV derived from the spectral function.
- **tunneling conductance (di/dv) measurement** — Differential conductance spectroscopy used to probe inelastic tunneling features.
- **tunneling current as function of twist angle and bias voltage** — Measurement that maps I(V,θ) to reveal momentum‑resolved phonon spectra.
- **tunneling spectra** — Differential conductance curves obtained from tunneling experiments.
- **tunnelling conductance (di/dv)** — Differential conductance measured as a function of bias and gate voltages.
- **tunnelling current map** — Spatial map of tunnelling current recorded as a function of tip position.
- **tunnelling current mapping** — Measurement of current I as a function of tip position and bias voltage.
- **tunnelling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic measurement of the local density of states via tunnelling current.
- **typical approach curve measured at ambient condition** — Approach curve from which the imaging‑signal slope, frequency noise and minimum detectable height are derived.
- **voltage measurement** — Measuring voltage across the junction.
- **voltage‑dependent raman measurement** — Specific measurement type used to study bias effects on Raman spectra.
- **yaml.txt data file** — File where angle, current, X, Y, R are saved.
- **zero‑bias conductance i₀** — Differential conductance measured at zero bias, used as a probe of nodal crossings.
- **zero‑bias conductance measurement (i₀)** — Conductance recorded at Vb≈0 used to detect nodal crossings.
- **zero‑bias di/dv** — Differential conductance at zero bias used to map spectral weight at the Fermi level.
- **zero‑bias di/dv map vs x and vtg** — Differential conductance recorded at zero bias while sweeping gate voltage across the moiré unit cell.
- **zero‑temperature di/dv measurement** — Differential conductance measured at zero temperature.

### phenomenon
- **1/f noise** — Low‑frequency noise component that scales approximately as 1/f.
- **1/√q divergence** — The singular behaviour of the electron‑phonon coupling for acoustic phonons before the gauge transformation.
- **abrikosov vortex** — Slow moving vortex in type‑II superconductors.
- **absorption** — Physical effect where light intensity is reduced while propagating through a material.
- **ac-in functionality** — AC-IN functionality of the ANM300 module.
- **acoustic noise** — Acoustic noise in the laboratory environment.
- **acoustic phonon mode** — Low‑energy lattice vibration mode.
- **acoustic plasmon linear dispersion** — Nearly linear dispersion of the acoustic plasmon staying just above the intraband gap.
- **acoustic plasmons** — Linearly dispersing plasmon modes in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **altermagnetic instabilities** — Unstable electronic or magnetic states in altermagnets.
- **altermagnetic lifting of kramers spin degeneracy** — Removal of Kramers spin degeneracy due to altermagnetism.
- **amplitude decrease with layer alignment** — Observed reduction of tunneling signal when the probe aligns with the opposite graphene layer.
- **amplitude reduction in stm signals** — Decrease of signal amplitude over the whole frequency range after optimization.
- **andreev reflection** — Process where an electron reflects as a hole at a superconductor interface.
- **angle dependence of tunneling matrix elements** — Variation of |T|² with twist angle.
- **angle‑dependent phenomena** — Physical effects that vary with the orientation of the magnetic field relative to an anisotropic sample.
- **anisotropic zeeman spin‑splitting** — Direction‑dependent Zeeman splitting of electron spins.
- **anomalous hall effect** — Hall voltage arising from intrinsic magnetic order rather than external field.
- **antenna‑coupled photon emission** — Enhanced photon emission from a tunnel junction using a nano‑antenna.
- **antiferromagnetic mott insulator** — Correlated insulating state with antiferromagnetic order.
- **antiferromagnetic phase transition** — AFM phase transition at the Néel temperature of 733 K.
- **antiferromagnetic state** — Phase of NiI₂ appearing near 75 K where spins order antiparallel.
- **arcing** — Electrical discharge that can occur at the edges of piezo electrodes.
- **asymmetry in i‑v characteristic** — Difference between forward and reverse bias I‑V curves despite nominally symmetric heterostructure.
- **atomic defects in wse₂** — Bright spots observed in AFM indicating point defects in the WSe₂ layer.
- **atomic resolution imaging** — Ability to resolve individual atoms on a surface using STM or AFM.
- **atomic resolution in stm** — Ability to resolve individual atoms
- **atomic‑resolution features** — STM image details at the scale of individual atoms, requiring detailed tip wavefunction modeling.
- **avoided crossing of to and lo modes near k point** — TO and LO phonon branches exhibit an avoided crossing along the measured momentum trajectory.
- **background elastic‑tunneling current** — Angle‑independent current arising from imperfect momentum conservation.
- **band bending** — Distortion of electronic bands near the barrier due to applied bias.
- **band flattening** — Reduction of electronic band dispersion.
- **band pinning to the fermi level** — Conduction (valence) flat band becomes pinned just above (below) EF with a shift of ≈15 meV upon electron (hole) doping.
- **band splitting** — Separation of electronic bands in momentum space.
- **band stretching** — Filling‑dependent widening of flat‑band dispersion caused by Hartree shifts.
- **band stretching near γ** — Observed widening of the flat bands around the Γ point due to Hartree effects.
- **bandwidth limit** — Bandwidth constrained by the time constant f·f·C·R due to parallel capacitance.
- **beam diameter as function of beam current** — Observed dependence of electron‑beam diameter on the beam current.
- **beam divergence** — Natural spreading of a Gaussian beam due to diffraction.
- **bending resonance** — Observed resonance of the bending motion at a specific frequency.
- **bending‑motion interference** — Low‑frequency bending modes that can degrade STM performance.
- **berrys phase** — Geometric phase acquired by electrons in graphene.
- **bias‑induced chemical‑potential shift** — Shift of the tip’s chemical potential that follows the applied bias voltage.
- **blueshift** — Shift of the resonant peak to higher energy as temperature is lowered.
- **blueshift with decreasing temperature** — Temperature‑dependent shift of the resonant peak consistent with exciton optical shifts.
- **blue‑shift of quasi‑particles** — Slight increase of quasi‑particle energies observed in 3D calculations.
- **blurred features at fermi edge** — Smeared signatures in d²I/dV² maps when the Fermi edge crosses flat or remote bands.
- **bogoliubov excitation spectrum** — Energy spectrum of Bogoliubov quasiparticles as a function of momentum K_θ.
- **bragg scattering processes** — Scattering events involving reciprocal lattice vectors GT and GB.
- **breaking of c3z symmetry** — The loss of three‑fold rotational symmetry caused by heterostrain or other perturbations.
- **broad continuum in excitation spectrum** — Continuum of excitations characteristic of quantum spin liquids.
- **broad peak** — A wide spectral feature observed when the flat bands are fully filled.
- **broken rotational symmetry** — Loss of rotational invariance in the electronic state.
- **buzzing sound** — Audible buzzing indicating possible piezo damage.
- **c3z symmetry breaking** — Loss of three‑fold rotational symmetry, e.g., caused by uniaxial strain or nematic order.
- **cantilever apex contact** — Unwanted contact of the cantilever apex with the sample before tip engagement.
- **capacitance measurement** — Measurement of the capacitance of an axis, performed after setting CAP mode.
- **cascade of electronic transitions** — Successive electronic phase changes observed as carrier density is varied in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **cascade of phase transitions** — Series of successive electronic transitions observed in magic‑angle graphene.
- **cascades** — Series of abrupt changes in electronic properties as a function of carrier density.
- **cascading behaviour of hubbard bands** — Systematic shift of Hubbard‑band energies with filling.
- **cdw/mott correlated tunneling** — Tunneling influenced by charge‑density‑wave and Mott physics.
- **charge fluctuations** — Fluctuating charge density in the sample that couples to electrons in tip and sample.
- **charge inhomogeneity** — Spatial fluctuations of carrier density that affect the ability to tune the Fermi level.
- **charge reshuffling** — Transfer of electrons between light and heavy sectors that underlies Dirac revivals.
- **charge transfer** — Transfer of carriers between layers in a heterobilayer.
- **charged skyrmions** — Topological spin textures carrying electric charge.
- **charge‑density suppression at aa sites** — Reduced electronic charge density at AA regions near the Γ point.
- **charge‑density waves** — Periodic modulation of the electronic charge density in a crystal.
- **charge‑transfer plasmon polaritons** — Plasmonic excitations arising from interlayer charge transfer.
- **chemical forces** — Attractive/repulsive interactions that appear when the tip approaches the sample within a few Å.
- **chern mosaic** — Domain pattern of alternating Chern numbers.
- **chern mosaic** — Spatial pattern of Chern numbers in moiré bands.
- **chern‑insulator phase** — Topological insulating phase with quantized Hall response.
- **chiral magnetoelectric oscillations** — Oscillatory magnetoelectric response with a handedness (chirality).
- **chiral magnon band splitting** — Band splitting of magnons due to chirality.
- **chiral magnon splitting** — Observation of two distinct magnon branches caused by altermagnetic interactions.
- **chiral plasmons** — Plasmon modes with a defined handedness.
- **chiral spin splitting** — Observation of spin‑split magnon branches along low‑symmetry directions.
- **chiral split magnon** — Observation of magnon splitting with chiral character.
- **chiral superconductivity** — Superconducting state with a complex order parameter that breaks time‑reversal symmetry.
- **chiral superconductivity** — Superconductivity with a definite handedness of the order parameter.
- **chiral superconductivity** — Superconducting state that breaks time‑reversal symmetry and carries a definite chirality.
- **circular dichroism in electromagnon modes** — Observation of circular dichroism for electromagnon modes EM1 and EM2, absent for phonon or magnon modes.
- **circular dichroism sign changes between sites and edges** — Observed reversal of CD sign when moving from site A to B or from L₃ to L₂.
- **circular photogalvanic effect (cpge)** — Non‑zero photocurrent that depends on the circular polarization (helicity) of light.
- **circular photogalvanic effect (cpge)** — Spin‑dependent intrinsic photocurrent generated under circularly polarized light.
- **coherence peak** — Sharp spectral feature whose intensity follows the Bogoliubov coherence factors.
- **coherent elastic tunneling** — Phase‑preserving tunneling of electrons between two graphene sheets without energy loss.
- **coherent tunneling** — Phase‑preserving tunneling across an extended contact.
- **coherent tunneling** — Tunneling where phase coherence leads to interference of trajectories.
- **collective excitations** — Coherent many‑body modes such as plasmons or phonons.
- **commensurate electronic states** — Electronic states that are periodic with a common superlattice of two misoriented layers.
- **commensurate electronic states** — Electronic states that are periodic with the underlying moiré lattice.
- **conductance enhancement** — Increase of conductance within the double X structure.
- **conductance peaks at commensurate angles** — Enhanced conductance observed when the twist angle matches a commensurate value.
- **conductance peaks at specific twist angles** — Sharp increases in dI/dV observed at θ ≈ 21.8° and 38.2°.
- **conduction-band tunneling** — Tunneling process that involves electronic states near the conduction‑band edge.
- **conductivity jumps** — Temporal jumps in conductivity observed up to 1e5 Simmens.
- **constant optical‑phonon coupling** — The intralayer electron‑phonon coupling approaches a constant value in the long‑wavelength limit for optical phonons.
- **conventional plasmons (√q dispersion)** — Plasmons in doped TBG that follow a √q dispersion at long wavelengths.
- **correlated insulating state** — Interaction‑driven insulating behavior in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **correlated insulator behaviour** — Insulating state driven by strong electron correlations.
- **correlated insulator behaviour** — Insulating state driven by strong electron‑electron interactions.
- **correlated insulators** — Insulating phases driven by strong electron‑electron interactions.
- **correlated states** — Electron‑electron interaction driven phases in flat‑band systems.
- **coulomb blockade** — Coulomb blockade observed in the current‑voltage curve of a 450 nm diameter quantum dot.
- **coulomb blockade peak** — Sharp conductance resonance occurring when the defect level aligns with one of the electrodes.
- **coulomb diamond** — Charge‑stability region in a quantum dot transport diagram.
- **coulomb diamond diagram** — Diamond‑shaped regions in dI/dV maps indicating Coulomb blockade of a quantum dot.
- **coulomb diamond doubling** — Experimental observation of two Coulomb diamonds.
- **coulomb staircase (phenomenon)** — Observation of discrete charge steps at room temperature
- **coupling strength increases with decreasing qm** — Observed intensification of EPC as the characteristic momentum qM becomes smaller.
- **coupling to flexural phonons** — Interaction between tunneling electrons and out‑of‑plane (flexural) phonon modes.
- **coupling to in‑plane phonons** — Interaction between tunneling electrons and in‑plane phonon modes.
- **crossing of za and ta modes** — Crossing of ZA and TA phonon modes around twist angle 6°.
- **crystal time‑reversal symmetry breaking** — Breaking of time‑reversal symmetry at the crystal level in collinear antiferromagnets.
- **current maximum at δε = 0** — In the hyperbolic‑intersection regime the tunneling current reaches a maximum at zero energy offset.
- **current signal between tip and sample** — Electrical current observed when the tip approaches the sample surface.
- **c‑electron energy shift** — Weak Hartree‑induced shift of c‑like states with filling.
- **d+id superconductivity** — A chiral superconducting state with combined d‑wave components reported for twisted bilayer graphene.
- **damage due to excess screw length** — Potential damage to the positioner when screws longer than allowed are used.
- **dc-in functionality** — DC-IN functionality of the ANM300 module.
- **defect‑assisted tunnelling (phenomenon)** — Observed increase of tunnelling current when the tip overlaps a defect.
- **defect‑mediated tunneling** — Electron tunneling facilitated by localized defect states.
- **defect‑mediated tunnelling** — Tunnelling that occurs via defect states in hexagonal boron nitride junctions.
- **dember effect** — Photocurrent arising from carrier diffusion imbalance.
- **density waves** — Charge or spin modulations in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **density‑dependent chemical potential µ(n)** — Variation of the chemical potential with carrier density in the graphene electrodes.
- **detector noise** — Overall noise of the frequency detector, including contributions from the amplifier.
- **device movement** — Movement of the positioner when a target position is commanded.
- **di/dv steps** — Steps observed in differential conductance due to discontinuous density of states at band edges.
- **dielectric breakdown** — Failure of h-BN insulating barrier at high electric field.
- **differential tunneling conductance di/dv** — Measured quantity reflecting tunneling current response to bias voltage.
- **dipole‑like feature** — Dipole‑shaped dip observed in dI/dVbg at integer fillings.
- **dirac point shift upward with filling** — Movement of the Dirac point toward the Fermi energy as carriers are added.
- **dirac revival** — Reappearance of Dirac‑like dispersion as charge is reshuffled between light and heavy sectors.
- **dirac revivals** — Observed reappearance of Dirac points in the tunnelling spectra.
- **dirac revivals phenomenology** — Revival of the c‑electrons Dirac point observed in the experiment.
- **dirac spectrum** — Linear dispersion characteristic of monolayer graphene, observed superimposed on TBG flat bands.
- **dirac trions** — Three‑particle bound states with Dirac‑like dispersion.
- **dirac-point singularity** — Singular feature in d^2I/dV^2 associated with tip Dirac point.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic probing of the Dirac point in flat‑band systems.
- **dirac‑δ singularities** — Sharp features in d²I/dV² occurring at specific bias voltages due to tunneling channels.
- **dirac‑δ singularities** — Delta‑like peaks in the second derivative of the tunnelling current at large bias.
- **direct exciton requires phonon momentum** — Direct excitons need an additional phonon to conserve momentum during tunnelling.
- **direct tunneling** — Coherent fast tunneling regime producing sharp interference spikes.
- **direction‑dependent conduction polarity** — Conduction polarity that varies with crystallographic direction in CrSb.
- **direct‑exciton feature at γ point** — Strong conductivity feature at the Γ point originating from direct excitons.
- **dirt movement observed as horizontal stripes** — Visual indication of contaminant displacement during AFM cleaning scans.
- **discontinuity across θ_c** — Abrupt change of tunneling matrix elements when the twist angle passes the commensurate value.
- **discontinuity at θ_c** — Phase change of π causing a jump in spectra at the commensurate angle.
- **discrepancy between theory and experiment** — Observation that the experimental moiré potential amplitude is roughly twice the theoretical prediction.
- **disorder** — Imperfections in a crystal lattice that affect electronic properties.
- **distribution of néel vector** — Spatial variation of Néel vector orientation across a sample.
- **divergence of tunneling current** — Theoretical 1/√|Δε| divergence of the tunneling current when the nesting condition is fulfilled.
- **domain memory effect** — Persistence of linear‑dichroism domain pattern after thermal or field cycles.
- **domain redistribution** — Rearrangement of spin‑helix domains after applying pulsed electric fields, visible as kinks in hysteresis loops.
- **drive busy indicator** — Indicator showing movement in progress (waveforms being generated).
- **drive overload indicator** — Indicator of internal current‑limit overload conditions.
- **dual nature of electrons** — Coexistence of localized (heavy) and itinerant (light) electronic behavior within the same MATBG flat bands.
- **dual nature of electrons in matbg** — Coexistence of light‑ and heavy‑electron characters in the same material.
- **dynamic polarization** — Frequency‑dependent response of the electron density to an external perturbation.
- **d²i/dv² singularities at dirac points** — Peaks in the second derivative of the tunneling current that signal density‑of‑states singularities at the Dirac points.
- **elastic electronic tunneling jel** — Momentum‑ and energy‑conserving tunneling component.
- **elastic momentum‑conserving tunnelling** — Elastic tunnelling that conserves in‑plane momentum.
- **elastic scattering** — Elastic scattering cross‑section associated with plasmon‑driven extinction (σ_scat).
- **elastic tip-sample tunneling** — Momentum non‑conserving tunneling between tip and sample without energy exchange.
- **elastic tunneling** — Energy‑conserving tunneling observed as strong conductance peaks.
- **elastic tunneling** — Tunneling process that conserves the quasiparticle energy, associated with quasiparticle peaks.
- **elastic tunneling (phenomenon)** — Dominant tunneling channel when tip and sample Fermi circles intersect.
- **elastic tunneling current** — Current that flows when electrons tunnel elastically between tip and sample.
- **elastic tunnelling** — Tunnelling without energy loss, defining the onset of the conductance threshold.
- **electrical 180° switching of néel vector** — Reversal of the Néel vector by electrical means in spin‑splitting antiferromagnets.
- **electrical control of spin chirality** — Switching spin chirality by electrically toggling ferroelectric polarization.
- **electrical force** — Force arising from electrostatic interactions probed by EFM.
- **electrical switching of a p‑wave magnet** — Voltage‑controlled reversal of the non‑relativistic spin polarization in a p‑wave magnetic state.
- **electroluminescence (el) emission** — Light emitted from the device under electrical bias.
- **electromagnon** — Collective spin‑lattice excitation in NiI2 with energy around 1 THz.
- **electromagnon excitations** — Hybrid magnon‑photon excitations in multiferroics.
- **electron (hole) scattering with plasmon emission** — Inelastic scattering of charge carriers accompanied by plasmon creation.
- **electron interactions** — Coulomb interactions that become dominant at the magic angle.
- **electronic cascades** — Filling‑dependent emergence of high‑energy bands that shift and sharpen as the filling approaches integer values.
- **electronic cascades** — Cascade of electronic transitions observed in magic‑angle graphene.
- **electronic correlations** — Interaction‑driven many‑body effects in the electronic system.
- **electronic structure imaging** — STM imaging that reflects variations in electronic charge distribution rather than pure geometry.
- **electron‑electron interaction** — Coulomb interaction between electrons, which can be ignored in Bardeen's theory.
- **electron‑electron interactions** — Coulomb interaction effects studied in graphene with the QTM.
- **electron‑hole asymmetry** — Asymmetry between conduction and valence bands introduced by NNN hopping.
- **electron‑phonon coupling** — Interaction between electrons and phonons arising from bond‑length changes and deformation potential.
- **electron‑phonon coupling** — Interaction between electrons and phonons, quantified via QTM measurements.
- **electron‑plasmon scattering** — Interaction process whereby electrons emit or absorb plasmons.
- **electron–phason coupling** — Coupling between electrons and phason phonon modes in twisted graphene.
- **electrostatic discharge** — Event that can deactivate the BNC output ports automatically.
- **electrostatic discharge** — Electrostatic discharge risks in low humidity conditions.
- **electro‑mechanical noise** — Mechanical vibrations induced by electronic noise on piezoelectric actuators.
- **elementary excitations** — Low‑energy collective modes of a one‑dimensional electron system.
- **encoder damage** — Failure of the encoder caused by applying high drive voltage through an incorrect gender changer.
- **end‑of‑travel detection** — Detection of the mechanical travel limit that stops the device motion.
- **energy gap at charge neutrality** — A clear gap observed at the KT point when the system is at charge neutrality.
- **energy-momentum conserving state** — States that satisfy both energy and momentum conservation within the bias window.
- **energy‑momentum conserving states** — Tunnelling states that satisfy simultaneous energy and momentum conservation across the junction.
- **enhanced epc for acoustic modes at small twist angles** — Observed increase of EPC as the twist angle decreases for acoustic phonons.
- **equilibrium of electrons, plasmons, and phonons** — Relaxation dynamics among electrons, plasmons and phonons in plasmas.
- **excess force on cryostat components** — Excess force on cryostat components caused by nearby steel.
- **excitation energy independent of filling** — Observed excited states whose energy does not change with carrier density.
- **excitations at ±15 mev** — Doping‑independent excitations observed at energies ±15 meV.
- **exciton binding‑energy tuning** — Change of exciton binding energy with applied electric field.
- **exciton polaron** — Coupled exciton‑phonon quasiparticle observed in 2D hBN and LiF.
- **exciton polarons** — Hybrid quasiparticles formed by coupling excitons to lattice vibrations.
- **exciton–phonon interaction** — Coupling between excitons and phonons observed directly in transport measurements.
- **exotic carriers** — Unconventional charge carriers beyond simple electron/hole quasiparticles.
- **exotic physics** — Unconventional phenomena emerging in magic‑angle graphene.
- **exponential dependence of tunneling current on tip‑sample distance** — Fundamental STM effect where current varies exponentially with distance.
- **exponential dependence of tunneling current on vacuum gap** — Current varies as exp(−d/λ), reflecting barrier tunneling physics.
- **external influences** — Environmental perturbations that increase sensitivity when cabling is longer.
- **external vibrations** — External vibrations identified in topographic scans as mirrored in forward and backward scans.
- **extraneous current interference** — Interference in sensor signals due to current flowing in coaxial shields or other ground paths.
- **fabry‑perot resonance (phenomenon)** — Resonant modulation of the tunneling current due to multiple reflections between electrostatic pn‑junctions.
- **fabry–pérot oscillations** — Oscillatory conductance due to cavity effects of the tip.
- **fermi-edge contribution** — Vanishing contribution due to zero density of states at the tip chemical potential.
- **ferroelectric hysteresis** — Polarization switching loop characteristic of ferroelectric materials.
- **ferroelectric hysteresis loop** — Loop observed in the zero‑bias photocurrent as a function of applied pulsed electric field.
- **fine positioning behavior** — The way the positioner moves within the fine positioning range, affecting stepping and noise following.
- **finite‑bias crossing of second coulomb diamond** — Second diamond crossing at ~20 meV bias.
- **flat bands** — Energy bands with very low dispersion that host correlated electronic phenomena.
- **flatband region** — Frequency region where input voltage noise is amplified by the feedback network.
- **flavour symmetry breaking** — Spontaneous breaking of valley (flavour) symmetry, e.g., in a valley‑polarized Chern insulator.
- **floor vibrations** — Floor vibrations that can affect sensitive equipment.
- **flux‑flow dissipation** — Dissipation observed as a function of the angle between magnetic field and FeAs layers.
- **fowler–nordheim tunnelling (phenomenon)** — Field‑emission tunnelling through the WSe₂ conduction band.
- **fractional chern insulators** — Topologically ordered states with fractional Hall conductance.
- **fractional quantum anomalous hall states** — Topological states with fractional Hall conductance without external magnetic field.
- **fractional quantum hall effect** — Quantized Hall conductance at fractional filling factors, discussed in several cited works.
- **fractionalization** — Emergence of fractional quantum numbers in magnetic heterostructures.
- **fractionally charged quasiparticles** — Excitations carrying a fraction of the electron charge, relevant to fractional quantum Hall physics.
- **frequency detector noise** — Noise contributed by the electronic frequency detection circuitry.
- **frequency response of dc-in input** — Response of DC-IN input up to ~100 kHz depending on output filters.
- **fresnel reflection coefficient** — Reflection coefficient of the sandwich for s and p polarizations; vanishes for s polarization.
- **friction observed in 8‑channel scan** — Additional signal that confirms successful cleaning of the surface.
- **friction resistance** — Resistance caused by friction between the alumina plate and the sapphire plate, limiting step size at low temperature.
- **friction‑induced heating of the bath** — Heat generated by mechanical friction of the positioners, raising the cryostat bath temperature by ~100–150 mK at full speed.
- **fully compensated antiferromagnetic order** — Fully compensated antiferromagnetic (altermagnetic) order in CrSb.
- **f‑electron energy shift** — Large Hartree‑induced upward shift of f‑like states with increasing filling.
- **gap in qtm spectrum** — Opening of a superconducting gap that appears only for specific tip rotation angles.
- **gap opening along the fermi line** — Opening of a superconducting gap on the Fermi line, producing a singularity at –eV = Δ when tip and sample Fermi lines cross.
- **gapped dispersion** — Dispersion relation showing an energy gap.
- **gaps not well developed** — Superconducting or tunneling gaps appear weak or smeared in the spectra.
- **gaps to remote bands** — Enhanced features at ν = ±4 due to gaps to remote dispersive bands.
- **gas runout** — Depletion of precursor gas during a long deposition run.
- **gnd functionality** — Ground (GND) functionality of the ANM300 module.
- **goldstone modes** — Low‑energy collective excitations of symmetry‑broken phases.
- **ground loops** — Ground loops that can affect delicate sensor measurements.
- **hartree stretching** — Momentum‑dependent Hartree energy shifts that stretch the Γ‑point band relative to other momenta.
- **heat load from superconducting contacts** — Thermal power injected into the device by the superconducting leads.
- **heavy fermions** — Quasiparticles with large effective mass emerging from strong correlations.
- **heavy quasiparticles** — Electron‑like excitations with strongly enhanced effective mass.
- **heavy‑electron flat bands** — Observation of flat bands populated by heavy electrons.
- **heavy‑electron spectral intensity decay** — Decrease of heavy‑electron signal by 80 % used to define the heavy‑light transition angle.
- **helimagnetic ground state** — Low‑temperature phase of NiI₂ (≈ 59.5 K) with a proper‑screw spin helix.
- **helimagnetic state** — Magnetic order in NiI₂ where spins form a spiral (helix) propagating in‑plane.
- **helimagnetic transition** — Transition to a helical magnetic state accompanied by ferroelectric polarization.
- **high precision angular motion** — Angular positioning stability in the m° range that yields high resolution and good signal‑to‑noise.
- **high sensitivity** — Enhanced detection capability of a high‑Q cantilever.
- **higher electron temperature** — Electron temperature elevated above the lattice temperature, reducing gap visibility.
- **high‑density regime** — The regime close to the surface where electron density is high and the potential is strong.
- **hole doping** — Shift of the graphene Fermi level toward lower energies (≈0.1 eV) due to charge transfer.
- **hole excitation** — Creation of a hole in the Fermi sea during tunneling processes.
- **hole polarons** — Polaronic states associated with holes in hBN.
- **homostrain** — Extrinsic uniform strain applied to the sample, another source of C3z symmetry breaking.
- **hot‑electron injection (phenomenon)** — Generation of high‑energy electrons during high‑bias tunnelling.
- **hubbard bands** — Upper and lower bands split by strong on‑site repulsion.
- **hybridization between plasmons and phonons** — Coupling of plasmonic and phononic excitations.
- **hybridization of energy bands** — Hybridization of electronic bands due to strong interlayer coupling.
- **hysteresis** — Piezo‑inherent hysteresis caused by saturation and ferroelectric properties.
- **hysteretic anomalous hall effect** — Large hysteresis in Hall resistance indicating ferromagnetic order.
- **image‑potential regime** — The regime at large distances from the surface where the image potential dominates the electron behaviour.
- **imbalanced fermi level** — Finite‑bias crossing of a second Coulomb diamond interpreted as a shift of the local Fermi level.
- **improved stm performance** — Observed increase in signal stability and bandwidth after design changes.
- **improvement in response** — Observed reduction of the magnitude of the transfer function after optimization.
- **impurity‑induced resonant spinon zero modes** — Zero‑energy spinon states created by impurities in Dirac quantum spin liquids.
- **incoherent tunneling** — Tunneling through many independent local contacts.
- **incoherent tunneling** — Tunneling where electrons tunnel independently at different locations without phase coherence.
- **increase in di/dv at the onset of plasmon‑assisted tunneling** — Observed rise in differential conductance when plasmon‑assisted channels open.
- **indirect exciton provides momentum** — Indirect excitons supply the missing in‑plane momentum for graphene‑to‑Au tunnelling.
- **indirect‑exciton maxima at q point** — Appearance of intensity maxima in the TMD conductivity at the Q point due to indirect excitons.
- **inelastic current flow** — Current arising from processes that exchange energy with phonons.
- **inelastic electron scattering** — Scattering of electrons off collective excitations, producing spectral weight away from quasiparticle peaks.
- **inelastic electron scattering during tunneling** — Energy‑loss processes of tunneling electrons mediated by the QSL excitations.
- **inelastic electron tunneling** — Tunneling process where electrons lose energy, here transferred to photons.
- **inelastic electron tunnelling mediated by phonon emission** — Electron tunnelling that occurs together with the emission of a phonon, observed in graphene tunnelling devices.
- **inelastic electronic tunneling jinel** — Energy‑conserving but momentum‑non‑conserving tunneling.
- **inelastic momentum‑conserving phonon emission** — Inelastic tunnelling accompanied by phonon emission conserving momentum.
- **inelastic phonon emission** — Process where tunnelling electrons lose energy by emitting a phonon, visible as bias‑independent conductance steps.
- **inelastic phonon‑assisted tunnelling (phenomenon)** — Tunnelling process assisted by phonon emission or absorption.
- **inelastic scattering of cooper pairs with pairing glue** — Process that creates additional features in tunneling spectra.
- **inelastic tunneling** — Tunneling process where electrons lose energy to excitations such as plasmons.
- **inelastic tunneling** — Tunneling processes accompanied by excitation emission.
- **inelastic tunneling current** — Current arising from tunneling processes that involve phonon emission or absorption.
- **inelastic tunneling current** — Current arising from electron tunneling accompanied by phonon emission or absorption.
- **inelastic tunneling features** — Spectral features arising from inelastic tunneling processes.
- **inelastic tunneling processes** — Tunneling events that involve emission or absorption of phonons to satisfy momentum conservation.
- **inelastic tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic technique that extracts information from the inelastic component of the tunneling current.
- **inelastic tunneling with phonon emission** — Tunneling process that creates a phonon to compensate momentum mismatch.
- **inelastic tunnelling** — Tunnelling accompanied by excitation of particle‑hole pairs or plasmons.
- **inelastic tunnelling** — Tunnelling process accompanied by phonon emission.
- **insensitivity of plasmon peaks to tip screening** — Plasmon peaks remain qualitatively unchanged away from the γ point despite tip screening.
- **instability of the second parabola** — Observed mechanical instability of the second parabola during alignment.
- **integer‑filling suppression** — Suppression of dI/dVb observed at integer filling factors ν = ±4.
- **interaction‑induced bandwidth renormalization** — Reduction of band dispersion caused by electron‑electron interactions in MATBG.
- **interband collective excitations** — Collective modes involving transitions between different electronic bands.
- **interference suppression of tunneling matrix elements** — Interference suppression of tunneling matrix elements near the bottom‑layer Dirac point.
- **interlayer coherence** — Phase‑coherent coupling between electronic states in two separate layers.
- **interlayer electron-plasmon scattering** — Scattering process between electrons in the tip and plasmons in the sample.
- **interlayer electron‑phonon coupling** — Electron‑phonon coupling that modifies interlayer tunneling.
- **interlayer mechanism dominates ta mode** — For the TA phonon, the interlayer EPC contribution is larger than the intralayer one.
- **interlayer tunneling** — Charge transport across the hBN dielectric separating two graphene layers.
- **interplay between light and heavy electron bands** — Coupling effect between light and heavy electron bands in twisted graphene.
- **intralayer coupling strengthens as twist angle decreases** — The averaged intralayer EPC contribution grows when the twist angle is reduced.
- **intralayer electron‑phonon coupling** — Electron‑phonon coupling within a single graphene layer due to changes in bond lengths.
- **intralayer mechanism dominates la mode** — For the LA phonon, the intralayer EPC contribution exceeds the interlayer contribution.
- **inverse square‑root singularities in di/dv** — Sharp features in conductance occurring when tip Fermi line is tangent to quasiparticle dispersion.
- **inverse square‑root singularity in di/dv** — Observed singularity in differential conductance at V* or V**.
- **in‑plane josephson vortex** — Josephson vortex centered in a non‑superconducting Sm(O,F) layer, weakly pinned.
- **isospin magnetism** — Magnetic order in the valley/isospin degree of freedom.
- **johnson noise** — Thermal noise that increases in the transimpedance amplifier.
- **josephson vortex** — Fast moving vortex in layered superconductors.
- **joule heating** — Resistive heating in graphene when an electric current flows.
- **kink in di/dv** — Weak conductance anomaly marking the onset of plasmon‑assisted tunneling.
- **kinks in di/dv** — Weak features in the differential conductance associated with plasmon‑assisted tunneling.
- **kondo phase** — Correlated state where localized moments are screened by conduction electrons.
- **kondo phase** — Screening‑induced heavy‑fermion state.
- **k‑resolved onset** — Alignment condition where the Dirac cones first intersect in momentum space, producing a distinct tunnelling feature.
- **landau damping of plasmons** — Decay of plasmons into particle‑hole excitations, limiting their lifetime at low energies.
- **landau level gaps** — Energy gaps in graphene’s spectrum that appear as steps in the Coulomb‑diamond edges under magnetic field.
- **landau quantization** — Quantization of electron energy levels in a magnetic field leading to discrete Landau levels.
- **laser action** — Coherent radiation emitted as a result of stimulated emission in the FIR laser.
- **lattice relaxation and corrugation** — Structural adjustments of the graphene layers that slightly modify tunneling matrix elements.
- **lifetime of two‑dimensional electrons** — Decay time of electronic excitations in a 2D electron gas.
- **light emission from inelastic electron tunneling** — Physical Review Letters 1976 pioneering report of light emission from inelastic electron tunnelling.
- **light emission from tunnel junctions** — Electroluminescence observed when a bias is applied across the graphene/h‑BN/graphene stack.
- **light‑electron band bending due to hartree** — Hartree‑induced shift that pushes light‑electron bands below the Fermi level near Γ.
- **limit exceeded display** — Display message when output power or voltage limits are reached.
- **limit functionality** — Limit functionality of the ANM300 module.
- **limit‑exceeded display** — The display shows “Limit exceeded” on an axis when a voltage, frequency or power limit is reached.
- **linear dichroism** — Differential absorption of orthogonal linear polarizations.
- **linear dichroism (ld)** — Differential absorption of linearly polarized light revealing domain orientation.
- **linear dichroism jump** — Sharp increase of linear dichroism at the helimagnetic transition temperature (TN₂ = 59.5 K).
- **linear photogalvanic effect (lpge)** — Photocurrent components proportional to sin(4φ) and cos(4φ) generated by linearly polarized light.
- **linear photogalvanic effect (lpge)** — Photocurrent generated by linearly polarized light.
- **linear photogalvanic effect (lpge)** — Photocurrent with both intrinsic and extrinsic contributions generated under linearly polarized light.
- **linear temperature dependence of resistivity** — Unusual resistivity scaling with temperature in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **linear translation of moving table** — The moving table translates linearly when DC voltage is applied.
- **linearity of movement** — Observed linear relationship between driving signal and macor displacement.
- **linear‑in‑t resistivity** — Strange‑metal behaviour with resistivity proportional to temperature.
- **lobes of a 4f orbital** — Observed lobes in the STM image that correspond to a 4f electron orbital.
- **local electronic structure** — Spatially resolved electronic states of a material.
- **local field effects** — Modifications of the interaction at large wave vectors due to the moiré superlattice.
