- **total filling n** — Sum of f‑ and c‑electron occupations, externally fixed and varied from 0 to 1.5 in calculations.
- **total pocket diameter** — Overall diameter of the pocket, possibly increased by 200 µm.
- **track distance (~4.4 µm measured)** — Measured spacing between adjacent magnetic tracks.
- **transconductance** — Gain parameter of the MESFET.
- **transient decay time (τ)** — Time constant τ = Q/(π·ω0) after which the transient decay term disappears.
- **transition probability** — Probability |⟨φ_j|ψ(t)⟩|² for electron transition between sample and tip states.
- **transition width σ** — Parameter of the sigmoid fit that quantifies the sharpness of the heavy‑light transition.
- **transmission amplitude |t_b|²** — Square of the tunneling matrix element that enters the current formula.
- **travel cycles** — Number of complete motion cycles performed by the positioner.
- **travel cycles lifetime** — Lifetime of ~50 000 travel cycles at ambient conditions.
- **travel distance** — Maximum linear travel of the coarse Z motor (~1 cm).
- **travel range (step mode) 360° endless** — Angular travel range in coarse (step) mode.
- **travel range: 2.5 mm** — Maximum linear travel of the ANPz51/RES/LT z‑nanopositioner.
- **travel range: 3 mm** — Maximum linear travel of the ANPx51/RES/LT x‑nanopositioner.
- **travel range: 5 mm** — Maximum linear travel of the ANPz101/RES/LT z‑nanopositioner.
- **travel range: 6 mm** — Maximum linear travel of the ANPx311/RES/LT x‑nanopositioner.
- **trigger input** — Input signal (rising edge) used to start a waveform cycle on the NP-Drive.
- **trigger input** — Digital input used to generate trigger pulses for up or down steps.
- **trigger number** — Identifier (1, 2, 3) for input or output triggers used in trigger commands.
- **trigger polarity** — Active‑high or active‑low setting for the Trigger protocol.
- **tube diameter 6 mm** — Diameter constraint for the tube carrying light in the QTM.
- **tube distance 1 m** — Required distance between tube lens and objective lens.
- **tunnel junction dimensions** — Geometric size of the tunneling region that limits the spatial and momentum resolution of the QTM.
- **tunnel junction resistance** — Resistance of the tunneling barrier, increased by WSe₂.
- **tunneling barrier shape v(z)** — Potential profile of the tunneling barrier as a function of the out‑of‑plane coordinate z.
- **tunneling current** — Current flowing through the QTM junction as a function of twist angle and bias.
- **tunneling matrix element w** — Single parameter controlling the strength of momentum‑conserving tunneling.
- **tunneling parameters w₀ and w₁** — Intrasublattice (w₀) and intersublattice (w₁) tunneling amplitudes.
- **tunneling rate (γ)** — Elastic tip‑sample tunneling rate used as a scale for the inelastic rate.
- **tunneling strength (w²)** — Squared tunneling matrix element used for normalization.
- **tunneling strength t₃₈.₂°** — Approximate interlayer tunneling amplitude (~1 meV) for graphene layers twisted by 38.2°.
- **tunneling strength w** — Amplitude parameter describing the coupling between tip and sample wavefunctions.
- **tunneling strength w²** — Overall prefactor for the tunneling probability.
- **tunnelling distance (d)** — Separation between the tip apex and the surface atom; controls resolution when d ≫ R.
- **tunnelling elements** — Matrix elements governing electron tunnelling between layers.
- **tunnelling resistance** — Electrical resistance of the STM junction, limited experimentally and influencing achievable d.
- **tunnelling voltage** — Bias voltage applied between tip and sample; lowering it is required to reduce d.
- **twist angle** — Twist angle between layered 2D materials.
- **twist angle (θ)** — Relative rotation between graphene and hBN lattices.
- **twist angle (θ)** — Rotational angle between two graphene layers, experimentally tunable in the QTM.
- **twist angle (θ)** — Relative rotation between the graphene layers; a sample with θ≈1.13° is referenced.
- **twist angle (θ)** — Control parameter that sets the relative rotation between tip and MA TBG.
- **twist angle q0** — Relative rotation angle between the two graphene layers.
- **twist angle θ** — Geometric rotation between the two graphene layers.
- **twist angle θ** — Control parameter representing the relative rotation between graphene layers.
- **twist angle θ = 0°** — Relative crystallographic orientation set to zero in the model.
- **twist angle θ_qtm** — Twist angle controlled by the QTM during dI/dV scans.
- **twisted pairs** — Six twisted pairs routed to Fischer connectors in Project 1.
- **twisted triplets** — Four twisted triplets routed to Fischer connectors in Project 2.
- **typical actuator capacitance fine (z) @300 k (0.35 µf)** — Actuator capacitance for fine z mode at 300 K.
- **typical actuator capacitance fine (z) @4 k (0.2 µf)** — Actuator capacitance for fine z mode at 4 K.
- **typical actuator capacitance fine @300 k (1.4 µf)** — Overall actuator capacitance at 300 K.
- **typical actuator capacitance fine @4 k (0.2 µf)** — Overall actuator capacitance at 4 K.
- **ultra‑high vacuum** — Vacuum levels below 10⁻⁹ mbar required for some attocube applications.
- **ultra‑high vacuum (uhv) conditions** — Vacuum environment required for techniques such as ARPES.
- **unipolar dc voltage** — Positive DC voltage applied to the piezo elements.
- **unit‑cell area (a)** — Area of a graphene unit cell, A = (a₀³)/2.
- **unit‑cell area (ω₁)** — Area of the quantum spin‑liquid unit cell appearing in prefactors.
- **upper ids stop limit (pm)** — Upper IDS stop limit position (pm) that deactivates the actuator when reached.
- **upper stop limit** — User‑defined upper bound for the allowed travel range.
- **used refractive index** — Refractive index set manually or taken from the ECU.
- **v*** — Parameter v* = –1 eV·nm used in the THF model.
- **v*** — Parameter v'* = 366 meV·nm used in the THF model.
- **v* (characteristic voltage)** — Voltage at which singularity occurs.
- **v0 = 45 ev** — Potential depth obtained from the atomic carbon 2p orbital model.
- **v_peak (bias voltage at ndc peak)** — Bias voltage where the negative differential conductance peak occurs.
- **vacuum (5×10⁻⁵ torr)** — Base pressure of the chamber during AFM measurements.
- **vacuum gap distance (d)** — Separation between tip and surface that determines exponential decay of the tunneling current.
- **vacuum pressure** — Chamber pressure that can reach 10⁻⁷ bars during deposition.
- **valley current** — Minimum current observed between peaks in the NDR region.
- **valley current density** — Valley current divided by the active area, reported as 320 A/cm² (first etch) and 640 A/cm² (second etch).
- **valley degeneracy gv = 2** — Number of valley states contributing to the graphene density of states.
- **valley index τ** — Index labeling the two graphene valleys related by time‑reversal symmetry.
- **vb** — Bottom gate voltage.
- **vdw adhesion pressure (~1 gpa)** — Typical van‑der‑Waals adhesion pressure between layered 2D crystals.
- **velocity** — Maximum input velocity, defined as Resolution divided by Clock.
- **vertical axis** — Linear actuator moved to defined positions (red and blue) during the procedure.
- **vertical line spacing (nm)** — Spacing between successive scan lines, recommended not larger than 40 nm for this tip.
- **vertical noise (δz)** — Vertical resolution noise derived from frequency‑noise via the slope of the frequency‑shift curve.
- **vertical resolution (~2 å)** — Achieved vertical imaging resolution of the cryogenic AFM.
- **vertical resolution ~2 å** — Achieved vertical resolution in the AFM topography of the gold film.
- **vertical tip‑sample distance d_t** — Separation between the graphene tip and the TBG layers.
- **vibration** — Mechanical vibrations that must be absent in the system work area.
- **vibration amplitude (x)** — Mechanical displacement amplitude of the QTF due to thermal energy.
- **voltage (60 v)** — Typical drive voltage applied to the rotator.
- **voltage (v)** — Drive voltage for the rotator (≈60 V).
- **voltage 200/220 v, 3‑phase, 50 hz** — Another voltage option for the compressor.
- **voltage 220/230 v, 3‑phase, 60 hz** — Another voltage option for the compressor.
- **voltage 380/420 v, 3‑phase, 50 hz** — Another voltage option for the compressor.
- **voltage 460 v, 3‑phase, 60 hz** — One of the voltage options for the compressor.
- **voltage amplitude** — Amplitude of the voltage waveform adjustable via UI sliders.
- **voltage bias (vb)** — Electrical bias applied during dI/dV spectroscopy.
- **voltage bias vb** — Bias applied between the QTM tip and the TBG layer.
- **voltage gain** — Amplifier gain.
- **voltage range** — Specified voltage range for the piezo stack elements.
- **voltage range** — Input voltage range of -10 V to +10 V.
- **voltage spike** — Sharp increase in voltage indicating vortex‑induced dissipation.
- **voltage step duration (ms)** — Minimum duration of each voltage step in the cyclic sweep (≥50 ms).
- **voltage supply** — AC voltage range required by the laser stabiliser, chopper and Q‑switch.
- **volume 100 ml** — Amount of water used to dissolve ammonium persulfite.
- **w_aa** — Interlayer hopping amplitude in AA stacked region (≈88 meV).
- **w_ab** — Interlayer hopping amplitude in AB stacked region (≈110 meV).
- **wafer z level reading** — Z‑level reading of the wafer (precision 0.0010 mm).
- **waiting time** — Time after which the ANC300 automatically returns to the Main Screen.
- **warming rate 0.5 k min⁻¹** — Rate used to warm the sample during zero‑bias photocurrent acquisition.
- **warming speed 3–4 k min⁻¹** — Temperature ramp used in pyroelectric current experiments.
- **warranty period** — Duration of the guarantee, extendable up to 4 years for SPS and SLC TWIN series.
- **wave vector (p*)** — Momentum at which the tip Fermi line and the sample band are tangent, defining the singular bias.
- **wave vector (q)** — Momentum variable used to describe plasmon dispersion and screening.
- **wave vector (q)** — Momentum of a plasmon or electron in the 2D Brillouin zone.
- **wave vector k** — Crystal momentum in the moiré Brillouin zone.
- **wave vector p** — Momentum variable summed over the 2‑D reciprocal space.
- **wave vector p*** — Momentum point where tip and sample bands intersect.
- **wave vector p* (θ)** — Momentum at which the tip Fermi circle touches the sample band, entering the singular tunneling contribution.
- **wave vector p**** — Momentum point of tangential intersection at V**.
- **wave vector q** — In‑plane momentum transferred to the plasmon during tunneling.
- **wave vector qθ** — Momentum transferred to the plasmon, Qθ = Kθ – Kθ_TBG.
- **wavelength (ftir)** — The infrared wavelength selected by the FTIR delay stage.
- **wavelength 1050 nm** — Laser wavelength used in some measurements.
- **wavelength 10 µm** — Wavelength considered for minimum spot size calculations.
- **wavelength 600 nm** — Broad spectral peak observed around 600 nm.
- **wavelength range 1–20 µm** — The overall tunable spectral range of the system.
- **wavevector (q)** — In-plane momentum variable.
- **wavevector magnitude q** — Magnitude of the propagation vector of the spin helix, influencing spin splitting.
- **wavevector q** — Momentum variable used in the interaction and response functions.
- **weight (68 g)** — Mass of the rotator.
- **weight 3 g** — Mass of ammonium persulfite weighed for the solution.
- **wire length 50 cm** — Target length of each pico‑coax wire for PCB 1.
- **wire resistance** — Maximum allowed wire resistance of 5 Ω.
- **wobble: ± 0.1 mrad** — Angular wobble specification of the rotator device.
- **work function** — Energy needed to remove an electron from a solid, tunable in graphene by electric field.
- **work function (p)** — Energy barrier height for electrons in the vacuum region.
- **working distance** — Distance between the sample and the beam column, e.g., >25 mm.
- **writing field size (200 µm × 200 µm)** — Maximum field size for good resolution; can be reduced to 50 µm × 50 µm for higher precision.
- **x** — X coordinate saved in the data file.
- **x and y offsets (set to zero)** — Lateral offsets of the scan area, initially zeroed.
- **x cm length** — Length of each pico‑coax line (unspecified numeric value).
- **xy coarse motor position** — Lateral position of the sample set by the coarse XY motors.
- **xy linear translation** — Linear translation capability in the XY plane provided by the micropositioner.
- **y** — Parameter Y = 45 meV used in the THF model.
- **y** — Y coordinate saved in the data file.
- **year of manufacture** — Manufacturing year of the AMC300.
- **youngs modulus (y)** — Elastic modulus of quartz used to compute the QTF spring constant.
- **z height** — Z height of the sample (around -8000 µm) used to focus on the surface.
- **z height (~‑8000 µm)** — Z‑position used to bring the sample into focus.
- **z-piezo signal (deflection)** — Deflection signal of the z‑piezo used to monitor tip‑sample distance.
- **za phonon gap** — Energy gap of the out‑of‑plane acoustic (ZA) phonon mode in bulk graphite.
- **zero magnetic field** — Magnetic field set to zero during the measurements.
- **zero temperature** — Assumed temperature condition (T = 0) for the theoretical calculations.
- **zero temperature** — Temperature regime assumed for the dI/dV calculation.
- **zero-bias differential conductance** — Differential conductance measured at zero bias voltage.
- **zero‑bias voltage vb** — Bias voltage set to (approximately) zero during the special measurement mode.
- **zero‑point motion amplitude (ℓzpm)** — Quantum amplitude of out‑of‑plane phonon zero‑point fluctuations.
- **zero‑point motion length ℓ_{r,q0}** — Length characterising the amplitude of zero‑point motion of phonon mode r.
- **z‑noise** — Noise in the vertical (z) direction, expressed in picometers.
- **z‑piezo voltage** — Voltage applied to the Z‑piezo actuator that moves the AFM tip vertically.
- **z‑signal (mv)** — Voltage signal proportional to tip‑sample distance.
- **|t(p*)|** — Magnitude of tunneling matrix element at p*.
- **|t|² (squared tunneling amplitude)** — Magnitude squared of the tunneling matrix element.
- **|ψ|² (squared wavefunction amplitude)** — Magnitude squared of the electronic wavefunction at a given momentum.
- **~60 mv potential amplitude** — The measured amplitude of the sub‑moiré electrostatic potential, larger than theoretical predictions.
- **α = v₀/v_d** — Ratio of the tip velocity v₀ to the sample velocity v_D, assumed to be < 1.
- **α factor** — Dimensionless factor entering the electron‑phonon coupling expression.
- **β (beta) derivative** — β = ∂t∥/∂a, derivative of the hopping amplitude with respect to bond length.
- **βj = 4π i k∥ σj / ω** — Dimensionless parameter appearing in the transmission/reflection coefficients at each graphene interface.
- **γ point** — High‑symmetry point at the centre of the moiré Brillouin zone (mBZ) of TBG.
- **γ_s (sample broadening)** — Intrinsic linewidth of the sample states.
- **γ_t (tip broadening)** — Intrinsic linewidth of the tip states.
- **γsc** — Superconducting broadening parameter, 0.04 meV.
- **γ₀ (baseline inverse lifetime)** — Energy‑independent part of the inverse electron lifetime, best‑fit value ≈ 4 meV.
- **γ₁ (energy‑dependent term)** — Coefficient of the linear energy dependence of the inverse lifetime (≈0.035).
- **δ_cc** — Momentum‑independent tunneling parameter.
- **δc** — Superconducting gap in dispersive c band, 0.8 meV.
- **δe ≈ 15 mev** — Typical energy offset of the flat band from the Fermi level when it is pinned.
- **δf** — Superconducting gap in flat f band, 0.4 meV.
- **δg (amplitude of conductance step)** — Amplitude of the conductance step associated with phonon modes.
- **δg_ta (change in conductance due to ta mode)** — Amplitude of the conductance step associated with the transverse acoustic phonon.
- **δθ** — Small deviation from a commensurate twist angle.
- **δθ = 2.7°** — Angular offset that displaces copies of the MLG Dirac bands in the spectra.
- **εx (in‑plane dielectric permittivity)** — Dielectric constant of the spacer parallel to the graphene layers.
- **θ = 20°** — Twist angle of 20 degrees in TBG.
- **θ = 30°** — Twist angle of 30 degrees in TBG.
- **θ_κ** — Angle approximated as π/2 in region I.
- **θ₀** — Small angle appearing in the analytical approximations.
- **λ (dimensionless epc constant)** — Dimensionless electron‑phonon coupling constant in BCS theory.
- **μ_s (sample chemical potential)** — Energy of the sample relative to its charge neutrality point.
- **μ_s (sample chemical potential)** — Electrochemical potential of the sample.
- **μ_t (tip chemical potential)** — Chemical potential of the tip.
- **μ_t (tip chemical potential)** — Electrochemical potential of the tip.
- **μ_t = 25 mev (tip chemical potential)** — Chemical potential of the tip set to 25 meV.
- **ξ_vh** — Energy of the Van Hove singularity in the flat conduction band, ≈ 1.2 meV.
- **ξ_vm** — Energy of the minimum of the remote valence band.
- **φ (moiré potential)** — Electrostatic potential landscape φ(x,y) generated by the moiré pattern.
- **ω (energy)** — Excitation energy entering the spin structure factor.
- **ω_1** — Area factor appearing in conductance expressions.
- **ω_1 (unit cell area)** — Area of the QSL unit cell.
- **ω_qm (phonon frequency at q_m)** — Phonon frequency at momentum q_M.
- **ω₀** — Characteristic plasmon energy ≈ 10 meV where a Van Hove singularity occurs.
- **ℓ** — Length scale of spatial inhomogeneity in the graphene layers.
- **ℓzpm/a** — Ratio of the atomic zero‑point motion to the graphene bond length.
- **≈1 nm bandwidth** — Spectral width of monochromated light.
- **⟨σz(k)⟩ spin expectation value** — Expectation value of the z‑component of spin as a function of momentum.
- **⟨σz⟩ spin polarization** — Spin polarization component along the z axis, influenced by spin‑spiral periodicity and spin‑orbit coupling.

### project
- **/pro feature upgrade** — Attocube feature package that adds multiple‑device control, daisy‑chaining, stepwise move settings, DC level adjustment, and end‑of‑travel detection.
- **1t‑tas₂ few‑layer — correlated cdw tunneling dynamics** — Studying photo‑induced collapse of charge‑density‑wave/Mott gap and photon‑assisted tunneling in 1T‑TaS₂.
- **adding twists and turns to magneto‑transport measurements with attocube tools** — Project describing use of attocube ANR, atto3DR and attoTMS for magneto‑transport.
- **advanced nanopositioner controller** — Project developing an advanced and versatile nanopositioner controller.
- **afm system design project** — Project to build an AFM capable of low‑temperature, high‑magnetic‑field operation.
- **agaur 2014-sgr-1400** — Catalan agency grant supporting the work.
- **amc/ids closed loop feature** — Feature that enables closed‑loop positioning of an AMC300 using an external IDS3010 interferometric displacement sensor.
- **angle‑dependent transport project** — Systematic study of angular magneto‑transport in SrₓBi₂Se₃.
- **anhui provincial key research and development program** — Funding program that provided grant 2022a05020052.
- **atomic set development** — Research effort to develop the scanning atomic SET with a single atomic defect sensor.
- **attocube project no. 21xxxx** — The installation project identified by the placeholder number 21xxxx.
- **attocube solutions** — Development program for piezo‑driven nano‑rotators and related measurement tools.
- **bsf grant (no. 2020260)** — Funding source for the research.
- **cas project for young scientists in basic research** — Funding program that provided grant YSBR-005.
- **cd‑rped for magnetic characterization of am surfaces** — Development of CD‑RPED as a technique for magnetic characterization of altermagnetic surfaces and thin films.
- **coarse z motor development project** — Research project focused on designing, building and testing the coarse Z motor.
- **crc 183** — DFG collaborative research centre supporting the work.
- **cryogenic qtm design project** — Design effort for the cryogenic Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **dfg‑crc 183 (c02) project (no. 277101999)** — Funding source for the research.
- **dilution fridge upgrade** — Upgrade of the dilution refrigerator, adding two towers.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy of flat‑band systems** — Research effort aimed at probing Dirac points in moiré flat‑band materials using tunneling spectroscopy.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy of flat‑band systems project** — Research effort to apply Dirac‑point spectroscopy with a QTM to flat‑band materials.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy project** — Research effort focusing on Dirac‑point spectroscopy of flat‑band systems.
- **direct probe of sublattice magnetization in altermagnets** — Research effort to develop RPED as a direct, element‑specific probe of local magnetic moments.
- **dukes approach** — A project that expounds Duke's interpretation of Bardeen's tunneling theory.
- **electrical switching of an unconventional odd‑parity magnet** — Study of electrical switching of an unconventional odd‑parity magnet.
- **energy bands of magic‑angle tbg** — Research effort to characterize the electronic structure of MATBG.
- **epc in tbg project** — Project investigating electron‑phonon coupling in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **erc grant for moiré visualization** — Funding proposal led by Carmen to visualize moiré patterns and study angle‑dependent physics.
- **erc grant proposal (carmen)** — ERC grant proposal to be written by Carmen.
- **erc‑adg qtm grant (no. 101097125)** — European Research Council Advanced Grant supporting the Quantum Twisting Microscope project.
- **filling‑dependent spectroscopy at key momenta** — Research effort using the QTM to map the evolution of electronic states in MATBG as a function of filling and momentum.
- **finite back‑gate voltage study** — Research project investigating how a non‑zero back‑gate voltage modifies momentum‑resolved tunneling spectra.
- **fp7-ict-2013613024-grasp** — European Commission FP7 project supporting the work.
- **fpu program (spanish mecd)** — FPU fellowship program that funded S. de V.
- **fractionalized excitations detection project** — Proposed effort to use the quantum twisting microscope and tunneling spectroscopy to probe quantum spin liquids.
- **general framework for inelastic tunneling spectroscopy with the qtm** — Theoretical effort establishing how QTM can probe collective electronic excitations.
- **hqma t grant (erc)** — European Research Council grant acknowledged for supporting E. B.
- **icfo / axon assembly project** — Assembly project for ICFO / AXON hardware, October 2025.
- **imaging the flat bands of magic‑angle graphene reshaped by interactions** — Research effort that uses the QTM to obtain high‑resolution momentum‑space images of MATBG’s interacting bands.
- **imaging the moiré potential of aligned g/hbn using the atomic set** — Research effort aimed at mapping the electrostatic landscape of a graphene/hBN moiré superlattice with a scanning SET.
- **implications for superconductivity in tbg** — Research project assessing EPC impact on superconductivity.
- **innovation program for quantum science and technology** — Funding program that partly supported the work (2021ZD0303306).
- **integrated plasmonic circuitry** — Design of modular devices that employ plasmon generation and detection in a double‑graphene sandwich.
- **israel science foundation grant (no. 1621/24)** — Funding source for the research.
- **leona m. and harry b. helmsley charitable trust** — Funding source for the research.
- **likun data recreation** — Project to recreate Likun experimental data.
- **matbg spectroscopy project** — Research effort aimed at characterising the electronic structure of magic‑angle TBG using QTM.
- **mathematics of nanoscale science and engineering conference** — Conference that motivated the present work.
- **measuring phonon dispersion with the cryogenic qtm** — Research effort to map graphite phonon dispersion via inelastic tunnelling.
- **microscopy upgrade** — Microscopy Upgrade feature activated when AMC300 is used within an attocube microscope system.
- **minerva grant (no. 74434)** — Funding source for the research.
- **mir‑puig** — Funding from Mir‑Puig
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling between twisted graphene monolayers** — Research effort aimed at mapping tunnelling as a function of twist angle and bias.
- **mose₂/wse₂ heterobilayer — interlayer excitons and tunable moiré potentials** — Probing photon‑assisted interlayer tunneling via long‑lived interlayer excitons in a MoSe₂/WSe₂ heterostructure.
- **mos₂ twisted bilayer — benchmark twistronic semiconductor** — Benchmark study of light‑induced interlayer tunneling and moiré miniband spectroscopy in twisted MoS₂.
- **mote₂ twisted bilayer — topological & superconducting phases** — Investigating photon‑induced Floquet topological transitions and tunneling into topological surface states in twisted MoTe₂.
- **national natural science foundation of china** — Funding agency that provided grants (22288201, 22173093, 21688102).
- **novotny paper recreation** — Project to reproduce results from the Novotny paper.
- **pcb workflow** — Documented workflow for PCB design and production.
- **piezo rotation system project** — Development of a piezo‑driven rotator for mK temperatures.
- **piezo tube scanner development project** — Project aimed at implementing the large‑area x/y/z piezo tube scanners.
- **plasmon generation via electron tunneling** — Investigation of plasmon excitation by inelastic electron tunneling in a double‑layer graphene system.
- **plasmon spectroscopy in charge‑neutral tbg** — Research effort aimed at understanding plasmons and their spectroscopic signatures in charge‑neutral twisted bilayer graphene.
- **plasmon spectroscopy with qtm** — Research effort to develop and apply QTM‑based plasmon spectroscopy.
- **plasmon‑based sensors** — Sensors comprising a plasmon generation stage, a transmission ribbon exposed to an analyte, and a detection stage.
- **preventive maintenance program** — Preventive maintenance program for attocube Premium Line positioners.
- **previous tunnelling experiment** — Earlier QTM experiment that used two layers of WSe₂.
- **probing quantum spin liquids with a quantum twisting microscope** — Research effort to use the QTM for IETS studies of QSLs.
- **project 1** — Cabling project that fabricates a 12‑Fischer male A to SB25 connector A and routes twisted pairs.
- **project 2** — Cabling project that fabricates various pin adapters from 12‑Fischer male B/C to SB9 connectors and SMP female.
- **project 21xxxx** — Installation project identified by number 21xxxx for the microscope system.
- **project 3** — Cabling project that solders coaxial wires to PCB D/E and Fischer female connectors.
- **project to develop a theoretical framework for probing superconductivity with qtm** — Research effort described in the PRL paper.
- **project: microscope mechanical design** — The design effort focused on the pocket, motor limits, and tip‑sample geometry.
- **qtm conference i (feb 2026)** — Conference where the presented work was discussed.
- **qtm for advanced 2d material spectroscopy** — Project aiming at low‑angle photocurrent, plasmon spectroscopy and nonreciprocity.
- **qtm grant (erc)** — European Research Council grant that funds the QTM setup.
- **qtm inelastic tunneling spectroscopy of collective excitations** — Research effort to use the Quantum Twisting Microscope for probing plasmons and charge fluctuations via inelastic tunneling.
- **qtm junction electrostatic model** — Application of the three‑capacitor electrostatic model to predict charge‑neutral Dirac point lines in the QTM device.
- **qtm local thermodynamics project** — Project aiming to use QTM for measuring local thermodynamic quantities.
- **qtm mapping of tbg** — Research effort to image the band structure of twisted bilayer graphene with the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **qtm measurements of graphene‑graphene junctions** — Research effort to use the Quantum Twisting Microscope for tunneling spectroscopy of graphene layers.
- **qtm research proposals** — Collection of proposals for advanced 2D material spectroscopy with QTM.
- **qtm studies of superconductivity in graphene‑based heterostructures and twisted tmds** — Apply QTM techniques to explore superconductivity in various 2D materials.
- **qtm study of graphene‑based nodal superconductors** — Research effort to probe nodal superconductivity in twisted graphene using the QTM.
- **qtm superconductivity measurement project** — Research project using QTM to study superconductivity in MA TBG.
- **qtm tip and sample fabrication** — Project focusing on tip and sample fabrication for the Quantum Twisting Microscope (QTM).
- **qtm tip fabrication** — Project aimed at producing functional QTM tips with optimized geometry and materials.
- **qtm valley symmetry study** — Project investigating K/K′ valley spectra using the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **qtwist project** — Research project under which the Quantum Twisting Microscope work is organized.
- **qtwist project** — Project focusing on twisted multilayer systems and THz spectroscopy.
- **quantitative theory of plasmon‑assisted tunneling in qtm** — Theoretical effort described in Section IV that models plasmon‑assisted tunneling in the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **quantum transport analysis** — Project aiming to study anisotropic Zeeman spin‑splitting with a rotating system.
- **quantum twisting microscope development** — Research effort that created the QTM platform for momentum‑resolved spectroscopy using twisted graphene.
- **quantum twisting microscope development** — Overall project to build and apply the QTM platform.
- **quantum twisting microscope phonon spectroscopy project** — Research effort developing QTM‑based phonon spectroscopy on graphene junctions.
- **quantum twisting microscope platform** — The research effort developing the QTM and associated scanning probes such as the atomic SET.
- **quantum twisting microscope project** — Research effort developing the QTM microscope.
- **quantum twisting microscope project** — Research effort developing and applying the QTM.
- **quantum twisting microscope project** — ERC‑AdG funded effort developing the QTM instrument.

- **quantum‑dot‑assisted spectroscopy project** — Project focusing on using a defect‑based quantum dot for local potential mapping.

- **rosa and emilio segre research award** — Funding source for the research.
- **rt‑qtm training project** — Training of team members on the room‑temperature QTM.

- **split‑gates fabrication** — Project to fabricate split‑gate devices using hBN and graphene.
- **start project (y‑137‑tec)** — Funding project supporting the research.

- **stm design optimization project** — Research effort that applied the optimization criterion to STM models.

- **theory of dirac‑point tunneling spectroscopy with qtm** — Theoretical work developing the Dirac‑point spectroscopy framework and its predictions.
- **theory of plasmon spectroscopy** — Research effort developing a theoretical framework for plasmon spectroscopy in TBG.

- **unconventional magnons in collinear magnets dictated by spin space groups. nature 640, 349–354 (2025).** — Theoretical work showing that spin‑space group symmetry can generate unconventional magnon excitations in collinear magnets.

- **wei, dirac-point spectroscopy of flat-band systems with the quantum twisting microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 111, 085128 (2025).

- **y. martin, c. williams, h. wickramasinghe, j. appl. phys. 61, 4723 (1987)** — Publication reference 37

- **zhou et al. superconductivity in rhombohedral trilayer graphene** — Nature 598, 434–438 (2021) reporting superconductivity in rhombohedral TLG.
- **šmejkal et al. 2020, crystal time‑reversal symmetry breaking and spontaneous hall effect in collinear antiferromagnets** — Paper on crystal time‑reversal symmetry breaking and spontaneous Hall effect in collinear antiferromagnets.
- **šmejkal et al. 2022, emerging research landscape of altermagnetism** — Review of altermagnetism research.

### researchquestion
- **achieve ultra‑low electron temperature while rotating** — How to keep electrons at ~25 mK while rotating a sample in high magnetic field.
- **afm / spm use** — Can the XYZ positioner be used for AFM or other scanning probe microscopy?
- **are chiral spin‑split magnons present in crsb?** — Investigates whether CrSb hosts chiral spin‑split magnon modes.
- **are plasmons the pairing glue in matbg superconductivity?** — Motivation for studying plasmon‑electron coupling in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **can an individual defect be used as a functional quantum dot to probe local potentials?** — Question addressed by the quantum‑dot‑assisted spectroscopy experiment.
- **can iets distinguish between different qsl phases?** — Explores whether features such as singularities and bias dependence allow identification of Dirac, gapped chiral, and spinon‑Fermi‑surface QSLs.
- **can iets distinguish different quantum spin liquids?** — Question of whether low‑bias IETS spectra can differentiate gapped, chiral, Z₂, and gapless QSLs.
- **can imaging be performed near zero bias to avoid hot‑electron and phonon injection?** — Motivation for using low‑energy defects.
- **can mcd‑rped map the néel‑vector distribution?** — Question whether the proportionality between MCD intensity and cos θ can be used for imaging.
- **can qtm detect spontaneous c3z symmetry breaking?** — Question addressed by the three C3z‑related tunneling channels.
- **can qtm resolve bogoliubov coherence factors with momentum resolution?** — Question concerning extraction of electron/hole intensity ratios.
- **can qtm reveal nodal excitations via zero‑bias conductance?** — Question about detecting gap nodes through zero‑bias features.
- **can spin chirality be reversed by switching ferroelectric polarization?** — Open question investigated by measuring electrical switching of the ferroelectric order in NiI₂.
- **can spin chirality be reversed by switching spin‑induced ferroelectric polarization?** — Open question addressed by the electrical switching experiments on NiI₂.
- **can the iets signal determine the spinon fermi momentum?** — Investigates whether the twist‑angle dependence of the IETS spectrum can be used to extract k_F,s.
- **can the performance of a particular stm design be improved?** — Open question addressed by the optimization study.
- **can the qtm resolve plasmon excitations?** — Question addressed by proposing plasmon spectroscopy with the QTM.
- **central problem in condensed matter physics** — Understanding the mechanism of high‑Tc superconductivity.
- **challenges in afm** — Issues such as nonmonotonic force curves and jump‑to‑contact that complicate AFM feedback.
- **coexistence of nodal pairing and nematicity in graphene‑based nodal superconductors** — Open question whether nodal superconductivity and electronic nematic order appear together.
- **detectability of valley symmetry breaking via band splitting** — Question if flavour symmetry breaking can be identified by simple energy‑split bands.
- **detecting nodal points geometrically** — Can the zero‑bias measurement protocol locate gap nodes via geometric analysis of the tip Fermi surface?
- **determination of band structure from dirac‑point spectroscopy** — How to extract band dispersion using Dirac‑point spectroscopy.
- **determine the spin hamiltonian of crsb** — Open question addressed by the present study to model CrSb’s magnetic excitations.
- **determine tunneling matrix elements \(\hat t_n\) for commensurate angles** — Goal of calculating the three dominant tunneling matrices that govern interlayer coupling.
- **determining magnetization direction from cd in rped** — How to infer the magnetization direction of ferromagnetic surfaces using CD in RPED.
- **determining microscopic magnetic structure of altermagnet candidates** — Need for reliable experiments that can reveal the sublattice magnetization in thin‑film altermagnets.
- **develop approximate methods for stm on complex nonperiodic structures** — How to create computationally feasible methods to interpret STM data from surfaces lacking periodicity.
- **differences of performance at ambient conditions and cryogenic temperatures** — Question asking how the positioners behave differently at room temperature versus low temperature.
- **direct imaging of electrostatic potential landscapes in moiré interfaces** — The open question of obtaining nanoscale, high‑sensitivity maps of the periodic potential in vdW moiré systems.
- **distinguish altermagnets from antiferromagnets via mcd** — How to use magnetic circular dichroism in RPED to differentiate altermagnetic and conventional antiferromagnetic materials.
- **does nrss of magnon bands occur in metallic altermagnets like crsb?** — Investigates the existence of non‑reciprocal spin splitting of magnon bands in CrSb.
- **dual‑nature puzzle of matbg** — Open question about the coexistence of light and heavy electronic states.
- **effect of applied pressure on tbg energy bands** — Question of how GPa‑scale pressure modifies the flat‑band and remote‑band bandwidths.
- **effect of arbitrary field orientation on cryogenic transport measurements** — How does freely changing the mutual orientation of magnetic field and sample improve cryogenic measurements?
- **effect of c3z symmetry breaking on d2i/dv2 singularities** — Question of how loss of three‑fold symmetry modifies the second‑derivative tunneling spectra.
- **effect of contact distance** — How does contact distance influence tunnelling?
- **effect of gate screening distance on plasmon‑assisted inelastic tunneling** — How does the gate‑screening distance d_g influence the plasmon‑assisted tunneling amplitude in TBG?
- **effect of gating** — How does gating modify the tunnelling behavior?
- **effect of interlayer coulomb interactions between tip and sample on inelastic tunneling spectroscopy** — Question addressed regarding how long‑range Coulomb coupling modifies the spectroscopy of charge fluctuations.
- **effect of strain on moiré superlattice** — How does strain‑induced distortion modify the electronic structure of TBG?
- **effect of tip radius** — How does tip radius affect the measured tunnelling?
- **effect of tip screening on tunneling spectrum** — Question whether dynamical screening from the tip causes qualitative changes in the tunneling spectrum of MLG–TBG junctions.
- **efficient plasmon generation via electron tunneling** — How to achieve high‑rate, high‑efficiency excitation of graphene plasmons using tunneling electrons.
- **electrical control of spin polarization** — Can the non‑relativistic spin polarization σ⊥ be switched by an electric field via C₂ₓ symmetry?
- **extract w₀/w₁ from differential conductance** — How to determine the ratio w₀/w₁ from differential tunneling conductance measurements.
- **high magnetic fields** — Can the positioner operate in high magnetic fields?
- **how accurate is the mcd‑normalization approximation?** — Question about the material dependence of the approximation that normalized MCD yields the XMCD of a sublattice.
- **how do plasmons behave in charge‑neutral tbg?** — Open question driving the project on plasmon spectroscopy in charge‑neutral twisted bilayer graphene.
- **how do singularities change when the fermi level coincides with a dirac point?** — Question about the modification of tunneling singularities at Dirac‑point alignment.
- **how does doping affect qtm tunneling spectra?** — Open question raised in the discussion.
- **how does geometry change affect stm performance?** — Investigation of how conical versus cylindrical sample holders influence resonance and signal quality.
- **how does graphene c6 symmetry affect polarization dependent current?** — Open question posed in the slides about the influence of graphene’s C6 symmetry on the measured current.
- **how does interlayer coupling affect superconductivity in tbg?** — Open question on the role of interlayer coupling for superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **how does moiré heterostrain affect the tip performance?** — Question examined through room‑temperature measurements of the aligned tip.
- **how does odd‑parity spin polarization couple to ferroelectric polarization in nii2?** — Open question addressed by the study of helicity‑dependent photocurrent.
- **how does strain affect the moiré potential?** — Investigation of whether lattice strain can account for the observed potential amplitude and symmetry.
- **how does strain affect tunneling matrix elements and singularities?** — Investigation of the influence of weak strain on the sum rule and d²I/dV² features.
- **how does temperature smear the k‑resolved onset features?** — Study of thermal broadening of the onset of momentum‑resolved tunneling.
- **how does the moiré potential vary with distance from the interface?** — Study of the spatial decay of φ(x,y) as the detector is moved away from the graphene/hBN plane.
- **how does the nesting condition affect the tunneling current?** — Investigation of the singular behavior of I when the Fermi surfaces match.
- **how does the twist angle affect electron‑phonon coupling?** — Investigation of the dependence of interlayer and intralayer EPC on the rotational misalignment.
- **how does twist angle affect electron-phonon coupling in graphene heterostructures?** — Investigation of the θ‑dependence of inter‑ and intralayer electron‑phonon interactions.
- **how does twist angle affect electron‑phonon coupling and inelastic tunneling spectra?** — Investigation of the influence of the twist angle on coupling strength and the resulting tunneling features.
- **how does twist angle affect epc in twisted bilayer graphene?** — Question about the twist‑angle dependence of electron‑phonon coupling.
- **how does δg depend on vbg?** — Investigation of the relationship between conductance step amplitude and back gate voltage.
- **how excitons supply energy and momentum** — What mechanisms allow excitons to provide the required energy and in‑plane momentum for tunnelling?
- **how to access the dispersion of plasmons in tbg** — Research question addressed by the project.
- **how to achieve a zero twist angle measurement?** — Developing protocols to locate and maintain exact alignment.
- **how to achieve precise coarse z motion at cryogenic temperatures?** — Open question driving the development of the coarse Z motor.
- **how to achieve zero twist angle** — Question about locating the exact zero‑degree twist configuration.
- **how to characterize nanostructures at cryogenic temperatures using afm** — Open question motivating the development of the cryogenic AFM.
- **how to directly extract pairing magnitude at each probed momentum?** — Question addressed by QTM measurements.
- **how to directly probe pairing symmetry in 2d superconductors?** — Question addressed by QTM momentum‑resolved spectroscopy.
- **how to directly visualize moiré potentials** — Open question addressed by developing the atomic SET.
- **how to distinguish pairing among flat f‑electron states versus dispersive c‑electron states?** — Goal of using tip‑angle‑dependent gaps to separate pairing channels.
- **how to efficiently couple thz gas laser to cryo‑snom?** — Open question driving the Gas THz to cSNOM project.
- **how to extract 3d fermi surfaces from magneto‑transport** — Question of extracting three‑dimensional Fermi surfaces using angular dependent transport.
- **how to extract band‑structure information from di/dv** — The open question motivating the development of band‑structure tomography.
- **how to extract information about plasmons in tbg from qtm tunneling spectra?** — Central question addressed by the study.
- **how to extract the sample band structure from elastic tunneling?** — Question addressed by Dirac‑point spectroscopy using the QTM.
- **how to fine‑tune optical transition energy via interlayer distance** — Question investigated by modulating interlayer spacing.
- **how to identify nodal points in the superconducting pairing potential?** — Goal of extending QTM measurements to detect zeroes of the order parameter.
- **how to improve snr with hbn thickness** — Question about the influence of hBN thickness on signal‑to‑noise ratio.
- **how to infer the ratio of intrasublattice and intersublattice tunneling parameters?** — Question about extracting effective model parameters from differential conductance data.
- **how to locate superconducting nodes without prior fermiology** — Question of determining node positions using only tunneling data.
- **how to map energy bands of tbg along the qtm trajectory** — Goal of obtaining momentum‑ and energy‑resolved band structure using QTM.
- **how to measure neutral collective modes with qtm?** — Investigation of the feasibility and requirements for probing neutral excitations using the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **how to measure the electronic structure of nearly flat bands** — Open question motivating the development of Dirac‑point spectroscopy with the QTM.
- **how to minimise heat load in low‑temperature piezo rotators** — Investigation of voltage and frequency reduction to lower dissipated power.
- **how to precisely control magnetic field orientation for anisotropic magneto‑transport measurements?** — Question motivated by the need to extract information from anisotropic samples.
- **how to probe phonon dispersions using the quantum twisting microscope** — Central question addressed by the PRB 2024 theory.
- **how to probe the breaking of c₃z symmetry in strained ma tbg?** — Question concerning detection of symmetry breaking via tunneling spectra.
- **how to reduce noise after one week** — Question about mitigating the increase of noise after prolonged measurements.
- **how to reduce the heat load on the cryostat** — Question concerning wiring and operational strategies to minimise thermal dissipation.
- **how to reduce the response of tip‑sample distance to external mechanical disturbances?** — Question addressed by the study.
- **how to separate isotropic and anisotropic g‑factor corrections in gaas quantum dots?** — Open question addressed by the g‑factor anisotropy experiment.
- **how to tune exciton binding energy** — Question about controlling exciton binding energy via electric field.
- **how to tune superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — Question addressed by experiments varying carrier density, pressure, and twist angle.
- **how twist angle affects tunneling** — Question of the dependence of tunneling conductance on twist angle.
- **increase scan speed** — How to accelerate AFM imaging without sacrificing resolution.
- **inverted polarity** — What happens if the polarity of the positioner connections is reversed?
- **justification of treating m as constant** — Question of whether the tunneling matrix element M can be taken as a constant in the analysis.
- **link between ferroelectric polarization and momentum‑space spin polarization** — How ferroelectric polarization determines momentum‑resolved spin polarization.
- **local vs. topological framework** — Question of whether MATBG’s correlated states are best described by local (Mott) or topological (Chern) theories.
- **maximizing optical and electronic signals** — How to choose h‑BN thickness to achieve strong light emission and measurable current.
- **measure van der waals stacks optoelectronic properties with spatial and twist degrees of freedom** — Open question pursued by the RT‑QTOM project.
- **mechanism of electron spin relaxation** — What physical process governs spin relaxation in semiconductor quantum dots.
- **minimize tip‑sample distance response** — Goal of reducing the response of the tip‑sample distance to base‑plate disturbances.
- **momentum-resolved tunneling spectroscopy of finite-momentum pairing superconductors** — Proposed study to apply momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy to finite‑momentum pairing superconductors.
- **momentum‑resolved filling‑dependent electronic structure in matbg** — How do electronic states at different momenta evolve with carrier filling in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene?
- **nature of quasiparticles around the fermi energy in ma tbg** — Question about quasiparticle characteristics near the Fermi level.
- **nature of unconventional superconductivity in srₓbi₂se₃** — What order parameter explains the observed two‑fold anisotropy of Bc₂.
- **observe spin‑split magnons in metals** — Open question addressed by the present work.
- **origin of ferromagnetism in tblg** — What is the microscopic origin of the observed ferromagnetism in magic‑angle tBLG?
- **origin of superconducting pairing in ma tbg (f vs c)** — Open question whether pairing arises from flat f band or dispersive c band.
- **origin of superconductivity in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Whether superconductivity arises from conventional electron‑phonon coupling or exotic electron‑electron correlations.
- **origin of superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — Open question motivating phonon‑spectroscopy studies with the QTM.
- **origin of the 15 mev excitation** — Open question about the microscopic nature of the observed 15 meV feature.
- **origin of the dual nature of electrons** — Investigation of why MATBG exhibits both heavy‑localized and light‑delocalized electronic characters.
- **origin of the ~15 mev excitation** — Question whether heterostrain or other mechanisms cause the observed excitation.
- **origin of the δe feature** — Open question whether the 15 meV splitting stems from strain or other mechanisms.
- **performance characteristics at ultra low temperatures using he3 or a dilution cryostat** — Question about how the positioners operate down to millikelvin temperatures.
- **possible causes for a “spike”** — Slide discussing mechanisms (relaxation competition, momentum mismatch, Fano resonance, etc.).
- **probe c₃z symmetry breaking** — How can tip scans detect breaking of three‑fold rotational symmetry in TBG?
- **probing order‑parameter symmetry** — How can the symmetry of the superconducting order parameter be determined in 2D superconductors?
- **probing quantum spin liquids** — Can the QTM detect signatures of quantum spin liquid states?
- **quantitative analysis of epc strength** — How to obtain an exact tip area to enable quantitative determination of electron‑phonon coupling strength.
- **real‑space vs momentum‑space matching relevance** — Question whether conductance is governed by real‑space registry or momentum‑space alignment in coherent tunnelling.
- **reduce noise** — How to lower frequency and vertical noise in FM‑AFM.
- **relation between triangular hubbard model and unconventional superconductivity** — How does the triangular Hubbard model relate to unconventional superconductivity in the TLG/hBN system?
- **research question: gap size optimization** — Question of how to choose the optimal gap and wall thickness for the pocket.
- **revealing electron‑electron interactions at room temperature** — Can QTM detect many‑body interactions in graphene without cooling?
- **role of indirect vs direct excitons in resonant tunnelling** — How do indirect and direct excitons each contribute to the observed resonant peaks?
- **role of interlayer vibrations in epc** — Investigation of how antisymmetric layer vibrations affect EPC.
- **role of low‑energy excitation in exotic physics of matbg** — Whether the newly found low‑energy excitation influences the exotic phases of magic‑angle graphene.
- **short-circuit caused by graphene residues** — Problem of unintended electrical shorts due to leftover graphene.
- **should we always use 4 layers?** — Question about whether a 4‑layer stack‑up is mandatory for all PCBs.
- **should we assume splitting of the dirac level?** — Question about possible Dirac level splitting.
- **should we use paste layers? can the flux in them create batteries?** — Question about using paste layers and potential battery formation from flux.
- **simultaneous optimization of bending and longitudinal motions** — Open question on achieving both bending and longitudinal resonance optimization together.
- **symmetry‑protected voltage‑based switching of non‑relativistic spin polarization** — How to achieve voltage‑controlled switching of non‑relativistic spin polarization in a compensated magnet.
- **symmetry‑protected voltage‑based switching of non‑relativistic spin polarization in a compensated magnet** — Open question on achieving voltage control of spin texture without relativistic mechanisms.
- **tip‑dependence question** — To what extent do the tunneling results depend on the shape and size of the STM tip.
- **tip‑distance accuracy research question** — How far must the tip be from the sample for solutions of (6) to approximate solutions of (5) up to one picosecond or longer?
- **understanding the moiré interface potential** — Question about why measured potential amplitude exceeds theoretical predictions.
- **voltage control of non‑relativistic spin polarization** — Can an electric field switch the non‑relativistic spin texture in compensated magnets?
- **voltage‑based control of spin polarization** — How to achieve electrical manipulation of non‑relativistic spin polarization in compensated magnets.
- **what are the energetics of the defects?** — Question investigated by bias‑dependent low‑temperature scans.
- **what hidden symmetries govern the ground state at even integer filling?** — Motivation for the 2020 PRX study.
- **what is the nature of the correlated insulator states in twisted bilayer graphene?** — Open question addressed by several 2020‑2021 works.
- **what is the optimal amplitude for minimal vertical noise?** — Question investigating the amplitude that minimizes vertical noise in the AFM.
- **when does perturbative theory break down near the magic angle?** — Open question on the validity of perturbative expansions at small twist angles.
- **why are there two coulomb diamonds on the same graph?** — Question raised by the experimental data.
- **why does ni++ charge distribution contract in the solid?** — Open question addressed by comparing experiment with theory.
- **why is each coulomb diamond doubled?** — Question about the origin of CD doubling.
- **why is the experimental potential roughly double the theoretical prediction?** — Open question concerning the origin of the amplitude discrepancy between measured and calculated moiré potentials.
- **why sample rotation improves magneto‑transport** — Question addressed in the white paper about the benefits of rotating the sample during angle‑dependent measurements.
- **why should we assume splitting of the fermi level?** — Question concerning the interpretation of the second CD crossing.
- **why thz?** — Motivation slide asking about relevance of THz excitations (phonons, plasmons, magnons, vortices).

### run
- **13cd3i 690 mm line** — FIR laser line at 690 mm using 13CD3I with typical power 20 mW.
- **13cd3i 806 mm line** — FIR laser line at 806 mm using 13CD3I with typical power 4 mW.
- **30 nm deposition run** — A single run that produces a 30 nm Al film in 13 min 30 s.
- **ch2f2 214.6 mm line** — FIR laser line at 214.6 mm using CH2F2 with typical power 100 mW.
- **ch3oh 118.8 mm line** — FIR laser line at 118.8 mm using CH3OH with typical power 150 mW.
- **ch3oh 133.1 mm line** — FIR laser line at 133.1 mm using CH3OH with typical power 1 mW.
- **ch3oh 96.5 mm line** — FIR laser line at 96.5 mm using CH3OH with typical power 90 mW.
- **field cooling run to 30 k with 1 mv/m field** — Run where NiI2 device is cooled to 30 K under a 1 MV m⁻¹ electric field.
- **hcooh 393.6 mm line** — FIR laser line at 393.6 mm using HCOOH with typical power 40 mW.
- **hcooh 418.6 mm line** — FIR laser line at 418.6 mm using HCOOH with typical power 30 mW.
- **hcooh 432.6 mm line** — FIR laser line at 432.6 mm using HCOOH with typical power 40 mW.
- **measurement at v_bg = +10 v** — Run measuring I, dI/dV and d2I/dV2 versus θ and V_bb with back‑gate set to +10 V.
- **pyramid deposition run** — A complete deposition sequence consisting of multiple size steps.
- **run action** — Pressing the green button to start the recipe.

### sample
- **1.05° twisted bilayer graphene sample** — A TBG device with a twist angle of 1.05°, studied at filling ν = –3.
- **1.07° tbg at charge neutrality** — Sample of TBG with twist angle 1.07 degrees and charge neutrality.
- **1.07° twisted bilayer graphene** — TBG with a twist angle of 1.07°, subject to strong gate/dielectric screening.
- **1.07°‑twisted bilayer graphene (charge‑neutral)** — A charge‑neutral TBG sample with a twist angle of 1.07°, studied under strong gate/dielectric screening.
- **1.07°‑twisted bilayer graphene sample** — TBG specimen with a twist angle of 1.07°, used as a reference for larger‑angle measurements.
- **1.20° tbg at charge neutrality** — Sample of TBG with twist angle 1.20 degrees and charge neutrality.
- **1.20° twisted bilayer graphene** — TBG with a twist angle of 1.20°, studied away from the magic angle.
- **100 nm gold** — Gold film of 100 nm thickness used for milling and deposition tests.
- **20‑nm‑thick nii2 flake** — A NiI2 flake of 20 nm thickness transferred with its principal axis [100] between two gold electrodes.
- **20‑nm‑thick nii2 flake** — A thin exfoliated NiI2 flake (≈20 nm) used for zero‑bias photocurrent experiments.
- **2d hbn** — Freestanding two‑dimensional hexagonal boron nitride investigated for exciton dispersion and polarons.
- **2pp‑printed pyramids** — Polymer pyramids fabricated by two‑photon polymerization and used as carriers for tip functionalization.
- **3×1 reconstruction of au(110)** — Au(110) surface exhibiting a 3×1 reconstruction with (111) microfacets.
- **3‑layer wse₂ region** — Small 3L‑WSe₂ area on the MLG/hBN/graphite stack used for tip‑contact imaging.
- **400 nm diameter quantum dot** — Quantum dot with 400 nm diameter used in I‑V measurements.
- **450 nm diameter quantum dot** — Quantum dot with 450 nm diameter used in I‑V measurements.
- **500 nm diameter quantum dot** — Quantum dot with 500 nm diameter used in I‑V measurements.
- **aligned g/hbn heterostructure** — A sample where a graphene layer is crystallographically aligned with an hBN substrate, creating a moiré superlattice.
- **aligned graphene/hbn heterostructure** — Graphene placed on hBN with near‑zero twist angle, producing a long‑wavelength moiré pattern.
- **aligned graphene/hbn layers** — Stack of graphene and hBN with a defined twist angle used to fabricate the QTM tip.
- **alsb/gasb interband tunneling diodes** — Semiconductor diodes studied with the cryogenic AFM.
- **alsb/gasb quantum dot array** — Array of quantum dots made from AlSb/GaSb heterostructure, studied with AFM and I‑V measurements.
- **alternating‑twist magic‑angle trilayer graphene** — Trilayer graphene with alternating twist angles.
- **anisotropic sample** — Sample with direction‑dependent electronic properties, such as quasi‑1D nanotubes or nanowires.
- **anpx101 positioner** — ANPx101 positioner (x‑axis) from attocube Premium Line.
- **anpx51 positioner** — ANPx51 positioner (x‑axis) from attocube Premium Line.
- **anpz101 positioner** — ANPz101 positioner (z‑axis) from attocube Premium Line.
- **anpz51 positioner** — ANPz51 positioner (z‑axis) from attocube Premium Line.
- **atomic defect quantum dot sensor device** — Device that uses a single atomic defect in bilayer WSe₂ as a quantum dot to probe the local chemical potential of the adjacent graphene layers.
- **atomic defects used to image tip contact area** — Atomic defects on the graphene surface that allow direct imaging of the QTM tip contact area (Ω = Atip).
- **attached piezo** — Piezoelectric element that can be attached to the ANM300 module and whose capacitance can be measured.
- **attoipconfig.json** — JSON file containing the device's network configuration.
- **back gate** — Electrostatic gate used to control carrier density in the TBG sample.
- **bernal bilayer graphene** — AB‑stacked bilayer graphene.
- **bernal bilayer graphene** — AB‑stacked bilayer graphene.
- **bilayer graphene sample with small twist angle** — A graphene‑based sample consisting of two stacked graphene layers twisted by a small angle θ_TBG.
- **bilayer wse₂ barrier** — Two‑layer WSe₂ crystal acting as the tunnelling barrier.
- **bilayer wse₂ tunnelling barrier** — Insulating barrier separating MLG tip from the TBG device.
- **bottom graphene layer** — The graphene electrode closest to the bottom gate.
- **bottom graphene layer** — The lower graphene sheet forming the second electrode of the device.
- **bottom graphene sample** — The graphene flake that forms the lower electrode of the QTM junction.
- **bulk nii2** — Thick NiI2 crystal representing the three‑dimensional material.
- **bulk nii2 sample** — A bulk crystal of NiI2 used for pyroelectric and photocurrent measurements.
- **bulk nii₂ sample** — Macroscopic NiI₂ crystal used for electrical switching experiments.
- **carbon‑doped hbn barrier** — Barrier layer containing carbon‑related defects studied with QTM.
- **charge neutral 1.07° twisted bilayer graphene** — A TBG sample with a twist angle of 1.07° that is tuned to charge neutrality.
- **charge-neutral magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene** — MA TBG at charge neutrality.
- **charge‑neutral 1.20° twisted bilayer graphene** — A TBG device tuned to charge neutrality with a twist angle of approximately 1.20 degrees.
- **charge‑neutral 1.20° twisted bilayer graphene** — Twisted bilayer graphene sample with a twist angle of 1.20°, used for tunneling spectroscopy.
- **chip** — Individual chip containing contacts and structures.
- **clean ag surface** — The Ag substrate on which tunneling current was measured.
- **coax wires** — 800 mm long copper / stainless‑steel coaxial wires used in Project 3.
- **cone‑shaped tip** — Cone‑shaped tip fabricated with NanoScribe.
- **crsb crystal** — Single crystal of CrSb used for neutron measurements.
- **crte** — Chromium telluride, part of CrTe‑CrSb solid solutions.
- **cvd graphene (graphenea)** — Commercially available CVD graphene supplied by Graphenea.
- **cvt‑grown nii2 single crystal** — NiI2 single crystal grown by chemical vapor transport, used for X‑ray diffraction and magnetic susceptibility measurements.
- **davids sample** — Sample marked as Zero (BL Marker) in the procedure.
- **defect d1** — A localized defect in the heterostructure used as a detector positioned ~1.5 nm from the moiré interface.
- **defect d1** — Defect located 1.5 nm above the graphene/hBN interface.
- **defect d2** — A localized defect used as a detector positioned ~0.8 nm from the moiré interface.
- **defect d2** — Defect located 0.8 nm above the graphene/hBN interface.
- **defect in carbon‑doped hbn** — Localized carbon‑related defect acting as a quantum dot in the carbon‑doped hBN barrier.
- **device** — Fabricated electronic device containing the split-gate stack.
- **device‑on‑tip (graphite/hbn/mlg)** — Graphite/hBN/monolayer‑graphene stack forming a tent over the platinum pyramid with a flat plateau.
- **diodes** — Semiconductor diode devices measured in the thesis.
- **dot array** — Array of circular holes/dots fabricated on the RITD surface.
- **dual-gated matbg sample** — MATBG sample equipped with metallic gates and high‑κ dielectric for strong gate screening.
- **electron-doped monolayer graphene (μ_t = 60 mev)** — Monolayer graphene with tip chemical potential +60 meV.
- **electron‑doped mlg tip** — Monolayer graphene tip with a finite chemical potential μ_T.
- **exfoliated α‑rucl3** — Thin flakes obtained by exfoliation.
- **e‑beam markers** — Alignment markers fabricated by e‑beam lithography at the chip corners.
- **e‑beam patterned conductive stripes on silicon** — Sample consisting of conductive metal stripes (500 nm spacing) fabricated on a silicon substrate.
- **few‑layer graphite tent** — Mechanical tent made of few‑layer graphite that holds the tip during measurements.
- **few‑layer graphite tent** — Few‑layer graphite used as a tent structure for the tip.
- **fischer female d** — Fischer female connector D used in Project 3.
- **fischer female e** — Fischer female connector E used in Project 3.
- **fischer female f** — Fischer female connector F used in Project 3.
- **fischer male a** — 12‑Fischer male connector used in Project 1.
- **fischer male b** — 12‑Fischer male connector used in Project 2.
- **fischer male c** — 12‑Fischer male connector used in Project 2.
- **flake** — 2D material flake to be transferred.
- **flake sample** — 2D material flake placed under the microscope for Raman measurement.
- **flat sample side** — The stationary substrate opposite the tip in the QTM geometry.
- **flat‑band sample** — Sample with dispersionless (flat) electronic bands studied with Dirac‑point spectroscopy.
- **free‑electron tip** — Metallic tip modeled as a free‑electron system providing the tip Fermi line.
- **g/hbn moiré drain electrode** — Purple electrode formed by aligned graphene on hBN, creating a moiré superlattice.
- **gaas heterostructure hall bar** — Hall‑bar device based on a GaAs/AlGaAs two‑dimensional electron gas, mounted on the ANR30/LT insert for angle‑dependent transport.
- **gaas single‑electron quantum dot** — Lateral GaAs quantum dot used to extract spin splitting via tunnel‑rate measurements.
- **gaas(110) surface** — Gallium arsenide (110) surface used to illustrate tunneling to a semiconductor.
- **gaas/(ga,mn)as core‑shell nanowire** — Individual semiconductor nanowire used for magneto‑transport measurements.
- **gaas/algaas 2deg hall bar sample** — Hall bar device based on a GaAs/AlGaAs two‑dimensional electron gas.
- **gold beads (au beads)** — Au beads used as reference for focus and astigmatism adjustments.
- **gold-plated pyramid tip** — Gold-plated pyramid tip used in THz QTM.
- **graphene aligned to hexagonal boron nitride (g/hbn)** — A canonical moiré interface where lattice mismatch between graphene and hBN creates a moiré superlattice.
- **graphene bilayer sample (tip and sample layers)** — Stack of two graphene layers used as tip and sample in tunneling experiments.
- **graphene double‑layer device** — Device consisting of two graphene layers separated by hBN.
- **graphene layers** — Stack of graphene sheets used as the tunneling electrodes in the QTM.
- **graphene monolayer placed on the tip of the qtm** — Sample consisting of a monolayer graphene flake attached to the tip of the quantum twisting microscope.
- **graphene on hexagonal boron nitride heterostructure** — Van‑der‑Waals stack of graphene placed on hBN that forms a moiré superlattice.
- **graphene pieces (1.5 x 1.5 cm) with batch number** — Graphene flakes stored in a desiccator behind the Raith ebeam, each labeled with a batch number.
- **graphene sample** — Graphene sheet acting as the sample, with chemical potential –eV_b/2.
- **graphene sample (mlg) with hbn barrier** — Rotatable graphene sheet separated from tip by hBN barrier.
- **graphene sample layer** — A graphene layer (layer l) of the sample.
- **graphene source electrode** — Grey graphene electrode that injects electrons in the QTM junction.
- **graphene superlattice sample** — The sample consisting of a graphene superlattice with flat and dispersive bands.
- **graphene tip** — Graphene probe whose Dirac point can be shifted by applied strain.
- **graphene tip (mlg‑coated)** — Tip electrode consisting of MLG on a pyramidal AFM tip.
- **graphene/hbn interface** — Sample consisting of graphene placed on an hBN substrate.
- **graphene/hbn moiré interface** — Van der Waals heterostructure where the moiré potential is imaged.
- **graphene/hbn/graphene heterostructure** — A van‑der‑Waals stack consisting of graphene layers separated by hexagonal boron nitride, used as the tunneling junction.
- **graphene/h‑bn/graphene heterostructure tunnel junction** — Device consisting of a top graphene electrode, an h‑BN tunnel barrier, and a bottom graphene electrode.
- **graphene/h‑bn/graphene tunnel junction** — Device consisting of two graphene electrodes separated by a thin h‑BN layer, fabricated with controlled twist angle.
- **graphene/sto heterostructure** — Stack of graphene on SrTiO₃ used to study nonreciprocal plasmons and STO phase transitions.
- **graphene‑graphene junction** — Finite‑area junction formed by two graphene layers, one on the tip and one on the substrate.
- **graphene‑graphene junction** — Contact region between two graphene layers used in the QTM.
- **graphene‑hbn‑graphene heterostructure** — Twisted stack used in FTIR‑QTOM and photo‑tunneling measurements.
- **graphene‑hbn‑graphene stack** — Stack consisting of graphene / hBN / graphene used to probe interlayer tunneling.
- **graphene‑hbn‑graphene stack** — Stack consisting of graphene, hBN, and graphene layers.
- **graphene–graphene junction** — Junction formed by two graphene layers probed in the experiment.
- **graphite** — Bulk graphite used to collect data at additional twist angles.
- **graphite** — Sample used for additional angle‑dependent data.
- **graphite hbn graphene heterostructure** — Sample consisting of graphite, hBN and graphene.
- **graphite on graphene** — Sample consisting of graphite on graphene.
- **graphite tip** — Graphite tip used in THz QTM.
- **hbn barrier** — Hexagonal boron nitride layer used as an insulating spacer.
- **hbn‑backed monolayer graphene tip** — Tip consisting of a hexagonal‑boron‑nitride backing and a monolayer of graphene.
- **hbn‑encapsulated graphene** — Graphene monolayer encapsulated between hBN layers.
- **hbn‑encapsulated graphene sample** — Graphene flake sandwiched between two hBN layers, used for tunneling experiments.
- **hexagonal boron nitride junction** — Vertical heterostructure where hBN acts as a tunnelling barrier.
- **high mobility 2deg sample** — GaAs‑based two‑dimensional electron gas used to probe electron temperature.
- **hole-doped monolayer graphene (μ_t = -60 mev)** — Monolayer graphene with tip chemical potential -60 meV.
- **hole‑doped ma tbg sample with empty flat bands** — A MA TBG flake that is hole‑doped and whose flat bands are initially empty.
- **individual defect in hbn** — Single atomic‑scale defect embedded in hBN.
- **junction** — Electrical junction where measurements are performed.
- **large sample (>10 µm × 10 µm)** — A relatively simple, macroscale sample used to test translational invariance.
- **lif** — Lithium fluoride crystal used to study exciton polarons.
- **ma tbg sample** — Magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene sample studied in the analysis.
- **ma tbg with weak strain (ε=0.1%, φ=15°)** — MA TBG subjected to a small uniaxial strain used to study C3z symmetry breaking.
- **magic‑angle bilayer graphene** — Bilayer graphene tuned to the magic twist angle.
- **magic‑angle graphene superlattices** — Graphene stacked at ~1.1° forming a moiré superlattice.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (ma‑tbg)** — Twisted bilayer graphene near 1.1° that hosts flat electronic bands.
- **matbg device 1** — Magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene device measured in the study.
- **matbg device 2** — Second magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene device measured in a separate cooldown.
- **matbg sample** — Physical device of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene measured with the QTM.
- **matbg sample under strong gate screening** — Magic‑angle TBG placed between metallic gates (or high‑κ dielectrics) with a 1 nm gate‑sample spacing.
- **mbe samples** — Molecular‑beam‑epitaxy grown samples supplied by Dr. M. J. Yang.
- **mesa** — Elevated region where the stack is placed.
- **metal marker** — Metallic fiducial markers on the sample used for alignment.
- **metal surface** — Typical metallic substrate used in STM imaging.
- **mlg / bilayer wse₂ / 2.7° twisted bilayer graphene tunnel junction** — Tunnel junction consisting of a monolayer graphene probe, a bilayer WSe₂ spacer and a 2.7° twisted bilayer graphene stack.
- **mlg layers** — Two multilayer‑graphene sheets forming the tunnelling junction.
- **mlg-mlg junction** — Vertical junction formed by two monolayer graphene sheets.
- **mlg/hbn/graphite sample** — Heterostructure consisting of monolayer graphene, hBN and graphite, used for MLG‑versus‑MLG experiments.
- **mlg/wse2/mlg tunneling junction** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling device consisting of monolayer graphene – trilayer WSe2 – monolayer graphene stack.
- **mlg‑2.7°‑tbg sample** — Stack consisting of a monolayer graphene probe and a 2.7° twisted bilayer graphene target.
- **mlg‑mlg (monolayer graphene – monolayer graphene)** — Device consisting of two monolayer graphene sheets forming a tunneling junction.
- **mlg‑mlg sample** — A tunneling junction formed by two monolayer‑graphene sheets.
- **mlg‑tbg (monolayer graphene – twisted bilayer graphene)** — Device where a monolayer graphene tip tunnels into twisted bilayer graphene.
- **mnte** — Manganese telluride, an altermagnetic material studied in several works.
- **mnte surface sample** — MnTe crystal surface studied with RPED at the Mn L₂,₃‑edge.
- **mnte(0001) surface (te‑terminated)** — Te‑terminated MnTe(0001) surface used for RPES and RPED calculations.
- **mn‑a site** — Magnetic sublattice A of MnTe.
- **mn‑b site** — Magnetic sublattice B of MnTe.
- **moiré graphene sample (ma tbg)** — Moiré‑aligned twisted bilayer graphene used as the spectroscopic target.
- **moiré superlattice (twisted bilayer graphene / hbn)** — Moiré pattern formed by twisted bilayer graphene encapsulated by hBN, used as a drain in the experiments.
- **moiré system** — Moiré superlattice acting as drain in QTM experiments.
- **moiré system on tip** — Moiré superlattice placed on the QTM tip.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg)** — Monolayer graphene backed by h‑BN and graphite placed on the QTM tip.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg) on qtm tip** — Probe electrode formed by a single graphene layer mounted on the QTM tip.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg) probe** — The graphene monolayer that acts as the tunnelling probe.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg) probe** — Monolayer graphene flake used as a spectroscopic reference (Dirac bands) in the QTM measurements.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg) sample** — The graphene layer that forms the active region of the junction probed by QTM.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg) tip** — A normal‑metal tip made of monolayer graphene used in tunneling experiments.
- **monolayer graphene tip (mlg tip)** — Graphene tip whose Dirac Hamiltonian is used in the tunneling model.
- **monolayer nii2** — Single‑layer NiI2 used in DFT simulations to compare with bulk.
- **monolayer‑graphene tip** — A monolayer graphene sheet mounted on the QTM tip, acting as the tunneling source.
- **mos₂ / mos₂ twisted bilayer** — Twisted bilayer of MoS₂ used as a benchmark for twistronics and tunneling spectroscopy.
- **multilayer graphene / hbn / graphite stack** — Van der Waals heterostructure used in figures 2 and 3.
- **nanostructures** — General class of nanoscale devices investigated with the cryogenic AFM.
- **nii2 device** — Fabricated NiI2 crystal contacted for photocurrent and transport measurements.
- **nii2 sample (100‑nm‑thick)** — A 100‑nm‑thick crystal of NiI2 used in CPGE measurements.
- **nii₂ crystal** — Bulk NiI₂ crystal used in photocurrent and other measurements.
- **nii₂ crystal measured below the néel temperature (tn = 59.5 k)** — Single‑crystal specimen of NiI₂ used to investigate spin‑helix order and magnetoelectric coupling.
- **nii₂ crystal sample** — Sample of NiI₂ crystal used in experiments.
- **nii₂ crystals** — Bulk NiI₂ crystals grown by PVD.
- **nii₂ monolayer** — Isolated single‑layer NiI₂ model employed in DFT simulations.
- **nii₂ thin flakes** — Thin exfoliated pieces of NiI₂ grown on SiO₂/Si.
- **normally doped semiconductor surface** — Semiconductor surface that requires a large (~2 V) bias to obtain a measurable tunneling current.
- **np nanopositioner** — Nanopositioner device equipped with piezoelectric actuators, used with the NP‑Drive UM100.
- **num positioner** — Numerically encoded positioner that can be tested with the AAC350/NUM cable.
- **optimized stm design (model 1)** — STM device after applying the optimisation criterion.
- **original stm design (model 1)** — Baseline STM device used as the reference in the optimisation study.
- **outer cylinder (sample)** — Sample holder whose bending resonance changes with the new design.
- **pcb d** — Printed circuit board D used in Project 3 for coaxial wire soldering.
- **pcb e** — Printed circuit board E used in Project 3 for coaxial wire soldering.
- **pentalayer graphene** — A thin flake consisting of five stacked graphene layers, used to probe the reach of the moiré potential.
- **perfectly aligned gr/h‑bn/gr device (θ = 0°, γ = 50 mev, 8 l h‑bn barrier)** — Device where the two graphene layers are crystallographically aligned, enabling momentum‑conserving tunneling.
- **petri dish** — Petri dish used as container for weighing and for zeroing the balance.
- **pmma‑graphene‑cu‑graphene stack** — Stack composed of PMMA, graphene, copper and graphene, with the PMMA side (orange face) facing up.
- **premium line positioner** — Attocube Premium Line positioner.
- **pt pyramid** — Platinum pyramid tip shaped by FIB.
- **pvd‑grown nii2 flake** — 40 nm‑thick NiI2 flake grown by physical vapor deposition, transferred onto a TEM grid and used for Raman, linear‑dichroism and SHG measurements.
- **qtf ecs‑3x8** — Specific Quartz Tuning Fork model whose dimensions are shown in Fig. 3.3(b).
- **qtf sensor with tip glued on one prong** — Quartz tuning fork sensor where a tip is attached to one prong for AFM probing.
- **qtm tip with aligned g/hbn** — Scanning tip in which graphene and hBN are rotationally aligned, producing a moiré lattice.
- **relaxed compressed twisted bilayer graphene** — Compressed TBG after structural relaxation under pressure.
- **res positioner** — Resistive encoded positioner used with vacuum‑compatible cables.
- **resonant interband tunneling diode (ritd)** — Interband tunneling diode structure grown by MBE on an n⁺ InAs substrate.
- **rhombohedral graphene** — Graphene with rhombohedral stacking (typically three or four layers).
- **rhombohedral tetralayer graphene** — Four‑layer graphene with rhombohedral stacking.
- **rhombohedral trilayer graphene** — ABC‑stacked trilayer graphene.
- **sample h20850** — The sample mounted on the STM, referenced as /H208491/H20850sample in the document.
- **sample holder** — Mechanical holder that mounts the sample over the Faraday cup.
- **sample layer** — Graphene layer that supplies the valence band for tunneling.
- **sb25 a** — SB25 connector paired with Fischer male A in Project 1.
- **sb9 b** — SB9 connector paired with Fischer B in Project 2.
- **sb9 c** — SB9 connector paired with Fischer C in Project 2.
- **seagate 2.1 g hard drive** — Magnetic storage medium used as a test sample for MFM.
- **sei‑110‑02‑g‑s‑ab** — Specific sample identified by the code SEI‑110‑02‑G‑S‑AB.
- **silicon wafer** — Si wafer used as the specimen for electron‑beam lithography.
- **silicone elastomer mixture** — Mixture of base and curing agent prepared with a 10:1 mass ratio.
- **single crystal of ucoge** — Single crystal sample of UCoGe used to measure anisotropy.
- **single low‑energy defect** — A localized defect in the QTM junction used as a spectroscopic probe.
- **single vacancy in a monolayer crystal** — Missing atom in a 2D crystal lattice.
