- **single‑defect resonant transistor** — Device where transport occurs through a single defect state.
- **single‑electron quantum dot in gaas 2d electron gas** — GaAs quantum dot employed in the spin‑relaxation experiment.
- **smfeas(o,f) layered superconductor** — Sample of SmFeAs(O,F) used to study vortex mobility.
- **smp female** — 6‑pin SMP female connector referenced in Project 2.
- **split‑gate device** — Device undergoing split‑gate fabrication, later covered with an hBN flake.
- **srₓbi₂se₃ topological superconductor sample** — Sample used for angle‑dependent magneto‑transport studies.
- **stack** — Layered heterostructure assembled from 2D materials.
- **strained ma tbg (θ_tbg=1.05°, ε=0.1%, φ=15°)** — MA TBG sample subjected to a small uniaxial heterostrain of 0.1 % at a rotation of 15°.
- **strained ma‑tbg** — MA‑TBG subjected to uniaxial strain, used to illustrate C₃z‑symmetry breaking signatures.
- **strain‑free ma tbg** — MA TBG without any applied heterostrain.
- **strain‑free ma tbg** — MA TBG without any applied strain, serving as a reference.
- **substrate / sample** — The material placed in the sample holder that receives the evaporated thin film.
- **target substrate** — Substrate onto which the transferred graphene is placed for final drying.
- **tbg heterostructure** — Twisted bilayer graphene heterostructure comprising bilayer WSe₂, TBG, h‑BN, Pt on a Si/SiO₂ pillar.
- **tbg sample at θ = 20°** — Twisted bilayer graphene device with a 20 degree twist angle.
- **tbg sample at θ = 30°** — Twisted bilayer graphene device with a 30 degree twist angle.
- **tbg sample with θ_tbg = 1.1°** — A magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene device studied in the experiment.
- **tbg sample with θ_tbg = 1.1° (magic angle)** — Twisted bilayer graphene region with a twist angle of ~1.1°, corresponding to the magic angle.
- **tbg sample with θ_tbg = 1.2°** — Twisted bilayer graphene region with a twist angle of 1.2°, larger than the magic angle.
- **tblg encapsulated in hbn flakes** — Sample of magic‑angle tBLG sandwiched between hexagonal boron nitride layers.
- **tio₂(110) surface** — Titanium dioxide (110) surface used in defect studies.
- **tip (graphene)** — Graphene tip whose chemical potential is set to +eV_b/2.
- **tip 1** — First graphite tip used in the QTM set‑up; area of the tip is the baseline geometry.
- **tip 2** — Second graphite tip with roughly twice the contact area of tip 1, used to test tip‑shape effects.
- **tip dirac point kθ** — Dirac point of the tip material, defining the trajectory Kθ along which the spectral function is probed.
- **tip graphene layer (layer 1)** — Upper graphene layer acting as tip in the tunneling geometry.
- **tip h20850** — The STM tip referenced as /H208492/H20850tip in the document.
- **tip 1 (hbn‑backed monolayer graphene tip)** — Specific tip with a measured contact area of 37 602 nm².
- **tip 2 (hbn‑backed monolayer graphene tip)** — Specific tip with a measured contact area of 18 106 nm².
- **tlg/hbn superlattice** — A heterostructure consisting of trilayer graphene aligned with hexagonal boron nitride, used as a model system.
- **tmd monolayer on graphene electrode** — The specific sample where a single‑layer TMD is transferred onto the graphene surface.
- **top graphene electrode** — Graphene layer serving as the top electrode in the tunneling device.
- **top graphene layer** — The graphene electrode closest to the top gate.
- **top graphene layer of tbg** — Upper graphene sheet of the twisted bilayer graphene sample.
- **top graphene/hbn layer** — The upper graphene sheet encapsulated by hexagonal boron nitride used in the heterostructure.
- **top monolayer graphene layer** — The upper graphene sheet in the tunneling stack.
- **trilayer wse2 on graphene electrode** — Sample used in defect‑imaging experiments with the QTM tip.
- **trilayer wse₂ barrier** — Three‑layer WSe₂ crystal used in the room‑temperature measurement.
- **tungsten surface** — Surface used in tip‑model calculations.
- **tunnel barrier (2–3 layers of wse₂)** — Barrier separating the top and bottom vdW layers in the QTM junction.
- **twistable mlg–mlg interface** — Two monolayer graphene sheets in direct contact whose twist angle is varied in situ.
- **twisted bilayer graphene** — Two graphene layers rotated relative to each other.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (ma tbg)** — Sample layer consisting of moiré bilayer graphene whose flat bands and mini‑Brillouin zone are probed.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (tbg)** — Bilayer graphene with a controlled twist angle.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (tbg) device** — Back‑gated TBG flake on a flat substrate, capped with a bilayer WSe₂ barrier.
- **twisted bilayer graphene / hbn moiré superlattice** — tBG encapsulated by hBN forming a moiré pattern.
- **twisted bilayer graphene sample** — Physical sample of TBG used for tunneling measurements.
- **twisted bilayer graphene with 4.41° twist angle** — TBG compressed at a 4.41° twist angle.
- **twisted double bilayer graphene** — Four graphene layers arranged as two bilayers with a relative twist.
- **twisted graphene junction** — The interlayer region where two graphene sheets are stacked with a controllable twist angle, probed by the QTM tip.
- **twisted graphene superlattices** — Periodic moiré structures formed by twisting graphene layers.
- **twisted interface** — Region where two layers are stacked with a finite twist angle.
- **twisted interface between two graphite layers** — Sample consisting of two graphite flakes, each several tens of nanometres thick, forming a twisted junction.
- **twisted transition‑metal dichalcogenide bilayer** — TMD bilayer with a finite twist angle.
- **twisted trilayer graphene** — Three graphene layers with a small relative twist.
- **van der waals devices used in the experiment** — Stacked heterostructures comprising graphene, hBN and graphite employed for the measurements.
- **van der waals heterostructure sample** — Deterministically assembled stack of 2D materials used as the test specimen in OQTM experiments.
- **van der waals stack sample** — The 2D material heterostructure placed on the sapphire window.
- **van‑der‑waals device** — Stacked heterostructure containing twisted bilayer graphene measured with the QTM.
- **vdw crystal 1** — Top active vdW layer in the QTM junction.
- **vdw crystal 2** — Bottom active vdW layer in the QTM junction.
- **vdw devices on tip** — Van‑der‑Waals heterostructure mounted on the scanning tip.
- **vdw device‑on‑tip** — Van‑der‑Waals heterostructure assembled on the tip of an AFM cantilever.
- **vdw heterostructure on flat substrate** — Van der Waals heterostructure assembled on a flat Si/SiO2 substrate with electrical contacts.
- **vdw heterostructure on pyramid** — Van der Waals heterostructure assembled on a platinum pyramid near the edge of an AFM cantilever.
- **vdw plateau on qtm tip** — Flat van der Waals plateau that self‑aligns parallel to the sample during contact.
- **vdw‑functionalized afm tip** — AFM tip whose apex is coated with a second vdW material via wet‑membrane transfer.
- **wse2 tunnelling barrier** — WSe2 layer acting as the tunnelling barrier in the QTM experiment.
- **wse₂ / wse₂ twisted bilayer** — Twisted bilayer of WSe₂ used to study Moiré excitons and light‑assisted tunneling.
- **wse₂–graphene heterostructure** — Stack of WSe₂ and graphene.
- **ws₂ / ws₂ twisted bilayer** — Twisted bilayer of WS₂ for high‑energy exciton tunneling studies.

### setup
- **"axis overview" page** — Page that provides an overview of axes.
- **"controller configuration" screen** — User interface screen for configuring the controller.
- **/pro feature package** — Feature package providing professional capabilities.
- **10.1” touchscreen user interface** — Touchscreen for configuring drive waveforms and moving the nanopositioner.
- **1024‑pixel ccd camera** — Detector for Raman spectra.
- **12‑pin fischer connector** — 12‑Pin Fischer connector on the breakout panel.
- **15‑pin d‑sub connector** — Connector on the feedthrough side of the attocube vacuum extension cable for positioning control and sensor connections.
- **19‑inch rack mount** — Standard 19‑inch equipment‑rack mounting hardware used for the NP‑Drive.
- **1‑m concave mirror** — Concave mirror with 1 m focal length used in the THz beam line.
- **20‑nm‑thick nii2 device (pvd‑grown)** — Thin‑film NiI2 device used for pyroelectric and photocurrent measurements.
- **215 gas handling system** — Gas handling system for the FIRL100 laser, with specified AC voltage and current ratings.
- **295stab laser stabiliser** — Stabiliser module (including ALS1 and chopper) supplied with the FIRL100 system.
- **2nd version optical setup: no beam reducer** — Optical setup version without a beam reducer, including periscope, FIR alignment, lenses and a movable platform with micrometers.
- **2‑pin connector** — Connector on the positioner side of the attocube vacuum extension cable for positioning control signals.
- **2’’ – efl 4’’** — Lens with 2‑inch diameter and 4‑inch effective focal length.
- **3d vector magnet** — Magnet system capable of generating magnetic fields in arbitrary directions, mentioned as an alternative but costly solution.
- **3rd version optical setup (new optical table)** — Third optical configuration on a new table, including ITO, YTO, and cSNOM pilot.
- **3‑pin connector** — Connector on the positioner side of the attocube vacuum extension cable for positioning sensor signals.
- **3 bar invar support frame** — Rigid support structure pressurised to 3 bar, used in model‑295.
- **4 quadrants detector visualization** — Visualization panel showing the four‑quadrant detector signals.
- **4 quadrants detector visualization** — Four‑quadrant detector used for visualizing and centering the laser spot.
- **40x optical microscope** — Optical microscope used to assist the QTF holder steps.
- **45° mirror** — Mirror at 45° that directs the laser beam upwards.
- **4th version optical setup (new optical table)** — Fourth optical configuration on a new table, featuring ITO and no collimation.
- **4‑inch parabolic mirror** — Parabolic mirror component of the THz beam path.
- **4‑layer shear mode piezo stack** — Stack of four shear‑mode piezo slabs that actuates the coarse z motor.
- **4 k plate** — 4 K plate for mounting samples.
- **50/50 beam splitter** — Optical component that splits the pump beam into two equal parts for the twin cavities of model‑395.
- **50× objective (2‑µm spot size)** — Microscope objective that focuses the laser to a ~2 µm spot.
- **5th version optical setup (new optical table)** — Fifth optical configuration on a new table, with ITO and collimation restored.
- **5 bar invar support frame** — Rigid support structure pressurised to 5 bar, used in model‑395 and FIRL100.
- **6 mm lens (manual placement)** — Focuses the laser onto the sample; currently aligned manually.
- **a100 model** — Model A100 offering piezo drive for open loop nanopositioner motion.
- **aac300 cable** — Cable connecting open‑loop positioners or scanners to ANC300; BNC on one side, two‑pole plug on the other, pins marked red for positive.
- **aac350/num cable** — 15‑pin Sub‑D cable for vacuum‑compatible NUM encoded positioners, limited to 3 m length.
- **aac350/res cable** — Cable connecting a resistive or open‑loop positioner to ANC350/RES axis; positive piezo drive contact marked red.
- **aac350/scn cable** — Cable powering an open‑loop scanner from ANC350/SCN output; positive contact marked red.
- **abort button** — Abort button on plasma asher used to start venting.
- **about screen** — Screen that displays upgrade icons such as dual‑channel and tri‑channel.
- **ac-in input** — BNC input on ANM300 for AC signals.
- **acc100 breakout panel** — ACC100 breakout panel for connecting AMC300 to the microscopy insert.
- **acc50 breakout panel** — ACC50 breakout panel for connecting AMC300 to the microscopy insert.
- **achromatic quarter‑wave plate** — Optical component used to convert linear polarization into circular polarization for CPGE.
- **achromatic quarter‑wave plate** — Optical component that converts linear to circular polarization for CPGE experiments.
- **act led indicator** — LED that lights up to indicate stepping activity.
- **actuator** — Piezoelectric actuator made of PZT ceramics.
- **ac‑in switch** — Switch to enable AC input (disabled in the UI).
- **adapter 24‑coax cryostat to breakout box 0.4 m** — Adapter that converts 24 coaxial lines from a cryostat to a breakout box, length 0.4 m.
- **adjust uv (global) field** — UI field used to enable automatic focus correction.
- **adjust uv (global) field** — Software field used to input origin and angle corrections.
- **adjustable afm head legs** — Legs that allow the AFM head to be elevated and mounted on a post, creating open space underneath.
- **adjustable ftir mirror** — Adjustable mirror used for FTIR measurements.
- **adwin pro ii data acquisition system** — Controls bias voltage and reads amplifier output.
- **afm** — Atomic force microscope system used for alignment and tip‑sample current detection.
- **afm cantilever with piezoelectric rotator** — Atomic force microscope cantilever equipped with a piezoelectric rotator for continuous angle control.
- **afm computer bys screen** — Screen on the AFM computer that displays markers and rulers for alignment.
- **afm computer bys screen marker** — Software on the AFM computer used to display markers and draw circles for alignment.
- **afm dimension3100 veeco** — Atomic force microscope used for spatially resolved current measurements.
- **afm feedback loop** — Atomic force microscope feedback circuit that incorporates the QTF sensor.
- **afm head** — Commercial atomic force microscope head that holds the cantilever and tip.
- **afm illumination** — Illumination of the AFM that must be turned off after measurements.
- **afm stage** — AFM stage used to bring the sample into the camera field of view and to engage the tip.
- **alpha hp module** — Delivers 1.3–4.5 µm high‑power beam; can be manually optimized.
- **als1 chopper** — Chopper component of the 295STAB laser stabiliser.
- **amc device** — Controller device that communicates with the IDS sensor and the positioner.
- **amc sensor** — Sensor option (AMC/IDS) required for closed‑loop operation.
- **amc/ids closed loop feature package (beta)** — Beta version of the AMC/IDS closed loop feature package for the device.
- **amc100 controller** — Controller displayed in attoDISCOVERY software, part of the network of attocube devices.
- **amc300** — Closed‑loop multi‑functional piezo controller with integrated feedback, available as tabletop (1‑3 axes) or rack‑mount (up to 30 axes).
- **amc300 closed loop piezo controller** — Closed loop piezo controller model AMC300.
- **amc300 controller** — Controller displayed in attoDISCOVERY software, part of the network of attocube devices.
- **amc300 interface** — Interface of the AMC300 controller where LUT files are uploaded and positioner presets are selected.
- **amc300 positioner control cable (room temperature)** — Cable for controlling AMC300 positioner in room temperature applications.
- **amc300 positioner control cable for vacuum applications** — Cable for controlling AMC300 positioner in vacuum applications.
- **amc300 positioning controller** — Controller for simultaneous operation of up to three attocube ANP series positioners.
- **amc300 – closed loop controller** — Closed‑loop controller used with attocube positioners.
- **amcc/1/res/5‑pin/2.0/rt cable** — Cable linking a resistive or open‑loop positioner to AMC300; D‑Sub HD 26‑pin on AMC300 side, 5‑pin connector to positioner, positive pins marked by bevels on PEEK connectors.
- **amcc/3/res/d‑sub‑15/2.0/(u)hv cable** — Cable that connects up to three open‑loop or resistive encoded AN* series positioners through a D‑Sub 15 feed‑through.
- **amplified photodiode (pda100a2, thorlabs)** — Detects backscattered light in dichroism measurements.
- **amplitude modulation afm** — Dynamic AFM method where the cantilever is driven at a fixed frequency close to its eigenfrequency.
- **anc 300 piezo positioning electronic** — Back‑panel electronics for piezo positioning modules, providing power, control dials, LEDs and various connectors.
- **anc controller** — Controller used to drive positioners, switched to GND before connecting cables.
- **anc250 scan voltage amplifier** — Scan voltage amplifier model ANC250.
- **anc250 scan voltage amplifier** — Attocube high‑end scan voltage amplifier for piezo scanning tubes and flexure scanners.
- **anc250 – controller for scanners** — Controller specifically designed for ANS scanners.
- **anc300** — The attocube ANC300 axis controller described in the manual, used for positioning and requiring careful installation and maintenance.
- **anc300 controller** — ANC300 controller device used for positioning and measurement.
- **anc300 controller** — The Attocube ANC300 controller board that receives feature codes and controls axis modules.
- **anc300 gui** — Graphical user interface (ANC300 GUI) for configuring the controller.
- **anc300 open loop piezo controller** — Open loop piezo controller model ANC300 used to drive attocube positioners.
- **anc300 – open loop controller** — Open‑loop controller used with attocube positioners.
- **anc350 controller** — Controller that receives rotation commands (e.g., –50° to 200°).
- **anc350 rotator** — ANC350 rotator with height 9 cm
- **ang goniometer** — Attocube ANG type goniometer used for angular (θ, φ) positioning around a common center of rotation.
- **ang goniometer (attocube)** — Attocube goniometer (ANG) that uses slip‑stick driving.
- **ang – goniometers** — Goniometer‑like positioners from attocube.
- **ang101 goniometer** — A goniometer model ANG101 that can be mounted from top or bottom.
- **ang50 goniometer** — A goniometer model ANG50 that can be mounted from top or bottom.
- **anm150 module** — ANM150 module for additional motion control.
- **anm150 open‑loop positioning module** — ANM150 module with nominal output 0‑+150 V, stepping up to 10 kHz and capacitance‑measurement capability.
- **anm200 axis screen** — Graphical user interface for the ANM200 axis, showing controls such as offset voltage, switches and measurement buttons.
- **anm200 module** — ANM200 module that can be used with the ANC300 system.
- **anm200 module** — ANM200 module for further motion functions.
- **anm200 module** — Open‑loop, low‑noise scanning module (Attocube) with nominal output voltage 0 .. +150 V, providing offset voltage, DC‑IN amplification, AC‑IN addition, low‑pass filtering, capacitance measurement and status LEDs.
- **anm300 module** — ANM300 module providing additional motion functions.
- **anm300 module** — Open loop positioning and scanning module with unipolar output 0–150 V, current limiter, and various control functions.
- **anm300 positioning module** — ANM300 module referenced for its stepping functionality, similar to ANM150.
- **anp linear positioner (attocube)** — Attocube linear positioner (ANP) that uses slip‑stick driving for nanometer‑scale steps.
- **anp series positioner** — Attocube positioner (dimensional, linear, rotary or goniometric) used with the AMC300 controller.
- **anp – linear positioners** — Linear positioners from attocube used for precise motion control.
- **anpx101 linear positioner** — ANPx101 linear positioner from attocube, integrated piezo ceramics housed in a titanium frame.
- **anpx101 piezoelectric actuator** — Piezoelectric actuator model ANPx101 used for scanning with a range of 5 µm and 800 nm at 4 K.
- **anpxyz101** — Coarse positioning stack combining two ANPx101 stages and one ANPz101 stage.
- **anpxyz101 3‑d positioning system** — ANPxyz101 system composed of two ANPx101 positioners and one ANPz101 positioner to provide full 3‑D nanopositioning.
- **anpz101** — Z‑positioner stage used in the ANPxyz101 stack.
- **anpz101 linear positioner** — ANPz101 linear positioner from attocube, used as the z‑axis in a 3‑D positioning stack.
- **anpz50/lt/uhv z-positioner** — Model ANPz50/LT/UHV z‑positioner used in scanning probe setups.
- **anr** — Attocube piezo‑driven rotator tool mentioned in the white paper.
- **anr** — Attocube nano‑rotator model.
- **anr positioner** — Attocube nanopositioner used for in‑plane and out‑of‑plane rotation.
- **anr rotator (attocube)** — Attocube rotator (ANR) that uses slip‑stick driving.
- **anr – rotators** — Rotary positioners from attocube.
- **anr101 positioner** — Beryllium‑copper piezo‑driven rotator with resistive readout.
- **anr101 rotator** — Rotator ANR101, mountable from bottom with M2.5 screws or from top via an adapter plate with M2 screws.
- **anr240 rotator** — Rotator ANR240, can be mounted with vertical or horizontal rotational axis using M2 or M2.5 screws.
- **anr240/res/lt/hv rotator (360° endless)** — A 360° endless rotator with beryllium‑copper/titanium body and PZT actuator.
- **anr30/lt rotator** — Ultra‑compact rotator for angle‑dependent transport measurements, operable up to 33 T and down to 40 mK, mounted on a plastic insert in a dilution refrigerator.
- **anr31** — Nano‑rotator capable of sub‑0.1° precision below 2 K.
- **anr51 rotator** — Rotator ANR51, mountable only from the bottom with two M2 screws.
- **anrv220 rotator** — Rotator ANRv220, mountable only from the bottom with two M2 screws.
- **anrv51 rotator** — Piezo‑driven rotator with resistive encoder, usable up to 35 T and down to millikelvin temperatures, used for precise in‑plane rotation of samples.
- **ans piezo scanners** — Large‑range ANS piezo scanners built from titanium with piezoelectric ceramic stack elements.
- **ans – scanners** — Scanning positioners from attocube.
- **ans100** — Scanner model ANS100 with mounting screws M2 × 8, M2.5 × 5 and M2 × 3.
- **ans50** — Scanner model ANS50 with mounting screws M1.6 × 4, M2 × 3 and M1.6 × 2.
- **ansx150** — Scanner model ANSx150 with mounting screws M2 × 8, M2.5 × 5 and M2 × 2.
- **ansxy100 piezo scanner** — Dedicated piezo scanner ANSxy100 that can be mounted on top of xyz‑positioners for large‑scale imaging.
- **ansxyz100** — Three‑dimensional xyz‑unit model of the ANS scanner.
- **ansxyz110** — Scanner model ANSxyz110 with mounting screws M2 × 8, M1.6 × 1 and M2.5 × 5.
- **ansxyz50/lt xyz-scanner** — XYZ scanner made from titanium with PZT ceramic actuator and copper wiring, designed for fine positioning at cryogenic temperatures.
- **ansz100** — One‑dimensional z‑unit model of the ANS scanner.
- **argon valve** — Valve controlling Argon flow, closed at the end of the procedure.
- **asc500** — ASC500 SPM controller from attocube.
- **aspherical silicon lens** — Silicon lens with aspherical surface used in the THz optical system.
- **aspherical silicon lens (45°)** — 45° tilted version of the aspherical silicon lens.
- **atocube coarse‑motion piezo stage** — Coarse‑motion mechanism used in one STM design.
- **atomic force microscopy (afmworkshop hr)** — AFM used to determine NiI2 flake thickness.
- **atomic set** — Scanning probe using a single atomic defect as a potential sensor.
- **atomic set (quantum twisting microscope) scanning probe** — Scanning probe setup that incorporates an atomic single‑electron transistor in a QTM geometry for sub‑nanometre resolution.
- **atomic single electron transistor (set)** — A scanning probe that uses a single atomic defect in a van der Waals material as an ultrasensitive, high‑resolution electrostatic potential sensor.
- **atto device** — The device whose network settings and factory defaults are managed.
- **atto3dr** — Attocube eucentric double rotator where two rotation axes intersect at the sample centre.
- **atto3dr** — Attocube 3D rotator integrated solution.
- **attocube 2200 positioner** — Piezo positioner model 2200 intended for fibre‑coupled optics at ICFO.
- **attocube 800 positioner** — Piezo‑driven coarse positioner used for normal optics in the cryo‑QTM.
- **attocube 800 positioner** — Piezo positioner model 800 used by the Weizmann group.
- **attocube anr nano-rotator** — Piezo‑driven nano‑rotator based on the slip‑stick principle, offering sub‑milli‑degree angular precision.
- **attocube anr350 rotational stage** — Cryogenic rotational stage with 0.01° angular resolution used to vary the twist angle between two vdW layers.
- **attocube anr51** — Piezo rotator used for angle‑dependent transport on SrₓBi₂Se₃.
- **attocube anrv220** — Piezo rotator used together with a compact dilatometer.
- **attocube anrv51** — Encoded piezo rotator used for spin‑relaxation measurements.
- **attocube ans scanner** — Attocube open‑loop ANS scanner driven by DC voltages, available in z, xy and xyz versions.
- **attocube ansxyz50 scanner** — Fine‑scan piezo scanner used for high‑resolution imaging and spectroscopy on the sample.
- **attocube atto3dr** — Model of attocube nano‑rotator mentioned in the white paper.
- **attocube attotms** — Model of attocube nano‑rotator mentioned in the white paper.
- **attocube coarse motor** — Commercial coarse‑approach actuator used in model 1.
- **attocube controller (anr)** — Controller used to set the twist angle of the rotator.
- **attocube controller anr** — Standard Attocube controller used to drive the rotator.
- **attocube positioner** — Attocube friction‑driven piezo positioner used for coarse and fine positioning.
- **attocube scanner** — Piezo scanner from Attocube used for scanning measurements.
- **attocube scanner** — Open‑loop scanner that can be powered from ANC300 or ANC350 outputs.
- **attocube system** — The scientific instrument system supplied by Attocube that requires installation and integration.
- **attocube tools** — Family of attocube rotation devices (ANR, atto3DR, attoTMS).
- **attocube vacuum extension cable avc/res** — Cable providing connections for positioners with resistive readout, featuring a 15‑pin D‑Sub connector on the feedthrough side and multiple 2‑ and 3‑pin connectors on the positioner side.
- **attocube xyz positioner** — Commercial XYZ nanopositioner from Attocube used for fine and coarse motion control.
- **attocube z‑stage** — Commercial piezoelectric z‑stage used as the coarse‑motion piezo.
- **attocube_driver.py script** — Python driver script that controls the attocube rotation stage.
- **attodiscovery application** — Software used to control multiple attocube devices when the /PRO feature is active.
- **attodiscovery controller tiles** — Controller tiles displayed on the attoDISCOVERY hardware.
- **attodiscovery device** — The attoDISCOVERY device comprising a positioner, axes and remote controller.
- **attodiscovery software** — PC‑based application for controlling and analyzing positioner movements, providing graphical user interfaces such as Find Devices and Operation screens.
- **attodiscovery webapp** — Web application for controlling attocube devices.
- **attodiscovery webapp** — Web application interface for controlling the attoDISCOVERY device.
- **attodiscovery webapp** — The web application used to control the positioner and its axes.
- **attodry2100 cryostat** — Automated cryostat integrated with the atto3DR in the attocube measurement platform.
- **attodry2100 dry cryostat** — Automated closed‑cycle cryostat with temperature range 1.65–300 K and magnetic field up to 9 T.
- **attodry2200** — A compact dry‑cryostat unit with a footprint of ~1.45 m × 0.79 m and height ~1.40 m.
- **attodry2200** — The main microscope system whose footprint and height are specified in the guide.
- **attodry2200 cryostat** — Cryogenic platform providing a base temperature of 1.6 K and a low‑vibration environment.
- **attotms** — Attocube piezo‑driven rotator tool mentioned in the white paper.
- **attotms** — Attocube torque magnetometer system.
- **attotms measurement system** — Integrated transport‑measurement solution combining atto3DR, attoDRY2100 and Nanonis Tramea electronics.
- **axis** — Mechanical axis of the ANC300 device whose filter settings can be adjusted.
- **axis module** — A removable module that slides into the ANC300 rail system and provides the mechanical axis for positioning.
- **axis module m1** — First axis module referenced in examples (e.g., m1:step, m1.acin).
- **axis module m2** — Second axis module referenced for pattern handling (e.g., m2.patu).
- **axis screen** — Screen for controlling an individual axis of the ANC300.
- **axis settings page** — Page for axis settings, including positioner selection.
- **axis setup/res sensors section** — Section of the Configuration screen to adapt the measurement mode of the AMC300.
- **axis tile** — UI element representing a single positioning axis.
- **axis tile configuration view** — Configuration view tile in the graphical user interface for setting up and monitoring an axis of the attocube positioner.
- **b100 model** — Model B100 adding position measurement and closed loop control with ACAPTM sensor.
- **b2985b electrometer (keysight)** — Instrument for sub‑picoampere current detection.
- **back panel connections** — Back panel connectors for external devices.
- **balance** — Analytical balance used to weigh the chemicals.
- **barrier region** — Spatial region between x_a and x_b where the potential barrier resides.
- **base flange** — Mounting interface onto which a positioning unit can be attached.
- **base plate** — Structural element to which components are attached and through which disturbances couple.
- **beam splitter** — Optical element that splits the laser beam into two paths.
- **beam_high** — Title entry in the table, likely a setup or experiment name.
- **beamblanker** — Beamblanker component used to open/close the electron beam during exposure.
- **beetle‑type stm** — STM design where coarse‑motion piezos are mounted on the base plate and the sample or scan piezo is held by a ramp.
- **beetle‑type stm with moving sample** — STM configuration (model 2) where the sample is moved by the piezo.
- **beetle‑type stm with moving tip** — STM configuration (model 3) where the tip is moved by the piezo.
- **bipolar magnet power supply** — Power supply that enables magnetic field reversal.
- **bottom gate (bg)** — Gate electrode below the sample layer, separated by hBN.
- **bpve measurement device** — Device with Ti/Au electrodes on SiO2/Si used to measure bulk photovoltaic effect.
- **breakout box** — Box that provides individual access to multiple coaxial lines.
- **break‑out panel (bop)** — Break‑out panel with D‑Sub and Fischer connectors.
- **building ups (corridor)** — Uninterruptible Power Supply located in the corridor.
- **building ups (main)** — Uninterruptible Power Supply located in the main building area.
- **built‑in atomic force microscope (afm)** — AFM integrated in the QTM to measure and maintain a constant normal force.
- **bulk nii2 device (cvt‑grown)** — Device fabricated from CVT‑grown bulk NiI2 with electric field applied along the [100] direction.
- **camera** — Camera used to locate the tip pyramid and to view the beam on the sample.
- **cantilever beam** — Rigid‑in‑two‑axes, compliant‑in‑third‑axis force sensor used in AFM.
- **capacitive position encoder** — Capacitive position encoder for precise positioning.
- **center for high‑resolution neutron scattering** — Collaborative center that supported the neutron‑scattering beam time.
- **ceramic lcc20 device package** — Ceramic package that holds the AlGaAs/GaAs heterostructure and provides wiring feedthroughs.
- **cf40 flange** — UHV‑compatible flange (copper‑gasket sealed) used for vacuum feed‑throughs.
- **cf40 flange with subd‑15pin feedthrough** — CF40 flange equipped with a SubD‑15pin feedthrough; requires inner tube diameter ≥38 mm.
- **cf63 flange** — UHV‑compatible flange (copper‑gasket sealed) used for vacuum feed‑throughs.
- **cf63 flange with three subd‑15pin feedthroughs** — CF63 flange equipped with three SubD‑15pin feedthroughs.
- **chamber mirror** — Mirror inside the optical chamber where power is measured.
- **channel configuration** — Channel configuration screen within the UI.
- **channel relay** — Relay that switches the high‑voltage signal lines on or off.
- **channel settings menu** — Menu accessed via the gear icon that contains channel‑specific settings.
- **charge‑neutral quantum tunneling transistor** — Transistor operating at charge neutrality, used to launch propagating plasmons.
- **chiller system** — Temperature‑controlled cooling unit required for stable laser operation.
- **circularly polarized light at the l₂ resonance (ℏω = 651.8 ev)** — Experimental illumination used for RPED measurements at the Mn L₂ edge.
- **circularly polarized light at the l₃ resonance (ℏω = 639.7 ev)** — Experimental illumination used for RPED measurements at the Mn L₃ edge.
- **clamped table** — Table that is clamped to the guiding rod during the slow flank of the voltage pulse.
- **clean room** — Clean‑room environment used for split‑gate fabrication and RIE processing.
- **clewin** — Software tool for handling GDSII structures and performing conversions.
- **closed‑cycle optical cryostat (opticool)** — Cryogenic environment for low‑temperature optical measurements.
- **closed‑loop motorized stage** — Stage that moves the 50× objective for scanning.
- **coarse motors** — Coarse positioning stages that move the tip and sample by ±2.5 mm.
- **coarse x stage** — Coarse positioning stage for x‑direction travel (±2 mm range).
- **coarse x/y stage** — Stage used to translate a selected dot to the centre of the AFM field of view.
- **coarse xy motors** — Two motors providing coarse positioning in the XY plane.
- **coarse xy motors** — Two coarse motors providing X‑Y translation of the sample stage.
- **coarse y stage** — Coarse positioning stage for y‑direction travel (±2.5 mm range).
- **coarse z motor** — Motor that provides reliable auto‑approach in the Z direction.
- **coarse z‑motor driving board** — Electronic board that drives the coarse Z‑piezo motor, shown in two variants.
- **coarse‑motion mechanism** — Component that provides large‑scale motion of the STM tip or sample.
- **coarse‑motion piezo** — Piezo element used for coarse motion, referenced as /H208494/H20850coarse-motion piezo.
- **commercial afm (bruker icon)** — Standard AFM used for force feedback and positioning in QTM.
- **compressor** — Compressor integrated into gas handling system.
- **compressor** — Compressor for the cryogenic system.
- **con27rt** — Cable connection labeled Con27RT, from fischer to SDC.
- **con2f** — Cable connection labeled Con2F, using 4 twisted triplets to fischer.
- **con3f** — Cable connection labeled Con3F, using 6 twisted pairs to fischer.
- **con3rt** — Cable connection labeled Con3RT, from fischer to SDC.
- **con7f** — Cable connection labeled Con7F, using 2 twisted triplets plus 6 SMP to fischer.
- **conductive afm (room‑temperature)** — Atomic force microscope operated at room temperature to map the moiré lattice conductivity.
- **conductive tip** — Metal‑coated AFM tip that enables DC current measurement through nanostructures.
- **configuration screen** — Screen to configure device and axis names.
- **configuration view** — Panel opened from an axis tile to edit its parameters.
- **configuring the network settings** — Sub‑step that configures network parameters.
- **connecting remote controller** — Step that connects a remote controller to the device.
- **connecting to network** — Step that connects the device to a network.
- **connecting to pc via usb** — Step that connects the device to a PC using a USB cable.
- **connecting to voltage supply** — Step that connects the device to a voltage supply.
- **connecting wires** — Insulated twisted‑pair copper wires for electrical connection.
- **connector** — Male or female electrical connector whose pins are marked by red marks and bevels.
- **connector panel** — Panel that provides connectors for the instrument’s subsystems.
- **connector s105a069‑130 (fischer)** — Fischer connector part listed in the adapter bill of materials.
- **connector s105a093‑130 (fischer)** — Fischer connector part listed in the adapter bill of materials.
- **control unit for driving piezo tubes** — Electronics that generate voltage signals based on the conversion‑ratio parameters.
- **controller config page** — GUI page used to configure the remote controller and its button functions.
- **controller device** — Remote controller hardware that drives the three axes.
- **controller tile** — UI element in attoDISCOVERY that displays controller properties and allows labeling and axis selection.
- **cooling water circuit** — Water circuit supplying inlet and outlet to the compressor.
- **co₂ laser** — Carbon‑dioxide laser used as the pump source for FIR lasers.
- **cpge measurement device** — NiI2 flake with gold electrodes prepared by field‑cooling and linear‑dichroism selection for CPGE studies.
- **crestec electron beam lithography system** — Electron beam lithography setup described in the procedure, including beamblanker, laser displacement meter and software.
- **crestec software** — Software used for pattern design, mask conversion and exposure control.
- **cross‑polarized imaging setup** — Imaging system with broadband LED, CMOS camera and Glan‑Thompson polarizers.
- **cryogenic atomic force microscope (afm)** — A low‑temperature AFM equipped with a conductive tip and quartz‑tuning‑fork sensor.
- **cryogenic atomic force microscope (afm)** — Low‑temperature AFM platform that integrates QTF sensor, lock‑in detection and MFM/EFM capabilities.
- **cryogenic nanopositioner** — Low‑temperature nanopositioning stage with reduced piezo capacitance and a built‑in capacitive position encoder.
- **cryogenic qtm microscope** — Quantum Twisting Microscope operating at cryogenic temperatures.
- **cryogenic qtm system** — Cryogenic QTM system operating at 4 K used for all measurements.
- **cryogenic quantum twisting microscope (cryo‑qtm)** — Cryogenic version of the Quantum Twisting Microscope used for THz experiments.
- **cryogenic quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — A custom‑built cryogenic atomic‑force‑microscope platform that forms a twistable vdW interface and enables elastic and inelastic momentum‑conserving tunnelling.
- **cryogenic stage (cryo)** — Low‑temperature environment used during QTM experiments.
- **cryosnom table** — The cryogenic SNOM (cSNOM) table on which the visible mirror is placed.
- **cryostat** — Cryostat connected to gas handling system via KF25/KF16 hoses.
- **cryostat** — Cryostat providing low‑temperature environment.
- **cubes** — Coarse mechanical manipulators used to move the sample towards the estimated centre of rotation.
- **custom built experimental setup** — Ambient‑temperature measurement rig that includes a Keithley SourceMeter, microscope, and detection line.
- **custom cryogenic atomic force microscope** — The mechanical core of the cryogenic QTM that allows formation of a twistable two‑dimensional interface between two vdW heterostructures.
- **custom rotating stage** — Rotating stage that holds vdW devices; includes goniometer, piezoelectric rotator, X/Y nanopositioners and a sample holder with electrical contacts.
- **custom translation stage** — Stage that provides lateral (XY) positioning of the tip and sample.
- **custom-built rotating stage** — Stage with goniometer, piezoelectric rotator, nanopositioners, and sample holder.
- **cylinder holder** — Cylinder that holds the sample, referenced as /H208496/H20850cylinder to hold the sample.
- **cylindrical housing** — Holder that secures the sample.
- **daisy app** — Application used to control the rotation stage.
- **db25-to-hd26 cable** — Proprietary preamp cable connecting NP-Drive to NP-Amp.
- **dc-in input** — BNC input on ANM300 accepting -10..+10 V.
- **dc‑in switch** — Switch to enable DC input (disabled in the UI).
- **desiccator** — Desiccator employed for storing graphene pieces and for bubble removal by heating at 55 °C under vacuum.
- **device name section** — Section of the Configuration screen used to label the device and its axes.
- **device page button** — Button on the application header that opens the Device Page.
- **device setup** — Overall procedure for setting up the device, including mounting, power, PC, network, and remote controller connections.
- **dewar isolation space pump** — Pump model used for the dewar isolation space vacuum.
- **di/dv measurement setup** — Instrumentation used to acquire differential conductance spectra as a function of bias voltage and filling.
- **diagnostic tools for positioner** — Software interface to measure positioner performance.
- **diagnostic tools page** — Page opened to access diagnostic tools (section 5.3.6).
- **dichroic output coupler** — Optical coupler that separates CO₂ and FIR beams for optimal system performance.
- **digital io** — Digital I/O interface.
- **dilution cryostat** — Cryogenic system providing a base temperature of 40 mK for low‑temperature transport experiments.
- **dilution refrigerator** — Cryogenic platform achieving millikelvin temperatures for experiments.
- **dilution refrigerator** — Dilution fridge being upgraded with two towers.
- **dimension3100 veeco afm** — Atomic force microscope used for spatial current mapping.
- **direct contact current‑voltage measurement** — Measurement setup where a conductive AFM tip makes direct electrical contact with a dot to record I‑V curves.
- **discarding the pairing connection** — Sub‑step that discards an existing pairing connection.
- **double‑tube scanner** — Piezoelectric scanner consisting of two concentric tubes.
- **dry scrolling pump** — Oil‑free pump option for evacuating gas‑laser systems.
- **dry vacuum pump (turbo/backing pump, min. 60 l/s)** — Vacuum pump used to evacuate the microscope vacuum tube.
- **dual‑gated graphene device** — Device with independent control of bottom and top graphene layer gates.
- **dut** — Device Under Test.
- **d‑sub hd 26‑pin connector** — D‑Sub HD 26‑Pin connector on the breakout panel.
- **easypll** — Phase‑locked loop that generates a DC voltage to achieve the chosen frequency‑shift set point.
- **easypll plus digital fm‑detector (nanosurf)** — Commercial PLL and frequency‑detector unit used in the experiments.
- **ebl raith (cleanroom)** — Electron‑beam lithography system (Raith) used in the cleanroom for patterning samples.
- **edinburgh firl‑100 source** — Far‑infrared/THz source for the OQTM.
- **electrical power outlets** — Mains and rotary current power outlets specified for the installation.
- **electrodes along [100]** — Pair of gold electrodes aligned with the ferroelectric polarization direction.
- **electrodes along [120]** — Pair of gold electrodes aligned with the propagation vector Qᵧ to detect odd‑parity spin photocurrent.
- **electronic control unit** — Electronic unit that drives attocube positioners and scanners.
- **elphyplus software** — Software interface for loading GDS‑II files, setting dose, and controlling the electron beam.
- **environmental compensation unit (ecu)** — Unit that compensates the refractive index of air using temperature, humidity and pressure.
- **esd safe environment** — Environment that prevents electrostatic discharge, used when handling modules.
- **establishing a cable connection (remote)** — Sub‑step that establishes a cable link between the remote controller and the device.
- **establishing a wireless connection** — Sub‑step that establishes a wireless link between the remote controller and the device.
- **establishing the cable connection** — Sub‑step that establishes a network cable connection.
- **ethernet** — Ethernet connection interface.
- **ethernet gblan interface** — Ethernet‑based remote interface using JSON‑RPC 2.0 protocol.
- **ethernet port** — Ethernet communication port on the ANC300.
- **ethernet up‑ and downlink sockets** — Ethernet ports on the device used for daisy‑chaining other devices.
- **evaporator controller** — Physical controller mounted on the left door of the evaporator used to switch between automatic and manual mode.
- **eware software** — Control software used to load samples, start venting, abort recipes and monitor deposition in the evaporator.
- **experiment dewar** — Cryogenic vessel housing the AFM head and related components.
- **experimental setup (section s1)** — Overall measurement rig described in the supporting information.
- **extended contact (area \omega_1 = l_x l_y)** — Coherent tunneling geometry central to the QTM, representing an extended graphene‑graphene contact.
- **extension cable for vacuum applications** — Extension cable used to lengthen connections in vacuum environments.
- **e‑beam computer** — Computer that runs the electron‑beam lithography software and stores GDSII files.
- **faraday cup** — Metallic cup used to collect the electron beam current during exposure.
- **fat32-formatted usb flash drive** — USB flash drive used to transfer network configuration files.
- **feature upgrade section** — Section of the Configuration screen for activating new features.
- **feedthrough mockup** — Mockup that enables the AAC350/NUM cable to be connected directly to a Sub‑D‑15 pin feed‑through.
- **femto dlpca‑200 current amplifier** — Amplifier that converts sample current to a voltage for the lock‑in.
- **fib‑sem crossbeam** — Dual‑beam instrument combining focused ion beam (FIB) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM).
- **filter setting** — Filter configuration for the stepping signal (disabled in the UI).
- **find devices screen** — Screen to locate devices and assign names to them.
- **finite cluster model (479 atoms)** — Finite atomic cluster employed in RPED calculations.
- **fir beam** — Far‑infrared beam generated by the FIRL100 system.
- **fir laser model 295** — Stand‑alone FIR laser designed for operation with high‑power CO₂ lasers, mounted in a 3 bar invar support frame.
- **fir laser model 395 twin laser** — Twin‑cavity FIR laser containing two identical FIR cavities in a single 5 bar invar stabilised frame.
- **firl 100 far infrared laser** — FIRL 100 far infrared laser instrument offered by Edinburgh Instruments.
- **firl100 laser system** — FIRL100 laser system from Edinburgh Instruments, includes CO2 and FIR beams, power supply, service panel, and optical units.
- **firl100 laser system** — The FIRL100 CO₂ laser system described in the safety manual, including its head, power supplies, gas handling, and cooling.
- **firl‑ps power supply** — One of the power supplies that can be used with the FIRL100 laser, with selectable AC voltage ranges.
- **firl‑qs** — A subsystem of the FIRL100 laser mentioned among the required accessories.
- **firmware update section** — Section of the Configuration screen for updating the device firmware.
- **fischer** — Device or connector named fischer.
- **fixed mounting plate** — Fixed mounting plate for the STM, referenced as /H208495/H20850fixed mounting plate.
- **fixed sample** — Stationary substrate holding the sample graphene layer.
- **fixed‑twist‑angle device** — Device fabricated with a predetermined twist angle between layers.
- **flake hunter** — Tool for locating and picking up 2D material flakes on substrates.
- **flange/bellow** — Flange and bellow components for vacuum/cryogenic lines.
- **flat mirror** — Flat mirror used for fine beam alignment before the iris.
- **flat‑side qtm device** — QTM device with a flat side used for tunnelling spectroscopy.
- **fm demodulator** — Electronic circuit that demodulates the frequency shift of the cantilever in FM detection.
- **fm detection system** — Frequency‑modulation detection scheme where the cantilever serves as a frequency‑deterring oscillator.
- **frequency modulation afm** — Dynamic AFM method that monitors frequency shift of an oscillating cantilever.
- **frequency modulation afm setup** — Instrument configuration that employs a quartz‑tuning‑fork sensor and a high‑Q cantilever for FM‑AFM.
- **front panel (anc300)** — Front panel of the ANC300 with display, knobs, and module slots.
- **front panel controls and indicators** — Front panel controls and indicator lights of the ANC300.
- **ftir‑qtom experiment** — FTIR‑based Quantum Twisting Microscope experiment performed on graphene‑hBN‑graphene heterostructures.
- **furnace** — High‑temperature furnace used for NiI2 growth.
- **gaas/(alga)as tunnel junction** — Semiconductor heterostructure used to study oscillatory structures in tunnelling.
- **gas handling system** — System (22 kg) that supplies CO₂ and FIR gases to the FIRL100.
- **gas injection system (gis)** — System that supplies precursor gases for ion or electron deposition.
- **gas mixing station** — System that mixes up to three component gases with individual needle‑valve control and flow meters.
- **gas-handling system** — Gas handling system with compressor and flexible hoses.
- **gate dielectric** — hBN layer that electrically isolates the gates from the graphene layers.
- **gblan ethernet connection** — Rear Ethernet port on the NP‑Drive for network communication, recommended to use a shielded cable.
- **generator** — Backup generator that starts in ~15 s.
- **gis nozzle** — Nozzle of the Gas Injection System that delivers precursor gas to the sample.
- **glass cover** — Glass cover used to seal the beakers while the chromium etchant acts on the graphene stack.
- **glass slide** — Glass slide used to mount the PDMS stamp.
- **goniometer** — Bottom component of the custom rotating stage.
- **gpio socket** — 37‑pin D‑Sub socket used for trigger and error signal communication.
- **gr/h‑bn/gr tunnel junction** — Vertical heterostructure consisting of graphene–h‑BN–graphene layers used for photon‑assisted tunneling experiments.
- **graphene tunneling device** — Device consisting of two graphene layers separated by a nanometer spacer, biased to allow electron tunneling.
- **graphene/hbn/au tunnel junction** — Vertical heterostructure where graphene is separated from Au by an hBN tunnel barrier.
- **graphene‑coated pyramid on a cantilever** — Part of the QTM where the tip is a graphene‑coated pyramid used to collect tunneling current.
- **graphene–quantum spin liquid–graphene junction** — The tunneling junction consisting of a top graphene layer, a magnetic quantum spin liquid barrier, and a bottom graphene layer.
- **green 532 nm laser** — 532 nm green laser used in Raman measurements.
- **green button** — Green button used to start a recipe run.
- **grounded wristband** — Wrist‑worn grounding device to avoid static charge buildup.
- **guiding rod** — Rod that is firmly connected to the piezoelectric actuator and drives the motion.
- **hall sensor** — Magnetic field sensor integrated in the rotator setup.
- **handset** — Handset component of the user interface.
- **hb‑1 polarized triple‑axis spectrometer (ptax)** — HB‑1 PTAX at the High Flux Isotope Reactor (HFIR), Oak Ridge National Laboratory.
- **he flex lines** — Helium flexible lines.
- **he laser** — Alternative laser source that shares the same optical path as the supercontinuum laser.
- **heater** — Heater set to 55 °C for short heating steps during the wet‑transfer protocol.
- **hemispherical tpc lens** — Hemispherical lens made of TPX used in the THz optical system.
- **hene laser** — Helium‑Neon laser used as an alternative light source.
- **hermetic feedthrough** — Hermetic feedthrough used to bring coaxial and D‑sub connections through a cryostat wall.
- **hexagonal macor (movable part)** — The movable macor element of the coarse Z motor that carries the capacitor electrode.
- **hexagonal piezo stack housing for coarse z motor** — Housing that contains the piezo stacks driving the coarse z motor.
- **high flux isotope reactor (hfir)** — Neutron source at Oak Ridge National Laboratory used for the experiments.
- **high voltage amplifier** — Amplifier that drives the channel when the relay is on.
- **high voltage drive circuit** — Circuit generating differential ±300 V sawtooth waveforms up to 4 kHz for piezoelectric actuators.
- **high-speed force sensor setup** — Setup utilizing quartz tuning fork for force microscopy
- **high‑q cantilever** — A cantilever with a quality factor Q≈50000 used in vacuum for atomic force microscopy.
- **hollow dielectric waveguide** — Low‑loss waveguide accessory that can be coupled to FIR lasers.
- **hollow waveguide** — A hollow waveguide used as a THz beam transport element.
- **hollow‑tube waveguide** — Hollow tube used as a multimode waveguide to bring the THz laser into the cryostat.
- **homemade afm system** — Custom atomic force microscope used for imaging and I‑V measurements.
- **homemade qtf holder** — Custom holder used to bend the gold wire and measure the Q of the quartz tuning fork.
- **homemade transimpedance amplifier** — Custom transimpedance amplifier that converts QTF current to voltage.
- **home‑built compact dilatometer** — Dilatometer mounted on ANRv220 for thermal expansion studies.
- **home‑built sample holder** — L‑shaped holder for up to four 5 mm × 5 mm samples.
- **horizontal single‑zone furnace** — Furnace used for physical vapour deposition of NiI₂.
- **hotplate** — Heated platform for baking and temperature‑controlled steps.
- **hssl cable** — Unidirectional cable used for the connection between AMC300 and IDS3010.
- **icfo evaporator (lab 18 – kurt. j. lesker)** — Vacuum deposition chamber used for electron‑beam and thermal evaporation of thin films.
- **ids sensor source** — Interferometric sensor head used for distance and displacement measurements.
- **ids webapp real‑time interface configuration** — Web application used to configure resolution, clock and other parameters.
- **ids3010 interferometric displacement sensor** — External contactless interferometric sensor used for position feedback in the AMC/IDS closed loop.
- **ids3010 interferometric distance sensor** — IDS3010 interferometric distance sensor, can be connected to the AMC300 controller.
- **iec power cord** — Power cord supplied with the AMC300, suitable for UK, Europe or USA.
- **iec60309 power cable with 3p+n+e 6h male connector** — Power cable delivered with the compressor, 32 A rating, 200–240/346–415 VAC, red housing.
- **iec60320 c13 power cable** — Connector used for power supply of the gas‑handling‑system pump.
- **iec60320 c19/20 power cable** — Power cable with IEC 60320 C19/20 connector for the device.
- **individual carbon nanotube transistor setup** — Experimental configuration using a single carbon nanotube transistor to excite plasmons.
- **insertable probe** — Probe mounted in the Leiden refrigerator that carries the rotator module.
- **integrated touch panel display** — Touch panel display integrated in the AMC300 device.
- **interface screen** — Screen in the webserver application where input mode and parameters are adjusted.
- **interferometric setup for resonance frequency measurements** — Interferometric setup used to measure resonance frequencies and stiffnesses of attocube positioners (Figure 31).
- **interlayer tunneling junction** — Device consisting of a tip layer and a sample layer that enables electron tunneling between them.
- **ion gauge (ig1)** — Gauge used to measure pressure in the main chamber.
- **ir beam** — Infrared beam used together with Laguerre‑Gauss analytics.
- **ir lens (znse)** — Infrared lens made of ZnSe mounted in the lens holder.
- **ir lens (znse)** — Infrared lens made of ZnSe.
- **iris** — Iris in the optical path that shapes the beam after alignment.
- **isolation transformer** — Transformer used for electrical isolation.
- **iwatsu controller** — IWATSU controller for the laser displacement meter.
- **kapton‑insulated twisted‑pair copper wiring** — Twisted‑pair copper wire (0.2 mm diameter) insulated with Kapton.
- **keithley 590 cv analyzer** — Instrument used to measure the capacitance at 100 kHz.
- **keithley sourcemeter** — Keithley SourceMeter with height 9 cm
- **kf40 flange** — Flange type that allows implementation of one Sub‑D feed‑through.
- **kf40 flange with subd‑15pin feedthrough** — KF40 flange equipped with a SubD‑15pin feedthrough; requires inner tube diameter ≥38 mm.
- **kf63 flange** — Flange type that can be equipped with three Sub‑D feed‑throughs.
- **l106 etching tool** — Etching equipment used for device processing.
- **laboratory space** — The laboratory area where the Attocube system will be placed, required to be free from vibration and electromagnetic disturbances.
- **labram hr evolution (horiba) confocal microscope spectrometer** — Confocal Raman spectrometer equipped with a 50× objective and 532 nm laser.
- **lan connection** — Network connection to the ANC300 controller via Ethernet.
- **lan port** — Ethernet port on the backside of the ANC300 used for network connection.
- **large signal board** — Circuit board variant for high‑amplitude Z‑motor signals.
- **laser** — Laser used for AFM deflection detection and for illumination during beam alignment.
- **laser displacement meter** — Laser displacement meter used to monitor the stage Z position and to approach zero displacement.
- **laser holder** — Holder for the laser beam, includes a 45° mirror and a focusing lens.
- **laser shutter** — Mechanical shutter to open/close laser beam.
- **laser system** — Laser system referenced in laser safety section.
- **laser with nd and od filters** — Laser whose power is reduced with neutral‑density (ND) and optical‑density (OD) filters.
- **lasers** — Laser sources for optical excitation.
- **leadless ceramic chip carrier (lccc)** — Sample holder with 20 contacts for wiring the sample.
- **left computer** — Computer that runs the ElphyPlus software and controls beam exposure.
- **leiden cf‑cs81‑600 dilution refrigerator** — Dilution refrigerator providing sub‑10 mK base temperature.
- **lens** — Optical lens employed in the QTM optical path.
- **lens holder** — Mechanical mount that can exchange an IR lens (ZnSe) and a THz lens (TPX/Si).
- **lens holder** — Holder that allows exchange between IR (ZnSe) and THz (TPX/Si) lenses.
- **lens simulation** — Simulation of silicon and TPX lenses using Zemax.
- **linear dichroism measurement setup** — Optical arrangement including supercontinuum source, PEM, objective, lock‑in detection for angular‑dependent linear dichroism.
- **linear stage** — Linear stage on top of the rotation stage used for fine positioning.
- **linear stage on top of the rotation stage (multifields tech)** — Linear translation stage used for fine positioning during rotation alignment.
- **linear x‑nanopositioner (anpx311/res/lt)** — Titanium x‑axis nanopositioner with linear ceramic bearings.
- **linear x‑nanopositioner (anpx51/res/lt)** — Titanium x‑axis nanopositioner with slip‑stick actuation and resistive encoder.
- **linear z‑nanopositioner (anpz101/res/lt)** — Titanium z‑axis nanopositioner with slip‑stick actuation.
- **linear z‑nanopositioner (anpz51/res/lt)** — Titanium z‑axis nanopositioner with slip‑stick actuation and resistive encoder.
- **lines** — Wiring lines connecting components inside the fridge.
- **liquid helium cryostat** — Cryogenic environment used for low‑temperature transport experiments.
- **litho computer (litho.icfo.es)** — Remote computer in the lithography room used to access the e‑beam software.
- **ll chamber** — Low‑level chamber that is vented, sealed, and pumped as part of the sample handling.
- **lmplanfl‑n 50× objective (olympus)** — Objective lens used to focus the ultrashort laser beam in SHG measurements.
- **load lock (ll)** — The load‑lock chamber used to introduce the sample into the sputtering system.
- **loading dock** — The dock area used for receiving the shipped Attocube system.
- **localarea_250** — Defined working area that contains the four e‑beam markers.
- **lock-in amplifier 7265** — Lock-in amplifier model 7265 used to detect the QTF signal and provide Fast Y output.
- **lock‑in** — Lock‑in amplifier for signal detection.
- **lock‑in amplifier** — Instrument used to integrate the QTF response around resonance and obtain rms voltage.
- **lock‑in amplifier (sr865a, stanford instruments)** — Detects second harmonic of PEM frequency for dichroism signal.
- **long l arm** — Long L‑shaped arm holding the lens below the rotating stage.
- **low pass filter** — Low‑pass filter used in bias measurements.
- **lua console** — Programmable Lua console accessed via Telnet on port 7231.
- **lua console** — Interactive Lua scripting console provided by the Attocube controller for advanced programming.
- **magic button** — UI button used to define the origin position for writing.
- **magnet** — Magnet used in cQTM experiments.
- **magnet power supply** — 230 V, 1‑phase power supply rated at 3 kVA for the magnet.
- **magnet supply** — Power supply for the magnet.
- **magnetic property measurement system (mpms‑3)** — Instrument for magnetic susceptibility measurements under field cooling.
- **main chamber (mc)** — The main deposition chamber where sputtering takes place.
- **main cpu** — Main CPU located behind the touch panel, main user interface.
- **main page** — Main page of the attoDISCOVERY WebApp where axis mode can be toggled.
- **main screen** — Primary user interface screen of the ANC300.
- **main switch** — Main power switch of the plasma asher.
- **marker sleeve tms‑sce‑3/8‑2.0‑4 te** — Marker sleeve component of the adapter.
- **mass spectrometer leak detector** — Instrument optionally used to verify vacuum space integrity.
- **mb ruler** — A graphical ruler used on the AFM screen to draw circles and measure distances.
- **mde266-v-lm dual axis micropositioner** — Dual axis micropositioner with XY linear translation, smooth backlash-free motion, small dovetail slides, vacuum compatible low outgassing grease, and low-magnetic materials for high magnetic field applications.
- **measurement circuit** — Circuit applying bias Vb between vdW layers and measuring current I.
- **measurement line** — Electrical lines requiring filtering and thermalisation.
- **measurement rack** — Rack that holds measurement electronics.
- **mechanical rotator** — Conventional rotator that suffers from mechanical hysteresis.
- **mesfet** — Metal–semiconductor field‑effect transistor forming the input stage of the pre‑amplifier.
- **metal transfer stick** — Vertical metal rod used to move the sample holder in and out of the chamber.
- **micro positioner** — Micro positioner used to adjust sample height and to minimise the spot size.
- **micromanipulator** — Device that lowers the Pt/Ir wire into the etchant with millimetre precision.
- **micrometer platform** — Movable platform equipped with micrometers that holds the powermeter and slit.
- **micrometric screws for lens alignment** — Proposed fine‑adjustment mechanism for the focusing lens.
- **microscope** — Optical microscope used to view and position samples.
- **microscope control section** — Part of the control software where writing fields are selected.
- **microscope image button (top left)** — UI button that opens the optical image of the sample.
- **microscope pocket** — The pocket that holds the PCB and defines the mechanical envelope of the QTM.
- **microscope system** — The main microscope instrument that requires AC power and specific site conditions.
- **microscope system** — Microscope system requiring AC power and space.
- **middle computer** — Computer used to copy CAD design files to the shared data folder.
- **mid‑infrared quantum cascade laser (mir qcl)** — Mid‑infrared QCL employed for spectroscopy on the L110 platform.
- **millikelvin scanning microscope** — A millikelvin‑temperature scanning microscope built for the measurements.
- **mir module** — Generates 4–20 µm radiation with decreasing power; lacks a power meter.
- **mlg tip tunneling spectroscopy setup** — Experimental configuration where a monolayer‑graphene tip tunnels into MA TBG while the twist angle is varied.
- **mlg‑mlg tunneling junction** — Vertical tunneling device formed by two monolayer‑graphene electrodes.
- **mlg‑tbg tunneling junction** — Vertical tunneling device formed by a monolayer‑graphene electrode and a twisted‑bilayer‑graphene electrode.
- **mlg–tbg tunnel junction** — Vertical tunneling device consisting of a monolayer graphene tip and a twisted bilayer graphene sample.
- **mn l₂,₃‑edge resonance** — Resonant photon energy used for RPED calculations on MnTe.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling setup** — Experimental rig for performing momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy between parallel 2D layers.
- **monochromator** — Spectral selector (2 nm resolution) used in SHG detection.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg) tip** — A scanning tip made of monolayer graphene used for tunnelling spectroscopy.
- **monolayer graphene tip scanning setup** — Experimental configuration where a monolayer graphene tip scans the electronic structure of a sample via tunneling spectroscopy.
- **monolayer‑graphene qtm tip** — Quantum Twisting Microscope tip made of a monolayer graphene sheet.
- **motion controller (amc300/rack/3/res)** — Rack‑mounted controller for closed‑loop control of up to three positioners.
- **motion controller (amc300/rack/6/res)** — Rack‑mounted controller for closed‑loop control of up to six positioners.
- **moulded part 382a012‑25/225‑0 te** — Moulded plastic part used in the adapter assembly.
- **mounting the rack and double rack device** — Step that describes how to mount the rack and the double‑rack device.
- **movable breadboard** — Compact platform for auxiliary optics such as parabolic mirrors and a visible‑light imaging path.
- **nanonis tramea™** — All‑in‑one acquisition electronics used in the attocube measurement platform.
- **nanopositioner** — Nanopositioner used in the instrument.
- **nanopositioning toolbox** — A toolbox that supplies the screws and mounting hardware for the attocube positioners.
- **nanoscribe** — 3‑D printing tool used to fabricate cone‑shaped tips.
- **nano‑rotator setup** — Rotator assembly designed to fit on a 25 mm diameter sample carrier.
- **navigation screen** — User interface screen used to start, stop and configure measurements.
- **network cable** — Bidirectional Ethernet cable connecting the AMC300 to the IDS3010.
- **networking section** — Section of the Configuration screen where network parameters are set.
- **neutral density filter** — Variable attenuation filter (0–4 OOM).
- **new stm design** — Optimized STM design featuring modified resonance frequencies.
- **new stm design** — A compromise STM design that keeps a conical sample holder while allowing space for bias voltage contact.
- **newport control system** — Control hardware (IP 192.168.254.254) for the delay stage.
- **newport delay stage** — Delay stage from Newport controlling the optical path length.
- **newport delay stage** — Motorised stage that controls optical delay.
- **nfl plasma asher** — Plasma asher instrument used in the described procedure.
- **nii2 cpge device** — Device fabricated from NiI2 to study the circular photogalvanic effect.
- **nii2 cpge device** — Device with transferred gold contacts for circular photogalvanic effect measurements.
- **nii2 device** — Device incorporating NiI2 crystal for photocurrent measurements.
- **nitrogen‑filled flight tube** — Nitrogen‑filled tube used to minimise atmospheric moisture absorption for long beam paths.
- **noe cabinet** — Cabinet where the chemicals are kept.
- **np cryogenic nanopositioner** — Cryogenic nanopositioner with a piezo‑drive connector and two position‑sensor connectors.
- **np-amp model c100** — Model C100 of the NP-AMP nanopositioner controller.
- **np-amp preamp connections** — Preamp connections for the NP‑Amp.
- **np-amp preamplifier** — High‑performance capacitive sensor preamplifier compatible with Model B100.
- **np-drive controller** — Controller for nanopositioner with touchscreen interface and channel configuration.
- **np-drive model a100** — Model A100 of the NP-Drive nanopositioner controller.
- **np-drive model b100** — Model B100 of the NP-Drive nanopositioner controller.
- **np-drive nanopositioner controller** — Nanopositioner controller described in the UM100 manual, supporting models A100, B100, and C100.
- **np-drive um100** — Remote Interface NP-Drive UM100 used to control sensors and motion channels.
- **np-drive um100 touchscreen user interface** — Touchscreen interface for controlling the NP‑Drive UM100 device.
- **np‑145‑x nanopositioner (coarse)** — Coarse lateral nanopositioner on the sample tower for navigation to regions of interest.
- **np‑145‑x renaissance stepper motor** — Nanopositioner used in the three‑axis positioning stack of the tip tower.
- **np‑145‑z stepper motor** — Nanopositioner providing Z‑axis motion in the tip tower.
- **np‑amp preamp** — Pre‑amplifier whose internal circuitry can be multiplexed to the position sensor.
- **nsl cable** — NSL cable included with AMC300 for connection to ASC500.
- **off‑axis parabolic mirrors** — Two off‑axis parabolic mirrors that form the telescope.
- **opa602 operational amplifier stage** — Second stage of the transimpedance amplifier using an OPA602 op‑amp.
- **operation screen** — Screen used for activating/deactivating axes and configuring positioner type.
- **operation view** — Default view showing axis parameters, status, and controls for positioning and diagnostics.
- **operational amplifier** — Amplifier that provides gain and defines the feedback network in the QTF circuit.
- **operator panel (double rack version)** — Operator panel designed for double‑rack configuration of the instrument.
- **operator panel (rack version)** — Operator panel designed for rack‑mount version of the instrument.
- **operator panel (table top version)** — Operator panel designed for tabletop version of the instrument.
- **optical chopper** — Mechanical chopper driven by the Zurich MFLI reference output.
- **optical density filter** — Fixed attenuation filter (~2 OOM) used during alignment.
- **optical encoder** — Encoder that enables closed‑loop angular control on the piezoelectric rotator.
- **optical quantum twisting microscope (oqtm)** — Cryogenic AFM‑based platform that performs in‑situ perpendicular transport, momentum‑ and energy‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy and photocurrent measurements on vdW heterostructures.
- **optical setup** — Experimental arrangement for collecting emitted photons (~700 nm).
- **optical table** — Vibration‑isolated table on which the THz‑laser coupling optics are mounted.
- **opticool cryomagnet (quantum design)** — Cryomagnet used in the work.
- **opticool cryostat (quantum design)** — Cryostat used for transport and photocurrent measurements.
- **optimized stm design** — A redesign of the STM where the sample holder is conical to raise resonance frequency.
- **original stm design** — The initial scanning tunneling microscope design with cylindrical sample holder.
- **original stm design** — Baseline design of the STM used for comparison with optimized designs.
- **orion 8 sputtering (clean room)** — The Orion 8 sputtering system used in the clean‑room for thin‑film deposition.
- **oscilloscope** — A measurement instrument suggested for connecting to the ANC300 outputs to observe voltage waveforms.
- **page composition** — Structure of pages within the Touch Panel Application.
- **pairing remote controller and device** — Sub‑step that pairs the remote controller with the device.
- **pallet trucks** — Equipment available to move the packed Attocube system.
- **pan‑design coarse‑motion mechanism** — Alternative coarse‑motion mechanism considered in the model.
- **parabolic mirror** — Large‑focus mirror used to direct light onto the sample inside the cryostat.
- **parabolic mirrors** — Focusing mirrors mounted on the movable breadboard.
- **parallel incoherent local tunneling contacts** — Reference geometry consisting of \Omega_1/\lambda_F^2 independent local contacts used for comparison with the extended contact.
- **pc** — Personal computers for control and data acquisition.
- **pcb 1** — Printed circuit board 1 used in the assembly, includes SEI-110-02-G-S-AB component and pico‑coax wiring.
- **pcb 2** — Printed circuit board 2 used in the assembly, includes PCX 42 K 10 AK component.
- **pcb 3** — Printed circuit board 3 used in the assembly, includes SEI-105-02-G-S-AB, BM24-10DP/2-0.35V(51) components and pico‑coax wiring.
- **pcb_wings_sample** — Connects to PCB Sample through spring connectors; the 10 pico‑coax lines from pico_end are soldered onto it.
- **pd‑30s controller** — Controller that operates the vacuum pump and other chamber functions.
- **periscope** — Optical periscope that connects the two tables and carries the VIS beam.
- **photocurrent measurement setup** — Experimental rig comprising contacts, a quarter‑wave plate, and electronics for I_total(φ) detection.
- **photoelastic modulator (pem‑200, hinds instruments)** — Modulator that switches linear polarization between ±45°.
- **photomultiplier tube (hamamatsu)** — Detector for second‑harmonic photons.
- **pico‑coax** — Pico‑coaxial cable used for high‑frequency connections.
- **piezo actuator** — Piezoelectric element used for sample rotation and actuation.
- **piezo controllers** — Actuators that move the parabolic mirror during backreflection alignment.
- **piezo drive output connector** — Connector for the piezo drive output.
- **piezo translators and controllers** — High‑voltage piezoelectric translators and controllers used in the ANC300.
- **piezo tube scanner** — Piezoelectric tube used for fine XY scanning of the sample.
- **piezoelectric rotator** — Device mounted on the AFM stage that rotates the bottom sample with sub‑degree precision.
- **pl5 co₂ laser** — CO₂ laser used as a pump source for FIR lasers; based on a diffraction grating and ZnSe optics.
- **pl6 co₂ laser** — Higher‑power CO₂ laser used as a pump source for FIR lasers.
- **planar junction between monolayer graphene tip and moiré graphene sample** — Elastic momentum‑conserving tunneling geometry used in Dirac‑point spectroscopy.
- **planar tunnel junction** — Finite‑area junction used in the QTM where energy and in‑plane momentum are conserved.
- **plasma etcher (graphene lab)** — Plasma etcher used to remove the bottom graphene layer with power 2.5, pressure 0.5 and a 3 min exposure.
- **platinum pyramid (custom‑made by fib)** — Custom‑made platinum pyramid deposited on the AFM cantilever tip.
- **position encoder** — Resistive sensor that reads out the angular position.
- **positioner body** — The main mechanical housing of the rotator.
- **positioner control cable for room temperature applications** — Cable that links the positioner controller to the instrument for room‑temperature operation.
- **positioner control cable for vacuum applications** — Cable that links the positioner controller to the instrument for vacuum operation.
- **positioner with resistive readout** — A positioner device that uses resistive sensors for position feedback, connected via the attocube vacuum extension cable.
- **positioning cubes** — Mechanical cubes used to coarse‑move the sample toward the estimated centre of rotation.
- **positioning unit with up to six degrees of freedom** — Composite device built by stacking attocube stages to obtain multi‑axis motion.
- **power cable for compressor** — Cable with a 3P+N+E, 6h male connector rated 32 A for the compressor.
- **power meter (1060 nm calibrated)** — Device used to monitor laser power, calibrated at 1060 nm.
- **power meter (p‑meter)** — Device used to monitor laser power on the bench.
- **power meter screen** — Display component of a power meter.
- **power supply** — Power supply connected via an umbilical to the laser head.
- **power_dependence.py script** — Python script that performs power‑dependent dI/dV sweeps.
- **preamp output adapter pcb** — PCB that plugs into DB9 connector on NP-Amp and provides connections for sensor drive.
- **precision linear translation stage** — Motorised stage that adjusts cavity length of each FIR section in model‑395.
- **pressure gauge controller** — Controller that reads the ion‑gauge pressure in the chambers.
- **profilometer** — Instrument to measure resist thickness.
- **ps4p power supply** — Another power supply option for the FIRL100 laser, with similar voltage and current specifications.
- **pulse tube** — Pulse‑tube cryocooler.
- **pulse tube compressor** — 3‑phase pulse tube compressor delivering up to 10 kW input power.
- **pump of gas handling system** — Pump powered via an IEC60320 C13 connector.
- **pumping port** — Vacuum connection used to evacuate the dewar.
- **purging box** — Sealed enclosure filled with dry gas to minimise THz absorption by air humidity.
- **pyroelectric current measurement** — Setup that records pyroelectric current to extract ferroelectric polarization in NiI2.
- **qdevil 1** — QDevil 1 instrument occupying 18 cm of rack height
- **qdevil 2** — QDevil 2 (does not occupy internal space) with height 18 cm
- **qtf force sensor setup** — Experimental rig comprising the quartz tuning fork, scanner, oven, and associated electronics.
- **qtm contact** — Quantum Twisting Microscope contact configuration with tip and sample.
- **qtm junction** — The tunnelling junction of the Quantum Twisting Microscope whose electrostatics are controlled by top gate, bottom gate and bias voltages.
- **qtm junction** — Vertical heterostructure where tunnelling occurs between top and bottom graphene layers.
- **qtm junction with top and bottom gates** — QTM device equipped with electrostatic gates to form a quantum‑dot spectroscopy platform.
- **qtm stage assembly** — Custom assembly for the Quantum Twisting Microscope that provides tilt compensation, translation stages and rotation capability.
- **qtm tip** — Metallic tip of the Quantum Twisting Microscope that defines the tunnelling area Ω and measures inelastic conductance steps.
- **qtm tunnel junction** — The tunnel junction of the Quantum Twisting Microscope used for differential conductance measurements.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — Instrument that enables differential conductance measurements G(V,θ) as a function of bias V and twist angle θ.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — Instrument used for THz and FTIR measurements on 2D heterostructures.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — Instrument for twisting 2D material layers and performing measurements.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — Instrument used for momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy on twisted materials.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — Instrument used to visualize electrostatic potential and perform defect‑assisted tunneling measurements.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm)** — The QTM instrument referenced in the ERC‑Adg grant.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm) tip** — Scanning tip that provides tunnelling spectroscopy with high spatial resolution.
- **quantum twisting optical microscope (qtom)** — Optical variant of QTM used for FTIR experiments.
- **quartz crystal monitor** — Two quartz crystals per source plus one at the sample holder that measure film thickness during evaporation.
- **quartz tube** — 0.5‑inch‑diameter tube inside the furnace.
- **quartz tube** — Tube inside furnace where growth takes place.
- **quartz tuning fork (qtf)** — Resonator whose resonance frequency shifts under electrostatic or atomic forces.
- **quartz tuning fork (qtf) force sensor** — Force sensor based on a quartz tuning fork that detects force gradient changes during scanning.
- **quartz tuning fork (qtf) with tip** — Force sensor formed by a quartz tuning fork carrying the scanning tip.
- **raith ebeam** — E‑beam system behind which the graphene pieces are kept in a desiccator.
- **ramp** — Structure that holds either the sample or the scan piezo depending on design.
