- **real time input tile** — Tile on the Interface screen to set parameters for incoming position commands.
- **real time output tile** — Tile on the Interface screen to set parameters for outgoing position commands.
- **red 785 nm laser** — 785 nm red laser used in Raman measurements.
- **red button** — Red button used to stop the process.
- **reference tunnel junction (tmd‑free)** — Identical graphene/hBN/Au stack without a TMD layer, used for comparison.
- **reinshaw raman spectroscopy** — Raman spectroscopy instrument setup used in the lab.
- **remote computer \\pc0676** — Computer used to control the Raman system remotely.
- **remote controller** — Handheld device paired with the positioner for movement commands.
- **renaissance motor** — High‑precision motor used for rotational control in the QTM.
- **renaissance scientific np cryogenic nanopositioners** — Cryogenic nanopositioners from Renaissance Scientific compatible with NP-Drive.
- **res encoder** — Encoder providing high‑resolution position feedback for ANRv51.
- **resonant photoelectron diffraction (rped) setup** — Experimental rig used for RPED measurements.
- **resonant photoemission spectroscopy (rpes) setup** — Experimental rig used for RPES measurements.
- **rhk control unit** — Control electronics used to operate the EFM measurement.
- **rhk measurement electronics** — Instrument that records current while sweeping voltage during I‑V measurements.
- **rhk scanner** — Scanning unit that receives Fast Y signal to generate MFM images.
- **rie (bosch process)** — Reactive ion etcher using C4F8 passivation and SF6 etching.
- **rie in clean room** — Reactive‑ion etcher used for graphene patterning with 20 W power and a 20 min pre‑conditioning step.
- **right computer** — Computer that controls chamber venting, door operation and sample holder mounting.
- **room t** — Experimental station where the QTM and associated equipment are installed.
- **room temperature quantum twisting optical microscope (rt-qtom)** — Instrument for measuring van der Waals stacks optoelectronic properties with spatial and twist degrees of freedom.
- **room‑temperature quantum twisting microscope (rt‑qtm)** — Non‑cryogenic version of the QTM used for training and preliminary tests.
- **room‑temperature quantum twisting microscope (rt‑qtm)** — Room‑temperature version of the QTM used for training.
- **rotary valve** — Rotary valve for gas handling.
- **rotating stage** — Rotating stage on which the sample is mounted for center‑of‑rotation alignment.
- **rotating stage with long‑l arm** — Rotating stage used to rotate the sample; the long‑L arm holds the lens during alignment.
- **rotation stage** — Motorised stage that rotates the sample; controlled open‑loop.
- **rotator** — Home‑built rotation stage for controlling twist angle.
- **rotator** — Rotating stage with side holes to couple light without obstructing the tip.
- **rotator (anr240/res/lt) 360° endless** — Rotator with vertical axis, made from titanium and CuBe, featuring a 315° resistive encoder.
- **rs232 port** — Serial RS232 port on the backside of the ANC300 for PC connection.
- **rt‑qtom instrument** — Complete optical‑electrical microscope integrating sample stage, tip, lasers and lock‑in detection.
- **sample** — The sample mounted on the rotating stage for measurements.
- **sample handling system** — The overall instrument and procedure used to manipulate and transfer samples, control gas, rotation, and various mechanical components.
- **sample holder** — Holder with opening and sapphire substrate for the sample.
- **sample space pump** — Pump model used to achieve the required sample space vacuum pressure.
- **sample tube/insert** — Sample holder tube or insert placed inside the cryostat.
- **scan all** — Menu command that scans the whole sample for markers.
- **scan manager field** — Software module that guides the alignment‑field procedure.
- **scan piezo** — Piezoelectric element that moves the STM tip for fine scanning.
- **scanning electron microscope (sem)** — Electron microscope used together with FIB for coincidence measurements.
- **scanning module (anm200)** — Plug‑in module for the open‑loop ANC300 controller, enabling scan voltages up to 150 V.
- **scanning set‑up** — Experimental arrangement used to move the tip across the bottom graphene sample and record spatially resolved spectra.
- **scanning single‑electron‑transistor** — Local probe used for compressibility measurements.
- **scanning squid** — Local probe used to image magnetic textures such as Chern mosaics.
- **scanning tunneling microscope** — Instrument that images surfaces via quantum tunnelling.
- **scanning tunneling microscope (stm)** — Instrument that measures tunneling current to image surface local density of states.
- **scanning tunneling microscope (stm)** — Instrument that measures tunneling current between a metallic tip and a sample surface with atomic resolution.
- **scanning tunneling microscope (stm)** — Instrument used to acquire dynamic STM images.
- **scanning tunneling microscope tip** — Metallic tip used to inject electrons and measure differential conductance in the TBG sample.
- **scanning tunneling microscopy (stm)** — Local probe that measures atomic‑scale tunneling current.
- **schwarzschild objective** — Diffraction‑limited objective covering the visible to THz spectral range.
- **sdc** — Device or connector named SDC.
- **sdc** — Experimental setup referenced as the destination for Fischer connectors.
- **second cpge device** — Device comprising the NiI2 crystal, electrodes, and additional electrode pair for scanning.
- **secure access over https section** — Section of the Configuration screen that enables HTTPS access.
- **separation surface** — Chosen surface within the barrier separating tip and sample for Bardeen analysis.
- **short‑path filter** — Filter that blocks wavelengths above 900 nm.
- **sigma software** — Software that displays thickness of the deposited layer and other sensor readings.
- **silicon wafer mount** — Mount that holds the cantilever upright during the transfer process.
- **silicon-based detector** — Photodetector with ~1.3 eV detection threshold.
- **slc twin series** — Series of Salicru SAI devices up to 3 kVA.
- **small signal board** — Circuit board variant for low‑amplitude Z‑motor signals.
- **small ups (lab)** — Small UPS dedicated to the cQTM laboratory.
- **smp male pcb mt connector** — Proprietary connector specified by SV Microwave with various material components.
- **software v614r1z** — Software version v614r1z running on the desktop.
- **sonicator bath** — Bath sonicator employed to clean glass slides with acetone and ISO for 5 min.
- **source** — Signal or radiation source.
- **source follower stage** — First stage of the transimpedance amplifier using GaAs MESFETs.
- **spacer 25 mm** — 25 mm thick spacer used to set component separations on the bench.
- **spin coater** — Equipment for spin‑coating polymers.
- **split-gate stack** — Device architecture consisting of split gates.
- **sps series** — Series of Salicru SAI devices up to 3 kVA.
- **sqs‑242 software** — Software that loads and runs deposition recipes (e.g., Cr_process_EB, Au_process_TH).
- **sr lockin** — SR lockin with height 13.5 cm
- **sr400 gated photon counter (stanford research)** — Dual‑channel counter for SHG photon counting.
- **stage** — Mechanical stage that moves the chip to different positions.
- **standard cabling** — Standard lengths of cabling (2 m outside, 0.5 m inside) made of woven Kapton flatband.
- **standard console** — Simple text‑based console accessed via Telnet or a virtual COM port.
- **standard sample carrier** — 25 mm diameter carrier holding the sample and Hall sensors.
- **steering mirrors** — Two mirrors that direct the CO₂ pump beam into the FIR resonator of FIRL100.
- **step generator** — Electronic block that generates stepping pulses before amplification to the final output voltage.
- **stm design model 1 (atto‑cube coarse motor)** — Design concept using an atto‑cube coarse motor as the coarse approach mechanism.
- **stm design model 2 (beetle‑type with moving sample)** — Beetle‑type STM where the sample is moved for coarse approach.
- **stm design model 3 (beetle‑type with moving tip)** — Beetle‑type STM where the tip is moved for coarse approach.
- **stm model 1** — First STM model with masses m1=15 g, m2=2 g, spring constants k1=200 N/m, k2=600 N/m, and additional parameters as listed in Table I.
- **stm model 2** — Second STM model with masses m1=20 g, m2=1.8 g, spring constants k1=79.2 N/m, k2=26.4 N/m, and other parameters as listed in Table I.
- **stm model 3** — Third STM model with masses m1=20 g, m2=1.8 g, spring constants k1=79.2 N/m, k2=26.4 N/m, and additional parameters as listed in Table I.
- **stm model 1** — Design that uses an attocube coarse motor and a stationary tip.
- **stm model 2** — Beetle‑type STM where the sample moves.
- **stm model 3** — Beetle‑type STM where the tip moves.
- **stm setup** — Instrument comprising tip, sample, and associated electronics for scanning tunneling microscopy.
- **stm tip** — Scanning tunneling microscope tip used as the tunneling electrode.
- **stronger dry vacuum pump (turbo/backing pump, < 1e‑6 mbar)** — Higher‑performance pump to speed up the initial pumping process.
- **stuttgart alpha hp mir laser** — Mid‑infrared laser that can be switched into the beam path.
- **stuttgart instruments alpha system** — Mid‑infrared laser source for the OQTM.
- **stuttgart laser alpha hp mir** — Mid‑infrared laser that can be switched into the optical path.
- **st‑500 cryostat (janis)** — Cryogenic environment for SHG measurements.
- **sub‑d feed‑through** — Sub‑D connector feed‑through that can be installed on KF40 or KF63 flanges.
- **superconducting magnet** — Magnet providing intense magnetic fields; the AFM head fits into its 38 mm bore.
- **superconducting vector magnet** — Conventional vector magnet limited to 1–2 T in most directions, contrasted with the atto3DR solution.
- **supercontinuum laser (superk compact)** — Broadband laser delivering 2 ns pulses at 20 kHz repetition.
- **supercontinuum light source (superk, nkt photonics)** — Broadband source monochromated to 633 nm for linear dichroism.
- **superk compact laser (2 ns pulse, 20 khz)** — Super‑continuum laser used as primary light source.
- **superk compact supercontinuum laser** — Provides 2 ns pulses at 20 kHz repetition rate for THz experiments.
- **superk supercontinuum laser (nkt photonics)** — Broadband laser monochromatized to ~1 nm bandwidth for photocurrent spectroscopy.
- **süss mjb4 mask aligner** — Mask aligner used for manual stacking of the graphene/h‑BN/graphene stack.
- **teflon isolator** — Teflon isolator for electrical isolation.
- **telescope (two coupled parabolic mirrors)** — Telescope built from two off‑axis parabolic mirrors to collimate and reduce the THz beam.
- **tepla** — Plasma tool used for O2 cleaning.
- **thorlab piezos** — Piezoelectric elements incorporated in the home‑built rotator.
- **thorlabs visible laser** — Primary visible‑range laser source for the OQTM.
- **thz beam** — Terahertz beam whose propagation is simulated.
- **thz gas laser** — THz source whose beam is aligned through the cryoSNOM system.
- **thz lens (tpx)** — Terahertz lens made of TPX mounted in the lens holder.
- **thz lens (tpx)** — Terahertz lens made of TPX (or Si).
- **thz qtm** — THz Quantum Twisting Microscope used for cryogenic measurements.
- **tip (stm tip)** — Metallic tip modeled as a non‑interacting electron gas that tunnels into the TBG sample.
- **tip assembly** — AFM tip holder offering ten electrical connections, bias/gate lines and a four‑wire bridge for force detection.
- **tip e** — The scanning tip labeled E used in the measurements.
- **tip equation of motion** — Mathematical model describing the dynamics of the AFM tip.
- **tip etching setup (fig. 2.9)** — Cell containing the Pt/Ir wire, metal clip, graphite rod, and micromanipulator for etching.
- **tip holder (tuna)** — Holder that secures the gold‑coated tip for AFM measurements.
- **tip-sample tunneling spectroscopy setup** — Experimental arrangement for measuring tunneling current between tip and sample.
- **tipless afm cantilever** — AFM cantilever used as the mechanical support for the QTM tip.
- **tip‑less cantilever** — Cantilever without tip used as substrate for metal evaporation.
- **tip‑sample tunneling junction** — Experimental configuration where a graphene tip tunnels into a 2D sample.
- **tip‑sample tunneling junction** — Combined tip and sample region where tunneling occurs.
- **tip‑stamp holder** — Holder that secures the tip and stamp during fabrication.
- **titanium waveguide** — ≈1 m hollow cylindrical waveguide delivering long‑wavelength (up to 2.5 THz) excitation to the tip‑sample junction.
- **tmd/graphene/hbn/au tunnel junction** — Device consisting of a graphene electrode, hBN barrier, Au electrode, with a TMD monolayer placed on top of graphene.
- **top gate (tg)** — Gate electrode above the tip layer, separated by hBN.
- **top xy translation stages (xeryon xls)** — Fine XY stages mounted above the rotation stage to locate the flat sample relative to the curved sample.
- **touch panel** — Touch‑screen hardware that interacts with the attoDISCOVERY WebApp.
- **touch panel application** — Application on the device for adjusting network settings and resetting to factory defaults.
- **touchscreen** — User interface on the NP‑Drive used to change settings such as the Auto‑Ground Selector.
- **touchscreen user interface** — Touchscreen‑based user interface.
- **transfer stage** — Stage used for flake transfer, accessed through collaborators.
- **transimpedance amplifier** — Low‑noise amplifier used to convert the tip current into a voltage signal.
- **transmission electron microscope (fei)** — TEM used for electron diffraction of NiI2 flakes.
- **trigger port** — Trigger port for external signal input.
- **ttl trigger input** — TTL Trigger Input initiates a waveform cycle per rising edge, using previously set parameters.
- **tubing dr‑25‑1/2‑0 te** — Half‑inch tubing component of the adapter.
- **tubing dr‑25‑1/4‑0 te** — Quarter‑inch tubing component of the adapter.
- **tubing rnf‑100‑3/8‑x‑sp te** — Eight‑mm tubing component of the adapter.
- **tubing rt375‑3/64‑x‑stk te** — Special‑size tubing component of the adapter.
- **tuna holder** — Holder that secures gold‑coated tips for AFM measurements.
- **tunneling junction** — Physical setup consisting of tip, sample, and barrier where tunneling occurs.
- **turbo molecular pump** — High‑vacuum pump used to evacuate gas‑laser systems.
- **turbo pump** — High‑speed pump whose speed (≈1500 Hz) indicates low pressure.
- **twistable junction** — The region where the two graphene layers overlap and can be rotated.
- **twisted graphene‑graphene junction** — Junction formed by two graphene layers with a relative twist angle.
- **twist‑controlled gr/h‑bn/gr tunnel junction** — Experimental platform used to study photon‑assisted inelastic electron tunneling and resonant light emission.
- **two-pin piezo drive connector** — Reversible connector for piezo drive signals.
- **two‑photon polymerization (2pp) printer** — Printer used to fabricate pyramidal structures for tip functionalization.
- **two‑position sliding mirror mechanism** — Precision sliding mirror that provides access to CO₂ beam diagnostics in FIRL100.
- **uhv compatible cabling for positioners with num encoder** — UHV compatible cabling for positioners equipped with a NUM encoder.
- **uhv compatible cabling for positioners with res encoder** — UHV compatible cabling for positioners equipped with a RES encoder.
- **uninterruptable power supply (ups)** — UPS recommended for locations where mains power may be disrupted.
- **uninterruptible power supply (sai)** — Backup power device sold by Salicru.
- **usb** — USB connection interface.
- **usb connection** — USB interface used to control the ANC300.
- **usb connector** — Front‑panel USB port used to install system updates on the NP‑Drive.
- **usb master port** — USB master port on the ANC300 used to connect slave devices such as USB sticks.
- **usb port** — USB communication port on the ANC300.
- **usb slave port** — USB slave device port on the backside of the ANC300 that creates a virtual COM port when connected to a PC.
- **usb stick** — USB storage device formatted as FAT16 or FAT32 used to transfer update files.
- **uv display** — Visualization tool to preview what will be written before exposure.
- **uv display option** — Software option to display UV coordinates of the current position.
- **vacuum chamber** — Enclosure that houses the suspended AFM head and provides isolation from air.
- **vacuum equipment** — Vacuum pumps and related hardware required for the Attocube system.
- **vacuum feed‑through (vft)** — General term for the vacuum‑compatible electrical feed‑throughs used with various flange types.
- **vacuum hot plate (besides orion sputtering machine)** — Hot plate used to dry the transferred graphene on the target substrate at 55 °C under vacuum.
- **vacuum pump** — Pump that evacuates the load‑lock and main chamber.
- **van‑der‑waals tip device on afm cantilever** — Specially designed pyramid at the edge of an AFM cantilever that hosts the active vdW layers forming the QTM tip.
- **vector magnet** — Magnet capable of providing magnetic field in an arbitrary direction.
- **veeco dimension 3100 scanning probe microscope** — The AFM instrument used for tapping‑mode measurements.
- **veeco otr8‑35 afm tip** — Contact‑mode cantilever tip with stiffness 0.15 N/m, the longest cantilever available.
- **vertical metal axis** — Adjustable axis that positions the sample holder at specific marks.
- **vertical stack device** — Stack consisting of top hBN protection layer, MoSe₂ monolayer, graphene monolayer, tunnel hBN layer and Au electrode.
- **vibration isolation swing** — Mechanical isolation component that reduces external vibrations.
- **virtual com port** — Virtual serial COM port created when the USB slave port is connected to a PC.
- **visible mirror** — The visible mirror whose alignment is affected by the magnetic mount.
- **visible‑light optical path** — Imaging path equipped with a camera and lens system for tip‑sample alignment.
- **visor/window** — Transparent cover opened to visually inspect the sample and stage.
- **waveguide simulation** — Numerical simulation of multimodal propagation in a hollow waveguide performed by Sergi, Sadeen and Sebastian.
- **weight‑balancing mirror** — Second mirror in the delay stage used for weight balancing.
- **white tower (old computer in the top rack)** — The old computer mounted in the top rack that hosts the control software.
- **white tower (old computer in the top rack)** — Old computer in the top rack referred to as the white tower.
- **wire 9432 wh033 te** — Wire component listed in the adapter parts list.
- **wire software** — Control software for the Renishaw Raman instrument.
- **x/y scanner piezo tube assembly** — Piezo tubes that move the tip laterally in X and Y.
- **x/y/z scanner assembly** — Mechanical assembly that integrates the x‑, y‑ and z‑piezo tube scanners with macor spacers and seats.
- **x10 voltage amplifier** — Amplifier that provides additional gain to the transimpedance output before the lock-in.
- **xfer gate** — Gate mechanism opened to transfer the sample in and out of the chamber.
- **xyz positioner** — General XYZ positioner device.
- **xyz scanner** — Scanner that moves the tip relative to the sample.
- **xyz scanner (ansxyz100std/lt)** — High‑precision XYZ scanner made from titanium for low‑temperature operation.
- **xyz‑positioners** — Coarse positioning stages used together with piezo scanners such as ANSxy100.
- **x‑ and y‑nanopositioners** — Fine translation stages on top of the piezoelectric rotator used to scan the twist angle and position the tip.
- **x‑axis nanopositioner** — Attocube piezoelectric nanopositioner that moves the device laterally in X.
- **x‑positioner** — Positioner axis that moves in the x direction.
- **x‑ray diffraction (cu kα, panalytical)** — Setup for Bragg geometry XRD of CVT‑grown crystals.
- **y‑axis nanopositioner** — Attocube piezoelectric nanopositioner that moves the device laterally in Y.
- **z motor** — Z motor used to move the cantilever in the Z direction and to focus on the surface.
- **z piezo tube** — Piezoelectric actuator that converts an applied voltage into a vertical displacement of the tip.
- **zeiss auriga crossbeam fib‑sem** — High‑resolution field emission scanning electron microscope combined with a focused ion beam.
- **zeiss crossbeam fib‑sem** — High‑resolution field emission scanning electron microscope combined with a focused ion beam.
- **zero‑bias photocurrent local probe** — Experimental setup that measures zero‑bias photocurrent to probe polar order in NiI2.
- **zero‑bias photocurrent measurement** — Experimental configuration for measuring photocurrent without applied bias, used to probe CPGE and bulk photovoltaic effect.
- **zero‑bias photocurrent measurement setup** — Instrument configuration for zero‑bias photocurrent experiments.
- **znse focusing lens** — ZnSe lens used to focus the CO₂ pump beam in FIRL100.
- **zurich** — Zurich instrument with height 10 cm
- **zurich mfli lock‑in amplifier** — Lock‑in amplifier providing reference and signal channels, also drives the chopper.
- **zurich mfli lock‑in amplifier** — Generates reference signal, drives chopper and measures photocurrent/conductivity.
- **zurich mfli lock‑in amplifier** — Lock‑in instrument that generates reference signals and demodulates measurements.
- **z‑micro positioner** — Micro‑positioner for fine Z‑adjustment of the sample relative to the beam.
- **z‑micro positioner and tilter** — Stage that provides Z focus and sample tilt (≈222–233°).
- **z‑positioner** — Positioner axis that moves in the z direction.
- **z‑scanner piezo tube assembly** — Long piezo tube that moves the tip in the Z direction; electrode polarity controls retraction.

### subteam
- **anne de visser group** — Research subteam at the Van der Waals‑Zeeman Institute.
- **attocube support** — Attocube support team for troubleshooting positioner detection.
- **attocube support team** — Support team that evaluates performance‑test results.
- **attocube systems ag** — Company providing ANR, atto3DR, attoTMS tools.
- **attocube systems gmbh** — Attocube Systems GmbH, manufacturer of the ANC300 and positioners.
- **cryogenic optical assembly** — Optical subsystem containing a Schwarzschild objective, a titanium waveguide and a movable breadboard for auxiliary optics.
- **department of materials science and engineering, mit** — Present address of Achint Jain.
- **freie universität berlin** — Research group contributing to the QTM project.
- **icfo** — Institute of Photonic Sciences (ICFO) in Barcelona.
- **icfo internal** — Internal research team at ICFO.
- **icfo the institute of photonic science** — Institute of Photonic Science located at Mediterranean Technology Park, Av. Carl Friedrich Gauss, 3, 08860 Castelldefels, Spain.
- **international center for materials nanoarchitectonics, nims** — Institute in Tsukuba, Japan associated with Takashi Taniguchi.
- **iqe** — Department mentioned in the table.
- **qnoe group** — Quantum Nano‑Optics and Electronics research group at ICFO.
- **qpg** — Group mentioned in the table.
- **sample tower** — Subsystem that provides coarse lateral positioning and fine scanning of the vdW sample.
- **sergio and katya** — Subteam consisting of Sergio and Katya working on THz collimation.
- **stuttgart laser engineers** — Team responsible for the laser setup and user support.
- **tip tower** — Subsystem that holds the rotational stage, nanopositioners and the AFM tip assembly.
- **van der waals - zeeman institute** — Institute at the University of Amsterdam.
- **weizmann group** — Collaborating group from the Weizmann Institute.
- **weizmann institute** — Research group contributing to the QTM project.
- **xi lin group** — Research subteam at Peking University working on low‑temperature rotation.
- **yale center for research computing** — Computational resource used for the calculations reported in the work.
- **yale university** — Research group contributing to the QTM project.

### technique
- **"configuration upload" button** — Button that opens a file explorer to upload a saved axis configuration.
- **"enable sensor" button** — Button that activates or deactivates the sensor attached to an axis.
- **"ground axis" button** — Button that grounds the axis when it is within the defined target range.
- **"version" overlay** — Overlay providing device information, firmware version, and controls for reboot and network settings.
- ***.dll library integration** — DLL library allowing integration of AMC300 functionalities into custom systems.
- **/io feature package** — Feature package that enables trigger‑based positioning movements and external axis information output.
- **/io feature upgrade** — Hardware/firmware upgrade required to enable trigger communication.
- **100:1 voltage divider** — Voltage divider used to attenuate the oscillator output before exciting the QTF.
- **4 khz square‑wave etching** — Electrical waveform (+10 V for 40 µs, –0.5 V for 210 µs) used during the H₂SO₄ polishing step.
- **532 nm laser illumination** — Laser excitation (532 nm) focused with a 20× objective to a ≈5 µm spot for photocurrent measurements.
- **680 nm laser illumination** — Laser excitation (≈680 nm) used for CPGE spectra, where the strongest dichroism is observed.
- **absorption coefficient measurement** — Measurement of the absorption coefficient of a sample, reported for RH≈40 % as 0.02 cm⁻¹.
- **ab‑initio calculation** — First‑principles computational method without empirical parameters.
- **ab‑initio van der waals electrodynamics** — First‑principles computational method for van der Waals electrodynamics.
- **ac-in addition** — Adding an AC‑IN voltage to the output after amplification.
- **acaptm capacitive sensor technology** — Capacitive sensor technology using differential capacitance for absolute position measurement with no contact.
- **acaptm capacitive sensor technology** — Capacitive sensor technology developed by Renaissance Scientific for precise position encoding.
- **acetic acid (h₂o₂:h₂o) etching recipe** — Selective etchant for InAs used in the first etching step.
- **acetone and isopropanol cleaning** — Solvent rinses to remove PMMA after transfer.
- **acoustic near‑field microscopy** — Microscopy technique in which a tuning fork was used as a force sensor (Ref‑46).
- **active damping** — Method to reduce vibrations using feedback control.
- **active force feedback** — Feedback loop that maintains a constant sub‑nano‑Newton force during rotation and measurement.
- **active optical antenna** — Nanostructured antenna that enhances photodetection, used in the 2011 Science work.
- **active stabiliser** — Stabilisation technique that locks laser frequency or power to reduce output fluctuations.
- **ac‑in functionality** — Adds an external AC voltage (up to 10 VAC) to the output with unity gain and optional low‑pass filtering.
- **adding tunneling barrier technique** — Insertion of trilayer WSe₂ between graphene layers to suppress hybridization.
- **adjusting axis parameters** — Changing parameters of an axis depending on the active control mode.
- **adjusting network settings via json file** — Modifying attoIPconfig.json on a USB flash drive and loading it back to the device.
- **adsorption energy scanning process** — Scanning of adsorption energy as a function of molecule position at a fixed height.
- **afm contact‑mode cleaning** — Scanning the heterostructure with the AFM in contact mode to remove polymer residues.
- **afm thickness verification** — Use of AFM to measure the thickness of h‑BN flakes.
- **afm topography imaging** — Atomic force microscopy used to map the surface of the vdW heterostructure on the tip.
- **afm‑based rotation control** — Using the AFM piezoelectric rotator to continuously vary the twist angle.
- **alignment procedure** — Series of steps using the pipette, yellow cross and green flag to align the writing field.
- **alignment to markers** — Procedure to align patterns using lithography markers.
- **amc webserver application** — Web interface for the AMC300 controller.
- **amplifying dc voltages** — Amplifying DC voltages using the ANC300.
- **amplifying dc‑in voltages** — Technique that amplifies DC‑IN voltages from –10 .. +10 V to the 0 .. 150 V output range.
- **analytic approximation** — Approximate analytical expression used to evaluate the second derivative of the current.
- **analytic continuation** — Procedure iωₙ → ε + iη that converts Matsubara results to real‑frequency retarded quantities.
- **analytical approximations** — Mathematical approximations used to simplify complex model parameters.
- **analytical continuation** — Procedure that converts Matsubara‑frequency results to real‑frequency observables.
- **analytical modeling** — Derivation of transfer functions and system response using analytical methods.
- **anc300_close.vi** — VI that closes the communication channel to the ANC300.
- **anc300_communicate.vi** — Software sub‑VI handling communication with the ANC300 controller.
- **anc300_feature_code.vi** — VI that writes feature codes to enable or disable special ANC300 functions.
- **anc300_filter.vi** — Software sub‑VI that writes and reads filter settings for a selected axis.
- **anc300_getserial.vi** — VI that reads the serial number of a given axis or of the controller.
- **anc300_input.vi** — VI that controls the AC‑IN and DC‑IN inputs of the ANC300.
- **anc300_mode.vi** — VI that reads or sets the operational mode of a selected axis on the ANC300.
- **anc300_open.vi** — VI that opens the communication channel to the ANC300 and returns a connection ID.
- **anc300_pattern.vi** — VI that writes or reads a stepping pattern (28 data points) for a selected axis and direction.
- **anc300_preferences.vi** — VI that reads/writes stepping, offset voltage, frequency, capacitance, and output values.
- **anc300_step.vi** — VI that starts a stepping sequence of a specified number of steps.
- **anc300_stop.vi** — VI that stops stepping on a selected axis.
- **anc300_trigger_setget.vi** — VI that reads and writes the trigger status of the ANC300.
- **anc300_waitforstep.vi** — VI that waits until a stepping command on a given axis has finished.
- **andreev spectroscopy** — Phase-sensitive spectroscopy technique.
- **angle‑dependent magnetoresistance oscillations (armo)** — Transport technique that benefits from constant high magnetic fields and sample rotation.
- **angle‑dependent transport measurement** — Transport measurement performed while rotating the sample relative to the magnetic field to study angular dependence.
- **angle‑dependent transport measurement** — Transport measurement as a function of sample orientation relative to magnetic field.
- **angle‑resolved photoemission spectroscopy** — Direct measurement of electronic band structure via photo‑emitted electrons.
- **angle‑resolved photoemission spectroscopy (arpes)** — Technique to map electronic band structure.
- **angle‑resolved photoemission spectroscopy (arpes)** — Experimental technique to map electronic band structure
- **angle‑resolved photoemission spectroscopy (arpes)** — Standard method for mapping occupied electronic bands over a region of the Brillouin zone.
- **angle‑resolved resonant photoemission** — Photoemission measured as a function of emission angle under resonant excitation.
- **angle‑resolved tunneling** — Measurement of tunneling conductance while varying the relative twist angle between layers.
- **angle‑resolved x‑ray photoelectron spectroscopy** — Technique for probing electronic structure with angular resolution.
- **angular‑dependence fitting** — Fitting of I_total(φ) to extract CPGE and LPGE coefficients.
- **angular‑dependent linear dichroism imaging** — LD imaging performed while rotating the sample to determine propagation direction.
- **angular‑dependent linear dichroism measurement** — Measurement of dichroism as a function of polarization angle.
- **anm150 axis screen elements and usage** — Screen elements and usage for ANM150 axis control.
- **anm300 axis screen elements and usage** — Screen elements and usage for ANM300 axis control.
- **anm300 front panel usage** — Usage instructions for the ANM300 front panel.
- **annealing** — Thermal treatment of the sample to remove contaminants before measurement.
- **annealing** — Thermal treatment of the sample to remove contaminants.
- **annealing step** — Thermal treatment applied to the device.
- **applying dc offset voltages** — Applying DC offset voltages via the ANC300.
- **approximate analytical solutions** — Semi‑analytical methods for interacting electron systems.
- **arrhenius analysis** — Extraction of activation energy from temperature‑dependent resistance data.
- **astigmatism correction** — Correction of beam astigmatism using the STIG tool.
- **atomic defect sensing technique** — Method that exploits a single atomic defect as a quantum dot sensor for electrostatic potential.
- **atomic force microscope (afm)** — Instrument that measures forces between a tip and a sample, allowing imaging of non‑conductive surfaces.
- **atomic force microscopy (afm)** — Technique to measure flake thickness.
- **atomic force microscopy (afm) imaging** — Topographic technique used to verify moiré periodicity of the TBG sample.
- **atomic multiplet theory** — Theoretical method used to calculate RPED spectra.
- **atomic set scanning probe technique** — A technique that uses a single‑electron transistor tip to map local electrostatic potentials with atomic‑scale proximity.
- **atomic simulation environment (ase)** — ASE software for setting up DFT calculations.
- **atomic stereography** — Imaging method that reconstructs three‑dimensional atomic arrangements from angular‑resolved electron data.
- **atomic‑scale visualization** — High‑resolution imaging (e.g., STEM) to directly see atomic arrangements.
- **attocube_driver.py (rotation_target function)** — Python script providing the rotation_target function to rotate the sample by a defined angle.
- **attodiscovery software** — Software interface for the AMC300 controller.
- **attofpsensor interferometric measurement technique** — Interferometric measurement technique employed by attocube for resonance frequency and stiffness measurements.
- **audio‑frequency lock‑in amplifier** — Software‑based lock‑in amplifier (1 µV accuracy) built from a Femto DPLCA‑200 current amplifier and NI‑9239 sampler.
- **auto measure c and r** — Automatic measurement of the positioner’s capacitance and resistance when the axis is activated.
- **automatic alignment process** — Procedure that automatically aligns the writing field using e‑beam markers.
- **automatic fine piezo motor step control** — Fine step control enabled by the closed‑loop system.
- **automatic focus correction** — Technique to correct focus automatically at specific chip locations.
- **automatic position controls** — Closed‑loop control system that moves a nanopositioner to a target position.
- **automation of measurement routines** — Software integration to control temperature, magnetic field and arbitrary parameter sweeps.
- **axis module removal** — Procedure to safely remove an axis module from the ANC300 controller.
- **bake** — Thermal treatment at controlled temperature.
- **baking** — Baking at 120 °C for 1 min 30 s to cure polymer films.
- **band basis** — Transformation from orbital to band representation using eigenvectors.
- **band‑structure tomography** — Generalized method to reconstruct momentum‑dependent band structure without gap assumptions.
- **bardeen approximation** — Approximation that replaces volume integral with flux through a separation surface.
- **bardeen formalism** — Theoretical framework by Bardeen to calculate tunneling matrix elements.
- **bardeens tunneling theory** — Quantitative theory of tunneling current, published 1961, applied to STM.
- **bardeen‑theoretic calculations** — Calculations based on Bardeen's theory that predict the appearance of the STM image.
- **beamer** — Software used to convert GDSII to CON files.
- **bias voltage scanning** — Applying a fixed bias voltage while scanning to record tunnelling current.
- **biased nanoscale contact** — Applying a bias to a nanoscale junction to drive a plasmonic antenna.
- **binary protocol** — Binary transfer mode used during FTP upload of the update file.
- **bistritzer‑macdonald model** — Continuum model for the electronic structure of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **bode plot analysis** — Method to compare magnitude response of different STM designs.
- **bode‑plot measurement** — Experimental acquisition of magnitude (dB) versus frequency for the STM transfer function.
- **bolometer alignment** — Procedure that uses a cube beamsplitter, flat mirror and VIS pilot to bring the VIS beam onto the bolometer window.
- **born approximation** — Lowest‑order perturbative method used to approximate the tunneling amplitude.
- **broadband illumination** — Using broadband light to probe electronic states.
- **browser‑based application** — Technique of using a web browser to interact with the controller via the webserver.
- **browser‑based software** — Browser‑based software used with the AMC300 controller.
- **bubble removal by heating in desiccator** — Heating the sample at 55 °C under vacuum for two days to eliminate trapped bubbles.
- **bulk photovoltaic effect (bpve) measurement** — Zero‑bias photocurrent arising from broken inversion symmetry.
- **bulk photovoltaic effect (ibpve)** — Photocurrent generated by the bulk photovoltaic effect, labelled IBPVE in the study.
- **cabling resistance measurement** — Measuring the resistance of the cabling; low‑ohmic required (<5 Ω).
- **cad design file loading** — Copying CAD files from a pendrive, opening GDS‑II files and preparing them for exposure.
- **cad model** — Computer‑aided design model that serves as the geometry input for FEM simulations.
- **calibrated junction electrostatics** — Method to deduce the electrostatic potential from the measurements.
- **calibration file selection** — Selecting a calibration file matching the nanopositioner serial number from system or USB drive.
- **capacitance measurement** — Experimental method to probe density of states and Landau levels in graphene.
- **capacitance measurement** — Measuring the capacitance of the piezo to detect breakage or disconnection.
- **capacitive position encoder interface** — Capacitive position encoder interface available only on Model B100.
- **ccd imaging** — Charge‑coupled device imaging used to capture spatial distribution of emitted light.
- **chemical deoxidation of tip** — Dip of Pt/Ir tip in HCl:H₂O (2:1) for ~30 s to remove surface oxides.
- **chemical etching** — Process used to reduce the size of the quantum dots in two successive steps (first etching, second etching).
- **chemical vapour transport (cvt)** — Growth method for single‑crystal NiI2 using a temperature gradient.
- **chirp signal excitation** — Chirp signal used to excite the positioner.
- **chopped visible‑to‑fir illumination** — Modulated light source used to generate photocurrent at the tip‑sample junction.
- **circular photogalvanic effect (cpge)** — Helicity‑dependent photocurrent measurement that reveals odd‑parity spin polarization.
- **circular photogalvanic effect (cpge) measurement** — Photocurrent generated by circularly polarized light, probed by rotating a quarter‑wave plate.
- **circular polarized light** — Circularly polarized X-ray photons used as illumination in RPED experiments.
- **cleaning with dust‑free air** — Blowing dust off the positioner surface using dust‑free air.
- **cleaning with solvent‑treated tissue** — Using a dust‑free tissue or cotton swab lightly soaked with isopropanol or acetone to clean metallic parts.
- **closed loop control** — Control mode where target position is entered and feedback maintains position.
- **closed loop functions** — UI controls that expand to show closed loop positioning options.
- **closed-loop positioning** — Positioning control that uses the IDS as an encoder in a feedback loop.
- **closed‑loop motion** — Closed‑loop motion to a target position using selected drive amplitude and frequency.
- **coarse‑motion mechanism implementation** — Design choice for large‑scale motion in STM.
- **coincidence point alignment** — Procedure to set up the coincidence point between SEM and FIB imaging.
- **cold solder point check** — Inspecting solder joints for loose contacts when cooling to cryogenic temperatures.
- **compressibility measurements** — Experimental determination of the chemical potential versus carrier density, providing inverse compressibility.
- **compressibility measurements** — Measurements of the electronic compressibility that provide chemical potential and inverse compressibility data.
- **computer‑aided design** — Use of CAD software to generate geometry for FEM simulations.
- **conductance measurement** — Measurement of conductance using voltage biases and current amplification.
- **conductance step analysis** — Analysis of conductance steps to separate contributions of phonon modes.
- **conductive afm imaging** — Room‑temperature conductive atomic‑force microscopy used to image the real‑space moiré pattern.
- **conductive afm measurements** — Method used to calibrate the back‑gate capacitance.
- **conductive afm scan** — Conductive atomic‑force‑microscopy scan used to characterize the TBG sample.
- **configuration upload** — Button that uploads a standardized positioner configuration (LUT‑file).
- **contact‑mode afm** — AFM operation mode in which the tip stays in continuous contact with the surface.
- **contamination spot burning** — Burning a contamination dot to fine‑tune focus and astigmatism.
- **continuous movement function** — Button function that moves the positioner continuously while the button is held.
- **continuous stepping** — Generating steps continuously while a switch is held.
- **continuum bistritzer‑macdonald (bm) model** — Theoretical model used to compute band structure and tunneling matrix elements.
- **continuum hamiltonian** — Effective low‑energy Hamiltonian describing twisted bilayer graphene.
- **continuum model** — Effective low‑energy description of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **continuum model of interacting tbg** — Effective low‑energy description of interacting electrons in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **contour representation** — Visualization of spectroscopy data used to locate local maxima.
- **convert userdefs** — Menu command in CleWin that converts user‑defined structures to regular GDSII.
- **coulomb diamond conductance diagram** — Spectroscopic mapping of conductance versus gate voltage and source‑drain bias that reveals charge states of the defect quantum dot.
- **coulomb‑diamond measurement** — Transport measurement that maps charge stability diagrams (Coulomb diamonds) as a function of gate and bias voltage.
- **cross‑polarized imaging** — Imaging of birefringent domains using orthogonal polarizers.
- **cross‑polarized imaging** — Optical microscopy with crossed polarizers to visualize ferroelectric domains.
- **cryogenic angle‑dependent magnetoresistance measurement** — Magnetoresistance measurement performed at millikelvin temperatures while rotating the sample relative to the magnetic field.
- **crystal field multiplet theory** — Theoretical method combined with RPED calculations for the resonant photoemission process.
- **current‑voltage measurement** — Electrical characterization of the tunnel junctions by sweeping bias voltage and recording current.
- **custom digital‑to‑analogue converter** — Instrument that applies combined AC and DC voltages for conductance measurements.
- **daisy app** — App used to control the rotation stage.
- **daisy‑chaining** — Connecting multiple AMC300 units via Ethernet and USB.
- **data preparation** — Preparation of data before exposure.
- **dc voltage fine positioning** — Use of a DC voltage on the piezo for µ° resolution positioning.
- **dc-in amplification** — Amplifying DC‑IN voltages from -10 V…+10 V to the output range.
- **decoupling** — Strategy to isolate the STM from external vibrations.
- **deep reactive ion etching** — Etching process to pattern ultrathin Si wafer.
- **defect imaging** — Scanning method that visualises individual atomic defects via enhanced tunnelling current.
- **defect‑assisted tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopy of band structure using defect‑mediated tunnelling pathways.
- **defect‑assisted tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method using defect states as probes.
- **density functional theory (dft) calculation** — First‑principles modelling of spin helices, propagation vectors and band structures in bulk and monolayer NiI2.
- **density functional theory (dft) calculations** — First‑principles computational method confirming the odd‑parity spin texture.
- **density functional theory (dft) calculations** — First‑principles computational method used to confirm spin‑polarization symmetry.
- **density functional theory (dft) with gga‑pbe** — Computational method used for structural relaxation of compressed TBG.
- **density functional theory calculations** — First‑principles computational method used to evaluate the electronic structure of the heterostructure.
- **density matrix renormalization group (dmrg)** — Tensor‑network method for low‑dimensional quantum systems
- **derivative rule** — Method for calculating tunneling matrix elements
- **deterministic dry‑transfer technique** — Method for assembling vdW heterostructures using polymer stamps.
- **deterministic placement** — Controlled positioning of exfoliated 2D flakes onto a target substrate.
- **develop** — Developing exposed resist.
- **developer mibk:ipa (1:3)** — Chemical developer (Methyl‑isobutyl‑ketone : Isopropanol) used after exposure.
- **di/dv measurement** — Differential conductance spectroscopy used to detect resonant tunnelling features.
- **di/dv spectroscopy** — Scanning tunneling spectroscopy measuring differential conductance as a function of bias and filling.
- **di/dv spectroscopy** — Differential conductance spectroscopy used to map the local density of states as a function of bias and filling.
- **di/dv spectroscopy** — Differential conductance spectroscopy technique used to probe electronic states.
- **diagnostic tools** — Tools in the GUI for diagnosing positioner performance.
- **diagonalizing the dynamic matrix** — Computational technique used to obtain phonon dispersion and polarization vectors of graphene.
- **differential conductance (di/dv) measurement** — Standard tunnelling spectroscopy method used to probe electronic density of states.
- **differential conductance theory** — Theoretical expression for dI/dV that includes electron‑plasmon scattering contributions.
- **differential tunneling conductance measurements** — Differential tunneling conductance measurements detecting d²I/dV² singularities.
- **dilatometer measurement** — Measurement of sample length change versus temperature or magnetic field.
- **dirac‑point scanning** — Scanning the tip such that its Dirac point follows a trajectory in momentum space.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling method that uses the Dirac points of the tip to map the sample band structure.
- **direct contact measurement** — Electrical measurement performed by pressing the tip onto a conductive sample.
- **doping correction by polynomial fitting** — Method to correct tip‑induced doping by tracing integer‑filling lines and applying a polynomial fit.
- **dose factor adjustment** — Setting the dose factor to a defined value (e.g., 1.0) before exposure.
- **double‑tube design** — Mechanical arrangement that fixes one end of the X/Y tube and one end of the Z tube together to cancel thermal expansion.
- **driving signal generation** — Generation of six delayed 0.1 ms signals that sequentially deform the piezo stacks.
- **drop down** — Releasing a stack onto a target substrate.
- **dry transfer** — Standard dry‑transfer technique used to fabricate the TBG heterostructure.
- **dry transfer technique** — Standard method to assemble vdW heterostructures using PPC-coated PDMS.
- **dual‑ and tri‑channel upgrades** — Upgrades providing dual or tri channel capabilities.
- **dual‑channel upgrade** — Upgrade that allows simultaneous driving of two positioners.
- **dual‑gated design** — Design that provides independent control of tip and sample doping levels in the QTM.
- **dynamic afm** — AFM mode that exploits cantilever oscillations for enhanced sensitivity.
- **dynamic atomic force microscopy** — AFM mode using frequency‑modulation to achieve atomic resolution.
- **dynamic band‑structure tuning** — Modifying electronic bands by applying pressure while measuring.
- **dynamic control** — Real‑time modulation of plasmon generation.
- **dynamic rotation** — In‑situ mechanical rotation of a heterostructure during measurement.
- **dynamic stm** — STM operated with an oscillating tip (mounted on a quartz force sensor) that can approach the surface very closely without crashing, enabling sub‑atomic resolution.
- **dynamical mean‑field theory** — Non‑perturbative method treating local quantum fluctuations exactly.
- **dynes broadening** — Parameterization of quasiparticle lifetime in the superconducting spectral function.
- **e-beam lithography** — Electron‑beam exposure to define split‑gate masks.
- **eco-mode** — Mode d’estalvi energètic que augmenta l’eficiència fins al 98 %.
- **eddy current damping** — Damping technique using induced currents, ineffective under magnetic fields.
- **edinburgh laser** — Laser source listed among the THz system components.
- **edit recipe** — Editing a recipe after step 10.
- **elastic momentum‑conserving tunneling** — Tunneling process that preserves both energy and in‑plane momentum without scattering.
- **elastic momentum‑conserving tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic technique that probes elastic, momentum‑conserving tunneling between tip and sample.
- **elastic momentum‑conserving tunnelling** — Elastic tunnelling process used to map electronic dispersions in the QTM.
- **electric field control** — Using an applied electric field to manipulate magnetic or optical properties.
- **electric force microscopy (efm)** — AFM‑based technique that measures electric forces.
- **electrical detection of plasmons via near‑field coupling** — Detection of plasmons by converting their near‑field into an electrical signal using various nanostructures.
- **electrical excitation** — Driving plasmons by applying an electrical bias.
- **electrical force microscopy (efm)** — AFM mode that detects electrostatic forces between tip and sample.
- **electrical measurements** — Room‑temperature and low‑temperature transport characterization.
- **electric‑field cooling** — Cooling procedure performed under a static electric field to set ferroelectric domain orientation.
- **electrochemical etching** — Method to shape STM tips by controlled electrochemical dissolution.
- **electron beam deposition** — Deposition of material using the electron beam and precursor gases.
- **electron beam lithography** — Lithography technique performed with the Crestec electron‑beam system.
- **electron beam lithography (ebl)** — Patterning method that uses a focused electron beam to expose a resist.
- **electron deposition** — Deposition of material using electron‑induced precursor dissociation.
- **electron diffraction** — TEM‑based diffraction to determine crystallographic orientation.
- **electron energy‑loss spectroscopy analysis** — Method borrowed to analyze inelastic tunneling by evaluating screened interaction.
- **electron spectroscopy** — Techniques that measure the energy distribution of electrons emitted from a material.
- **electron transport measurement** — Experimental method measuring current as a function of bias voltage across a tunnel junction.
- **electron tunnelling through atomically flat and ultrathin hbn** — Method to achieve high‑quality tunnelling using atomically smooth hBN barriers.
- **electronic transport measurement** — General transport experiments probing tunnelling currents through the heterostructure.
- **electron‑beam evaporation** — Deposition of Ti/Au metal layers for electrodes.
- **electron‑beam lithography** — The lithographic technique used to write patterns on the sample.
- **electrostatic force microscopy (efm)** — Technique that measures the frequency shift of a quartz tuning fork caused by electrostatic forces.
- **electrostatic force microscopy (efm)** — AFM mode that maps electrostatic forces.
- **electrostatic modeling** — Calculating charge‑neutrality lines using a capacitance model of the QTM junction.
- **encoder for position readout** — Encoder used to read the angular position of a rotator.
- **endless stepping** — Generating steps endlessly in a chosen direction until stopped.
- **engineered waveguides** — Waveguide structures used to select plasmon frequencies during transmission.
- **epc measurement at q_m** — Measurement of EPC at momentum q_M using conductance steps.
- **equipotential surface fitting procedure** — Fitting of electrostatic potential surfaces above TBG to extract tip radius and potential ratios.
- **error isolation** — Exchanging positioner, cabling, and electronics to locate the source of a malfunction.
- **etching steps** — Material removal process to eliminate graphene residues.
- **ethernet communication** — Alternative transport method used by LabView drivers to talk to the ANC300.
- **ethernet settings** — Configuration of network interface using DHCP or manual settings.
- **eucentric adjustment** — Procedure to align tilt axis and focus so that the sample stays centered during tilting.
- **exact diagonalization** — Numerical method solving many‑body Hamiltonians by full diagonalization
- **exfoliation** — Exfoliation of 2D flakes using plasma cleaning and blue tape.
- **exposure execution (raster)** — Raster‑mode exposure controlled by the beamblanker.
- **exposure execution (vector)** — Vector‑mode exposure of patterns.
- **external mode current measurement** — Technique to read the beam current by switching the system to external mode.
- **extraction of g_za vs twist angle** — Extraction of ZA mode coupling strength as a function of twist angle.
- **fabricate** — Mechanical technique used to build connector adapters.
- **fabrication of tips and samples** — Procedures to create atomically sharp tips and high‑quality 2D material devices.
- **factorial function** — Recursive Lua function that computes n!.
- **fast fourier transform** — Numerical method used to evaluate frequency integrals.
- **feedback loop using resistor fr** — Feedback mechanism in the transimpedance amplifier that sets the DC offset.
- **fem modal analysis** — Finite‑element method used to calculate resonance frequencies of STM components.
- **femto amplifier** — Low‑noise current amplifier used to measure tunnelling current.
- **fermi distribution functions** — Statistical occupation factors f(ω) entering the tunneling current expression.
- **fermionic monte carlo** — Stochastic numerical method for simulating fermionic many‑body systems.
- **fermis golden rule** — Perturbative rule for transition rates in quantum systems.
- **fermi‑edge spectroscopy** — Scanning tunneling spectroscopy that uses the tip’s Fermi‑edge singularity (at the tip’s Fermi circle) to probe the sample.
- **fermi’s golden rule** — Theoretical framework used to model the tunnelling current.
- **fermi’s golden rule** — Perturbative method for transition rates.
- **fermi’s golden‑rule calculation** — Perturbative evaluation of tunneling current using matrix elements between tip and sample states.
- **ferroelectric hysteresis loop measurement** — Electric‑field‑driven polarization switching with pulsed bias.
- **feynman diagram analysis** — Use of diagrammatic perturbation theory to represent interaction processes.
- **feynman diagram calculation** — Diagrammatic perturbation theory employed to compute the electron self‑energy and screening potential.
- **fft analysis** — Fast Fourier Transform of conductive AFM images used to determine moiré lattice parameters.
- **fft spectrum analysis** — Fast Fourier Transform of the optical signal to identify spectral peaks.
- **fib deposition** — Deposition process using the focused ion beam together with a gas precursor.
- **fib deposition** — Focused ion beam deposition used to grow a Pt pyramid on the cantilever.
- **fiber‑feedback optical parametric oscillator (ff‑opo)** — OPO technology that provides passive stability, reproducibility and shot‑noise‑limited performance for the Alpha laser.
- **fiber‑feedback optical parametric oscillator (ff‑opo)** — The core technology that provides the laser’s stability and shot‑noise‑limited performance.
- **field cooling** — Cooling the sample under a static electric field (±1 MV m⁻¹) to select a single ferroelectric/spin‑helix domain.
- **field cooling** — Cooling protocol with magnetic field applied.
- **field orientation control** — Precise adjustment of the angle between the magnetic field vector and the sample surface.
- **fine positioning** — Applying DC voltages to the piezos for nanometer‑scale positioning.
- **fine tuning control loop** — Adjustment of closed‑loop parameters (e.g., sensitivity, slew rate) for optimal IDS operation.
- **finite element method** — Numerical simulation technique used to compute resonance frequencies of STM models.
- **finite element method (fem) simulation** — Numerical simulation used to compute resonance frequencies of the STM designs.
- **firmware update** — Process of uploading and applying a new firmware image to the device.
- **firmware upgrade** — Procedure to load new firmware onto the ANC300 controller.
- **first-order golden rule calculation** — Computation of tunneling contributions using Fermi's golden rule at lowest order.
- **first-order perturbation theory** — Perturbative method used to obtain the Hinter contribution.
- **first-order perturbation theory** — Perturbative method used to calculate the interlayer electron‑phonon contribution.
- **first-principles simulation** — Computational method based on ab initio calculations
- **first‑order perturbation theory** — Approximation method employed to evaluate the contribution of the interaction Hamiltonian to the tunneling current.
- **first‑principles calculations** — Ab‑initio computation of non‑local conductivity of TMD monolayers.
- **first‑principles modelling (dft)** — Non‑collinear density‑functional theory calculations of spin helices in NiI₂.
- **first‑principles simulation** — Computational modeling (e.g., Yambo) used to study polarons and excitonic effects.
- **fishing procedure** — Use of tweezers and glass slides to transfer graphene between chemical baths.
- **fitting of divergence** — Procedure to fit beam divergence after free‑space propagation.
- **flake dropping** — Depositing a 2D flake onto a target area.
- **flake dropping on tip** — Process of depositing an exfoliated flake onto the tip.
- **flake flip** — Flipping the flake onto the tip by dissolving the PVA layer.
- **flux through separation surface** — Technique that evaluates tunneling matrix elements via surface flux.
- **focus and astigmatism adjustment** — Procedure to fine‑tune focus and astigmatism on Au beads.
- **focus and astigmatism correction** — Procedure to optimise focus and astigmatism using lens alignment and image stability.
- **focused ion beam (fib)** — Focused ion beam shaping of the tip.
- **focused ion beam (fib) deposition** — Method used to create the platinum pyramid on the AFM cantilever.
- **focused ion beam (fib) milling** — Technique for milling and deposition using a focused ion beam.
- **focused ion beam milling** — Material removal using a Ga ion beam in the Zeiss Crossbeam.
- **focused ion beam patterning** — Fabrication method used to define the devices.
- **focused‑ion‑beam deposition** — Method used to create a platinum pyramid tip for the vdW device.
- **fourier analysis of stm signals** — Spectral analysis of tunneling current and z‑piezo signals to assess noise and bandwidth.
- **fourier transform** — Transformation of Green's functions from time/momentum to frequency/momentum space.
- **fourier transform of hopping amplitude** — Transforms the real‑space hopping function \(t(\mathbf r)\) into momentum space \(\hat t(\mathbf q)\).
- **fourier‑transform infrared spectroscopy (ftir)** — Broadband infrared spectroscopy used in QTOM.
- **four‑wave mixing** — Nonlinear optical process mixing three photons to generate a fourth.
- **fowler–nordheim tunnelling** — Field‑emission tunnelling through the WSe₂ conduction band.
- **frequency demodulator** — Technique that extracts frequency information from the input signal.
- **frequency modulation technique** — Method used in AFM to improve resolution and scan speed by modulating cantilever frequency.
- **frequency‑modulation afm (fm‑afm)** — AFM mode that controls tip‑sample distance by locking to the resonance frequency of the QTF.
- **ftir measurement driver (ftir_measurement)** — Python driver that provides programmatic control of the FTIR system.
- **ftir_step_scan.py** — Script that runs FTIR step scans using the delay stage.
- **ftir‑qtom spectroscopy** — Fourier‑transform infrared spectroscopy performed with the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **ftp** — FTP protocol used to upload update files to the ANC300.
- **ftp session** — File Transfer Protocol session used to upload firmware updates to the ANC300.
- **gas injection system (gis)** — System for delivering precursor gases for deposition or etching.
- **gas injection system operation** — Releasing precursor gases for deposition or ablation via the GIS.
- **gate voltage tuning** — Simultaneous adjustment of tip chemical potential and electrostatic shift while keeping Vb≈0.
- **gate‑tunable resonant tunneling** — Control of resonant tunnelling by electrostatic gating.
- **gaussian decomposition of spectroscopy** — Fitting of spectral lines with a sum of Gaussian functions.
- **gaussian fitting procedure** — Procedure to fit averaged spectra with a number of Gaussians determined by peak counting.
- **gaussian peak fitting** — Fitting of spectral peaks with Gaussian functions to determine amplitude and quasiparticle energy.
- **gdsii exposure** — Standard file format used to define patterns for electron‑beam exposure.
- **gender changer** — Connector that cross‑connects cabling; its use can lead to high drive voltage on the encoder.
- **getdrivechannel** — Command that returns the number of the currently active drive channel, or 0 if none.
- **getposition** — Command that returns the position measurement (in meters) for a selected channel.
- **getstatuspositioning()** — Command getStatusPositioning gets the status of any closed loop control in progress.
- **getstoplimit(channel)** — Command getStopLimit retrieves the stop limit threshold.
- **gis nozzle positioning** — Procedure to position the GIS nozzle relative to the sample.
- **gocontinuousforward** — Command that generates forward steps continuously at a set amplitude and frequency until a stopMotion command is received.
- **gocontinuousreverse** — Command that generates reverse steps continuously at a set amplitude and frequency until a stopMotion command is received.
- **gointerval(channel, delta, amplitude, frequency)** — Command goInterval moves a channel by an interval using closed loop control.
- **goposition(channel, target, amplitude, frequency)** — Command goPosition moves a channel to a target position using closed loop control.
- **gostepsforward method** — JSON‑RPC method used to advance steps on the NP‑Drive.
- **gostepsreverse** — Command that moves a selected channel reverse by a specified number of steps with given amplitude and frequency.
- **gpio control** — Using general‑purpose input/output pins for device control.
- **graphene plasmon‑enhanced vibrational sensing** — Use of graphene plasmons to boost the vibrational signal of surface‑adsorbed molecular layers.
- **graphical solution** — Method used to solve the self‑consistency equation and obtain (n_f, n_c) for each total filling.
- **graphite transfer** — Process of placing a thin graphite flake onto the prepared tip.
- **group‑theory analysis** — Symmetry‑based analytical method used to classify spin‑polarization patterns.
- **gw approximation** — Perturbative method accounting for dynamical screening.
- **gw method** — Many‑body technique that constructs the Green’s function and spectral function.
- **gw plus cumulant method** — Advanced many‑body perturbation technique combining GW self‑energy with cumulant expansion.
- **hartree-fock mean field potential** — Mean‑field approximation used to construct the counterterm.
- **hartree‑fock approximation** — Self‑consistent mean‑field method incorporating exchange interactions.
- **hbn pulling method** — Method to detach top hBN flake using a PC pyramid at elevated temperature.
- **heating and drying at 55 °c** — Thermal steps on heater and vacuum hot plate to evaporate water and dry graphene.
- **heating and outgassing** — Procedure to heat the sample and remove adsorbed gases.
- **heavy‑fermion (hf) framework** — Mean‑field model of localized f‑electrons hybridising with itinerant Dirac‑like c‑electrons.
- **helimagnet encapsulation** — Embedding a material between layers of a helimagnet to induce spin texture.
- **helium scattering** — Surface‑sensitive probe using low‑energy helium atoms.
- **hf:h₂o₂:h₂o etching recipe** — Selective etchant for AlSb used in the second etching step.
- **high-speed force sensor** — Force detection using quartz tuning fork
- **high‑resolution electron energy‑loss spectroscopy (hreels)** — Surface‑sensitive measurement of energy loss of electrons due to excitations.
- **high‑resolution inelastic neutron scattering** — Enhanced INS method that can resolve subtle magnon splittings.
- **high‑resolution scanning probe imaging** — Atomic‑scale imaging of forces or potentials with a sharp tip.
- **holdposition(channel, target, amplitude, timeout)** — Command holdPosition holds a position by taking small steps toward a target.
- **homemade filters** — Custom electrical filters reducing heat load from measurement wires.
- **home‑built dac array** — Custom digital‑to‑analogue converter array used to source voltages for the experiments.
- **home‑built thermal links** — Custom thermal connections improving cooling of the rotator.
- **http** — HTTP protocol supported by the LAN adapter; provides an integrated web‑server.
- **https secure access** — Secure HTTPS communication using a private key and certificate.
- **hubbard‑i approximation** — Non‑perturbative treatment of the on‑site f‑f interaction used in the THF model.
- **h₂so₄ polishing** — Application of a 4 kHz square wave in acidic solution to remove the droplet and sharpen the tip.
- **ids api** — APIs that allow operation of the IDS during AMC/IDS closed‑loop applications.
- **imaging moiré electrostatic potential** — Using the defect as a local probe to obtain a two‑dimensional map of the G/hBN moiré potential.
- **impedance measurement** — Technique to measure the QTF impedance.
- **in situ imaging** — Imaging of the tip contact area via an atomic defect acting as a localized tunnelling channel.
- **inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy (iets)** — Spectroscopic technique probing vibrational and electronic excitations via tunneling.
- **inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy (iets)** — Technique employed by QTM to probe plasmons.
- **inelastic momentum‑conserving tunnelling** — Inelastic tunnelling process that conserves in‑plane momentum and provides a quantitative measure of mode‑resolved electron‑phonon coupling.
- **inelastic momentum‑resolved scanning technique** — Scanning method that resolves energy and momentum of inelastic excitations.
- **inelastic neutron scattering** — Technique used to probe spin‑wave excitations.
- **inelastic neutron scattering (ins)** — Technique used to map magnon dispersions in CrSb.
- **inelastic phonon spectroscopy** — High‑resolution spectroscopy that resolves phonon emission processes as horizontal lines in conductance maps.
- **inelastic phonon‑assisted tunnelling** — Tunnelling channel that involves emission or absorption of a phonon.
- **inelastic tunneling spectroscopy** — Technique that probes collective excitations by measuring the differential conductance arising from inelastic electron tunneling.
- **inelastic tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that probes collective excitations via momentum‑conserving interlayer tunneling.
- **inelastic tunnelling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method to probe electron‑phonon interactions via tunnelling conductance.
- **infrared nanoscopy** — Scanning near‑field optical microscopy in the infrared regime.
- **infrared spectroscopy** — Optical method that has probed interband plasmons in twisted graphene.
- **interfaces** — Electrical and communication interfaces provided by the NP-Drive controller.
- **interferometric experiment** — Method using a broadband source and interferometry to obtain FFT‑resolved photocurrent.
- **interferometric photocurrent measurement** — Technique that extracts amplitude and phase of the photocurrent via optical interference.
- **interlayer tunneling** — Tunneling of electrons between two graphene layers.
- **interlayer tunneling spectroscopy (di/dv)** — Differential conductance measurement used to probe the electronic states of TBG via the MLG tip.
- **interlocked protective cover** — Protective cover with interlock that shuts the laser when opened.
- **inverse ids direction** — Option to invert the measurement direction of the IDS to match the positioner movement direction.
- **inverse spin hall effect** — Conversion of spin current into transverse charge voltage, used to probe spin liquids.
- **in‑situ hall resistance calibration of tilt angle** — Method to accurately control in‑plane and out‑of‑plane field orientation using Hall resistance.
- **in‑situ manipulation** — Real‑time adjustment of layer alignment inside a measurement setup.
- **in‑situ perpendicular transport measurement** — Transport measurement performed across the vdW stack while varying twist angle.
- **in‑situ twistronics measurement** — Simultaneous control of twist angle and conductance measurement while tip remains in contact.
- **ion deposition** — Deposition of material using ion‑induced precursor dissociation.
- **ion‑assisted deposition** — Deposition technique that uses ion assistance (e.g., Si) to increase film density.
- **isolation of ground loops** — Measures such as isolating the NP‑Drive enclosure, avoiding ground currents in sensor coax shields, and preventing external equipment from creating earth‑ground loops.
- **iterative perturbation theory** — Computational method for obtaining electronic spectral functions.
- **i–v measurement** — Current–voltage characterization of the tunnelling devices.
- **json‑rpc** — Remote‑procedure‑call protocol used to send commands to the NP‑Drive.
- **json‑rpc 2.0 protocol** — Remote‑control protocol over TCP/IP.
- **junction electrostatics conversion** — Method that translates the gate‑voltage position of a Coulomb‑blockade peak into an electrostatic potential φ(x) at the interface.
- **k-points mesh sampling** — Sampling of the Brillouin zone with k‑points mesh density ≃0.009 Å⁻².
- **kondo‑like interaction model** — Model describing itinerant electrons coupled to a winding magnetic texture, used to explain odd‑parity Zeeman fields.
- **kondo‑like interaction model** — Model describing itinerant electrons coupled to winding magnetic moments.
- **k·p approximation** — Perturbative method expanding around Dirac points.
- **labeling axes** — Procedure to assign a name to an axis via the UI.
- **labeling device** — Procedure to assign a name to a device via the UI.
- **labview** — LabVIEW software environment mentioned in the manual.
- **labview drivers** — LabView VIs that expose the same commands as the Lua interface and communicate with the ANC300.
- **laser attenuation** — Control of laser intensity using ND and OD filters.
- **laser safety program** — Program for safe use of lasers.
- **laser stabiliser** — Technique that compensates laser output fluctuations caused by ambient condition changes.
- **laserfib** — Laser‑based material ablation add‑on for the Crossbeam.
- **lateral force microscopy (lfm)** — AFM‑based technique that measures lateral forces.
- **lattice quantum monte carlo** — Numerical many‑body technique applied to graphene.
- **led indicators** — OUT LED indicates positive output voltage; ACT LED indicates stepping activity.
- **lens simulation** — Optical design of silicon and TPX lenses performed with Zemax.
- **lift-off** — Process to remove unwanted metal using acetone.
- **light‑assisted tunneling protocol** — Experimental scheme combining laser illumination with QTM tunneling measurements.
- **light‑assisted tunneling spectroscopy** — Tunneling spectroscopy performed under laser illumination to resolve photon sidebands.
- **limits configuration dialog** — Dialog to set voltage, frequency and temperature limits per axis.
- **limits configuration dialog** — Dialog for setting motion limits.
- **line integral over equal‑energy contours** — Rewriting the tunneling current as an integral along contours where the band‑energy difference equals φ.
- **line scan along kₙ(θ)** — Scanning the sample band structure along three symmetry‑related lines Kₙ(θ) in each valley.
- **linear dichroism measurement** — Temperature‑dependent linear dichroism measurement on a single‑domain region of the PVD‑grown NiI2 flake.
- **linear dichroism microscopy** — Imaging of linear dichroism contrast across NiI2 domains.
- **linear dichroism scanning** — Polarization‑resolved optical imaging used to distinguish and select chiral domains.
- **linear fit of vs(iexp)** — Method to obtain RS from VS versus Iexp data.
- **linear response theory** — Theoretical framework used to derive the tunneling current from the tunneling Hamiltonian.
- **linear spin‑wave calculations** — Theoretical method used to compute magnon spectra from a given spin Hamiltonian.
- **linearized augmented‑plane‑wave (lapw) method** — Self‑consistent electronic‑structure calculation used to compute p(r,EF) for Au(110) surfaces.
- **linecut analysis** — Extraction of spectra along specific cuts (A and B) in the data.
- **line‑cut measurement** — Spatially resolved measurement of the moiré potential along a line.
- **little pyramids of tungsten atoms model** — More realistic tip model consisting of small tungsten pyramids.
- **loading sample** — Procedure to load a sample into the vacuum chamber of the Crestec CABL.
- **local electrostatic imaging** — Mapping of surface charge distribution via electrostatic force microscopy.
- **lock-in modules for signal generation and acquisition** — Fast and powerful lock‑in detection modules used in the measurement.
- **locking bar** — UI control to lock or unlock the display to prevent unintended changes.
- **lock‑in detection** — Lock‑in detection using the Zurich MFLI for extracting AC signals.
- **lock‑in detection** — Sensitive measurement technique using modulation.
- **lock‑in detection on gate excitation** — Signal‑enhancement method that modulates the gate voltage and detects the response synchronously.
- **lock‑in measurement** — Phase‑sensitive detection used to acquire dI/dV.
- **lorentzian curve fitting** — Fit Raman peaks with Lorentzian functions.
- **lorentzian fit** — Fitting procedure used to extract peak widths and thus energy or momentum resolution.
- **lua programming** — Scripting language used to define functions and control the device.
- **magnetic field sweeping** — Varying magnetic field to study its effect on transport.
- **magnetic force microscopy (mfm)** — Technique that detects magnetic forces using a magnetized tip and a frequency‑modulation feedback loop.
- **magnetic force microscopy (mfm) scanning** — MFM technique that maps magnetic force gradients by monitoring QTF phase.
- **magnetic susceptibility measurement** — Measurement of magnetic response during field cooling.
- **magnetic susceptibility measurement** — Temperature‑dependent magnetic susceptibility measurement revealing antiferromagnetic and helimagnetic transitions.
- **magneto-transport measurement** — Technique of measuring electrical transport under magnetic fields.
- **magneto-transport measurement** — Electrical transport measurement performed under a magnetic field, often used to probe Fermi surface properties.
- **magnetometry characterization** — Measurement of magnetic properties such as transition temperatures.
- **magnetoresistance measurement** — Transport measurement of resistance as a function of magnetic field orientation.
- **magnetotunneling** — Tunnelling spectroscopy performed in a magnetic field to probe Landau‑level physics.
- **magnetotunneling** — Tunnelling measurements performed under magnetic field.
- **magneto‑transport measurements** — Measurements of electrical transport properties under magnetic fields, often angle‑dependent.
- **magnon dispersion mapping** — Systematic measurement of magnon energies over reciprocal‑space paths.
- **magnon injection** — Generation of magnons in a quantum Hall ferromagnet.
- **many‑body perturbation theory** — Framework for treating interacting electron systems beyond mean‑field.
- **map image acquisition** — Acquisition method for raster scanning Raman maps.
- **mapping néel vector distribution using mcd‑rped** — Use of the proportionality between MCD intensity and cos θ to image Néel‑vector textures.
- **mapping of second derivative d2i/dv2** — Extraction of the curvature of the I‑V characteristic to locate singularities in the density of states.
- **mapping of the fermi surface** — General approach to correlate electronic structure with material properties, using ARPES, ARMO or QO.
- **marker alignment** — Placing green flags in the design and detecting them to align the sample.
- **mask design** — Designing mask patterns using Crestec software or converting from Beamer.
- **matsubara sum** — Summation over fermionic Matsubara frequencies used to evaluate susceptibilities.
- **matsubara summation** — Summation over discrete imaginary frequencies to obtain frequency‑domain Green’s functions.
- **mean‑field approximation** — Theoretical method that approximates many‑body interactions by an effective single‑particle field.
- **measurement initialization** — Procedure to start the IDS distance measurement and set the reference position.
- **metal evaporation** — Evaporation of Ti and Au (or Cr and Au) onto a tip-less cantilever.
- **milling (ion beam milling)** — Removal of material by focused ion beam.
- **milling small dots** — Procedure to mill a series of small holes to improve focus on a clean sample.
- **minimal heisenberg model fitting** — Data analysis approach that reproduces CrSb magnon spectra with J₁, J₂, J₃ and D.
- **mixing ac voltages** — Mixing in AC voltages with the ANC300.
- **mla contacts and markers** — Fabrication of MLA contacts and markers with positive resist.
- **modal analysis** — Procedure to extract modal frequencies and shapes from FEM models.
