- **mode expansion** — Expansion of atomic displacements into phonon modes using creation and annihilation operators.
- **moiré fft analysis** — Technique to extract heterostrain from the Fourier transform of the moiré pattern.
- **molecular beam epitaxy (mbe)** — Growth technique employed to fabricate the RITD heterostructure.
- **momentum‑ and energy‑resolved tunneling** — Measurement method enabled by the QTM that resolves tunneling processes in both wave‑vector and energy.
- **momentum‑ and energy‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that probes the electronic band structure of vdW stacks.
- **momentum‑conserving elastic tunnelling** — Background tunnelling process that conserves in‑plane momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved di/dv spectroscopy** — Scanning tunnelling spectroscopy that records dI/dV as a function of momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved energy‑shift measurement** — Experimental method used to obtain δE(k) with momentum resolution.
- **momentum‑resolved imaging** — High‑resolution elastic momentum‑resolved tunnelling spectroscopy used to map electronic states near the commensurate twist angle.
- **momentum‑resolved spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic measurement that resolves both energy and in‑plane momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved spectroscopy** — Spectroscopy performed at selected momenta along the flat‑band region.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling** — Tunneling configuration that conserves in‑plane momentum, enabling access to band‑structure information.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy** — Technique that measures differential conductance as a function of tip‑sample twist angle and bias to resolve band dispersion.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic technique probing tunneling between twisted layers with momentum resolution.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling spectroscopy** — Experimental method implemented with the QTM to map dI/dV versus momentum and energy.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that resolves energy versus in‑plane momentum via tunnelling across a twistable interface.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method to probe tunnelling as a function of momentum.
- **mounting technique** — Procedure of attaching positioners to a base plate using specified screws and orientations.
- **mounting technique** — The set of steps and hardware used to attach devices to plates.
- **multiple scattering** — Treats electron propagation as successive scattering events.
- **multiple scattering theory** — Theoretical framework employed in RPED calculations.
- **mössbauer spectroscopy** — Nuclear resonant spectroscopy used to study magnetic and electronic states.
- **nanoscale imaging** — Imaging methods with spatial resolution at the nanometre scale.
- **near‑field optical spectroscopy** — Optical technique that probes long‑wavelength plasmons within a small fraction of the moiré Brillouin zone.
- **network configuration dialog** — Dialog to set the ANC300 network address via DHCP or manual configuration.
- **network configuration dialog** — Dialog for configuring network settings.
- **neutron diffraction** — Technique to investigate crystal and magnetic structures.
- **neutron scattering** — Bulk‑sensitive probe of magnetic order, unsuitable for thin films.
- **ni tip etching** — Chemical etching process to fabricate Ni AFM tips.
- **noise analysis** — Study of noise sources in frequency‑modulation AFM.
- **nonresonant circularly polarized x‑ray diffraction analysis** — X‑ray method to probe spin helicity without resonance.
- **normalization of mcd with structural pattern** — Procedure that divides the magnetic CD pattern by the structural (A‑B) pattern to approximate XMCD of a sublattice.
- **nsl‑/mic‑mode** — NSL‑/MIC‑mode in AMC300 interface for GPIO triggering.
- **nuclear magnetic resonance (nmr)** — Spectroscopic technique probing nuclear spins to study magnetic properties.
- **numerical computation of screening potential** — Numerical evaluation of Eq. (B5) for the in-layer screening potential.
- **numerical computation of |m|²** — Numerical evaluation of the normalized inelastic tunneling rates for specific twist angles and gate distances.
- **numerical derivative analysis** — Computational extraction of derivatives from measured data.
- **numerical derivative to obtain second derivative (d²i/dv²)** — Post‑processing of the lock‑in data to compute the curvature of the I‑V characteristic.
- **numerical input overlay** — Overlay that appears when a numerical input field is touched, allowing entry of values.
- **numerical integration over momentum space** — Computational method used for the calculations presented.
- **numerical simulation** — Computational modeling of dI/dV curves and plasmon‑assisted tunneling.
- **numerical simulation of tunneling spectroscopy** — Computational approach used to calculate I(V) and d²I/dV² for the junction.
- **n₂ blow‑dry** — Nitrogen blow‑dry of the tip after cleaning.
- **o2 plasma clean** — Oxygen plasma cleaning to remove hardened resist layer.
- **offset setting** — Setting an offset voltage after amplification.
- **offset voltage feature** — Function to add a constant offset voltage to the outputs.
- **one‑shot gw approximation** — A perturbative many‑body method used to compute the self‑energy and spectral function.
- **one‑shot gw calculation** — Computational method to obtain self‑energy corrections in a single iteration.
- **open loop control** — Control mode where the position is set without feedback.
- **open loop control** — Control mode where fixed steps are commanded via touchscreen arrows.
- **open loop motion commands** — Methods such as goStepsForward, goStepsReverse, goContinuousForward, goContinuousReverse for controlling motion.
- **open loop movement** — Open‑loop positioning movements controlled from the operation view.
- **open loop movement control** — Control of open‑loop positioning movements via the graphical user interface.
- **open loop positioning** — Positioning mode without feedback, controlled via UI controls.
- **open loop positioning controls** — UI elements to initiate or stop movement in open loop mode.
- **optical alignment using straight edges** — Alignment method that uses the long straight edges of SLG flakes to set the twist angle.
- **optical conductivity measurement** — Spectroscopic probe of frequency‑dependent conductivity.
- **optical illumination (shining light)** — Shining light onto a sample.
- **optical imaging** — Microscopic imaging of the NiI2 CPGE device.
- **optical measurements** — Photoluminescence and spectroscopy performed with an inverted microscope.
- **optically pumped fir laser** — FIR laser that uses tunable infrared radiation to create population inversion in a polar gas.
- **optics alignment** — Procedure to align the optical path before starting displacement measurement.
- **organic plasmon‑emitting diode technique** — Using an organic diode to emit surface plasmons.
- **out-of-plane transport measurement** — Electrical measurement of current perpendicular to the layers.
- **output filter** — Filter applied to DC-IN output affecting frequency response.
- **oxygen plasma cleaning** — 5 min, 100 W O₂ plasma treatment of substrates to improve adhesion of 2D materials.
- **passive damping** — Method to reduce vibrations using material damping.
- **patented inertial drive technology** — Attocube’s proprietary inertial drive method that enables motion in extreme environments.
- **pattern commands** — Commands (setpu, getpu, setpd, getpd) that modify the step up/down patterns on an axis module.
- **pdms/pc stamping procedure** — Stamp‑based transfer method using a hemispherical PDMS block and a polycarbonate film to pick up and stack 2D layers.
- **peak area analysis** — Quantify Raman peak area for comparing etching steps.
- **perturbation theory** — Perturbation theory used to analyze TBG wave functions near the bottom‑layer Dirac point.
- **perturbative treatment** — Approximate method applied to photon‑assisted tunneling using Fermi’s golden rule.
- **phonon spectroscopy** — Measurement of phonon energies via inelastic tunnelling features in the conductance.
- **phonon spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that can be performed with the QTM by avoiding electrostatic pn‑junctions.
- **phonon spectroscopy based on qtm** — Method that uses QTM to probe phonon dispersions and electron‑phonon interactions.
- **phonon spectrum fitting** — Fitting phonon measurements to determine geometric capacitance.
- **phonon‑assisted inelastic tunnelling** — Theoretical technique modelling inelastic tunnelling via phonon emission using Fermi’s golden rule.
- **photocurrent measurement** — Measurement of current generated by illuminating the tip‑sample junction with chopped Vis‑FIR light.
- **photocurrent measurement** — Detection of current generated by light‑induced carriers in the graphene stack.
- **photocurrent measurements** — Technique used to probe spin polarization via the generated photocurrent.
- **photocurrent spectra measurement** — Spectrally resolved photocurrent using a monochromatized supercontinuum laser.
- **photoelectron diffraction** — Diffraction of photo‑emitted electrons used to probe surface structure.
- **photoelectron diffraction (ped)** — Element‑ and site‑resolved structural characterization at crystalline surfaces.
- **photonic crystals** — Periodic dielectric structures that control the flow of light.
- **photon‑assisted inelastic electron tunneling** — Process where tunneling electrons emit photons by losing energy, enabling light emission in the device.
- **photo‑tunneling spectroscopy** — Measurement of tunneling currents induced by optical excitation.
- **physical vapour deposition (pvd)** — Method for growing thin flakes by evaporating source material.
- **pick up** — Lifting a flake or stack with a stamp.
- **pico‑coax assembly** — Assembly procedure that attaches pico‑coax wires to PCBs and grounds shields.
- **piezo based sample rotator** — Piezoelectric driven sample rotation enabling vector magnetic fields.
- **piezo drive** — Piezoelectric actuation used in the NP-Drive controller.
- **piezo scanning** — Scanning driven by piezoelectric ceramic stack elements.
- **piezo‑driven rotation** — Use of piezoelectric actuators to rotate a sample at millikelvin temperatures.
- **piezo‑driven rotators** — Attocube’s rotator technology that works in ultra‑high vacuum and cryogenic conditions.
- **pilot laser** — Visible red pilot laser to ease optical alignment, automatically disables after a set time.
- **pin assignments** — Specification of pin functions and mappings.
- **plane-wave basis set** — Plane-wave basis set with cutoff energy 500 eV.
- **plasma cleaning** — Plasma cleaning of substrate before exfoliation.
- **plasma etching** — Low‑power plasma process to remove the bottom graphene layer.
- **plasmon spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic technique used to probe collective‑mode excitations (plasmons) in twisted van der Waals materials.
- **plasmon spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic approach aimed at measuring plasmon frequencies and dispersions.
- **plasmon‑assisted tunneling spectroscopy** — A variant of tunneling spectroscopy that focuses on features arising from plasmon emission.
- **point defect control** — Engineering of individual atomic defects to tune material properties.
- **polarity check** — Method to verify correct polarity by applying low voltage and observing motion direction.
- **polarization‑dependent photocurrent measurement along ferroelectric polarization direction** — Measurement probing photocurrent when light polarization is aligned with the ferroelectric axis.
- **polarization‑dependent photocurrent measurement along qy** — Measurement of photocurrent along the Qy direction as a function of light polarization.
- **polarized neutron diffraction** — Technique that confirmed the compensated Ising nature of CrSb.
- **polarized raman spectroscopy** — Backscattering Raman measurement with controlled polarization.
- **polymer membrane transfer** — Polymer‑membrane transfer technique used to fabricate the QTM tip.
- **polymer membrane transfer technique** — A method using polypropylene carbonate (PPC) to pick up and place vdW crystals.
- **polymer membrane transfer technique** — Method that uses a PPC film to pick up and place vdW crystals onto the Pt pyramid.
- **polymer‑based transfer technique** — Method to pick up and place NiI2 flakes using polymer dissolved in chloroform.
- **polymer‑membrane transfer** — Process to place graphite, hBN and graphene onto the pyramid.
- **position list** — Software list that stores positions and designs for the microscope control.
- **power dependence measurement** — Measurement of differential conductance dI/dV as a function of laser power and angle.
- **power_dependence.py** — Python script used to perform power‑dependent IV measurements.
- **prepare stamp** — Creating polymer stamps for transfer.
- **presets** — Saving and recalling channel configurations via preset buttons.
- **principal component analysis (pca)** — Multivariate analysis applied to Raman maps.
- **print function** — Built‑in Lua function used to output values to the console.
- **probing** — Experimental technique used to investigate atomic shapes in real space.
- **probing magnon chemical potential** — Method using QTM to measure the magnon chemical potential and density.
- **probing quantum spin liquids** — Use of the QTM to detect signatures of quantum spin liquid states.
- **probing sto phase transitions via graphene** — Transport‑based approach that correlates graphene resistivity, mobility and carrier density with STO soft‑phonon modes.
- **program for mesfet comparison** — Software used to compare MESFET characteristics and select the best‑matched pair.
- **programmatic control** — Control of controllers via Python, Matlab, LabView, C, C# or JSON‑based REST API.
- **programming new patterns** — Method to define a custom 256‑value voltage pattern for an axis of the ANC300.
- **projector-augmented wave (paw) method** — Projector-augmented wave pseudopotentials used for Ni and I valence states.
- **pt/ir tip etching** — Chemical etching process to fabricate Pt/Ir AFM tips.
- **pt/ir tip etching procedure** — Multi‑step process (NaCl etch, droplet formation, H₂SO₄ polish) to obtain tips of controlled curvature.
- **pump the ll chamber** — Procedure to pump the LL chamber after it has been sealed.
- **pumping down** — Procedure to pump down the chamber to vacuum.
- **purging** — Procedure to purge the chamber before venting.
- **pyprocar** — Python library for electronic‑structure post‑processing, used for band‑structure and spin‑texture plots.
- **pyramid deposition** — Layer‑by‑layer deposition that builds a pyramidal structure.
- **pyroelectric current measurement** — Experimental method used to detect ferroelectric polarization by measuring current during temperature changes.
- **pyroelectric current measurement** — Current recorded during temperature ramps after field‑cooling.
- **python code analysis of wire files** — Using Python packages to read and analyse Raman data exported from WIRE.
- **python driver ftir_measurement** — Python driver that controls the FTIR system.
- **python remote interface client** — Example Python program that communicates with the NP‑Drive using JSON‑RPC over a TCP socket.
- **p‑wave stm tip** — Scanning tunneling microscopy tip with p‑wave symmetry used for high‑resolution molecular‑orbital imaging.
- **q factor measurement** — Technique to determine the quality factor of the QTF.
- **qtm probing of k and k′ valleys** — Technique using QTM to measure the combined contribution of the K and K′ valleys.
- **qtm trajectory mapping** — Scanning of θ_QTM to trace the Γ–K_T–M–K_B–Γ path in momentum space.
- **qtm tunneling** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy performed with the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **qtm tunneling spectroscopy** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling measurement performed with the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **quantitative thermodynamic measurements** — Measurements of electronic compressibility and entropy enabled by the atomic SET probe.
- **quantum capacitors** — Capacitive structures probing quantum capacitance.
- **quantum dot model** — A simplified model for f‑like electrons assuming flat bands and a phenomenological lifetime broadening.
- **quantum hall effect measurement** — Transport measurement that probes the quantized Hall resistance of a 2DEG under magnetic field.
- **quantum lifetime spectroscopy** — Method to extract quasiparticle lifetimes from tunnelling line shapes.
- **quantum monte carlo** — Numerical method to solve many‑body quantum problems using stochastic sampling.
- **quantum monte carlo** — Stochastic many‑body simulation method.
- **quantum twisting microscopy** — Experimental technique based on the QTM for probing electronic and vibrational properties.
- **quantum‑dot spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that uses a gate‑tuned quantum dot to map electrostatic potentials.
- **quartz‑crystal thickness measurement** — Technique that uses the frequency shift of quartz crystals to determine film thickness.
- **raman analysis** — Data processing of Raman spectra using Python tools.
- **raman excitation of graphene** — Excitation of graphene Raman modes with a 532 nm CW laser.
- **raman spectroscopy** — Raman spectroscopy used to measure mechanical gaps in bilayer graphene.
- **raman spectroscopy** — Optical technique probing vibrational modes, used to study graphene.
- **random phase approximation** — RPA used to treat screened Coulomb interactions.
- **reading out network settings via usb** — Procedure to read network information from the device using a FAT32 USB flash drive and attoIPconfig.json.
- **real space multiple scattering theory** — Method used to describe propagation of the photoelectron wave in RPED calculations.
- **realistic theoretical simulations** — Computational modelling that incorporates experimental parameters to predict plasmon behaviour.
- **recalibration** — Re‑calibration of encoders performed by Attocube.
- **reflection microscopy** — Microscopy technique based on reflected light used in the QTM.
- **remote control** — External command interface to initiate stepping.
- **remote design** — Designing structures remotely using server software.
- **remote interface** — Control of the NP‑Drive via a JSON‑RPC server over TCP.
- **remote interlock connector** — Connector used to link the laser interlock to an external safety circuit.
- **renormalization group** — Theoretical method used to study logarithmic corrections to the dispersion.
- **renormalization group** — Analytical method to study scale‑dependent behavior of interactions
- **repolarization procedure** — Step‑wise DC voltage protocol to repolarize a piezo after wrong polarity.
- **reset axis** — Function that sets the current axis position as a new reference position.
- **resetting device to factory defaults via webserver** — Procedure to reset the device to factory defaults using the webserver application or touch panel.
- **resistance measurement** — Measuring resistance between connector pins to detect short circuits.
- **resistive encoder** — Optional encoder that uses resistive wires to sense angular position.
- **resistive encoder** — Integrated resistive encoder used for position feedback in the devices.
- **resistive readout** — Position sensing method based on resistance change.
- **resistive wiring** — Use of normal (high‑resistance) wires to connect the positioners, which can limit current and increase heat load.
- **resonance frequency measurement** — Technique to determine the QTF resonance frequency.
- **resonant photoelectron diffraction (rped)** — RPED combines magnetic information from XMCD with structural information from PED.
- **resonant photoelectron diffraction (rped)** — Technique that records diffraction patterns of resonantly emitted electrons.
- **resonant photoemission** — Photoemission enhanced by tuning photon energy to an absorption edge.
- **resonant photoemission diffraction (rped)** — Technique measuring circular dichroism in resonant photoemission diffraction.
- **resonant photoemission spectroscopy (rpes)** — Spectroscopic technique used to record valence‑band resonant photoemission.
- **resonant photoemission spectroscopy (rpes)** — Technique that measures photon‑energy‑dependent photoemission intensity.
- **resonant tunneling** — Tunnelling through discrete energy states that align with the bias.
- **rf sputtering** — Radio‑frequency sputtering technique employed for Al deposition.
- **rie etching** — Reactive‑ion etching step for graphene patterning and split‑gate definition.
- **rotation python script** — Python script that rotates the sample by a given angle (≈50°) and records feature trajectories.
- **rotation_target function** — Function inside attocube_driver.py that executes a rotation sweep.
- **rpa calculation** — Computation of the screened interaction using the RPA formalism.
- **rpa screening calculation** — Numerical computation of the in‑layer screening potential using the random‑phase approximation.
- **sample mounting** — Mounting a sample holder onto a positioner using appropriate screws while avoiding torque.
- **sample transfer** — Procedure to move a specimen into the chamber, secure it on the rail, and close the valve.
- **save as** — Menu command in CleWin that saves the current file as a .GDS database.
- **sawtooth voltage drive** — Application of a sawtooth voltage to the piezo element to generate incremental rotation steps.
- **sawtooth voltage pulse technique** — Application of sawtooth‑shaped voltage pulses to a piezoelectric actuator to generate stick‑slip motion.
- **scan function** — Lua function that ramps voltage continuously on an axis with a defined step width.
- **scan piezo actuation** — Fine positioning of STM tip using a piezo element.
- **scanning** — AFM scan initiated by pressing ‘scan’ and ‘engage’ in the software.
- **scanning atomic set technique** — Scanning probe approach where the system of interest is placed on the tip and scanned over a fixed defect.
- **scanning conductance microscopy (scm)** — AFM‑based technique that measures local conductance.
- **scanning d²i/dv² mapping** — Spatially resolved measurement of the second derivative of the tunneling current to reveal band features.
- **scanning electron microscopy (sem)** — Technique for imaging surfaces with a focused electron beam.
- **scanning electron microscopy (sem) imaging** — SEM used to visualise the Pt pyramid and tip geometry.
- **scanning electron microscopy imaging** — High‑resolution imaging using the electron beam column of the Crossbeam.
- **scanning flat bands as a function of filling** — Experimental mapping of the flat‑band spectra while varying carrier density.
- **scanning interferometer** — Conceptual mode of the QTM where multiple tunnelling paths interfere, enabling phase mapping.
- **scanning near‑field optical microscopy** — Microscopy technique that employed a tuning fork for distance control (Ref‑47).
- **scanning near‑field optical microscopy (snom)** — AFM‑based optical imaging technique.
- **scanning photocurrent mapping** — Raster‑scan of a focused laser spot (2 µm) to record spatial photocurrent distribution.
- **scanning probe measurement** — General scanning‑probe approach used for the AFM scan.
- **scanning quantum dot microscopy** — Using a quantum dot as a local probe of electrostatic potentials.
- **scanning single‑electron transistor microscopy** — Imaging of local electrostatic potential by a movable SET.
- **scanning tip technique** — Use of a scanning tip to probe the sample surface.
- **scanning tunneling microscopy (stm)** — Surface‑sensitive technique that maps local density of states.
- **scanning tunneling microscopy (stm)** — Tunneling‑based SPM technique referenced for tip fabrication.
- **scanning tunneling spectroscopy** — Local probe of electronic density of states.
- **scanning tunnelling microscopy** — Imaging surface electronic structure via tunnelling current.
- **scanning tunnelling microscopy (stm)** — Conventional STM that averages over all momenta, mentioned for comparison.
- **scanning voltage supply** — Voltage source that drives each scan direction of an ANS scanner.
- **scattering approach** — Theoretical method to calculate tunneling currents using scattering theory.
- **scattering approach** — Theoretical technique that treats the tip‑sample junction as a scattering problem to estimate the tunneling current.
- **scattering‑type near‑field optical spectroscopy** — Infrared nano‑spectroscopy used in prior studies of high‑energy interband plasmons in MATBG.
- **second derivative tunneling spectroscopy (d²i/dv² maps)** — Measurement of the curvature of the tunneling current to reveal singularities at band alignments.
- **second harmonic generation (shg)** — Nonlinear optical process that converts photons to twice their frequency.
- **second harmonic generation (shg) measurement** — Temperature‑dependent SHG in parallel configuration on the PVD‑grown NiI2 flake, showing a jump at the helimagnetic transition.
- **second toy model** — Simplified theoretical model used to reproduce features observed in tunneling measurements of MLG‑TBG heterostructures.
- **second-order calculation** — Higher‑order computation required for contributions involving intralayer coupling with interlayer tunneling.
- **second-order perturbation theory** — Perturbative method treating Hintra and HT contributions.
- **second-order perturbation theory** — Perturbative method used to calculate the intralayer electron‑phonon contribution.
- **second‑harmonic generation (shg) measurement** — Nonlinear optical measurement detecting frequency‑doubled light.
- **self‑consistency equation** — Equation derived from stationarity of the total energy that determines μ_f and μ_c.
- **self‑consistent calculations** — Numerical method used to verify the analytical approximation for Au(110) STM corrugation.
- **self‑consistent electronic structure calculation** — Accurate computational approach for surface LDOS, feasible only for small unit cells.
- **self‑consistent hartree approximation** — Approximation used in the THF model for all interactions other than the on‑site f‑f term.
- **self‑consistent hartree calculations** — Mean‑field electrostatic modeling of screening effects in the graphene/hBN system.
- **sensor type selection** — Drop‑down selection in the UI to choose between RES and IDS sensor types for closed‑loop positioning.
- **set up coincidence point** — Procedure to set the spatial coincidence point between ion and electron beams.
- **setactorparametersjson** — API call used to programmatically set closed‑loop control parameters.
- **setdrivechannel** — Command that turns on the high‑voltage drive channel relay for a given channel and turns all others off.
- **setdrivechannelsoff** — Command that turns off all high‑voltage drive channel relays.
- **setfc console command** — Console command used to enter Feature Codes.
- **setmode function** — Function of the RES interface to configure measurement mode.
- **setsensorsoff()** — Command setSensorsOff() turns off all sensors by using the NP-Amp multiplexor to disconnect them from internal circuitry.
- **setstoplimit(channel, threshold)** — Command setStopLimit sets the stop limit threshold for closed loop motion.
- **setting step voltage** — Command or property used to set the voltage increment applied at each step of an axis.
- **shared lines for res‑position** — Shared ground and supply lines for RES elements across all three axes.
- **shared mode** — Shared mode option for RES sensor read‑out.
- **shear‑mode piezo actuation** — Actuation of the piezo stacks in shear mode to produce linear motion of the macor.
- **shielded ethernet cable** — Cable recommended for the GbLAN Ethernet connection to reduce susceptibility to ground loops.
- **shock damping with foam or pad** — Placing foam or a pad under the scanner to dampen shocks during handling.
- **shock‑absorption technique** — Use of a soft foam plate or similar pad to protect positioners from mechanical shocks during handling.
- **short wiring connections** — Guideline to keep all wiring between preamp and nanopositioners as short as possible to minimise parasitic resistance and inductance.
- **shubnikov‑de haas effect** — Technique based on oscillations in longitudinal resistivity used to extract Fermi‑surface shape, 2D electron density and effective mass.
- **siesta method** — Order‑N ab‑initio density‑functional method for materials simulation.
- **sigmoid fitting** — Fit of dI/dV versus twist angle with a sigmoid function to locate the heavy‑light transition.
- **simulation of structural rped patterns** — Computational method to generate purely structural RPED maps for each sublattice.
- **sine voltage excitation** — Sine voltage of varying frequency applied to the piezo to excite the positioner.
- **single‑domain selection procedure** — Procedure combining linear dichroism imaging and electric‑field cooling to isolate a single spin‑helix domain.
- **single‑particle approximation** — Theoretical treatment that neglects many‑body interactions.
- **single‑spin inelastic tunneling spectroscopy** — A spectroscopic method to probe individual spin excitations via tunneling.
- **slice** — Cutting chips as instructed by Vittoria Finazzi.
- **slip‑stick drive technique** — Slip‑stick driving mechanism that translates a table by applying sawtooth voltage pulses to a piezo.
- **slip‑stick motion** — Patented slip‑stick actuation mechanism employed in the attocube positioners.
- **slip‑stick principle** — Actuation method where a sawtooth voltage expands a piezo element, then friction releases a small step of motion.
- **slope detection** — Amplitude‑based detection method that is unsuitable for high‑Q cantilevers in vacuum.
- **smearing in energy** — Artificial broadening applied to the spectral function to mimic lifetime effects observed experimentally.
- **solder** — Technique used to join coaxial wires to PCB vias and Fischer female connectors.
- **soldering** — Process of joining components with solder.
- **sonication cleaning** — Ultrasonic cleaning of glass slides in acetone and ISO.
- **spatial current measurements** — Spatially resolved current measurements performed with the AFM.
- **spatial scanning tip imaging** — Scanning of the tip over atomic defects to map the tip shape in situ.
- **specifying positioner type** — Selecting the type of positioner (e.g., linear, rotational) in the UI.
- **spectral function peak and lifetime analysis** — Extraction of peak positions and linewidths versus quasiparticle energy.
- **spectral‑function calculation** — Evaluation of the imaginary part of the screened interaction to obtain plasmon spectral weight.
- **spectral‑function peak and lifetime analysis** — Procedure to extract peak positions and quasiparticle lifetimes from tunneling spectra.
- **spectroscopic signatures** — Spectroscopy used to identify many‑body correlation features.
- **spectroscopy** — Measurement of the local density of states used to probe flat bands.
- **spherical-tip approximation** — Approximation that treats the tip as a locally spherical s‑wave source for tunneling calculations.
- **spin transport** — Transport of spin angular momentum used to detect spinon excitations.
- **spin-coating** — Spreading polymer films by rapid rotation.
- **spin‑resolved circularly polarized resonant photoemission** — Combines spin resolution, circular polarization, and resonant excitation in photoemission.
- **spin‑resolved rpes** — Proposed spin‑resolved resonant photoemission probe for local magnetic moments in antiferromagnets.
- **stacking several stages** — Method of assembling multiple positioner stages to obtain multi‑axis motion.
- **stacking with flipping** — Transfer method involving flipping the stack using PMMA and PVC stamps.
- **stacking with pc** — Transfer method using polycarbonate solution.
- **stage z movement** — Moving stage Z up/down until the laser displacement meter reads zero.
- **standard noise analysis technique** — Technique described by Yang et al. to evaluate the noise spectrum density of the QTF circuit.
- **standard photolithography** — Photolithographic process to pattern Ti/Au electrodes.
- **standard pin numbering** — Standard pin numbering scheme applied to Fischer female connectors.
- **state‑space representation** — Formulation of the equations of motion in state‑space matrices A, B, C.
- **static afm** — AFM mode in which the cantilever is held at a fixed deflection.
- **step method** — Method invoked as :step(DIR,N) to move N steps in direction DIR.
- **stepper protocol** — Communication protocol where line A indicates increment and line B indicates direction, available in LVTTL/LVDS and open/closed loop.
- **stepping** — Generating discrete motion steps with the ANM300 module.
- **stepping pulses** — Voltage pulses applied to piezo actuators to produce discrete mechanical steps.
- **stepwise move** — Technique to set the number of steps implemented with each move.
- **stepwise movement** — Stepwise movement of the positioner to verify IDS measurement direction.
- **step‑function fitting** — Fit of the conduction‑band edge intensity to extract the larger‑angle twist θ_TBG.
- **stig** — STIG tool used for astigmatism correction.
- **stm design optimization** — Application of the optimization criterion to improve STM performance.
- **stm studies** — Scanning tunneling microscopy investigations of monolayer 1T‑TaSe₂.
- **stop method** — Method invoked as :stop() to halt any ongoing actions.
- **stop on ids stop limit** — Option that halts motion when the IDS stop limit is reached to avoid measurement interruption.
- **stopmotion** — Command that stops any motion in progress.
- **stoppositioning()** — Command stopPositioning stops any closed loop control in progress.
- **substrate modification** — Altering substrate properties to affect graphene's electronic behavior.
- **sum rule** — Method for calculating tunneling matrix elements
- **superconducting wiring** — Use of superconducting wires to connect the positioners, minimizing resistance and heat transfer.
- **sweeping drain bias** — Method of varying the drain bias to obtain I‑V curves of the MESFET.
- **switching axis mode** — Changing the operating mode of an axis (e.g., step, offset, ground) via console commands or Lua properties.
- **symmetry analysis** — Detailed examination of crystal and magnetic symmetries.
- **symmetry analysis** — Analytical approach to determine allowed spin‑polarization patterns from crystal symmetry.
- **t matrix** — Transition‑matrix element describing the inelastic tunneling process.
- **tapping mode afm** — Atomic force microscopy operated in tapping (intermittent contact) mode.
- **tcp socket** — Network socket used by the Python client to connect to the NP‑Drive’s JSON‑RPC server.
- **telnet** — Telnet protocol used to access the standard and Lua consoles on ports 7230 and 7231.
- **temperature variation measurement** — Measurements performed at 300 K, 77 K and 4.2 K to evaluate motor performance.
- **temperature‑dependent cpge measurement** — Experimental protocol measuring CPGE while varying temperature.
- **temperature‑dependent measurement** — Series of I‑V and dI/dV recordings performed while varying the sample temperature.
- **test function** — Simple Lua function that prints numbers from 1 to n.
- **theoretical calculation** — Numerical evaluation of in‑layer and interlayer contributions to electron‑phonon coupling.
- **theoretical calculation of fermi surface touching conditions** — Modeling of conditions where Fermi surfaces intersect.
- **theoretical calculations** — Analytical and numerical modeling of interacting dispersion and tunneling spectra.
- **theoretical calculations** — Development of theoretical models and calculations
- **theoretical derivation** — Analytical calculation of electron‑phonon matrix elements and Hamiltonians presented in the text.
- **theoretical epc model** — Model describing in‑layer and inter‑layer EPC mechanisms for TA, LA, TO and LO modes.
- **theoretical tunneling calculation** — Analytical derivation of the T‑matrix for inelastic tunneling.
- **theory model for inelastic tunnelling through phonon emission** — Analytical framework describing two mechanisms of electron‑phonon coupling in tunnelling.
- **thermal anchoring** — Customized thermal anchoring of measurement lines to reduce heat load.
- **thermal card verification** — Use of a thermal card to visualise the THz spot after coarse alignment.
- **thermal drift correction** — Method to correct thermal drift during AFM scans by tracking defect positions.
- **thermal evaporation** — Evaporation method that melts material in a heated tungsten boat.
- **thermal excitation calibration** — Method that uses the thermal motion of the QTF to calibrate the sensor sensitivity.
- **thf model** — Model with strong c–f hybridization used to capture Dirac revival phenomenology.
- **three‑capacitor electrostatic model** — Electrostatic description of the QTM junction using two graphene‑graphene capacitances and a back‑gate capacitance.
- **threshold spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method probing the onset of excitations to extract statistical information.
- **thz coupling via waveguide** — Delivery of THz radiation to the sample through a multimode hollow‑tube waveguide.
- **thz laser coupling technique** — Coupling a THz laser into the cryostat via a hollow‑tube waveguide and interchangeable lenses.
- **tight-binding hamiltonian model with pz orbital** — TB model that includes carbon pz orbitals to compute charge density and ESP.
- **tight‑binding description** — A simplified lattice model used to express the transformed graphene Hamiltonian after the gauge transformation.
- **tilt eucentricity adjustment** — Procedure to adjust tilt for eucentric alignment of the sample.
- **time-dependent schrödinger equation** — Equation governing the evolution of the STM system.
- **tip fabrication** — Process of making AFM tips suitable for QTM measurements.
- **tip mounting on qtf** — Series of steps to attach a sharp tip to a quartz tuning fork and achieve high Q factor.
- **tip preparation (fib deposition & graphite transfer)** — Focused‑ion‑beam deposition of a short pyramid tip followed by transfer of a 2‑D material (graphite) onto the tip.
- **torque avoidance during mounting** — Holding the scanner plate to prevent applying torque to the piezo stacks.
- **touch‑screen gui** — Touch‑screen GUI for menu navigation and parameter setting.
- **toy model calculation** — Simplified analytical model that includes Dirac and parabolic bands to reproduce onset and Dirac‑matching features.
- **transfer function derivation** — Analytical method to relate horizontal disturbances to tip‑sample deviations.
- **transfer‑function measurement** — Method to determine the frequency response of the tip‑sample distance.
- **transmission‑function correction** — Post‑processing step that corrects spectra for the measured transmission of the optical path.
- **transport measurement** — Electrical measurement of conductance, resistance, or related quantities.
- **triangulation method** — Procedure that locates superconducting nodes by intersecting tip Fermi circles at different twist angles.
- **triangulation of nodal momenta** — Geometric method using two tip rotation angles to locate nodal points.
- **trigger commands** — Commands (settu, gettu, settd, gettd, setto, getto) that modify input or output trigger settings.
- **trigger communication** — Communication protocol for sending and receiving real‑time trigger signals.
- **trigger configuration dialog** — Dialog to link trigger inputs to axes and motion directions.
- **trigger configuration dialog** — Dialog for configuring trigger settings.
- **tri‑channel upgrade** — Upgrade that allows simultaneous driving of three positioners.
- **ttl triggering** — Digital triggering method using TTL levels supplied via the SubD connector.
- **tungsten surfaces with protruding atoms or clusters model** — Tip model that includes protruding tungsten atoms or clusters.
- **tunnel rate measurement** — Technique that extracts spin splitting by measuring electron tunneling rates as a function of magnetic field direction and strength.
- **tunneling current calculation** — Theoretical calculation of tunneling current using Bistritzer‑MacDonald model and Fermi’s golden rule.
- **tunneling hamiltonian formalism** — Theoretical framework used to derive the expression for the tunneling current.
- **tunneling measurement** — Current‑voltage spectroscopy across the hBN barrier.
- **tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that probes electronic states via tunneling current.
- **tunnelling current measurement (i‑v)** — Recording of differential conductance dI/dV as a function of bias voltage.
- **tunnelling spectroscopy** — Measurement of dI/dV as a function of bias and momentum.
- **tunnelling theory** — Theoretical framework describing quantum tunnelling.
- **turning knobs (view and edit)** — Two turning knobs (VIEW and EDIT) for navigation and value adjustment.
- **twist_measure.py** — Python file that runs FTIR+twist‑angle measurements.
- **twist_measure.py** — Script that measures FTIR together with twist angle.
- **twisted josephson interferometry** — Interferometric technique that probes the symmetry of the superconducting order parameter by twisting Josephson junctions.
- **twisted wires** — Practice of twisting paired conductors (e.g., CH1+ and CH1-) for piezo drive connections.
- **two‑center approximation** — Assumes the interlayer hopping amplitude depends only on the distance between two atomic sites.
- **ultrafast atomic‑scale scanning tunnelling spectroscopy** — Time‑resolved STM probing electronic states with sub‑picosecond resolution.
- **ultrafast optical switching** — Rapid modulation of infrared plasmon polaritons with optical pulses.
- **ultrasensitive plasmonic detection** — Highly sensitive detection of molecules using graphene‑based plasmonic enhancement.
- **ultrastable tunable laser** — Laser source from Stuttgart Instruments Alpha used in the THz setup.
- **unpolarized inelastic neutron scattering (ins)** — Time‑of‑flight neutron spectroscopy used to measure spin excitations.
- **usb communication** — One of the transport methods used by LabView drivers to talk to the ANC300.
- **using zemax** — Ray‑tracing software employed for lens design.
- **vacancy spectroscopy** — Probing electronic states by creating and measuring atomic vacancies.
- **valence‑band photoemission** — Spectroscopy that probes occupied and unoccupied valence states.
- **vapor deposition** — Vapor deposition method for synthesizing magnetic van‑der‑Waals NiI₂ crystals.
- **venting** — Procedure to vent the chamber using N2.
- **vertical graphene heterostructures** — Stacked 2D material architecture used to build tunnelling transistors.
- **vibration isolation** — Use of a swing and acoustic enclosure to suppress vibrations during rotation.
- **vibration source (internal piezo)** — Internal piezo of a positioner used as vibration source by applying sine voltage or chirp signal.
- **virtual instrument (vi)** — LabVIEW VIs that control the ANC300.
- **voltage-dependent spectral measurements** — Recording emitted spectra while varying applied voltage.
- **wait method** — Method invoked as :wait() to wait until stepping completes.
- **water‑assisted pdms pick‑up** — Water‑assisted PDMS method to pick up flakes.
- **waveguide simulation** — Numerical modelling of multimodal propagation in a hollow waveguide.
- **weak‑coupling theory** — Perturbative approach assuming interactions are small compared to kinetic energy.
- **webserver application** — Webserver application that enhances usability of the AMC300 controller.
- **wet chemical etching** — Selective etching method employing acid mixtures for InAs and AlSb.
- **wet transfer of cvd graphene** — Procedure to move CVD graphene from copper to a target substrate using chemical etching and fishing steps.
- **wet‑membrane transfer technique** — Method for attaching vdW material to AFM tip apex.
- **wide‑field cross‑polarized imaging** — Large‑area cross‑polarized microscopy used at 30 K to map domain patterns.
- **xcrysden** — XCrySDen software for visualizing crystalline structures and spin helices.
- **xmcd spectroscopy** — Measurement of XMCD spectra using the crystal‑field multiplet model.
- **xmcd sum rules** — Relations that allow extraction of spin and orbital moments from XMCD spectra.
- **x‑ray absorption spectroscopy (xas)** — Technique used to obtain absorption spectra in the study.
- **x‑ray circular dichroism** — Technique using circularly polarized X‑rays to probe magnetic properties.
- **x‑ray diffraction** — Bragg geometry diffraction using Cu Kα radiation.
- **x‑ray diffraction** — Structural characterization technique applied to the CVT‑grown NiI2 crystal.
- **x‑ray magnetic circular dichroism** — XMCD technique probing magnetic properties with X‑rays.
- **x‑ray magnetic circular dichroism (xmcd)** — Element‑selective spectroscopy that measures the difference in absorption of left‑ and right‑circularly polarized X‑rays.
- **x‑ray magnetic circular dichroism (xmcd)** — Technique that measures magnetic circular dichroism in X‑ray absorption.
- **x‑ray powder diffraction** — Powder diffraction used to verify the phase purity of CVT‑grown NiI2 crystals.
- **yambo code** — First‑principles software for many‑body perturbation theory (RPA, GW, BSE, etc.).
- **yambo many‑body calculations** — Software suite employed for RPA, IPA, BSE, GW‑QP, LRC, etc.
- **z focusing with micro‑positioner** — Fine adjustment of sample height (≈‑8000 µm) to achieve focus.
- **zero‑bias coulomb peak tracking** — Scanning the tip across the defect while monitoring the gate‑voltage position of the zero‑bias Coulomb peak to map the local electrostatic potential.
- **zero‑bias measurement protocol** — Protocol that uses the tip Fermi surface at zero bias to detect nodal points in the pairing potential.
- **zero‑bias photocurrent measurement** — Photocurrent recorded at zero applied bias while warming the sample.
- **zero‑field cooling** — Cooling the sample without an external electric field.
- **zurich api** — Zurich API accessed at 127.0.0.1:8006 to control signal output and acquire data.
- **μsr spectroscopy** — Muon spin rotation spectroscopy used to probe magnetic order.

- **aa region** — Region of AA stacking where lattice reconstruction occurs.
- **aa site** — High‑symmetry region of the moiré pattern where electron‑deficient molecules tend to bind.
- **aa stacking area** — Region of TBG where the two layers are directly on top of each other, hosting the strongest localized fields.
- **aa stacking region** — Moiré region where carbon atoms of the two layers sit on top of each other.
- **ab site** — Moiré region where electron‑sufficient molecules tend to bind.
- **ab stacking area** — Region of TBG with AB stacking, attracting electron‑sufficient molecules.
- **ab stacking region** — Moiré region with Bernal (AB) stacking between the two graphene layers.
- **abraham a. mancilla** — Co‑author of the publication.
- **abrikosov fermion representation** — Fermionic representation of spin operators used in the mean‑field treatment.
- **abrikosov fermions** — Fermionic representation of spins used to describe gapless quantum spin liquids.
- **absence of la mode** — Missing longitudinal acoustic phonon signal in the measurements.
- **absence of longitudinal acoustic (la) phonon peak while za and ta are observed** — Acoustic phonon spectroscopy shows ZA and TA features but no LA feature, indicating mode‑selective EPC.
- **absence of magnon band splitting in mnf₂** — Experimental observation that MnF₂ does not exhibit altermagnetic magnon band splitting.
- **absorption coefficient (m⁻¹)** — Exponential attenuation coefficient per metre.
- **absorption coefficient finding** — Absorption coefficient for 40 % RH measured as 0.02 cm⁻¹, in agreement with literature.
- **absorption measurement (rh)** — Experiment that determines the absorption coefficient of RH using the FIR beam.
- **ab‑initio derived tight‑binding model** — Microscopic electronic model including long‑range hopping beyond NNN.
- **acceleration voltage 30 kev** — Voltage applied to the ion beam during FIB deposition.
- **accurate identification of twist angles from i‑v** — How to determine the twist angle of a graphene stack using measured I‑V characteristics.
- **acoustic enclosure** — Enclosure that reduces acoustic noise during continuous rotation measurements.
- **acoustic phonon branches (ta, za)** — Low‑energy phonon modes identified in the graphite dispersion.
- **acoustic phonon coupling diminishes as momentum → 0** — Standard acoustic phonons show decreasing EPC when their momentum approaches zero.
- **acoustic phonon mode** — Low‑energy lattice vibration with linear dispersion.
- **acoustic plasmon** — Lower‑energy plasmon branch with nearly linear dispersion, weighted toward the low‑doped layer.
- **acoustic plasmon linear dispersion above intraband gap** — Acoustic plasmon exhibits a nearly linear dispersion slightly above the intraband transition region.
- **acoustic plasmon weight dominates low‑doped layer 2** — Acoustic plasmon carries larger charge density weight on the low‑doped graphene layer.
- **acoustic plasmons** — Low-energy collective charge oscillations in the double-layer graphene system.
- **acoustic ta mode energy** — Measured energy of the transverse acoustic phonon as a function of twist angle.
- **acoustic‑phonon epc dominated by interlayer coupling** — Observation that the electron‑acoustic‑phonon (phason) coupling strength is mainly due to interlayer mechanisms.
- **acoustic‑phonon‑mediated superconductivity** — Superconductivity driven by acoustic phonons.
- **acs photonics 2017 doi:10.1021/acsphotonics.7b00695** — Paper containing the presented theory, supporting information, and derivations.
- **active area (a)** — Geometrical area of the tunneling device that enters the current‑density formulas.
- **additional resonance near 2.26 v appears at low temperature (ws₂)** — A second feature emerges in the WS₂ device at low temperature, indicating coupling to higher‑energy excitons.
- **adi stern** — Acknowledged for theoretical discussions.
- **adjustable legs** — Mechanical legs that allow precise control of the tilt angle between cantilever and sample.
- **adjustable mirror** — Mirror used for FTIR alignment.
- **adsorption energy (e_ad)** — Energy associated with molecule adsorption on TBG.
- **adsorption energy difference** — Variation of adsorption energy between AA and AB regions for different molecules.
- **adsorption energy measurement** — Quantitative determination of molecule‑sample binding energy.
- **adsorption energy measurement of alcl₃ on tbg** — Specific run measuring AlCl₃ adsorption energy.
- **adsorption energy measurement of ccl₂ on tbg** — Specific run measuring CCl₂ adsorption energy.
- **adsorption energy measurement of nh₃ on tbg** — Specific run measuring NH₃ adsorption energy.
- **adsorption energy measurement of so₃ on tbg** — Specific run measuring SO₃ adsorption energy.
- **adsorption energy scanning** — Computational scan of molecule adsorption energies on different stacking domains.
- **adsorption energy scanning on tbg** — Experimental scan of adsorption energy for several molecules across AB‑AA‑BA path.
- **adsorption energy variation with position** — Adsorption energy depends on molecule location (AB, AA, BA).
- **adsorption preference** — Tendency of a molecule to adsorb at AA, AB, or BA regions of the moiré pattern.
- **adsorption scanning experiment** — Systematic measurement of molecule adsorption energy and LDOS along the AB‑AA‑BA path.
- **advances in coherent magnonics** — Nat. Rev. Mater. 6, 1114 (2021) covering coherent magnonic phenomena.
- **afm applied force** — Use of an atomic‑force‑microscope tip to exert a controllable normal force, thereby setting the local pressure on the junction.
- **afm approach procedure** — Standard AFM tip‑sample approach with reduced set‑point force (10–20 nN).
- **afm brooming cleaning protocol** — Standardized set of AFM steps for removing residues from 2D material devices.
- **afm cleaning finding** — Scanning the heterostructure with AFM in contact mode removes polymer residues.
- **afm force applying pressure** — Atomic‑force‑microscope tip used to generate GPa‑scale pressure across the junction.
- **afm laser** — The laser that probes the AFM tip and must be aligned with the four quadrants.
- **afm legs** — Adjustable legs of the atomic force microscope used to tune tilt angle for stable tip‑sample contact.
- **afm optical camera alignment** — Coarse alignment using the AFM’s built‑in optical camera to centre the tip over the rotation axis.
- **afm scan (tapping mode) of substrate** — A tapping‑mode AFM measurement performed on a substrate.
- **afm tip stored in plastic box labelled frank** — Silicon cantilever tip used for the AFM scans.

- **ag mirror** — Silver‑coated mirror used in the THz optical train.
- **agarwal and mishchenko, dynamic response functions of two-dimensional dirac fermions with screened coulomb and short-range interactions, phys. rev. b 102, 125421 (2020)** — Theoretical paper on dynamic response functions of 2D Dirac fermions with screened Coulomb and short‑range interactions.

- **agreement of stm theory with au(110) experiment** — The simple STM theory reproduces experimental STM images of the Au(110) surface.
- **agreement with electrostatic model for |vb| > 0.3 v** — Experimental G band shift matches model predictions at higher bias.
- **ag‑wave altermagnet** — Altermagnetic material with Ag‑type electronic bands.
