- **airy pattern** — Diffraction pattern observed when the iris is nearly closed, used to centre the beam.
- **alannah m. hallas** — Corresponding author of the publication.
- **alcl₃ molecule** — Aluminum trichloride used in adsorption‑energy measurements.
- **alcl₃ molecule** — Electron‑deficient molecule studied for adsorption on TBG.
- **alex d. gottlieb** — Author of the tutorial article on Bardeen's tunneling theory in STM.
- **align backreflection** — Procedure that uses the golden mirror, iris and a white card to superpose the reflected beam with the incoming beam.
- **align to iris** — Procedure that uses the top mirror and the iris to centre the VIS beam.
- **aligning thz to hene** — Aligning the THz beam to a HeNe visible reference.
- **alignment accuracy** — Precision with which the twist angle between graphene layers is set.
- **alignment cube with internal mirror** — Cube containing a mirror that turns the beam by 90° to align the detector path.
- **alignment of fir** — Alignment stage for the far‑infrared (FIR) laser beam.
- **als1 controller** — Controller used with the 295STAB Laser Stabiliser.
- **als1 unit** — ALS1 unit that can be placed on the FIRL‑PS platform.
- **altermagnetic instabilities from quantum geometry** — Phys. Rev. B 111, 174407 (2025) discussing instabilities driven by quantum geometry.
- **altermagnetic lifting of kramers spin degeneracy** — Nature 626, 517 (2024) reporting spin‑degeneracy lifting in altermagnets.
- **altermagnetic textures** — Spatially varying order in altermagnets.
- **altermagnetism in crsb project** — Research effort aimed at uncovering altermagnetic phenomena in CrSb.
- **alternating twist quadrilayer graphene** — Quadrilayer graphene with alternating twist angles.
- **alternating‑twist magic‑angle trilayer graphene** — Trilayer graphene with alternating twist angles showing electric‑field‑tunable superconductivity.
- **altitud màxima de treball** — Altitud màxima operativa fins a 2400 m.s.n.m.
- **altvater et al., revealing the charge density wave proximity effect in graphene on 1t-tas2, arxiv:2201.09195** — Preprint reporting the charge‑density‑wave proximity effect in graphene on 1T‑TaS₂.
- **aluminum wirebonds** — Aluminum wires used to bond the gold pads to external circuitry.
- **ambient conditions at room temperature (t = 300 k)** — Measurement environment: ambient atmosphere and 300 K temperature.
- **ambient temperature** — Recommended workspace ambient temperature of 18 °C–22 °C.
- **ambiguity** — Uncertainty arising from multiple intersection points or rotational symmetry.
- **ambiguity resolved by stronger trigonal warping tip** — Demonstration that using a bilayer‑graphene tip reduces intersection ambiguity.
- **ammonia‑15 (15nh3)** — Isotopically enriched ammonia used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **amplitude 25 v** — Voltage amplitude applied to the rotation stage when in contact.
- **amplitude decreases with probe alignment** — Signal amplitude gradually drops when the MLG probe aligns from the top to the bottom TBG layer.
- **analytical expression for spin‑dependent tunneling current at t=0** — Derived formula linking the tunneling current to the spin structure factor S(q,ω) in the zero‑temperature limit.
- **analytical solutions** — Approximate analytical solutions for spectral properties.
- **andor ixon ultra camera** — Camera for detecting emitted light.
- **andor ixon ultra emccd camera** — Camera used for emission‑area imaging and intensity‑map acquisition.
- **andreev reflection in scanning tunneling spectroscopy of unconventional superconductors** — Paper discussing Andreev processes in STM of unconventional superconductors.
- **andreev versus tunneling spectroscopy of unconventional flat band superconductors** — ArXiv preprint comparing Andreev and tunneling probes in flat‑band systems.
- **andrei k. geim** — Physicist, co‑author of several graphene papers.
- **angle φ_k** — Angular variable in momentum space.
- **angle φ_{k}** — Angle between the tip Dirac point and the K‑valley direction.
- **angle+ftir experiment** — Measurement combining angle control with FTIR spectroscopy.
- **angle‑bias experiment** — Measurement combining twist angle and bias.
- **angle‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy** — A spectroscopy method that maps d²I/dV² as a function of bias voltage and twist angle to probe electronic dispersion.
- **angle‑tuned gross‑neveu quantum criticality in twisted bilayer graphene: a quantum monte carlo study (2025)** — Quantum Monte Carlo investigation of Gross‑Neveu criticality in TBG.
- **angular dependence of spin structure factor** — Variation of S(q, ω) with the twist angle between graphene and the QSL barrier.
- **angular dependence of tip wave function (l,m)** — Higher‑order angular components of the tip wave function beyond the s‑wave term.
- **angular width (~0.2°)** — The angular width of the tunneling peak around θ = 0°, used to estimate momentum conservation.
- **anisotropic dielectric breakdown** — Direction‑dependent dielectric breakdown strength in hBN crystals.
- **anisotropic dielectric breakdown strength of single crystal hexagonal boron nitride** — ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 2016 paper measuring dielectric breakdown anisotropy in hBN.
- **anisotropic gaps** — Superconducting gaps that vary with momentum direction.
- **anisotropic highly‑dispersive antiferromagnetic spin waves** — Spin waves in CrSb with different velocities in‑plane and out‑of‑plane.
- **anna kuzmina** — Co‑author of the Nano Lett. 2021 paper on resonant light emission from graphene/h‑BN/graphene tunnel junctions.
- **anomalous thermal conductivity** — Unusual thermal‑conductivity behavior observed in α‑RuCl₃.
- **anomalous thermal conductivity and magnetic torque response in the honeycomb magnet α‑rucl₃** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 118, 187203 (2017) by I.A. Leahy, C.A. Pocs, P.E. Siegfried, D. Graf, S.-H. Do, K.-Y. Choi, B. Normand, M. Lee.
- **antenna coupling** — Integration of nano‑antennas with tunnel junctions to boost photon emission.
- **antenna‑coupled photon emission from hbn tunnel junctions demonstrated** — Hexagonal boron nitride tunnel junctions emit photons when coupled to nano‑antennas.
- **antiferromagnetic resonance** — Microwave spectroscopy probing antiferromagnetic order.
- **antiferromagnetic resonance and terahertz continuum in α‑rucl₃** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 227201 (2017) by A. Little et al.
- **antitip** — Tip configuration that attracts electron‑sufficient molecules due to lattice symmetry.
- **anti‑freeze (coolant additive)** — Anti‑freeze added to the water coolant of the closed‑cycle thermocirculator to prevent freezing.
- **apl mater. 2024, 12, 010901** — Research article authored by N. Pryds et al. published in APL Materials in 2024.
- **applications in photonic integrated circuits** — Potential use of Gr/h‑BN/Gr light‑emitting tunnel junctions as on‑chip photon sources.
- **approach to equilibrium of electrons, plasmons, and phonons in quantum and classical plasmas (1962)** — Foundational work on the dynamics of electrons, plasmons, and phonons.
- **approximation methods for stm theory** — Simplifications (e.g., point probe, small bias) used to obtain tractable expressions for the tunneling current.
- **approximations adequate for quantitative stm** — The approximations of Sec II A are sufficient for quantitative understanding of STM.
- **are plasmons the pairing glue in the superconducting state of matbg?** — Question about the role of plasmons in superconductivity of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **are the observed inelastic processes momentum‑conserving?** — The central question addressed by varying the twist angle to see whether phonon‑assisted tunnelling restores momentum conservation.
- **argon‑filled glovebox** — Glovebox with O₂ and H₂O below 10 ppm used for contamination‑free assembly.
- **arxiv 2023, 2312.09169** — Preprint authored by G. Menichetti et al. posted on arXiv in 2023.
- **arxiv:2506.05485v1** — Pre‑print presenting the inelastic tunneling spectroscopy study of TBG.
- **arxiv:2509.22902v1 (cond‑mat.mes‑hall) 26 sep 2025** — Pre‑print reporting the theoretical analysis of momentum‑conserving tunneling in moiré superconductors.
- **assumption b2** — Assumption that tip and sample act as large reservoirs with virtually unlimited electron capacity.
- **assumption b3** — Assumption that tunneling does not significantly change the density of states of tip or sample.
- **asymmetric bell‑shaped nesting line** — Observed shape of the nesting line when a finite back‑gate voltage is applied.
- **asymmetric bias leads to bell shape** — Finite back‑gate voltage transforms the symmetric X shape of d²I/dV² into an asymmetric bell‑shaped curve.
- **asymmetric bias response** — Observed asymmetry of d²I/dV² features when a finite back‑gate voltage is applied.
- **asymmetry due to electronic density of states in au** — Faster increase of differential conductance for negative bias caused by Au’s high DOS.
- **asymmetry in di/dv** — Difference in peak intensity on opposite sides of a node due to coherence factor changes.
- **asymmetry in i–v due to au density of states** — Differential conductance rises more rapidly for negative bias.
- **asymmetry in phonon peak intensity** — Difference in phonon peak heights for opposite bias, attributed to band‑structure asymmetry.
- **atom adsorbed onto jellium surface model** — Simplified tip model used in Bardeen‑type tunneling calculations.
- **atom diffraction methods** — Computational approach adapted from atom diffraction to calculate tunneling currents.
- **atom superposition approximation** — Crude method for estimating surface LDOS used to compute STM images for large or non‑periodic cells.
- **atom superposition calculation** — Computational technique using the atom superposition approximation to generate STM images.
- **atom superposition gives good results for au(110)** — The atom‑superposition approximation yields accurate STM predictions for Au(110).
- **atom superposition underestimates corrugation by ~30 %** — Atom superposition calculation yields corrugation amplitudes about 30 % lower than self‑consistent results.
- **atomic adsorbates** — Individual atoms deposited on a surface to modify its electronic properties.
- **atomic carbon 2p orbital** — Atomic orbital used as the basis function φpz in the derivation of V0 and a.
- **atomic force microscopy (afm)** — Contact‑mode SPM technique referenced for tip fabrication.
- **atomic force microscopy (afm)** — Force‑based imaging technique for surface topography.
- **atomic reconstruction** — Structural relaxation of the moiré lattice at small twist angles.
- **atomic resolution** — Imaging capability where individual surface atoms can be distinguished.
- **atomic resolution requires tip apex atom to be nearest to sample** — Atomic resolution is achieved when a single tip atom dominates the tunneling pathway.
- **atomic‑scale imaging** — Observation of surface structure at the scale of individual atoms using STM.
- **atomic‑thin α‑rucl3** — Monolayer‑like α‑RuCl3 crystals.
- **attocube 2200 positioner** — Fine positioner reserved for fibre‑coupled optics in the cryo‑QTM.
- **attocube electronic rack** — Attocube electronic rack with height 132 cm
- **attocube server** — Control computer that stores the backreflection position for the parabolic mirror.
- **au mirror** — Gold‑coated mirror used in the THz optical train.
- **au nanoparticles target cancer** — Jain, P. K. et al., Nano Today 2007, discussing gold nanoparticles for cancer targeting.
- **au(110) 2×1 surface** — Gold (110) surface with a 2×1 reconstruction studied by STM.
- **au(110) 3×1 surface** — Gold (110) surface with a 3×1 reconstruction studied by STM.
- **au(110) surface** — Gold (110) surface on which 2×1 and 3×1 reconstructions were studied.
- **autonomia (100 % càrrega)** — Temps d'autonomia de 11 min amb 100 % de càrrega.
- **auxiliary field** — Field introduced to decouple the quartic spinon interaction.
- **au‑zno** — Hybrid gold–zinc‑oxide nanostructure supporting plasmonic heating.
- **avishek maity** — Co‑author of the publication.
- **azomethine molecules** — Small conjugated molecules studied for electron transport
- **b. a. bernevig** — Co‑author of the Nano Letters 2026 paper.
- **b. g. park** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 PRL on broken Kramers degeneracy in MnTe.
- **b. hillebrands** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2021 Nature Reviews Materials on coherent magnonics.
- **b. jiang** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2025 Nat. Phys. on metallic d‑wave altermagnet.
- **b. jäck** — Co‑author of the Nature 2019 paper on many‑body correlations.
- **b. lake** — Lead author of the 2013 multispinon study.
- **b. lian** — Co‑author of the Nature 2019 paper on many‑body correlations.
- **b. putzer** — Lead author of the 2025 Eliashberg theory paper.
- **b. s. jessen** — Co‑author of the 2020 Nano Lett. paper.
- **b. seok** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 PRL on broken Kramers degeneracy in MnTe.
- **b. t. le** — Co‑author of the Nano Letters 2026 paper.
- **b. van duppen** — Co‑author on quantum non‑local effects in graphene plasmonics.
- **b. y.** — Co‑author who contributed to the theoretical models.
- **b.-s. yeo** — Co‑author of the TERS status and outlook paper.
- **ba site** — Moiré region symmetric to AB, also favored by electron‑sufficient molecules.
- **ba stacking area** — Region of TBG with BA stacking, similar to AB for molecule adsorption.
- **back gating** — Use of a back‑gate electrode to control carrier density in the sample.
- **back reflection** — Reflection of a beam from a flat surface used as an alignment reference.
- **background current** — Current component that rises with bias independent of the spectral features.
- **backlash** — Mechanical play in the rotation stage mitigated by moving +1° then –1°.
- **backreflection** — The reflected VIS beam used as a reference for alignment.
- **backreflection alignment** — Procedure that aligns the THz beam by maximizing its backreflected signal on the parabolic mirror.
- **backreflection alignment setup** — Configuration used to align the THz beam perpendicular to the cSNOM parabolic mirror.
- **backward waves with quantum cascade gain** — Plasmonic modes propagating opposite to energy flow with gain from quantum cascade.
- **back‑folded phonon** — Phonon replica that appears at momentum qK ≈ 2 sin(θ) MD/60 − 2 due to Brillouin‑zone folding.
- **back‑gate dependence** — Variation of tunneling characteristics with back‑gate voltage.
- **back‑gate dependence experiment** — Additional measurements of momentum‑resolved tunneling as a function of back‑gate voltage.
- **ballistic transport** — Electron motion without scattering over micrometre distances, enabled by weak EPC.
- **ballistic transport phenomenon** — Long‑range, scattering‑free electron motion in monolayer graphene.
- **band anticrossing** — Pressure‑controlled hybridisation between flat and remote bands observed as they approach each other in energy.
- **band bending** — Surface barrier that can create a voltage drop in semiconductor STM.
- **band dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relationship of electronic bands, probed by momentum‑resolved tunneling.
- **band insulator to mott insulator transition in 1t‑tas₂** — Observation of a transition from a band‑insulating to a Mott‑insulating state in 1T‑TaS₂.
- **band maximum (ξ_vm)** — Energy offset of the top of the filled valence band relative to the flat‑band manifold.
- **band structure of twisted bilayer graphene: emergent symmetries, commensurate approximants, and wannier obstructions** — Reference (9) analyzing band structure, symmetries and Wannier obstructions in tBG.
- **band theory of graphite** — Early theoretical description of graphite electronic bands.
- **band tracing** — Observation that the MLG Dirac point follows TBG flat or remote bands.
- **bandwidth (30 ghz)** — Modulation bandwidth achieved by the graphene electro‑optic modulator.
- **bandwidth at the m point** — Energy width of the flat band measured at the M point of the moiré Brillouin zone.
- **bandwidth b** — Bandwidth of the flat bands, comparable to U (≈10 meV).
- **band‑structure effects** — Influence of the electronic band structure of the spacer material on electron spill‑out and tunneling.
- **band‑structure engineering** — Design of electronic bands via control of twist angle, strain, and doping.
- **band‑structure landscape** — Spatially varying electronic structure caused by strain, residues, and local orientation locking.
- **band‑structure tomography project** — Research effort focused on developing QTM‑based momentum‑resolved spectroscopy.
- **band‑structure tuning** — Methods to adjust electronic band dispersion, e.g., pressure, interlayer distance.
- **bardeen 1961** — Original paper presenting the tunneling theory.
- **bardeen 1961 – tunnelling from a many‑particle point of view** — Physical Review Letters 1961 foundational paper on many‑particle tunnelling.
- **bardeen tunneling theory** — Perturbative description of tunnelling between two electrodes.
- **bardeens approximation of the matrix element** — Bardeen's approximation of the tunneling matrix element derived via surface integral.
- **bardeens formalism** — First‑order perturbation theory used to derive the tunneling current expression.
- **bare tunneling hamiltonian ĥtun** — Hamiltonian describing tunneling without interaction corrections.
- **bare velocity (v_0)** — Unrenormalized Fermi velocity corresponding to NN hopping t = 2.5 eV.
- **barium gallium germanium selenide (baga2gese6)** — A barium‑based crystal suitable for mid‑IR generation.
- **barium gallium germanium sulfide (baga2ges6)** — A barium‑based crystal suitable for mid‑IR generation.
- **barium gallium sulfide (baga4s7)** — A barium‑based crystal suitable for mid‑IR generation.
- **barrier penetration** — The penetration of wavefunctions into a classically forbidden region.
- **barrier region** — Spatial region of the insulating barrier separating two electrodes.
- **barrier thickness (d)** — Thickness of the hBN tunneling barrier (1.2 nm).
- **barrier thickness d_wse2** — Thickness of the WSe2 barrier, taken as 0.58 nm per monolayer.
- **battery formation** — Potential creation of a galvanic cell (battery) due to flux residues.
- **battery replacement** — Changing batteries at end of life, not included in the warranty.
- **beam adjustment** — Adjustment of beam current and alignment using the Faraday cup.
- **beam alignment** — Process of aligning the laser beam with the tip pyramid and the detector quadrants.
- **beam collimation** — Achieving a parallel THz beam.
- **beam collimation close to wall (2.5 thz)** — For 2.5 THz the collimation point is very close to the wall, requiring caution.
- **beam current** — Current of the ion or electron beam, e.g., 10 pA, 120 pA.
- **beam diameter** — Width of the THz beam, typically measured in millimetres.
- **beam diameter (mm)** — Beam width measured in millimetres.
- **beam diameter 9 mm at 75 cm** — Beam diameter measured as 9 mm at 75 cm from the entrance to the cSNOM table.
- **beam divergence fwhm** — Full width at half maximum of the beam divergence, measured as 17 mm at the cSNOM chamber.
- **beam instability** — Fluctuations in beam shape caused by mechanical issues.
- **beam profile display** — Measurement of the laser beam profile using thermal imaging plates, UV lamp, mirrors and crystal paper.
- **beam reduction** — Reducing the beam diameter by a factor of 1/4 using the telescope.
- **beam shape** — Spatial intensity profile of the THz beam.
- **beam size** — Spatial width of the optical beam, used as an indicator of collimation.
- **beam size measurement technique** — Technique using a powermeter and slit to determine the beam FWHM.
- **beamsplitter (bs)** — Optical element that splits or combines beams in the interferometer.
- **bell‑shaped feature** — Bell‑shaped d²I/dV² curve that replaces the symmetric X shape under asymmetric bias.
- **bench** — Laboratory bench supporting the laser system and ancillary equipment.
- **berry‑curvature magnetism** — Magnetic response arising from Berry curvature in momentum space.
- **best‑fit γ₀ ≈ 4 mev** — The inverse lifetime parameter γ₀ that best matches the low‑bias tunneling data.
- **bf₃ molecule** — Electron‑deficient molecule whose orbital attraction overcomes electronegativity and adsorbs at AA.
- **bh₃** — Electron‑deficient molecule attracted to the AA region of TBG.
- **bias (ev)** — Energy scale set by the applied bias voltage, measured in meV.
- **bias affects tunnelling current magnitude** — Increasing bias voltage raises the overall tunnelling current while also changing the relative contribution of plasmon channels.
- **bias electron energy (evb)** — Energy of electrons injected by the applied bias voltage.
- **bias regime** — Electrical bias range (low bias vs. high bias) that determines whether elastic or inelastic tunneling dominates.
- **bias shifts bands** — In the thick‑barrier limit, most of the applied bias shifts the energy bands.
- **bias voltage (ev)** — Voltage applied between tip and sample during tunneling.
- **bias voltage evtb** — Applied voltage bias across the tunneling junction.
- **bias voltage range (10 mv to 10 v)** — Voltage applied across the tunneling gap during STM operation.
- **bias voltage v** — External voltage applied across the QTM tunnel junction.
- **bias voltage v_b** — Applied voltage across the tunnel junction, varied between 0.5 V and 2.5 V in the study.
- **bias voltage vbb** — Interlayer bias voltage applied across the tunneling junction.
- **bias voltages** — Applied voltages that control plasmon frequencies and provide additional spectral tuning.
- **biased nanoscale contact as active element for electrically driven plasmonic nanoantenna. acs photonics 2017, 4, 1501‑1505** — Shows a biased nanoscale contact acting as an active element for a plasmonic nanoantenna.
- **bias‑dependence measurement** — Experiment measuring the bias dependence of top/bottom chemical potentials and electrostatic potential in an MLG‑MLG tunneling junction.
- **bias‑dependent frequencies** — Frequencies of the generated plasmons that depend on the applied bias voltage.
- **bias‑tunable emission peak** — The emission peak energy can be shifted by changing the bias voltage.
- **bias‑voltage sweep** — Systematic variation of the bias voltage V to probe the energy dependence of tunneling.
- **bilayer graphene tip** — Graphene tip consisting of bilayer graphene, providing stronger trigonal warping at the Fermi level.
- **bilayer wse₂** — A bilayer of tungsten diselenide used as the tunneling barrier between graphene layers.
- **bilayer wse₂** — Bilayer tungsten diselenide used as a layer in the TBG heterostructure.
- **binnig and rohrer stm experiments** — First demonstration of STM imaging atomic positions on Si(111)-7×7.
- **bioimaging** — Imaging of biological samples using optical probes.
- **birkbeck et al., nature 2025, “quantum twisting microscopy of phonons in twisted bilayer graphene”** — Reference reporting quantum twisting microscopy measurements of phonons in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **bistritzer** — Co‑author of theoretical work on commensurate twist angles.
- **bistritzer & macdonald (2011) publication** — Original theoretical paper introducing the continuum model for twisted bilayer graphene.
- **bistritzer‑macdonald (bm) model** — Continuum model describing the band structure of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **bistritzer‑macdonald continuum model** — Continuum model for twisted bilayer graphene employed in the full calculation.
- **bistritzer–macdonald model** — Continuum theoretical model describing the electronic structure of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **blades** — Two blades placed in front of the powermeter to form a slit.
- **bloch band energies ξλ(k)** — Band energies of the sample entering the spectral function.
- **bloch basis** — Bloch basis for electronic states in the crystal.
- **bloch wave functions ψ_{λ}(k+g l σ)** — Normalized reciprocal‑space Bloch functions used in the tunneling matrix elements.
- **bloch wave vector k** — Crystal momentum of surface electronic states; often neglected for large unit cells.
- **blue lines (theoretical curves)** — Curves computed from Eq. (47) that include only electron‑plasmon scattering in TBG.
- **blueshift matches optical spectroscopy** — Temperature‑dependent blueshift of the resonant peak follows the shift of exciton energies measured optically.
- **bm expression** — Bardeen‑McCumber expression used to calculate momentum‑resolved tunneling between parallel layers.
- **bm24-10dp/2-0.35v53 connector** — Standard connector used on pico_end for soldering on both sides.
- **bm24-10dp/2-0.35v53 connector** — Connector type used on the Pico‑End PCB.
- **bm24-10dp/2-0.35v53 connector** — Connector used on Pico End PCB.
- **bm24-10ds_2-0.35v_53 connector** — Connector specified in requirements, used on both sides of Inter PCB, rated 0.35 V.
- **bm24-10ds_2-0.35v_53 connector** — Standard connector used on Inter for soldering on both sides.
- **bm24-10ds_2-0.35v_53 connector** — Connector type specified for the Inter PCB on both sides.
- **bm24-10ds_2-0.35v_53 connector** — Standard connector used on Inter PCB.
- **bm24‑10dp/2‑** — BM24‑10DP/2‑ connector used for tip interfacing.
- **bogoliubov dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relation of Bogoliubov quasiparticles.
- **bogoliubov fermi surface** — Fermi surface of Bogoliubov quasiparticles in superconductors.
- **bogoliubov quasiparticle dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relation of quasiparticles in a superconductor.
- **bogoliubov quasiparticle peak** — Peaks in the spectral function at energies ±E_S(k) with coherence‑factor weights.
- **bogoliubov quasiparticle spectra** — Energy‑momentum dispersion of quasiparticles in a superconductor.
- **bogoliubov quasiparticles** — Elementary excitations of a superconductor described by Bogoliubov‑de Gennes theory.
- **bogoliubov quasiparticles** — Quasiparticle excitations in a superconductor described by Bogoliubov theory.
- **boix-constant et al., out-of-plane transport of 1t-tas2/graphene-based van der waals heterostructures, acs nano 15, 11898 (2021)** — Paper measuring out‑of‑plane transport in 1T‑TaS₂/graphene van der Waals heterostructures.
- **bolometer** — THz detector that must be aligned so that the VIS pilot reaches it without clipping.
- **boron nitride (bn)** — Layered material used as a tunnel barrier in graphene devices.
- **bottom graphene electrode** — Graphene layer serving as the bottom electrode in the tunneling device.
- **bottom graphene layer** — Lower graphene sheet of the device.
- **bottom graphene layer** — Lower graphene electrode in the heterostructure.
- **bottom graphite sample** — Graphite substrate that is electrically grounded and serves as the bottom electrode.
- **bottom layer carrier density (nb)** — Electron density in the bottom graphene layer.
- **bottom monolayer graphene layer** — The lower graphene sheet in the tunneling stack.
- **bottom vdw layer (sample side)** — Active van‑der‑Waals layer on the sample side of the junction.
- **bottom xy translation stages** — Coarse XY stages that position the rotation axis directly beneath the tip.
- **bottom‑up synthesis** — Chemical routes to grow nanometer‑scale graphene fragments.
- **brant, j. c.** — Co‑author of the 2014 fast pick‑up technique paper.
- **breakdown voltage** — Maximum bias that can be applied before the device fails, setting an upper limit for plasmon generation.
- **breakdown voltage** — Maximum bias magnitude before the device current becomes uncontrolled.
- **bright visible light emission from graphene** — Nature Nanotechnology 2015 report of bright visible‑range light emission from graphene.
- **brillouin zone (bz)** — Reciprocal‑space unit cell of a crystal.
- **broad continuum is characteristic of qsl behavior** — Observation that a broad excitation continuum signals a quantum spin liquid.
- **broadening of the spectrum** — Increase of spectral width characterized by γ_S.
- **broadening parameter γ finding** — The broadening parameter γ is typically in the 50–100 meV range for the devices studied.
- **broadening parameter γ_t** — Lifetime broadening applied to the tip density of states.
- **broadening parameter η** — Lifetime broadening applied to the sample density of states.
- **broadening parameter η** — Additional small broadening used in the simulations (η = 0.015 meV).
- **broadening γ** — Energy broadening parameter (e.g., 50 meV) used in the model for the tunneling resonance.
- **broadening γ** — Energy broadening (e.g., 50 meV) used in the model for the tunneling resonance.
- **broken kramers degeneracy** — Lifting of spin degeneracy protected by time‑reversal symmetry.
- **broken kramers degeneracy in altermagnetic mnte** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 036702 (2024) reporting Kramers degeneracy breaking in MnTe.
- **bruker icon atomic force microscope** — Commercial AFM used for force feedback, lateral positioning and contact operations in the QTM setup.
- **bruker innova afm** — Atomic force microscope employed to verify h‑BN flake thickness.
- **bulk crystals** — Source material for exfoliated flakes.
- **bulk graphite** — Three‑dimensional graphite used as reference for phonon dispersion models.
- **bulk graphite phonons** — Phonon branches of three‑dimensional graphite used as a reference spectrum.
- **bulk samples** — Three‑dimensional crystals used in thermodynamic measurements.
- **bulk‑graphite phonon dispersion model** — Theoretical description of graphite phonons using force‑constant matrices and interlayer coupling.
- **bull. am. phys. soc. 28, 260 (1983)** — Reference authored by Y. Kuk.
- **buried graphite gate** — Conductive graphite layer underneath the bottom graphene flake that provides electrostatic gating.

- **c. rubio‑verdú** — Co‑author of the probing quantum spin liquids paper.

- **c. b. duke interpretation** — Work describing the traditional approach linking Bardeen's theory to Oppenheimer's perturbation theory.
- **c. j. chen derivation** — Paper presenting the derivation of the Tersoff‑Hamann formula.
- **c2h2f2** — Molecule used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **c3z‑invariant superconductor** — Superconductor with threefold rotational symmetry.
- **cadmium selenide (cdse)** — A semiconductor crystal used in mid‑IR applications.
- **calculated work functions for au(110) reconstructions** — LAPW yields work functions of 5.7 eV (2×1) and 5.2 eV (3×1).
- **calculating total power** — Computing expected THz power after mirror and humidity losses.
- **calibration** — Calibration procedure employing the CO2 spectrum analyser and other test equipment.
- **calibration of energy shift using mlg dirac bands** — Procedure that uses the known MLG Dirac dispersion to convert Vb into an energy offset.
- **can alternative alignment approaches reduce misorientation?** — Motivation for testing optical alignment after observing fragmentation limitations.
- **can iets in a qtm directly probe qsl excitations?** — Question of whether the IETS signal can access the spin structure factor of a magnetic insulator.
- **can qtm resolve collective excitations and their coupling to electrons?** — Question whether the quantum twisting microscope can detect plasmons, phonons, etc.
- **can stm distinguish different surface geometries?** — Question about STM sensitivity to missing rows in Au(110) reconstructions.
- **can subtle band‑structure modifications be resolved at room temperature?** — Motivation for improving the QTM resolution with hBN dielectrics.
- **can tbg serve as a functional tip for scanning probe microscopy?** — Open question motivating the proposed QTSPM.
- **can the flux in paste layers create batteries?** — Question about the possibility of battery formation from flux residues.
- **candidate quantum spin liquid material** — Material under investigation that may lack C6z symmetry.
- **cantilever angle 9‑12°** — Native tilt of commercial AFM cantilevers relative to the sample surface.
- **cantilever heating** — Heating the cantilever to 50 °C to improve van‑der‑Waals adhesion.
- **cantilever length l = 232 µm** — Length of the AFM cantilever.
- **cantilever width w = 37 µm** — Width of the AFM cantilever.
- **cantilever‑sample tilt angle** — Adjustable angle between the AFM cantilever and the sample.
- **cao, y.; et al. correlated insulator behaviour at half‑filling in magic‑angle graphene superlattices. nature 2018, 556 (7699), 80-84.** — Observation of a correlated insulating state at half‑filling in magic‑angle twisted graphene.
- **capacitance values** — Electrostatic capacitances measured independently and used as input for the theory.
- **capacitive readout** — Technique of reading out capacitance using Renaissance motors.
- **capacitive readout** — Readout method using capacitance, employed by Renaissance motors.
- **carbon dioxide** — CO₂ component of the laser gas mix (≈99.995 %).
- **carbon dioxide (co₂) gas** — CO₂ exhaust gas produced by the FIRL100 laser’s gas section.
- **carbon monoxide (co) gas** — Toxic CO gas co‑produced with CO₂ in the FIRL100 laser exhaust.
- **carbon nanotube** — Common material for AFM tips mentioned as a reference.
- **carbon nanotube tip** — Carbon nanotube used as a high‑aspect‑ratio scanning probe tip.
- **carbon nanotube tips for scanning probe microscopy: fabrication and high aspect ratio nanometrology** — Meas. Sci. Technol. 2005 paper on CNT tips for SPM.
- **carbon nanotubes** — One‑dimensional carbon nanostructures studied for synthetic electric fields and phonon damping.
- **caroli** — One of the authors who initiated the use of NEGF in tunneling theory.
- **carrier densities nb and nt vary linearly with vbg** — Both bottom and top layer carrier densities change linearly with backgate voltage.
- **carrier density** — Electron (or hole) concentration in the graphene tip, tuned by gating.
- **carrier density** — Electron density in each graphene layer, tuned by Vbg.
- **carrier multiplication** — Generation of multiple charge carriers from a single photon in graphene.
- **carrier transport theory** — Theoretical description of charge transport.
- **carrier transport theory for twisted bilayer graphene in the metallic regime (nat commun 12, 5737, 2021)** — Transport theory for the metallic phase of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **cascade of electronic transitions in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (wong et al., 2020)** — Observation of a cascade of electronic transitions in tBLG.
- **cascade of phase transitions and dirac revivals in magic-angle graphene** — Nature 582, 203 (2020) by Zondiner et al.
- **cascade of phase transitions and dirac revivals in magic‑angle graphene (zondiner et al., 2020)** — Report of multiple electronic phase transitions and Dirac band revivals.
- **cascades in transport and optical conductivity of twisted bilayer graphene (2025)** — ArXiv pre‑print on cascade phenomena observed in transport and optical conductivity of TBG.
- **catalonia agaur** — Generalitat de Catalunya AGAUR funding
- **catalonia cerca** — Generalitat de Catalunya CERCA funding
- **catalytic reduction of co₂** — Photocatalytic conversion of carbon dioxide to reduced chemical species.
- **cathodoluminescence** — Optical emission measured from electron‑excited samples, employed to detect plasmons.
- **ccl₂ molecule** — Dichloromethyl radical used in adsorption‑energy measurements.
- **ccl₂ molecule** — Electron‑deficient molecule studied for adsorption on TBG.
- **cd3cl 383.2 mm line** — FIR laser line at 383.2 mm using CD3Cl with typical power 25 mW.
- **cd3od 184 mm line** — FIR laser line at 184 mm using CD3OD with typical power 30 mW.
- **cd3od 255 mm line** — FIR laser line at 255 mm using CD3OD with typical power 5 mW.
- **cd3od 355.5 mm line** — FIR laser line at 355.5 mm using CD3OD with typical power 2 mW.
- **cd3oh 292.1 mm line** — FIR laser line at 292.1 mm using CD3OH with typical power 10 mW.
- **cd₃od (deuterated methanol)** — Deuterated methanol used as a lasing gas.
- **cd₃oh (deuterated methanol)** — Deuterated methanol used as a lasing gas.
- **center of rotation alignment** — Procedure to locate the geometric centre of rotation by tracing features while rotating the sample.
- **center plane (layer 2)** — Middle PCB layer used for routing traces.
- **central wavelength (raman shift) 2100 cm⁻¹** — Target Raman shift used for graphene characterization.
- **ch. gerber** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **ch2f2 122.4 mm line** — FIR laser line at 122.4 mm using CH2F2 with typical power 40 mW.
- **ch2f2 184.3 mm line** — FIR laser line at 184.3 mm using CH2F2 with typical power 150 mW.
- **ch2f2 193.9 mm line** — FIR laser line at 193.9 mm using CH2F2 with typical power 10 mW.
- **ch3od 86.7 mm line** — FIR laser line at 86.7 mm using CH3OD with typical power 1 mW.
- **ch3oh 163 mm line** — FIR laser line at 163 mm using CH3OH with typical power 36 mW.
- **ch3oh 62 mm line** — FIR laser line at 62 mm using CH3OH with typical power 3 mW.
- **ch3oh 70.6 mm line** — FIR laser line at 70.6 mm using CH3OH with typical power 20 mW.
- **characteristic bias** — Bias voltage at which dI/dV steps occur (eV = ξ_vm − ℏω_Q).
- **characteristic bias voltage v*** — Bias voltage at which a dI/dV singularity appears, denoted V* = V*(kF).
- **characterization of crsb magnetic interactions** — Research effort combining polarized neutron scattering and INS to map CrSb’s magnetic Hamiltonian.
- **charge and pair density waves in a spin and valley‑polarized system at a van‑hove singularity** — 2025 arXiv preprint by A. Gil and E. Berg.
- **charge density p(r,ef)** — Charge density at the Fermi level used to model STM tunnelling currents.
- **charge distribution** — Spatial distribution of unpaired electron charge around an ion.
- **charge doping** — Modification of carrier concentration via external means.
- **charge fluctuations** — Collective charge density variations that generate the screening potential.
- **charge friedel oscillations** — Spatial oscillations of charge density around impurities in a Mott insulator.
- **charge gap** — Energy required to add an electron to the system.
- **charge gap, goldstone modes, and absence of cooper pairing** — The 2021 PRB reports exact analytic many‑body excitations showing a charge gap, Goldstone modes, and no Cooper pairing.
- **charge neutral 1.07° twisted bilayer graphene** — A TBG device with a twist angle of 1.07° and overall charge neutrality.
- **charge neutrality** — Electronic condition where the carrier density is zero.
- **charge neutrality point** — Energy where the density of states of graphene vanishes.
- **charge order and broken rotational symmetry in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Nature 573, 91 (2019) by Jiang et al.
- **charge-carrier screening** — Screening of electric fields by charge carriers.
- **charge‑density‑wave proximity effect** — Induced CDW order in graphene when placed on 1T‑TaS₂.
- **charge‑density‑wave proximity effect** — Induction of CDW order in a neighboring layer.
- **charge‑density‑wave proximity effect in graphene on 1t‑tas₂** — Revealing of CDW proximity effect when graphene is placed on 1T‑TaS₂.
- **charge‑neutral edge states** — Edge excitations that carry no net charge, relevant in quantum magnets.
- **charge‑to‑spin conversion** — Conversion of charge current into a spin current.
- **charline k. r. kirchert** — Author listed on the Ann. Phys. 2022 paper.
- **chart recorder** — Chart recorder used for stability measurements (mV range).
- **chatterjee & sachdev, phys. rev. b 92, 165113 (2015)** — Probing excitations in insulators via injection of spin currents.
- **chatterjee, rodriguez‑nieva & demler, phys. rev. b 99, 104425 (2019)** — Diagnosing phases of magnetic insulators via noise magnetometry with spin qubits.
- **chem. rev. 2024, 124, 1950** — Review article authored by B. P. Bloom, Y. Paltiel, R. Naaman and D. H. Waldeck published in 2024.
- **chemical and electromagnetic mechanisms of tip‑enhanced raman scattering** — Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys. 2009 paper dissecting the origins of TERS enhancement.
- **chemical characterisation** — Determination of chemical composition using spectroscopic signatures.
- **chemical interaction between tip and sample** — Interaction that modifies atomic positions and the electrostatic potential at short tip‑sample distances.
- **chemical mechanism (ters)** — Enhancement of Raman signal due to charge‑transfer interactions at the tip.
- **chemical potential of top layer (μt)** — Electrochemical potential of the upper graphene electrode, shifted by the applied bias.
- **chens derivative rule** — Extension of the Tersoff‑Hamann model that includes partial derivatives of the tip wavefunction to capture higher‑resolution STM features.
- **chern mosaic and berry‑curvature magnetism in magic-angle graphene** — Nat. Phys. 18, 885 (2022) by Grover et al.
- **chip carrier** — Mechanical holder for the silicon chip.
- **chiral bands** — Band structures with a handedness that can give rise to unconventional superconductivity.
- **chiral electrons** — Electrons with defined handedness in Dirac materials.
- **chiral finite‑momentum superconductivity** — Superconducting state with finite Cooper‑pair momentum and chirality.
- **chiral finite‑momentum superconductivity in the tetralayer graphene** — 2024 arXiv preprint by Q. Qin and C. Wu.
- **chiral magnetic excitations** — Magnetic excitations with a defined chirality in g‑wave altermagnets.
- **chiral magnon splitting** — Splitting of magnon bands due to chirality in altermagnets.
- **chiral magnons** — Magnons with a definite spin chirality, observed in CrSb.
- **chiral spin‑split magnons** — Chiral spin‑split magnons present in CrSb.
- **chiral splitting of magnon dispersion** — Splitting of magnon branches caused by inequivalent higher‑order exchange couplings.
- **chiral transport effects** — Transport phenomena that depend on the handedness of the moiré pattern.
- **chiral‑induced spin selectivity (ciss)** — Spin‑selective transport effect observed in chiral systems.
- **chopper drive unit** — Chopper drive unit that is part of the 295STAB assembly.
- **chopper removal restores beam shape** — Removing the chopper restored the normal (non‑gaussian) beam shape.
- **christine trinh** — Contributed to the crystal growth of CrSb.
- **chromium** — Element chromium, host of spin‑density‑wave antiferromagnetism.
- **chromium adhesion layer (4 nm)** — Thin Cr layer deposited to promote adhesion of gold on the cantilever.
- **ch₃oh (methanol)** — Methanol used as a lasing gas.
- **classical charging energy** — Electrostatic energy contribution to bias distribution.
- **classical qtm** — The standard Quantum Twisting Microscope measurement protocol.
- **clean contact surfaces** — Procedure to keep tip and sample surfaces free of polymer residue.
- **cleaning of hbn/graphene surfaces** — Removal of dirt and PC residues by applying controlled AFM tip forces.
- **cleanroom facilities at the first center** — Micro‑ and nanoscience cleanroom used for device fabrication.
- **closed‑cycle thermocirculator (th4)** — The TH4 thermocirculator that maintains the FIRL100 coolant at about 15 °C.
- **closed‑loop angular control** — Technique that uses the optical encoder to keep the rotator angle stable with milli‑degree precision.
- **co-aligned crsb single crystals (5 g total)** — A set of about 45 co‑aligned CrSb crystals used in neutron scattering experiments.
- **co2 beam** — Carbon‑dioxide laser beam generated by the FIRL100 system.
- **co2 meter** — Power meter dedicated to measuring the CO2 beam output.
- **co2 operation** — Mode of the FIRL100 that emits at the CO₂ wavelength.
- **coal** — Abundant source of graphene quantum dots as reported in 2013 Nat. Commun.
- **coarse alignment** — Initial alignment using the optical camera and bottom XY stages.
- **cobalt atoms** — Magnetic adatoms used to probe spinon Kondo effect on 1T‑TaSe₂.
- **coherence factor** — Factor governing the amplitude of tunneling matrix elements, jumping between 0 and 1.
- **coherence factors** — Factors determining the amplitude of quasiparticle tunneling.
- **coherence factors** — Factors that modify transition probabilities in superconductors.
- **coherence factors are absent in tunnelling** — Coherence factors do not affect tunnelling probabilities.
- **coherent commensurate electronic states at the interface between misoriented graphene layers** — Nature Nanotechnology 11, 752–757 (2016) reporting coherent commensurate electronic states in misoriented graphene.
- **coherent magnonics** — Magnonic processes preserving phase coherence.
- **coherent spin‑wave transport** — Concept of phase‑preserving propagation of spin waves.
- **coherent spin‑wave transport in an antiferromagnet** — Nat. Phys. 17, 1001 (2021) reporting coherent spin‑wave transport in antiferromagnets.
- **cold cleaving** — Low‑temperature cleaving of samples performed inside a cryogenic QTM.
- **collective electronic excitations** — Electronic collective modes probed in correlated moiré materials.
- **collective excitations of quantum anomalous hall ferromagnets** — The 2020 PRL characterizes collective modes in quantum anomalous Hall ferromagnets realized in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **collective excitations of quantum anomalous hall ferromagnets in twisted bilayer graphene (2020)** — Physical Review Letters study of collective modes in QAH ferromagnets.
- **collimation** — Alignment of the beam to make it propagate with parallel wave‑vectors.
- **collimation before beam compression is preferable** — Conclusion that collimating first and compressing later avoids excessive divergence.
- **collimation check by beam size** — Method that verifies collimation by measuring beam size (FWHM) instead of power.
- **collimation check via beam size** — Procedure that verifies beam collimation by measuring the beam size instead of power.
- **collinear antiferromagnet** — Antiferromagnet where spins are aligned along a common axis.
- **collinear scattering** — Ultrafast carrier scattering process observed in graphene.
- **commensurate angle (θ_c)** — Specific twist angle at which the two lattices become periodic (e.g., 0° or ≈38.2°).
- **commensurate angle 21.8°** — Specific twist angle where the moiré superlattice becomes commensurate, used as a reference in the experiments.
- **commensurate approximants** — Finite‑size periodic structures used to approximate incommensurate moiré lattices.
- **commensurate twist angles** — Twist angles where the moiré superlattice is periodic.
- **commercial atomic force microscope platform** — Standard AFM instrument used as the hardware basis for the QTM.
- **commercial piezo‑rotator** — Rotational stage that introduces a small lateral displacement (~1 µm) during rotation.
- **comparing measurements of the same tbg sample with two different tips (section s12.2)** — Study that evaluates reproducibility of QTM data using two distinct QTM tips.
- **comparing measurements with different contact modalities (section s12.3)** — Study that compares QTM data obtained with alternative electrical contact schemes.
- **competing orders** — Multiple electronic orders coexisting or competing.
- **compressed twisted bilayer graphene** — TBG with reduced interlayer distance.
- **compressibility** — Finite electronic compressibility that couples carrier density changes between sample and probe.
- **compression of flatbands** — Reduction of flat‑band bandwidth under hydrostatic pressure.
- **compression ratio 1/4** — Beam size reduction factor set by the telescope.
- **conductance dips tracing neutrality points** — Reduced conductance lines that follow the calculated charge‑neutral Dirac point of each graphene layer.
- **conductance d²i/dv² map** — Second derivative of the tunnelling current with respect to bias voltage, recorded by QTM.
- **conductance enhancements near 21.8° and 38.2°** — Increased conductance observed at the commensurate twist angles 21.8° and 38.2°.
- **conductance g** — Differential conductance measured as a function of bias.
- **conductance g** — Differential conductance measured in the experiment.
- **conductance map measurement** — Measurement of junction conductance as a function of bias (Vb) and back‑gate voltage (Vbg) for a given twist angle.
- **conductance peak** — Sharp increase in conductance observed at specific commensurate twist angles.
- **conductance peak at 21.8°** — Enhanced conductance near 21.8° attributed to intervalley tunneling.
- **conductance peak at 38.2°** — Enhanced conductance near 38.2° attributed to intravalley tunneling.
- **conductance step measurement** — Measurement of G versus bias and back‑gate voltage revealing inelastic phonon steps.
- **conductance step δg** — Measured change in conductance due to phonon‑assisted tunnelling.
- **conductance steps (δg)** — Discrete changes in conductance associated with phonon emission.
- **conductance steps scale with tip area** — Normalized conductance curves collapse when divided by the measured tip contact area.
- **conductance vs angle measurement** — Measurement of differential conductance as a function of rotation angle between two MLG layers.
- **conduction band** — Lowest unoccupied electronic band in a semiconductor.
- **conduction band edge of wse₂** — Energy level at the bottom of the conduction band in WSe₂.
- **conductivities** — Material property entering equation 3 of the formalism for different materials.
- **confined gap plasmons** — Plasmons confined in the nanometer gap of a metal‑insulator‑metal structure.
- **constant current contour of p(r,ef)** — The set of surface points where the local density of states yields a constant tunneling current.
- **constant force** — Target force level kept constant while the cantilever twists.
- **constant transfer matrix finding** — Transfer matrix elements can be treated as constant across the tunneling gap in the simulations.
- **constant‑current scanning mode** — STM operation mode where tip height is adjusted to keep tunneling resistance constant.
- **constant‑energy slice measurement** — Neutron‑scattering experiment recording constant‑energy slices at 130, 180 and 230 meV.
- **continuous approach curves** — Procedure in which the tip is moved up and down repeatedly to record deflection versus Z‑piezo voltage.
- **continuous contact method** — Contact modality where the tip stays in contact with the sample throughout the measurement.
- **continuous contact modality** — Measurement mode where tip and sample stay in contact while the stage rotates.
- **continuous twist motion** — Rotational motion of the cantilever while maintaining contact, required for QTM operation.
- **continuous wave (cw) mode** — Operating mode of the FIRL100 delivering a steady infrared beam.
- **continuum model** — Effective low‑energy theoretical description of moiré systems.
- **continuum model** — Effective low‑energy theory for twisted bilayer graphene.
- **continuum model of twisted bilayer graphene** — Realistic model of TBG employed to study superconducting gap structures.
- **contraction of charge distribution** — Observed reduction of Ni++ charge radius in the solid compared with the free atom.
- **control angle between graphene and qsl barrier** — Goal to vary the relative orientation of graphene and the QSL barrier.
- **control of light emission with twist angle** — Can the twist angle be used as a degree of freedom to control light emission from Gr/h‑BN/Gr tunnel junctions?
- **controller** — Controller electronics for the system.
- **controlling charge‑density‑wave states in nano‑thick crystals of 1t‑tas2** — 2014 work on CDW control in nano‑thick 1T‑TaS2 crystals.
- **controlling electron‑phonon interactions in graphene at ultrahigh carrier densities** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 256805 (2010) showing tuning of electron‑phonon coupling via ultrahigh carrier density.
- **controlling inelastic light scattering** — Experimental method to manipulate quantum pathways of inelastic scattering in graphene.
- **controlling inelastic light scattering quantum pathways in graphene** — Nature 2011 paper on manipulation of inelastic light scattering in graphene.
- **conventional superconductor** — Standard s‑wave superconducting material.
- **convolution of spectral functions** — Method representing tunneling current as convolution of tip and surface spectral functions.
- **coolant flow rate** — Required coolant flow of 2 to 3 L/min for both thermocirculator and tap‑water cooling.
- **coolant temperature (~15 °c)** — Target temperature of the water coolant maintained by the closed‑cycle thermocirculator.
- **cooling power** — Rate at which a system can dissipate heat.
- **cooper pairing** — Formation of bound electron pairs leading to superconductivity.
- **cooper pairs** — Bound electron pairs responsible for superconductivity.
- **copper pads** — Copper pieces glued to the coverslip edges to provide electrical connection to the devices.
- **correlated ground states** — Electronic phases in 2D materials where many‑body interactions dominate the low‑energy physics.
- **correlated insulator behaviour** — Interaction‑driven insulating state at half‑filling in twisted graphene.
- **correlated insulator behaviour at half‑filling in magic‑angle graphene superlattices** — Cao et al., Nature 556, 80 (2018).
- **correlated insulators** — Interaction‑driven insulating phases in moiré systems.
- **correlated phases** — Electronic states driven by strong interactions.
- **corrugation amplitude** — Amplitude of surface height variation measured in STM.
- **corrugation amplitude measurement** — Measurement of surface corrugation amplitude via STM tunneling current.
- **coulomb interaction, phonons, and superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 118, e2107874118 (2021)
- **coulomb interaction, phonons, and superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene (2021)** — Study of the interplay between Coulomb repulsion, phonons, and superconductivity in TBG.
- **coulomb screening of superconductivity in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (2024)** — ArXiv pre‑print on how Coulomb screening influences superconductivity at the magic angle.
- **coulomb staircase at room temperature in a self-assembled molecular nanostructure** — Science 272 (5266), 1323-1325 (1996)
- **coupled parabolic telescope** — Two aligned parabolic mirrors that refocus the THz beam and reduce its divergence.
- **coupling arises from modulation of interlayer tunnelling by phason** — The enhanced EPC is traced to the modulation of interlayer tunnelling by a layer‑antisymmetric phason mode.
- **coupling strength** — Magnitude of interaction between electrons and a specific phonon (or phason) mode.
- **co₂ analyzer** — Instrument that measures CO₂ concentration in the gas lines.
- **co₂ gas mix** — Pre‑mixed laser gas consisting of CO₂, N₂ and He.
- **co₂ laser (13c isotopic variant)** — Isotopic variant of CO₂ containing carbon‑13, used in some pump configurations.
- **co₂ laser beam** — The high‑power CO₂ laser beam emitted by the FIRL100 system.
- **cqtm lab** — The laboratory where the Quantum Twisting Microscope (cQTM) is operated.
- **cr/au coating (4 nm/100 nm)** — Metallic layer providing electrical connection for the active vdW layer.
- **crbr₃** — Chromium bromide crystal used in magnon‑assisted tunnelling heterostructures.
- **crcl₃** — Transport reagent that produces needle‑shaped CrSb crystals.
- **crestec cabl** — Crestec CABL electron beam lithography system used for sample loading, beam alignment, and exposure.
- **critical exponent β** — Exponent describing the order‑parameter scaling near Tₙ.
- **critical twist angle θc ≈ 38.2°** — Specific twist angle at which the moiré superlattice consists of seven graphene unit cells.
- **crossing point shift** — Shift of the crossing point of the curved –X from zero bias to a finite bias when V_BG ≠ 0.
- **crsb** — Metallic altermagnet CrSb studied in the paper.
- **crsb** — Chromium antimonide, an altermagnetic compound studied in multiple papers.
- **crsb crystal sample** — Physical sample of CrSb used in the inelastic neutron scattering experiments.
- **crsb exhibits anisotropic spin waves with velocities 61 km s⁻¹ (in‑plane) and 58 km s⁻¹ (out‑of‑plane)** — Observed in INS experiments.
- **crsb magnon dispersion is described by a minimal heisenberg model up to third neighbours** — Fit yields J₁, J₂, J₃ and D values.
- **crsb shows clear chiral spin splitting along the γ‑l direction** — First observation of this effect in a metallic altermagnet.
- **cryogenic qtm maps phonon dispersions and epc** — The cryogenic quantum twisting microscope can directly map both phonon spectra and mode‑resolved EPC.
- **cryogenic quantum twisting microscope** — Instrument for low‑temperature twist‑angle resolved measurements.
- **cryogenic quantum twisting microscope** — Low‑temperature implementation of QTM for phonon and collective‑mode measurements.
- **cryogenic quantum twisting microscope (cryo‑qtm)** — Low‑temperature microscope that combines scanning probe with THz/IR optics.
- **cryogenic scanning near‑field optical microscope (csnom)** — The cryo‑SNOM table where the THz beam must be delivered.
- **cryosnom** — Cryogenic scanning near‑field optical microscope used for THz measurements.
- **cryosnom tip** — The scanning probe that receives the focused THz beam inside the cSNOM box.
- **crystal momentum (k)** — Momentum measured relative to the Dirac point in the Brillouin zone.
- **crystal morphology control** — Control of CrSb crystal shape via transport reagents: CrCl₃ yields needle‑shaped crystals, I₂ yields plate‑like crystals.
- **crystallographic alignment** — Precise angular alignment of the graphene lattices during stacking.
- **crystal‑symmetry‑paired spin‑valley locking** — Spin‑valley locking dictated by crystal symmetry in a layered antiferromagnet.
- **csnom** — Scanning near‑field optical microscope employed in the THz–VIS alignment.
- **csnom** — Scanning near‑field optical microscope used in later optical‑table versions.
- **csnom box** — Enclosure that houses the cryogenic scanning near‑field optical microscope tip.
- **csnom box path** — The optical path inside the cSNOM box that the visible mirror must align with.
- **csnom optical setup** — Optical setup used for scattering-type scanning near‑field optical microscopy (cSNOM).
- **csnom table** — cSNOM table where the THz beam is delivered to the near‑field microscope.
- **cube beamsplitter** — Cube‑shaped beamsplitter that directs part of the VIS pilot toward the bolometer window.
- **current article, nature materials (2023)** — Main manuscript describing fabrication, measurements and theory of hBN/graphene/TMD heterostructures.
- **current density is sum of elastic and inelastic contributions** — Nearly aligned devices exhibit combined elastic and inelastic tunnelling currents.
- **current density j** — Current per unit area flowing through the device.
- **current density operator** — Operator J(x) whose matrix element appears in the tunneling amplitude.
- **current instability field (~1 v/nm)** — Field at which the devices start showing current instability, limiting usable bias.
- **current operator i_tb** — Operator derived from the tunneling Hamiltonian to calculate current.
- **current spectral density γ_ph** — Frequency‑dependent magnitude of the photon‑assisted tunneling current.
- **current vs bias (i‑v) trace** — Direct current versus interlayer bias voltage trace extracted from the tunneling measurement.
- **current‑voltage characteristic model** — Model describing the I‑V behavior of the tunnel junction.
- **current‑voltage characteristics model** — Project modeling I‑V behavior of Gr/h‑BN/Gr tunnel junctions.
- **curved sample** — Sample attached to the QTM tip, typically a 2D material on a curved substrate.
- **cut-off photon energy ħω_cut** — Highest emitted photon energy, equal to eVB.
- **cutoff energy λ** — Λ = 3.3 eV used in the Fock dispersion model.
- **cutoff energy ℏω_cut = ev_b** — Maximum photon energy set by the applied bias voltage.
- **cxarcia et al.** — Authors of a theoretical STM study focusing on vacuum tunneling.
- **cxarcia et al. paper** — Publication modeling STM with periodic tip approximation.
- **cyclic voltage sweep protocol** — Voltage‑step sequence (≥50 ms per step) used to acquire I‑V characteristics.
- **d. a. tenne** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **d. akinwande** — First author of the 2019 Nature review on graphene and 2D materials for silicon technology.
- **d. arcon** — Co‑author of the high‑temp quantum spin liquid paper.
- **d. brida** — First author of the 2013 Nature Communications study on ultrafast collinear scattering in graphene.
- **d. chowdhury** — Co‑author of the 2021 PRB on pairing in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **d. călugăru** — Co‑author of reference 26.
- **d. deng** — Co‑author of the 2017 Nano Lett. paper.
- **d. f. mross** — Co‑author of the 2011 Friedel‑oscillations paper.
- **d. feng** — Co‑author of the gate‑tunable phase transition paper.
- **d. g. nocera** — Co‑author of the 2020 Science review on quantum spin liquids.
- **d. goldhaber‑gordon** — Researcher and co‑author of the emergent ferromagnetism paper.
- **d. h. najafabadi** — Co‑author of the 2021 Science paper on alternating‑twist trilayer graphene.
- **d. hayashi** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nat. Commun. on handedness manipulation.
- **d. j. scalapino** — Co‑author of the 1963 and 1966 superconductivity papers.
- **d. j. smith** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **d. k. efetov** — Senior author of the Nano Letters 2026 paper.
- **d. kim** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 PRL on broken Kramers degeneracy in MnTe.
- **d. lee** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **d. m. kennes** — Co‑author of publication_41.
- **d. mihailovic** — Co‑author of the high‑temp quantum spin liquid paper.
- **d. n. basov** — Researcher, co‑author of multiple graphene/α‑RuCl₃ publications.
- **d. pan** — 
- **d. parker** — 
- **d. r. hamann** — Co‑author of the 1985 theory paper on the scanning tunneling microscope.
- **d. r. hamann** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **d. r. hamann** — Co‑author of the STM theory paper.
- **d. sedov** — Researcher co‑author of the 2025 arXiv preprint on probing superconductivity in rhombohedral graphene.
- **d. sánchez‑portal** — Co‑author of the SIESTA method paper.
- **d. turnbull** — Editor mentioned in the reference to Duke's work.
- **d. usanov** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nature paper on altermagnetic lifting of Kramers spin degeneracy.
- **d. watanabe** — Lead author of the 2016 paper on magnetic excitations in kagomé volborthite.
- **d. wolf** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **d. wulferding** — Lead author of the 2010 study on herbertsmithite.
- **d.-f. shao** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2021 Nat. Commun. article on spin‑neutral currents.
- **d.-s. park** — Author listed on the APL Mater. 2024 paper.
- **dahm** — Author listed on the Ann. Phys. 2022 paper.
- **damped plasmons** — Collective charge oscillations that acquire a finite linewidth due to coupling to particle‑hole continua.
- **daniel vaz** — Provided access to the transfer stage.
- **data analysis** — Analysis of experimental data
- **data interpretation** — Interpretation of data
- **david** — Person referenced for beam current settings.
- **david a** — Person referenced for the astigmatism correction procedure.
- **de juan, f.; et al. renormalization of coulomb interaction in graphene: determining observable quantities. phys. rev. b 2010, 82, 125409.** — Investigation of Coulomb interaction renormalization in graphene and its observable consequences.
- **decay constant (κ)** — Exponential factor governing the decrease of STM corrugation with tip‑sample distance.
- **decay constant κ_j** — Decay constant κ_j = sqrt(2m|E_j|)/ħ governing exponential decay of tip wavefunction.
- **decay length (λ)** — Characteristic length (~0.4 Å for typical metals) governing exponential decrease of tunneling current with gap.
- **decoupling of quartic term** — Method of introducing auxiliary fields to obtain a quadratic spinon Hamiltonian.
- **defect state** — Localized electronic states in hBN that can give rise to resonant features in tunnelling spectra.
- **deflection error** — Voltage difference between the setpoint and the actual cantilever deflection, used to infer force.
- **deflection setpoint** — Deflection voltage setpoint (e.g., 0.3 V corresponding to 0.5 µm displacement).
- **degradation of phonons in disordered moiré superlattices (prb, 2019)** — Investigation of phonon lifetime reduction due to disorder in moiré superlattices.
- **delta‑function integration** — Use of Dirac delta functions to perform momentum integrals analytically.
- **demonstrated light emission from gr/h‑bn/gr tunnel junctions** — Core experimental result of the work.
- **dennis v. christensen** — Author listed on the Ann. Phys. 2022 paper.
- **dense k‑grid convergence** — Use of a 3600×3600 k‑point grid to achieve numerical convergence.
- **density dependence** — Variation of properties with carrier density.
- **density functional theory (dft)** — First‑principles electronic‑structure method used to calculate ESP of TBG.
- **density of states (dos)** — Electronic density of states influencing the tunnelling conductance.
- **density of states (tip)** — Density of electronic states in the tip as a function of energy.
- **density of states and zero landau level probed through capacitance of graphene** — Physical Review Letters 2010 paper probing the zero Landau level via capacitance measurements.
- **density of states of undamped plasmon d(ω)** — Theoretical density of states for an undamped plasmon mode.
- **density of states per unit volume of probe tip (d)** — Density of electronic states in the tip material.
- **density of states ν_c(ε)** — Density of states of the dispersive conduction band.
- **density of states ν_v(ε)** — Density of states of the dispersive valence band.
- **density‑density response function χ** — Linear‑response kernel describing how charge density reacts to an external potential.
- **density‑of‑states factor dominates tunnelling current difference** — The only significant difference in current comes from the DOS factor.
- **dependence of emission on twist angle** — Question investigated through device geometry.
- **depletion of electron spill‑out finding** — Observed dramatic depletion of electron spill‑out when moving away from Γ point.
- **derivation of the tersoff‑hamann formula by c. j. chen** — Argument that refines the original Tersoff‑Hamann approximation.
- **derivative of differential conductance d²i/dv²** — Second derivative of current with respect to bias, used to map tunneling features.
- **design of cryogenic quantum twisting microscope** — Engineering effort to build a low‑temperature QTM with THz/IR capabilities.
- **design prevents mechanical interference from cantilever clip at all rotation angles** — Stage geometry avoids contact between AFM parts and the sample during rotation.
- **detect chiral spin splitting in metallic altermagnets** — Open question addressed by the present work.
- **detect fractionalized excitations** — How to observe signatures of fractionalized excitations in 2D quantum spin liquids using tunneling spectroscopy.
- **detector path** — Optical path that carries the beam to the detector.
- **determination of surface structure via stm** — Fundamental problem of extracting atomic‑scale surface geometry using STM.
- **determine microscopic exchange interactions that stabilize magnetic order in crsb** — Key question addressed by the project.
- **determine whether a moiré superconductor is nodal and locate nodes within the mbz** — The central question addressed by the QTM tunneling spectroscopy study.
- **deuterated methyl chloride (cd3cl)** — Deuterated methyl chloride used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **device fabrication** — Fabrication of devices
- **device fabrication project** — Effort to produce graphene/h‑BN/graphene tunnel devices with controlled twist angles.
- **device with 8 l h‑bn barrier, twist angle ≈0.5°** — One of the three devices measured in the I‑V experiments.
- **device with 8 l h‑bn barrier, twist angle ≈1.1°** — Second device in the I‑V series.
- **device with 8 l h‑bn barrier, twist angle ≈2.9°** — Third device in the I‑V series.
- **device with 9 l h‑bn barrier, misaligned (θ ≈ >5°)** — Device showing no resonant tunnelling peak.
- **device with θ = 0.5°, 8 l h‑bn** — A tunnel‑junction sample with a 0.5° twist angle and eight layers of h‑BN.
- **device with θ ≈ 3°** — A tunnel‑junction sample with a twist angle close to 3°, where the resonant peak almost vanishes.
- **device 1** — Device with 7 L h‑BN barrier, θ ≈ 0.8°, area 44 µm², fabricated by fragmentation.
- **device 2** — Device with 12 L h‑BN barrier, θ ≈ 0.6°, area 24 µm², fabricated by fragmentation.
- **device 3** — Device with 8 L h‑BN barrier, θ ≈ 2.9°, area 58 µm², fabricated by fragmentation.
- **device 4a** — Device with 8 L h‑BN barrier, θ ≈ 0.9°, area 79 µm², fabricated by fragmentation.
- **device 5a** — Device with 11 L h‑BN barrier, θ > 5°, area 29 µm², fabricated by fragmentation.
- **device 6** — Device with 8 L h‑BN barrier, θ ≈ 0.5°, area 97 µm², fabricated by optical alignment.
- **device 7** — Device with 9 L h‑BN barrier, θ > 5°, area 25 µm², fabricated by optical alignment.
- **device 8** — Device with 9 L h‑BN barrier, θ > 5°, area 68 µm², fabricated by optical alignment.
- **device 9** — Device with 8 L h‑BN barrier, θ ≈ 1.1°, area 162 µm², fabricated by optical alignment.
- **di/dv** — First derivative of current with respect to bias.
- **di/dv (differential conductance)** — First derivative of the tunnelling current with respect to bias voltage.
- **di/dv characterization** — Measurement of differential conductance versus bias voltage on the tunnel junction.
- **di/dv curve** — Differential conductance trace showing resonant peaks.
- **di/dv enhancement** — Increase in differential conductance observed in certain regions.
- **di/dv map of nematic nodal tbg** — dI/dV map exploring nodal‑gap features in superconducting TBG.
- **di/dv map of superconducting tbg** — Two‑dimensional map of differential conductance as a function of bias and tip wavevector on superconducting TBG.
- **di/dv peaks** — Peaks observed in the differential conductance spectra.
- **di/dv peaks** — Peaks in the differential conductance spectrum indicating electronic states.
- **di/dv peaks arise from tangency of tips fermi line and equal‑energy contours** — Identification of the tangency condition as the origin of dI/dV peaks.
- **di/dv reveals collective excitations** — Observation that differential conductance contains signatures of collective modes.
- **di/dv singularity** — Sharp features in the differential conductance arising from momentum‑conserving tunneling.
- **di/dv spectroscopy** — Differential conductance spectroscopy used to probe electronic states versus voltage bias and filling factor.
- **di/dv spectrum measurement** — Acquisition of differential conductance as a function of bias, twist angle and tip momentum.
- **di/dv steps arise from discontinuous density of states across band edges** — For 2D parabolic bands the density of states is discontinuous at the edges, producing steps in the differential conductance.
- **di/dv vs twist angle experiment** — Measurement of differential conductance spectra while varying the tip twist angle.
- **diagonalization of single‑particle hamiltonian** — Numerical solution of the band structure for each k‑point.
- **diagrammatic approach** — Many‑body perturbation method using Feynman diagrams to compute self‑energy and tunneling rates.
- **dielectric constant (ε⊥)** — Out‑of‑plane dielectric constant of hBN.
- **dielectric constant ε_hbn** — Relative permittivity of hBN, ≈3.2.
- **dielectric environment** — Surrounding medium characterized by εr.
- **dielectric function, screening, and plasmons in two‑dimensional graphene** — Phys. Rev. B 2007 paper by E. H. Hwang & S. Das Sarma on dielectric function and plasmons in graphene.
- **dielectric permittivity εbn = 3.56** — Relative permittivity of h‑BN used in the geometric capacitance expression.
- **dielectric waveguide resonator** — Large‑bore dielectric waveguide forming the resonant cavity of the FIRL100 laser.
- **difference in polarization dependent current** — Observation that polarization dependent current varies with symmetry.
- **difference of tunnelling current between superconducting and normal states** — Observed change in current due to the superconducting density of states.
- **differential conductance measurement** — Experiment measuring dI/dV between tip and TBG while varying q and tip doping.
- **differential conductance measures momentum‑resolved spectral function** — The differential conductance of the QTM tunnel junction directly measures the momentum‑resolved one‑particle spectral function.
- **differential conductance reveals the nearly‑flat part of the plasmon spectrum** — The calculated dI/dV shows features corresponding to the flat region of the plasmon dispersion.
- **differential conductivity (di/dv) measurement** — Derivative of the I‑V curve used to resolve resonant features.
- **diffraction** — Wave phenomenon that causes spreading when the beam has a finite cross‑section.
- **difluoromethane (ch2f2)** — Molecule used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **dilution fridge upgrade** — Installation of a second tower and vibration‑reduced platform to lower electron temperature.
- **dilution fridge upgrade** — Installation of a second tower and improved vibration isolation for lower electron temperatures.
- **dimensionless coupling λ** — Dimensionless measure of electron‑phonon coupling strength.
- **dimensionless coupling λ** — Calculated dimensionless electron‑phonon coupling (e.g., λ_gauge_phason = 1.1/W).
- **dipole feature in d²i/dv²** — Plus‑minus dipole observed in the second derivative at the onset condition.
- **dirac band** — Linear dispersion band in a graphene-like material.
- **dirac cone alignment** — Geometric alignment of the Dirac cones of the two graphene layers in momentum space.
- **dirac cone alignment in momentum space** — Relative positioning of the graphene Dirac points in k‑space, controlled by twist angle and bias.
- **dirac cone band structure** — Linear dispersion relation of electrons in graphene that lacks a band gap.
- **dirac cone dispersion** — Linear energy-momentum relation near the Dirac point in graphene.
- **dirac cone shift at k and k′** — Opposite shifts of the Dirac cones in the two valleys (Δ₁K′ = –Δ₁K).
- **dirac cones** — Linear band‑structure features of graphene that determine momentum conservation.
- **dirac cones reshaped by interaction effects in suspended graphene** — Nat. Phys. 2011 paper on interaction‑induced reshaping of Dirac cones in suspended graphene.
- **dirac density of states (ν)** — Density of states at the Dirac point for each graphene layer.
- **dirac dispersion** — Linear energy‑momentum relation describing the light electrons near Γ.
- **dirac equation analogy** — Relation Vb = ± ħ v_F K θ resembling the Dirac dispersion with Vb as energy and Kθ as momentum.
- **dirac fermions** — Massless charge carriers in graphene described by Dirac equation.
- **dirac hamiltonian** — Low‑energy Hamiltonian for a single graphene layer.
- **dirac plasmons** — Collective charge oscillations in doped graphene with linear (Dirac) dispersion.
- **dirac point** — Linear band crossing point in graphene.
- **dirac qsl** — A QSL with Dirac-like spinon dispersion.
- **dirac sea** — Filled negative‑energy states that contribute to the self‑energy.
- **dirac spin liquid** — QSL with Dirac‑like low‑energy spinon dispersion.
- **dirac trions as origin of missing spectral weight** — The 2025 paper identifies Dirac trions as the cause of the missing spectral weight in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **dirac-matching condition** — Condition when a Dirac point of TBG aligns with a point on MLG bands.
- **dirac-point spectroscopy of flat-band systems with the quantum twisting microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 2025 study of flat‑band Dirac points using QTM.
- **dirac-point spectroscopy of flat-band systems, phys. rev. b 111, 085128 (2025)** — Paper presenting the theoretical analysis.
- **dirac‑flat‑band matching** — A flat‑band feature appears whenever the MLG Dirac point aligns in energy and momentum with a TBG flat band.
- **dirac‑matching features are largely temperature‑insensitive** — The Dirac‑matching peaks persist even at elevated temperatures, unlike the onset features.
- **dirac‑point singularities provide better band‑structure resolution** — Dirac‑point singularities, though weaker, give clearer imaging of flat‑band dispersion regardless of μ_T.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy** — Spectroscopy probing the Dirac point in flat‑band systems.
- **dirac‑point spectroscopy of flat‑band systems with the quantum twisting microscope (2025)** — Introduces Dirac‑point spectroscopy as a probe of flat‑band physics using the QTM.
- **direct bandgap** — Electronic band structure feature of hBN enabling ultraviolet lasing.
- **direct determination of mode‑projected electron‑phonon coupling in the time domain** — Science 366, 1231–1236 (2019) presenting a time‑domain measurement of mode‑projected electron‑phonon coupling.
- **direct exciton** — Exciton formed with electron and hole in the same momentum valley.
- **direct imaging of the energy bands of monolayer and twisted bilayer graphene** — Momentum‑resolved tunnelling maps that reveal the band dispersion of both monolayer graphene and TBG.
- **direct lattice vectors a1, a2** — Primitive vectors defining the graphene unit cell.
- **direct measurement of epc for individual phonon modes** — How can the electron‑phonon coupling of each phonon mode be measured directly?
- **direct observation of the electron‑phonon coupling between empty states in graphite via high‑resolution electron energy‑loss spectroscopy** — Phys. Rev. B 95, 165408 (2017) reporting HREELS observation of electron‑phonon coupling in graphite.
- **direct on-chip optical plasmon detection with an atomically thin semiconductor** — Nano Letters 2015 paper by K. M. Goodfellow et al. demonstrating on‑chip plasmon detection in an atomically thin semiconductor.
- **direct resolution of electron‑electron interaction effects with qtm at room temperature** — Goal to observe many‑body effects in momentum space using QTM.
- **direct tunneling suppression** — The reduction of elastic tunneling current when the twist‑angle‑induced momentum mismatch exceeds the bias window.
- **direct visualization of the charge transfer in graphene/α‑rucl₃ heterostructure** — Direct imaging of interlayer charge transfer in a graphene/α‑RuCl₃ heterostructure.
- **direct‑bandgap properties and evidence for ultraviolet lasing of hexagonal boron nitride single crystal** — Nat. Mater. 2004 paper by K. Watanabe et al. on hBN direct bandgap and UV lasing.
- **direct‑contact graphene bilayer (no barrier)** — Two monolayer graphene sheets placed in direct atomic contact for the study.
- **discarded mirror‑image solutions produce incorrect fermi line** — The alternative set of solutions to Eq. (B2) would generate a mirrored Fermi line and is therefore omitted.
- **discount** — 10 % extra discount when contracting maintenance at the time of purchase.
