- **half‑and quarter‑metals in rhombohedral trilayer graphene (nature 2021)** — Nature 598, 429 (2021) reporting half‑ and quarter‑metal behavior in RTG.
- **half‑filling** — Electron (or spinon) filling of ½ per lattice site.
- **half‑filling condition** — Constraint that each spinon band is half occupied.
- **half‑metal** — Material conducting for one spin channel and insulating for the other.
- **half‑metal behavior** — Conductivity for only one spin channel.
- **hamiltonian (h)** — Total low‑energy Hamiltonian for TBG, composed of intra‑layer and inter‑layer terms.
- **handedness manipulation** — Control of the chirality of propagating excitations.
- **handedness manipulation of propagating antiferromagnetic magnons** — Nat. Commun. 15, 9750 (2024) demonstrating control of magnon handedness.
- **hartree diagram** — First‑order diagram that yields a hermitian contribution to the self‑energy.
- **hartree potential** — Mean‑field electrostatic potential arising from the carrier density.
- **hartree theory** — Self-consistent mean-field approach for lattice-relaxed twisted bilayer graphene.
- **hartree‑fock self‑consistent‑field calculation** — Theoretical method used by Watson and Freeman to compute Ni++ wave functions.
- **hbn band gap ~6 ev** — Energy gap of hBN, comparable to graphene work function.
- **hbn capping** — Encapsulation of TMD samples and tips with a thin hBN layer to enable ambient transfer.
- **hbn thickness** — Vertical dimension of the hBN barrier, a key control knob for tunneling.
- **hbn/graphene‑coated substrate on rotating platform** — Part of the QTM that holds the hBN dielectric and graphene layer and can be rotated to set the twist angle.
- **hcooh 513 mm line** — FIR laser line at 513 mm using HCOOH with typical power 20 mW.
- **hcooh 742.6 mm line** — FIR laser line at 742.6 mm using HCOOH with typical power 2 mW.
- **he vessel, valves and scroll pump** — Helium vessel together with valves and a scroll pump.
- **heat sensitive crystal paper** — Crystal paper that changes color with temperature, used for FIR beam profiling.
- **heat transport study** — Measurement of thermal conductivity to probe low‑energy excitations.
- **heaviside function** — Step function representing zero‑temperature limit of the Fermi‑Dirac distribution.
- **heavy quasiparticles and cascades without symmetry breaking in twisted bilayer graphene (2023)** — Nature Communications paper reporting heavy quasiparticles and cascade phenomena in TBG.
- **heisenberg hamiltonian** — Spin Hamiltonian employed to describe the tunneling barrier.
- **heisenberg model** — Minimal spin Hamiltonian used to describe the magnon dispersion in CrSb.
- **helical trilayer** — Trilayer graphene with a helical stacking order.
- **helical trilayer graphene** — Trilayer graphene with a helical stacking order at the magic angle.
- **helium** — He component of the laser gas mix (≈99.999 %).
- **helium‑neon laser** — Helium‑neon laser employed for re‑alignment of the optical path.
- **hematite (fe₂o₃)** — Altermagnetic hematite investigated for chiral magnon band splitting.
- **herbertsmithite** — Kagome lattice compound ZnCu₃(OH)₆Cl₂, known quantum spin liquid candidate.
- **heterostructure sample** — Van der Waals heterostructures that are cleaned by AFM contact‑mode before measurement.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (hbn) crystal** — High‑quality hBN crystals supplied by K.W. and T.T. for device fabrication.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (h‑bn)** — Layered insulator used in graphene/h‑BN heterostructures.
- **hexagonal boron nitride (h‑bn)** — A wide‑bandgap 2D insulator often used as a dielectric spacer.
- **hexagonal brillouin zone of graphene** — Ideal six‑fold symmetric BZ whose corners host the six Dirac points.
- **hexagonal lattice symmetry** — Six‑fold rotational symmetry of graphene/graphite that leads to 60° periodicity.
- **hidden symmetry at even integer filling** — The 2020 PRX uncovers a hidden symmetry of magic‑angle graphene at even integer filling.
- **high carrier density limit** — Regime where carrier density is large.
- **high in‑plane momentum components** — Large lateral momentum parts of the wavefunction that decay rapidly in the out‑of‑plane direction.
- **high magnon group velocity** — Observed magnon group velocities exceed those of known magnonic materials such as α‑Fe₂O₃.
- **high reproducibility of splitting features** — Statistical clustering of nesting‑line positions shows consistent splitting across measurements.
- **higher bias leads to shorter lifetimes** — Observation that increasing the bias (toward the Fermi energy) reduces the electron lifetime.
- **higher-order tunneling processes** — Third-order (and beyond) contributions to interlayer tunneling.
- **higher‑order altermagnetic exchange interactions** — Exchange terms responsible for chiral spin splitting of magnons.
- **higher‑order exciton** — Excitonic states at energies above the A exciton.
- **higher‑order peaks are orders of magnitude weaker** — Observation that peaks at 27.8°, 32.2°, 13.2°, 46.8° are 2–4 orders of magnitude smaller and buried in background.
- **highly confined tunable mid‑infrared plasmonics in graphene nanoresonators** — Nano Lett. 2013 paper by Brar, V. W. et al. on mid‑infrared plasmonics in graphene nanoresonators.
- **highly mobile gapless excitations in a two‑dimensional candidate quantum spin liquid** — Science 328, 1246 (2010) by M. Yamashita et al.
- **high‑bias inelastic prevalence** — At high bias the current rises non‑linearly due to the increasing importance of inelastic tunneling.
- **high‑mobility graphene** — Graphene with high carrier mobility used for ultrafast switching experiments.
- **high‑resolution elastic momentum‑resolved tunnelling near 21.8°** — Research effort to perform momentum‑resolved tunnelling spectroscopy close to the commensurate angle and to bound strain.
- **high‑resolution momentum‑resolved imaging experiment** — Cryogenic experiment using the same tip and sample to acquire a sharp momentum‑resolved map near 21.8°.
- **high‑resolution stm measurement** — Scanning tunneling microscopy with atomic resolution used by Binnig et al. to image Au(110) surfaces.
- **high‑symmetry directions** — Reciprocal‑space directions where NRSS vanishes and earlier INS missed magnon splitting.
- **high‑velocity magnons** — Spin‑split magnons in CrSb that propagate with high group velocity.
- **high‑velocity spin waves** — Spin waves traveling at high group velocities.
- **hollow‑tube thz waveguide** — 10 mm diameter multimode tube that guides THz radiation into the cryostat.
- **home‑built motorized transfer setup** — Motorized transfer system built inside an argon glovebox for assembling heterostructures.
- **home‑built qtm setup** — Experimental configuration described in Fig. 1a,b.
- **homogeneous intensity distribution** — Uniform light intensity across the entire gap area observed in graphene‑based tunnel devices.
- **honpas** — Linear‑scaling open‑source software for large‑system quantum‑chemical simulations.
- **honpas: a linear scaling open‑source solution for large system simulations** — Int. J. Quantum Chem. 2015 paper introducing the HONPAS software.
- **horizontal conductance lines from inelastic phonon emission** — Bias‑independent horizontal features in Vb‑Vbg conductance maps attributed to phonon emission.
- **hot electron tunneling** — Tunneling of high‑energy electrons that can enhance plasmon decay.
- **hot‑electron generation** — Creation of energetic charge carriers in metals upon plasmon decay.
- **hourglass-shaped feature** — Prominent high‑bias feature in d²I/dV² maps.
- **how are excitons involved in momentum conservation during tunnelling?** — Open question addressed by the WS₂ device experiments.
- **how can graphene plasmons be electrically detected?** — Exploration of detection schemes based on inelastic tunnelling and charge separation.
- **how can long‑lived plasmons be achieved?** — Study of conditions (bias, gap region, doping) that maximize plasmon lifetimes.
- **how can the plasmon frequency be controlled with bias voltage?** — Investigation of the relationship between applied bias and the peak frequency of excited plasmons.
- **how does bias voltage affect stm imaging of semiconductors?** — Question about the role of voltage polarity and doping on STM images of Si(111).
- **how does carrier density affect fermi velocity and compressibility?** — Investigation of the density dependence of v_F and electronic compressibility.
- **how does epc depend on twist angle?** — Investigation of the variation of electron‑phonon coupling with the moiré twist angle.
- **how does in‑situ twist‑angle control affect probing the spin structure factor?** — Question about using the twist angle as a tuning knob for momentum‑resolved spectroscopy.
- **how does misalignment affect resonant tunneling behavior?** — Investigation of the θ‑dependent evolution of resonant features.
- **how does pressure tune moiré band structure?** — Investigation of pressure as a continuous knob for twist‑angle‑dependent electronic structure.
- **how does twist angle affect conductance in graphite?** — Question investigating the relationship between twist angle and interlayer conductance.
- **how does twist angle control the electronic environment and tip radius?** — Open question investigating angle‑dependent electronic properties.
- **how does twist angle influence electronic transport in layered materials?** — Central question motivating the development of the QTM.
- **how phason modes couple to electronic tunnelling** — Why does a layer‑antisymmetric phason mode strongly modulate interlayer tunnelling?
- **how to achieve light emission from gapless graphene** — Question addressed by stacking and twisting graphene layers with an h‑BN spacer.
- **how to achieve momentum‑ and energy‑resolved probing of quantum spin liquids in 2d?** — Seeks experimental probes with resolution comparable to inelastic neutron scattering for monolayer quantum spin liquids.
- **how to achieve precise control of the twist angle?** — Question driving the development of fragmentation and optical alignment methods.
- **how to correctly account for electrons in the vicinity of the k′ valley?** — Open question regarding the treatment of electrons near the opposite valley.
- **how to couple thz gas laser to csnom with minimal absorption and divergence** — Open question driving the design of the optical path.
- **how to efficiently generate and detect graphene plasmons in compact devices?** — Open question driving the development of integrated plasmonic components.
- **how to improve snr using hbn thickness?** — Optimization of barrier thickness to maximize signal quality.
- **how to map phonon dispersion using twist angle and bias voltage?** — Question addressed by measuring conductance steps across the θ‑Vb plane.
- **how to maximize electron interactions at the magic angle** — Question explored by precise angle control and gating.
- **how to measure momentum-resolved spectral function** — Question of extracting momentum-resolved spectral function via dI/dV.
- **how to measure the spin structure factor of quantum spin liquids** — Investigation of probing QSLs via tunneling spectroscopy.
- **how to optimize tilt angle for qtm operation** — Open question addressed by the QTM tip‑fabrication effort.
- **how to probe an electron at several locations simultaneously** — Open question addressed by developing the QTM.
- **how to probe tmds with momentum‑resolved tunneling** — Investigation of strategies to achieve momentum‑resolved spectroscopy on TMD materials with small Fermi surfaces.
- **how to reduce heat load from superconducting contacts?** — Design strategies to minimize thermal injection from leads.
- **how to resolve the plasmon spectrum via inelastic tunneling?** — Question of whether the plasmon dispersion can be extracted from the differential conductance.
- **how to resolve the two‑fold ambiguity in determining p* from di/dv singularities** — Question about removing the ambiguity in locating the momentum point p*.
- **how to tune exciton binding energy with an electric field?** — Exploring electric‑field control of excitonic states in twisted graphene.
- **how to tune the emission peak with bias voltage** — Question addressed by the study.
- **hubbard bands** — Higher‑energy features showing cascading behaviour in the flat‑band spectra.
- **hubbard model** — Model describing interacting electrons on a lattice.
- **humidity effect** — Impact of relative humidity on THz transmission.
- **humidity impact on thz transmission** — How does relative humidity affect THz transmission power?
- **humitat relativa** — Humitat màxima admissible fins al 95 % sense condensació.
- **hybrid surface‑phonon‑plasmon polariton modes in graphene/h‑bn heterostructures** — Observed hybrid polariton modes resulting from coupling between graphene plasmons and h‑BN phonons.
- **hybrid surface‑phonon‑plasmon polariton modes in graphene/monolayer h‑bn heterostructures** — Nano Lett. 2014 paper by Brar, V. W. et al. on hybrid surface‑phonon‑plasmon polariton modes.
- **hybrid systems** — Combinations of graphene with other materials to achieve new functionalities.
- **hybridization** — Interaction between the two plasmon branches that leads to repulsion and energy splitting.
- **hybridization of valence and conduction bands** — Mixing of valence and conduction states near the magic angle.
- **hybridization pushes optical plasmon to higher energy** — Hybridization repels the optical plasmon toward higher energies.
- **hydrodynamic behaviour** — Collective fluid‑like motion of electrons observed in monolayer graphene with weak EPC.
- **hydrodynamic behaviour phenomenon** — Fluid‑like electron flow in monolayer graphene due to weak EPC.
- **hydrodynamics of electrons in graphene** — J. Phys.: Condens. Matter 2018 review of electron fluid behavior.
- **hyperbolic intersection of dirac cones** — At very low bias the intersection of the cones forms two hyperbolas.
- **hyperbolic intersection singularity** — Singularity arising from a hyperbolic band intersection contributing a single branch to tunneling current.
- **h‑bn barrier thickness (l)** — Number of h‑BN layers (e.g., 7 L, 8 L, 9 L, 11 L, 12 L) separating the two graphene electrodes.
- **h‑bn layer count l** — Number of h‑BN layers forming the tunnel barrier (e.g., 8 L).
- **h‑bn‑graphene heterostructure** — A stack of graphene and h‑BN layers.
- **h₂o molecule** — Water molecule used to probe adsorption preferences on TBG.
- **h₂o molecule** — Molecule used to probe adsorption curves on compressed TBG.
- **h₂o prefers ab and ba stacking centers** — Water adsorption is energetically favored at AB and BA sites.

- **i. das** — Co‑author of the Nano Letters 2026 paper.
- **i. j. vera‑marun** — Co‑author of the fractionalization tunneling spectroscopy paper.
- **i. k. robinson** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **i. martin** — Co‑author of the 2018 theory of phonon‑mediated superconductivity.
- **i. martin** — Co‑author of the 2018 PRL on phonon‑mediated superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene.

- **i. yudhistira** — Co‑author of the screening‑gate superconductivity paper.
- **i. yudhistira** — Co‑author of the 2019 PRB on gauge‑phonon dominated resistivity in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **i.a. leahy** — Co‑author of the 2017 study on anomalous thermal conductivity in α‑RuCl₃.
- **iets measurement** — Measurement of the differential conductance to obtain the IETS signal.
- **iets signal** — Inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy signal measured in the quantum twisting microscope.
- **iets signal** — Signal measured in inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy.
- **iets signal measurement** — Measurement of the inelastic contribution to the tunneling current as a function of voltage and twist angle.
- **iets signal proportional to dynamical spin structure factor** — The inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy signal contains a contribution directly proportional to the dynamical spin structure factor of the quantum spin liquid.
- **iets signature of qsl excitations** — Clear inelastic tunneling feature directly linked to QSL spin structure factor.
- **ilani** — Author of the PRB 2024 paper on quantum spin liquids.
- **ilani et al., 2025, quantum twisting microscopy of phonons** — Nature 641, 345 (2025) paper on phonon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope.
- **imaged inter‑valley coherent order** — Direct imaging of inter‑valley coherence in twisted trilayer graphene (Kim et al., 2023).
- **imaging coulomb interactions** — Spatial mapping of electron-electron interactions.
- **imaging coulomb interactions and migrating dirac cones in twisted graphene** — Observed Coulomb interaction effects and Dirac cone migration.
- **imaging electric fields in sers and ters using the vibrational stark effect** — J. Phys. Chem. Lett. 2013 paper demonstrating electric‑field imaging via the vibrational Stark effect.
- **imaging energy bands via momentum‑resolved tunnelling** — Goal of the QTM’s momentum‑resolved tunnelling modality.
- **imaging inter‑valley coherent order in magic‑angle twisted trilayer graphene (kim et al., 2023)** — Direct imaging of inter‑valley coherence in twisted trilayer graphene.
- **imaging the sub-moire potential landscape using an atomic single electron transistor** — Nature 2026 paper on imaging sub‑moire potential landscape with an atomic single electron transistor.
- **imaging tip contact area in situ** — Scanning the QTM tip laterally across a region with a TMD layer to visualise the contact.
- **impact of many-body effects on landau levels in graphene** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 2018 study of Landau‑level renormalization.
- **impact of spin–orbit coupling** — Effect of spin–orbit interaction on superconductivity.
- **impact of spin–orbit coupling on superconductivity in rhombohedral graphene** — 2025 Nature Materials paper by J. Yang et al.
- **improve tip characterization** — Need for better experimental knowledge of tip structure to refine STM models.
- **in preparation** — A forthcoming work authored by Y. Waschitz, A. Stern and Y. Oreg.
- **in situ twistronics experiments** — Project investigating angle‑dependent tunneling using the QTM.
- **in-layer screening potential** — Screening potential computed within the layer using Eq. (B5).
- **inability to distinguish missing row** — STM cannot reliably detect the presence or absence of a missing row in the second layer of Au(110).
- **inbar et al., nature 614, 682–687 (2023)** — Original paper describing the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **incident neutron energy (eᵢ)** — Energy of neutrons incident on the sample during INS.
- **incommensurate twist angles** — Twist angles without a periodic moiré superlattice.
- **incorporate tip potential consistently** — How to include the tip contribution to the potential in STM modeling.
- **increase in di/dv at plasmon‑assisted onset** — Observed increase in differential conductance when plasmon‑assisted inelastic tunneling into remote bands begins.
- **independent occupation probabilities** — Assumption B2 that tip and sample occupations are independent.
- **independently tunable flat bands and correlations in a graphene double moiré system (2025)** — Phys. Rev. Lett. paper on flat‑band engineering and correlation effects in a graphene double‑moiré heterostructure.
- **indirect excitons dominate in mo‑based tmds** — Peak positions in Mo‑based devices match K–Q indirect‑exciton energies.
- **indirect tmd exciton** — Exciton with momentum outside the light cone, requiring umklapp processes.
- **indirect‑to‑direct bandgap crossover** — Thermally driven transition observed in 2D semiconductors.
- **individual quantum system plasmon control setup** — Setup enabling dynamic control of plasmon generation with a single quantum emitter.
- **inelastic current** — Component of the tunnelling current that transfers energy to excitations such as plasmons or phonons.
- **inelastic electron transitions** — Excitation and detection of plasmons via inelastic tunnelling of electrons.
- **inelastic electron tunneling** — Electron tunneling accompanied by energy loss to excitations such as plasmons.
- **inelastic electron tunneling excitation** — Exciting plasmons via inelastic tunneling of electrons.
- **inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy** — Technique to probe the spin structure factor via the tunneling current through the heterostructure.
- **inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method that resolves the probability of energy‑momentum transfer during tunneling.
- **inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy (iets)** — Spectroscopic technique that measures the differential conductance as a function of bias voltage to probe excitations in a sample.
- **inelastic electron tunnelling spectroscopy (iets)** — Detection of vibrational excitations through changes in tunnelling conductance.
- **inelastic features in tunneling spectra** — Additional conductance structures arising from Cooper‑pair scattering with phonons.
- **inelastic light scattering quantum pathways** — Quantum pathways governing inelastic light scattering in graphene, controlled in the 2011 Nature work.
- **inelastic momentum-conserving tunneling spectroscopy** — Technique that probes inelastic, momentum‑conserving electron tunneling with the QTM.
- **inelastic momentum‑conserving electronic tunnelling** — Inelastic tunnelling process that emits a phonon to conserve momentum between twisted layers.
- **inelastic momentum‑resolved spectroscopy** — Measurement that probes inelastic tunnelling while conserving in‑plane momentum.
- **inelastic scattering by thermal phonons** — Temperature‑dependent scattering process that creates a slowly varying background in conductance measurements.
- **inelastic tunneling conductance** — Conductance arising from inelastic electron‑plasmon interactions across the tip‑sample junction.
- **inelastic tunneling into flat-band manifold yields stronger spectral features than into dispersive bands** — Result of calculations showing larger normalized inelastic tunneling rate and density of states for flat bands.
- **inelastic tunneling into the flat‑band manifold is not momentum selective** — Because electron velocity is low, tunneling into flat bands couples to the momentum‑unresolved plasmon density of states.
- **inelastic tunneling rate ≈ 4×10⁻³** — Normalized rate for plasmon‑assisted tunneling into remote bands.
- **inelastic tunnelling (phenomenon)** — Discrete steps in conductance indicating phonon‑assisted tunnelling.
- **inelastic tunnelling spectroscopy** — Measurement of differential conductance to resolve phonon‑related steps.
- **inelastic tunnelling steps** — Conductance jumps associated with phonon emission during tunnelling.
- **inelastic x‑ray scattering** — Momentum‑resolved probe of phonon dispersion using X‑rays.
- **infrared nanoimaging** — Near‑field infrared microscopy used to visualize graphene plasmons.
- **infrared spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic probing in the infrared frequency range.
- **infrared spectroscopy for diagnosing superlattice minibands in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene** — arXiv:2404.05716
- **infrared spectroscopy of tunable dirac terahertz magneto‑plasmons in graphene** — Nano Lett. 2012 paper by Yan, H. et al. on infrared spectroscopy of Dirac terahertz magneto‑plasmons.
- **ins spectrum** — Energy‑ and momentum‑resolved neutron intensity map revealing magnon dispersions.
- **instrumental resolution** — Energy resolution of the neutron spectrometer (≈20 meV).
- **integral gain 0.8** — Integral feedback gain setting for the AFM controller.
- **integrated photonics** — Application area that could benefit from tunable on‑chip light sources.
- **integration by parts** — Mathematical technique used in derivations.
- **intensity of di/dv peaks** — Amplitude of the differential conductance peaks.
- **intensity‑map measurement** — Photon‑count maps obtained from EMCCD images, normalized by integration time and pixel area.
- **inter** — Connects electrical lines from the tip/sample (through BM24-10DS_2-0.35V_53 adapter) to lines in the fridge.
- **inter pcb** — PCB that connects electrical lines from the tip/sample (through BM24-10DS_2-0.35V_53 adapter) to lines in the fridge.
- **inter-valley electron-phonon coupling** — Coupling between electrons in different valleys mediated by phonons.
- **interacting chiral electrons at the 2d dirac points: a review** — Rep. Prog. Phys. 2021 review of chiral electron interactions.
- **interacting dispersion** — Band dispersion that includes electron‑electron interaction effects (Eq. 1).
- **interacting electrons in graphene: fermi velocity renormalization and optical response** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 2017 paper on Fermi‑velocity renormalization.
- **interacting energy bands of magic angle twisted bilayer graphene revealed by qtm** — Observation that QTM can resolve interacting bands in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **interaction phenomena in graphene seen through quantum capacitance** — PNAS 2013 paper using quantum capacitance.
- **interaction-driven nonlinear dispersion (fock)** — Nonlinear graphene band dispersion arising from electron‑electron interactions.
- **interband scattering** — Scattering process between different electronic bands.
- **interband superconductivity** — Cooper pairing between different electronic bands.
- **interface polaritons** — Hybrid light‑matter modes at the interface of black‑phosphorus heterostructures.
- **interface quality** — Degree of cleanliness and flatness at the graphene/h‑BN interfaces.
- **interference arm** — Arm of the interferometer where the THz and reference beams interfere.
- **interferometric tunneling mechanism** — Mechanism in QTM that enhances sensitivity to small band‑structure modifications.
- **interlayer coulomb interaction** — Coulomb interaction between the tip layer and the TBG sample.
- **interlayer coulomb interactions** — Coulomb coupling between tip and sample layers.
- **interlayer coulomb interactions** — Coulomb coupling between layers that gives rise to vertex corrections.
- **interlayer coupling matrix t_q,ξ(q,q)** — Matrix describing tunneling amplitudes between top and bottom graphene layers, dependent on momentum and valley.
- **interlayer coupling mechanism** — Phonon‑mediated electron coupling that involves motion of the two graphene layers relative to each other.
- **interlayer coupling strength** — Interaction between the two graphene layers that depends on twist angle and distance.
- **interlayer coupling strongly affects plasmon dispersion** — The paper reports that the strength of interlayer coupling in twisted van der Waals materials markedly modifies the plasmon dispersion.
- **interlayer distance** — Vertical spacing between the graphene layers, influencing electronic coupling.
- **interlayer distance d** — Interlayer separation between the two graphene sheets, set to 1 nm.
- **interlayer distance relaxation under pressure** — Structural relaxation of TBG under a target pressure of ~1.5 GPa.
- **interlayer electron‑boson coupling strength (g_inter)** — Coupling strength defined in Eq. (B18).
- **interlayer electron‑plasmon coupling** — Coupling between electrons in different layers mediated by plasmons.
- **interlayer exciton** — Bound electron‑hole pair spanning two graphene layers.
- **interlayer hopping amplitude** — Finite‑range hopping term t(k_B′)+G_B that couples the two graphene layers.
- **interlayer hopping amplitude (t(r))** — Distance‑dependent tunneling amplitude between p_z orbitals on different layers.
- **interlayer mechanism** — Mechanism governing interlayer phonon coupling.
- **interlayer spacing** — Atomic‑scale distance between the two graphene sheets separated by hBN.
- **interlayer tunneling amplitude (|tₖ|²)** — Squared magnitude of the k‑dependent tunneling matrix element, governing conductance peak strength.
- **interlayer tunneling hamiltonian** — Theoretical setup describing tunneling between twisted graphene layers.
- **interlayer tunneling hamiltonian (h_tun)** — Effective Hamiltonian describing electron tunneling between two twisted graphene layers.
- **interlayer tunneling hamiltonian (ĥtun)** — Operator describing electron tunneling between tip and sample, conserving crystal momentum.
- **interlayer tunneling parameters (w₀, w₁)** — Amplitudes describing interlayer tunneling in MA TBG.
- **interlayer tunnelling** — Quantum tunnelling of electrons between the two graphene layers of the TBG stack.
- **interlayer tunnelling** — Quantum tunnelling of electrons between two stacked layers, modulated by lattice deformations.
- **intermediate regime finding** — Experiments operate in a regime where geometric and quantum capacitances are of comparable magnitude.
- **interplay of thermal and quantum spin fluctuations in the kagome lattice compound herbertsmithite** — Phys. Rev. B 82, 144412 (2010) by D. Wulferding et al.
- **intervalley gaps** — Energy gaps between different valley bands.
- **intervalley tunneling** — Tunneling between K and K′ valleys of adjacent layers.
- **inter‑layer term h_tt** — Part of the Hamiltonian describing tunneling between the two graphene layers.
- **inter‑valley coherent order** — Coherent superposition of electronic states from different valleys in graphene.
- **inter‑valley spin‑singlet superconductivity** — Superconducting state where Cooper pairs involve electrons from opposite valleys with opposite spin.
- **intraband transitions** — Electron‑hole pair excitations within the same band, defining a low‑energy gap region.
- **intralayer and interlayer electron–phonon interactions in twisted graphene heterostructures** — Nature Communications 9, 1221 (2018) studying intra‑ and inter‑layer electron‑phonon interactions in twisted graphene.
- **intralayer nearest‑neighbor tunneling amplitude t∥** — Intralayer hopping amplitude, t∥≈2.7 eV.
- **intra‑band pairing** — Superconducting pairing that occurs within a single electronic band.
- **intra‑layer term h_bb** — Part of the Hamiltonian describing electrons within a single graphene layer.
- **intrinsic lifetime (τ)** — Characteristic decay time of a plasmon limited by material quality and acoustic phonon scattering.
- **intrinsic lifetime (τ)** — Fundamental plasmon lifetime limited by material quality and acoustic phonon scattering.
- **intrinsic quantized anomalous hall effect in a moiré heterostructure (science 2020)** — Science 367, 900 (2020) demonstrating QAHE in a moiré system.
- **intrinsically undamped plasmon modes in narrow electron bands (2019)** — Theoretical prediction of long‑lived plasmons in narrow‑band systems.
- **inverse square‑root singularity of ν** — Density‑of‑states singularity scaling as 1/√ε, analogous to a 1D parabolic band.
- **inverse temperature (β)** — Factor β = 1/(k_B T) appearing in Matsubara sums.
- **inverse‑square‑root divergence** — A divergence of dI/dV that scales as |V‑V*|^{-1/2} when the sign of β is positive.
- **inverted nikon te300 microscope** — Host platform for the oil‑immersion objective and detection optics.
- **inverted slope on hole side** — Change of sign of the linear dependence when the device is tuned to the hole side.
- **investigate ciss in solid‑state systems** — How does chiral‑induced spin selectivity manifest in twisted quantum materials?
- **investigation of exciton‑assisted tunnelling in tmds** — Research effort aiming to understand exciton‑mediated transport phenomena.
- **in‑layer coupling mechanism** — Electron‑phonon coupling arising from lattice vibrations confined within a single graphene layer.
- **in‑layer electron-plasmon interaction** — Electron‑plasmon coupling occurring within a single graphene layer.
- **in‑layer electron‑phonon coupling** — EPC that changes intralayer hopping amplitudes.
- **in‑layer epc** — Electron‑phonon coupling confined to a single graphene layer.
- **in‑plane momentum (k∥)** — Momentum component parallel to the graphene layers that appears in the energy‑momentum conservation condition.
- **in‑plane wave vector (k∥)** — Component of electron momentum parallel to the layers, conserved during tunnelling.
- **in‑plane wave vector k∥** — Magnitude of the conserved parallel momentum component.
- **in‑series sum of quantum capacitances** — Effective quantum capacitance when two layers with possibly different carrier densities are combined in series.
- **in‑situ twistronics** — Measurement of transport properties while continuously varying the twist angle between two vdW layers.
- **ion beam current 10 pa** — Current of the ion beam used in FIB deposition.
- **ionizing air blower** — Device used to neutralise surface charges on substrates before stacking.
- **iris** — A small aperture used as a reference point for VIS alignment.
- **ising altermagnet** — Altermagnet with Ising‑type anisotropy, as realized in CrSb.
- **ising order** — Magnetic ordering with spins constrained to a single axis, leading to a gapped magnon spectrum.
- **ising single‑ion anisotropy d** — Anisotropy term (0.15 meV) that gaps the CrSb magnon spectrum.
- **isospin excitations** — Collective excitations involving spin, valley, or layer pseudospin.
- **isospin magnetism** — Magnetic ordering in the isospin degree of freedom.
- **isospin magnetism and spin‑polarized superconductivity in bernal bilayer graphene** — Science 2022 paper on isospin magnetism and spin‑polarized superconductivity.
- **isotropic coupling** — Coupling assumed to depend only on boson energy, not on momentum.
- **isotropic superconducting gap** — A uniform energy gap Δ assumed for the superconductor in the analysis.
- **it t-5 tc 230/230 transformer** — Transformador de tensió de la sèrie IT de SALICRU, amb entrada i sortida de 230 V, potència nominal 5000 VA/5000 W i factor de potència 1.
- **iterative scanning and position adjustment** — Fine alignment performed by repeatedly scanning and moving the XY stages until the rotation centre matches the scan centre.
- **ito mirror** — Indium‑tin‑oxide mirror used in the THz optical train.
- **iv run** — Current‑voltage measurement executed via Zurich API or Python script.
- **ivanov** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2021 Nature Physics article.
- **ivar giaever** — Physicist author of a 1960 paper on tunnelling.

- **kitaev magnets** — Materials described by the Kitaev model.
- **kitaev spin liquid** — Quantum spin liquid described by the exactly solvable Kitaev model.
- **knif3** — Potassium nickel fluoride, a host crystal used for powder form‑factor measurements of Ni++.
- **knife‑edge** — Beam‑profiling technique that uses a sharp edge to scan the beam.

- **l110 quantum twisting microscope** — The main experimental platform (QTM) used by the QNOE group.
- **l208** — Laser system L208 used in the optical experiments.
- **la phonon mode (longitudinal acoustic)** — Longitudinal acoustic phonon.
- **lake et al., phys. rev. lett. 111, 137205 (2013)** — Multispinon continua at zero and finite temperature in a near‑ideal Heisenberg chain.
- **landau damping of plasmons** — Suppression of plasmon intensity when its energy enters the interband transition region.
- **landau theory** — Phenomenological framework describing phase transitions via order parameters.
- **landauer formula** — A formula for conductance that is generalized by NEGF.
- **large exciton binding energy in tmd monolayers** — High binding energy that enables excitonic resonances to be observed at room temperature.
- **large in‑plane wave‑vector transfer (~17 nm⁻¹)** — Magnitude of in‑plane momentum needed to bridge graphene–gold mismatch.
- **large‑area graphene film** — Continuously grown graphene sheet transferred onto substrates for transparent electrodes.
- **large‑area synthesis** — Method to fabricate extensive heterostructures.
- **large‑u limit** — Regime of large on‑site interaction U in Hubbard models.
- **laser bench for l110** — Optical bench that holds all laser and detection components for the L110 setup.
- **laser cut and stack method** — Method used to create twisted bilayer graphene.
- **laser displacement meter** — Instrument for measuring wafer levelling.
- **laser gas fill lifetimes** — Lifetime of the gas fill inside the FIRL100; insufficient evacuation can cause failure to meet specifications.
- **laser head fittings** — 9 mm internal‑diameter hose and securing clips supplied with the FIRL100 head.
- **laser rack + bbs for l208** — Laser rack equipped with beam blocks (BBs) that serves the L208 system.
- **laser safety** — Safety considerations for operating class IV lasers.
- **laser safety officer** — The appointed officer responsible for all aspects of laser safety for the FIRL100 system.
- **laser safety procedures** — Recommended safety actions such as wearing spectacles, appointing a safety officer, and interlocking equipment.
- **laser voltage 5 v** — Voltage readout of the AFM laser detector during alignment.
- **lateral displacement (~1 µm)** — Typical lateral shift caused by the piezo‑rotator during rotation.
- **lateral shift (d)** — In‑plane displacement between the two graphene layers.
- **lateral shift d** — Relative lateral displacement between layers.
- **lateral size** — In‑plane dimension of graphene nanostructures; <10 nm required for visible plasmons.
- **lattice collective modes** — Collective vibrations of the electronic lattice.
- **lattice collective modes from a continuum model** — The 2021 PRB derives lattice collective modes using a continuum model of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **lattice collective modes from a continuum model of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (2021)** — Physical Review B work deriving lattice collective modes via a continuum model.
- **lattice collective modes from a continuum model of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (phys. rev. b 2021)** — Phys. Rev. B 104, 035119 (2021) presenting lattice collective modes via continuum modeling.
- **lattice relaxations** — Structural adjustments of the moiré lattice.
- **layer hybridization** — Mixing of electronic states from the two graphene layers that localizes charge in the middle of the bilayer.
- **layer subspace** — Hilbert‑space sector distinguishing tip (layer 1) and sample (layer 0) degrees of freedom.
- **layer‑antisymmetric za phonon (often called zo′)** — Specific out‑of‑plane phonon mode observed near the commensurate angles.
- **ldos map** — Local density of states map measured before and after molecule adsorption.
- **lead** — Element used in early tunneling studies of phonon spectra.
- **lead phonon spectrum calculated from superconducting density of states** — Early work extracting phonon spectra from tunneling data on lead.
- **least‑squares optimization** — Fitting procedure used to extract α, ϵ_⊥/d, and Λ from the spectra.
- **lee** — Author of the PRB 2024 paper on quantum spin liquids.
- **lee & morampudi, phys. rev. b 107, 195102 (2023)** — Proposal to detect emergent gauge field and its Meissner effect in spin liquids using NV centers.
- **lee et al. measurement of the elastic properties and intrinsic strength of monolayer graphene** — Science 321, 385–388 (2008) reporting elastic properties of monolayer graphene.
- **lee et al., 2025, revealing electron‑electron interactions in graphene at room temperature with the quantum twisting microscope** — arXiv preprint describing QTM measurement of electron‑electron interactions.
- **length x cm** — Specified length of pico-coax lines.
- **leonid i. glazman** — Author Leonid I. Glazman
- **lever-arm effect** — Enhancement of temperature smearing due to velocity ratio.
- **lifetime broadening γ₀ = 4 mev** — Extracted lifetime broadening from fitting the tunneling peak.
- **lifetime of two‑dimensional electrons measured by tunneling spectroscopy (murphy et al., 1995)** — Paper reporting the lifetime of 2D electrons using tunneling spectroscopy.
- **lifting of kramers spin degeneracy** — Removal of Kramers-protected spin degeneracy in altermagnets.
- **light at the end of the tunnel** — ACS Photonics 2018 perspective on light emission from tunnelling devices.
- **light concentration at the nanometer scale** — Álvarez‑Puebla, R. A. et al., J. Phys. Chem. Lett. 2010, on nanometer‑scale light focusing.
- **light cone** — Region in momentum‑frequency space accessible to propagating photons.
- **light emission** — Photon generation from inelastic tunneling in Gr/h-BN/Gr junctions.
- **light emission from gr/h‑bn/gr tunnel junctions (paper)** — The manuscript describing the results presented in the text.
- **light emission from inelastic electron tunnelling demonstrated** — Inelastic tunnelling electrons generate photons, as shown in early PRL work.
- **light emission irrespective of crystallographic alignment** — Observation that photon emission occurs for all twist angles, though resonant features require near alignment.
- **light emission measurement** — Experiment in which the optical output of the tunnel junction is recorded.
- **light from van der waals quantum tunneling devices** — Nature Communications 2019 report of light emission from van‑der‑Waals heterostructure quantum tunnelling devices.
- **light modulation** — Control of transmitted or reflected light using electrically tunable graphene plasmons.
- **light‑emission imaging** — Acquisition of CCD images of the electroluminescent area under bias.
- **light‑emission spectroscopy experiment** — Recording of electroluminescence spectra from the full device area under constant bias.
- **light‑emitting gr/h‑bn/gr devices project** — Research effort aimed at demonstrating tunable photon emission from tunnel junctions.
- **limitations affecting calculation accuracy** — Thin slab geometry and limited plane‑wave expansion introduce errors in work function and charge density.
- **lindhard permittivity** — Bulk metal permittivity used in the specular‑reflection model.
- **line cut analysis** — Extraction of fixed‑q cuts from the screening‑potential maps to compare tip‑on and tip‑off cases.
- **line cut at θ = 0°** — Extraction of I‑V and d²I/dV² traces along a line in the θ‑Vbb map at zero twist angle.
- **line trace** — Spatially resolved tunneling current trace.
- **linear combination of states** — Method of superposing initial and final many‑particle states to solve the Schrödinger equation.
- **linear decrease of current with bias** — Current decreases linearly with bias after the first onset until Dirac matching.
- **linear dependence of δgta on vbg** — Observed proportionality between the TA conductance‑step amplitude and back‑gate voltage.
- **linear dirac dispersion** — Idealized graphene dispersion with constant Fermi velocity.
- **linear dispersion** — Ideal Dirac band model with a constant Fermi velocity, which predicts a single nesting line.
- **linear growth of resonant peak intensity with photon frequency** — Observed proportional increase of the resonant spectral‑efficiency peak as ω increases.
- **linear increase of current with bias after dirac matching** — Current rises linearly with bias after the Dirac matching condition as the matching circle grows.
- **linear nanopositioner** — Nanopositioner mounted on the rotator to place the point‑of‑interest at the centre of rotation.
- **linear response calculations** — Linear‑response theoretical calculations that incorporate tight‑binding band structure and self‑consistent electrostatics.
- **linear response theory** — Theoretical framework relating tunneling current to response functions.
- **linear spin‑wave theory** — Theoretical framework for single‑q incommensurate magnetic structures.
- **linear term in magnetic moment vanishes without spin‑polarized leads** — Result that the contribution to the current linear in ⟨s_qn⟩ disappears when the leads are not spin‑polarized.
- **linear x‑shaped feature** — Linear feature indicating the smallest twist‑angle‑dependent bias for momentum‑conserving tunneling.
- **linearized band** — Approximation of the system’s band as linear near the intersection with the probe dispersion.
- **linearized dispersion** — Approximation of dispersion near Dirac point.
- **linearized gap function δ(p*) ≈ ħ v_δ·(p*−p_n)** — First‑order expansion of the superconducting gap near a node.
- **linear‑in‑t resistivity** — Resistivity proportional to temperature.
- **linear‑response formalism** — Theoretical framework used to evaluate transition probabilities.
- **line‑cut analysis** — Extraction of data along fixed momentum lines in the screening‑potential maps.
- **lingyun wan** — Co‑author of the Nano Letters 2024 paper on a quantum twisting scanning probe microscope.
- **lingyun wan** — Author affiliated with Hefei National Research Center for Physical Sciences at the Microscale, University of Science and Technology of China.
- **liping yu** — Peer reviewer Liping Yu
- **lisa wesoloski** — Co‑author of the tutorial article on Bardeen's tunneling theory in STM.
- **lithography mode** — Lithography mode for exposure.
- **liu et al., crystal‑symmetry‑paired spin‑valley locking in a layered room‑temperature antiferromagnet, nat. phys. 21, 760–767 (2025)** — Paper reporting crystal‑symmetry‑paired spin‑valley locking in a layered room‑temperature antiferromagnet.
- **local density approximation (lda)** — A density‑functional approximation employed in the calculations, though it fails to reproduce the correct image form of the correlation potential at large distances.
- **local density of sample states** — Local density of sample electronic states per unit volume near the tip center.
- **local density of states (ldos) measurement** — Spatially resolved LDOS mapping, used to detect changes after molecule adsorption.
- **local electric potential φ(r,t)** — Real‑space electric field generated by the screened interaction, entering the coupling Hamiltonian.
- **local interference experiments** — Experiments performed at the QTM tip that exploit coherent superposition of many tunnelling trajectories.
- **local orientation locking** — Microscopic effect that can fix the relative twist locally, leading to alignment variations.
- **local permittivity tensor** — Anisotropic dielectric response of hBN.
- **local pressure** — Mechanical force applied locally by the AFM tip to the twisted bilayer graphene.
- **local probes for charge‑neutral edge states in two‑dimensional quantum magnets** — Phys. Rev. B 102, 134423 (2020) discussing local probing of charge‑neutral edge states.
- **local quantum oscillations** — Spatially resolved quantum oscillation measurements.
- **local spectroscopy** — Spatially resolved spectroscopic measurement of electronic states.
- **local-density potential** — Approximate potential used in the STM model based on local electron density.
- **localized electric field** — Electric field concentrated in the AA region of TBG due to localized electronic states.
- **localized electronic state** — Spatially confined electronic states in TBG that generate the ESP tip.
- **localized states** — Electronic states confined to specific regions (e.g., AA stacking) of a moiré superlattice.
- **locally oxidized silicon surface‑plasmon schottky detector for telecom regime. nano lett. 2011, 11, 2219‑2224** — Describes a silicon‑based Schottky detector for telecom‑range plasmons.
- **locate nodes in the brillouin zone of a nodal superconductor** — Determine positions of gap nodes using QTM data.
- **lock‑in measurement of differential conductance (di/dv)** — Phase‑sensitive detection method used to record the first derivative of the tunnelling current with respect to bias.
- **logarithmic correction to dirac spectrum** — Interaction‑induced modification of the graphene dispersion that varies logarithmically with energy.
- **logarithmic correction to dispersion** — Interaction‑driven term that enhances the Fermi velocity near neutrality.
- **longitudinal acoustic (la) phonon mode** — In‑plane acoustic phonon.
- **longitudinal resistivity measurement** — Measurement of longitudinal resistivity ρ with current i∥c.
- **long‑distance coherent propagation** — Coherent transport of high‑velocity antiferromagnetic spin waves over long distances.
- **long‑distance coherent propagation of spin waves** — Observation of coherent propagation of high‑velocity antiferromagnetic spin waves over long distances.
- **long‑lived isospin excitations** — Isospin collective modes with unusually long lifetimes.
- **long‑lived magnons** — Spin‑split magnons that persist well above room temperature.
- **long‑lived plasmons** — Plasmons whose lifetimes approach the intrinsic τ when they reside in the low‑doping graphene gap region.
- **long‑lived plasmons in gap region** — Plasmons entering the momentum gap of the low‑doping graphene layer exhibit lifetimes approaching the intrinsic τ.
- **long‑lived spin waves** — Spin waves with extended lifetimes in metallic antiferromagnets.
- **long‑lived spin‑split high‑velocity magnons persist above room temperature** — Demonstrates suitability of CrSb for magnonic devices.
- **long‑range coulomb interaction** — Electrostatic interaction mediating the coupling between tip electrons and sample excitations.
- **lorentzian distribution** — Normalized Lorentzian used to model finite lifetime of electronic states.
- **lorentzian model for optical phonons in hbn** — Approximation of hBN dielectric response using Lorentzian oscillators for in‑plane and out‑of‑plane phonons.
- **low & avouris 2014 – graphene plasmonics for terahertz to mid‑infrared applications** — ACS Nano 2014 review on graphene plasmonics.
- **low bias voltage** — Small voltage across the tunneling junction where the Tersoff‑Hamann formula is valid.
- **low temperature** — Temperatures used in earlier hBN‑encapsulated graphene studies.
- **low-temperature measurements** — Measurements performed at low temperature
- **lower bound on coherence length** — The same analysis yields a lower bound of >150 nm on the quantum coherence length.
- **low‑bias run (vb = 40 mv)** — Specific measurement run performed at a small bias of 40 mV.
- **low‑bias tunneling conductance di/dv** — Conductance at low bias voltage expressed by Eq. (C7) using the tunneling density of states.
- **low‑energy band** — The flat band near the Dirac point in twisted bilayer graphene whose flattening is tuned by pressure.
- **low‑energy fractionalized excitations** — Fractionalized quasiparticles appearing at low energies in quantum spin liquids.
- **low‑energy mode coupling increases with decreasing twist angle** — The EPC of a low‑energy phonon in TBG grows as the twist angle is reduced.
- **low‑energy phason mode coupling phenomenon** — Increasing EPC strength of a layer‑antisymmetric phason mode as the twist angle decreases.
- **low‑pass filter time constant** — Time constant of the lock‑in low‑pass filter, set to ~60 ms for bias measurements.
- **lujun wang** — Co‑author of the Nature Materials 2023 article on exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling.
- **lukas novotny** — Co‑author of the Nano Lett. 2021 paper on resonant light emission from graphene/h‑BN/graphene tunnel junctions.
- **lundeberg et al. 2017 – tuning quantum nonlocal effects in graphene plasmonics** — Science 2017 article on quantum nonlocal response of graphene plasmons.
- **m. a. altvater** — First author of the 2022 arXiv and 2021 Nano Lett. papers on 1T‑TaS₂.
- **m. a. h. vozmediano** — Researcher co‑author of the non‑Fermi‑liquid honeycomb lattice study.
- **m. a. huber** — First author of the 2017 Nat. Nanotechnol. paper on femtosecond switching of interface polaritons.
- **m. ahmed** — Researcher author of the arXiv preprint on next‑nearest‑neighbor effects in 2D materials.

- **magic angle** — Specific twist angle (~1.1°) where flat bands emerge in twisted bilayer graphene.
