- **magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene as a topological heavy fermion problem** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 047601 (2022).
- **magic‑angle graphene multilayers** — Graphene multilayer system tuned to a magic twist angle.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene as a topological heavy fermion problem** — Reference (12) interpreting magic‑angle tBG as a topological heavy‑fermion system.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene as a topological heavy fermion problem (2022)** — Physical Review Letters article presenting a heavy‑fermion perspective on magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene as a topological heavy‑fermion problem** — The 2022 PRL proposes a heavy‑fermion description for magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene sample** — TBG tuned to angle where flat bands appear.
- **magnetic correlations** — Strong spin correlations present in the quantum spin liquid barrier.
- **magnetic correlations j** — Characteristic energy scale of spin interactions in the quantum‑spin‑liquid barrier.
- **magnetic excitations and continuum of a possibly field‑induced quantum spin liquid in α‑rucl₃** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 227202 (2017) by Z. Wang et al.
- **magnetic field induced phases** — Phases that appear under applied magnetic field.
- **magnetic fluctuation‑structure relation** — Correlation between magnetic fluctuations and structural changes.
- **magnetic impurity** — Localized magnetic moment used as a probe of host electronic states.
- **magnetic impurity as a local probe of the u(1) quantum spin liquid with spinon fermi surface** — Phys. Rev. B 105, 195156 (2022) proposing magnetic impurities to probe U(1) QSLs.
- **magnetic insulator** — Magnetic insulator used as the tunneling barrier.
- **magnetic moment of the barrier ⟨s_qn⟩** — Spin expectation value in the barrier that could contribute linearly to the tunneling current.
- **magnetic moment orientation** — Orientation of atomic magnetic moments in CrSb.
- **magnetic moment orientation in crsb** — Neutron diffraction determination of atomic magnetic moment orientation in CrSb.
- **magnetic mount for vis laser** — Mount that holds the VIS laser mirrors and allows fine angular adjustment.
- **magnetic quantum spin liquid barrier** — The insulating magnetic barrier hosting a quantum spin liquid.
- **magnetic stm probe** — STM tip with magnetic coating used to study magnetic surfaces.
- **magnetic susceptibility measurement** — Technique used to probe the magnetic response of CrSb.
- **magnetic texture** — Spatial variation of magnetization used to guide magnons.
- **magnetic texture based magnonics** — Phys. Rep. 905, 1 (2021) reviewing magnonics based on magnetic textures.
- **magnetic torque response** — Torque experienced by a material in a magnetic field.
- **magnetic torque response** — Torque response measured in magnetic field.
- **magnetism** — General magnetic ordering phenomena.
- **magnetism in dilute electron gas** — Magnetic ordering emerging in a low‑density electron system.
- **magnetism in moiré heterostructures** — Magnetic ordering phenomena that arise in moiré‑engineered two‑dimensional stacks.
- **magnon dispersion** — Energy‑momentum relation of magnons measured by INS.
- **magnon drag** — Process where moving magnons transfer momentum to charge carriers, generating spin currents.
- **magnon excitations** — Collective spin‑wave excitations in magnetically ordered two‑dimensional materials.
- **magnon gap** — Magnon gap of 13 meV measured by inelastic neutron scattering.
- **magnon interference tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopy that exploits interference of magnon‑mediated tunneling paths.
- **magnonics** — Study and manipulation of spin‑wave (magnon) excitations.
- **magnons** — Spin‑wave excitations that can couple to tunnelling.
- **magnon‑assisted tunnelling** — Tunnelling process mediated by magnon excitations.
- **mains frequency** — Allowed mains frequency (50 Hz / 60 Hz) for the devices.
- **maintenance contract** — Service offered by Salicru after the warranty period, with optional 10 % extra discount.
- **majorana fermions** — Quasiparticles that are their own antiparticle, of interest for topological quantum computing.
- **majorana zero modes** — Zero‑energy quasiparticle excitations in topological superconductors.
- **many-body interactions** — Collective interactions beyond the single‑particle picture.
- **many-body interactions in quasifreestanding graphene** — PNAS 2011 study of many‑body effects.
- **many‑body excitations** — Excitations arising from electron‑electron interactions.
- **many‑body perturbation theory for moiré systems (2025)** — ArXiv pre‑print presenting many‑body perturbation theory applied to moiré heterostructures.
- **many‑body resonances** — Resonant features arising from electron‑electron interactions.
- **many‑particle physics (2013)** — Textbook covering theoretical methods for interacting many‑body systems.
- **map angular dependence of spin structure factor** — Goal to obtain a full angular map of S(q, ω) by varying the twist angle.
- **map superconducting gap along the sample fermi line** — Obtain gap magnitude as a function of momentum around ξk=0.
- **mapping the evolution of the quantum mechanical phase in space** — Goal of the scanning interferometer mode of the QTM.
- **masaaki matsuda** — Co‑author of the publication.
- **massless dirac dispersion** — Linear spinon energy ξ = ħ v_s |k|.
- **massless dirac particles** — Low‑energy excitations in graphene described by a linear dispersion around the Dirac points.
- **mat2014-59096-p** — Spanish MINECO grant supporting the work.
- **material properties particularly suited to be measured with helium scattering** — Phys. Chem. Chem. Phys. 23, 7653–7672 (2021) discussing helium‑scattering suitability for 2D materials.
- **material‑independent electron localization in hbn** — Similar ESP localization observed in hexagonal boron nitride, indicating a generic effect.
- **matrix element** — Overlap integral ⟨ψ_f|H‑W|ψ_i⟩ that determines the tunneling probability.
- **matrix element calculation** — Evaluation of the tunneling matrix element using the current density operator.
- **matrix elements** — Quantities appearing in Bardeen's tunneling current expression.
- **matsubara formalism** — Finite‑temperature Green’s function technique employed in the derivation of the tunneling current.
- **matsubara frequencies** — Imaginary‑frequency summation technique used to evaluate finite‑temperature Green’s functions.
- **matsubara frequency** — Imaginary frequency νₗ or Ωₗ used in finite‑temperature Green’s function formalism.
- **maximized electron interactions at the magic angle** — Demonstration of strongest electron interactions at the magic twist angle.
- **maximized electron interactions at the magic angle in twisted bilayer graphene** — Nature 2019 paper reporting strongest electron interactions at the magic twist angle.
- **maximum momentum probed up to θ_max = 30°** — Experimental limit on the accessible momentum range.
- **mazzilli, levchenko & könig, phys. rev. b 108, 014425 (2023)** — Drag resistance mediated by quantum spin liquids.
- **measured fermi velocity v_f = (1.05 ± 0.02)×10⁶ m s⁻¹** — Experimental determination of the Dirac slope from the traced points.
- **measured j11 = 1.8(4) mev** — Quantitative value of the second‑nearest‑neighbor exchange extracted from the splitting.
- **measurement at v_bg = 0 v** — Run measuring I, dI/dV and d2I/dV2 versus θ and V_bb with back‑gate set to 0 V.
- **measurement at v_bg = –10 v** — Run measuring I, dI/dV and d2I/dV2 versus θ and V_bb with back‑gate set to –10 V.
- **measurement instabilities** — Irregular features in the data at high temperature or high bias, attributed to experimental noise.
- **measurement of phonon spectrum and epc in tbg** — Comprehensive QTM experiment mapping conductance steps, phonon lines and EPC as a function of Vb, Vbg and θ.
- **measurement of surface‑plasmon dispersion in oxidized aluminum films** — Phys. Rev. B 1975 paper by R. B. Pettit et al. measuring surface‑plasmon dispersion in oxidized Al.
- **measurement procedures** — Experimental protocols for acquiring tunneling spectra with QTM.
- **measurement scan** — AFM scan performed after alignment, with defined deflection setpoint and force.
- **measurement setup** — General laboratory setup to which samples and tips are transferred for measurement.
- **measurements** — General experimental measurements
- **measuring phonon dispersion and electron‑phason coupling in twisted bilayer graphene with a cryogenic quantum twisting microscope** — arXiv:2407.13404
- **measuring phonon dispersion and electron‑phason coupling in twisted bilayer graphene with a cryogenic quantum twisting microscope (birkbeck et al., 2024)** — Experimental observation of phonon dispersion and electron‑phason coupling using the QTM.
- **mechanical exfoliation** — Method to obtain thin flakes from bulk crystals.
- **mechanical manipulation of tip** — Deliberate deformation of the tip to change its contact area.
- **mechanical properties** — Elastic and strength characteristics of a material.
- **merging of fermi‑edge and dirac‑point singularities** — When the tip Fermi level is tuned to the Dirac point, the two singularities merge into a single feature in dI/dV.
- **metal** — Conducting electrode material forming the left and right contacts.
- **metal a** — Left‑hand metal electrode (region x < x_a).
- **metal b** — Right‑hand metal electrode (region x > x_b).
- **metal nanospheres** — Spherical metallic nanoparticles used in nanolens structures.
- **metal waveguide** — Metallic waveguide employed for FIR wavelengths longer than ~600 µm.
- **metallic gates** — Two metallic gates surrounding the TBG sample, separated by ~1 nm.
- **metallic radius (≈2κ)** — Typical metallic atomic radius used as an estimate for a single‑atom tip.
- **metallic regime** — Parameter region where the system behaves as a metal.
- **metallic surface** — Typical sample type imaged by STM.
- **metals** — Generic metallic electrodes used in tunnelling measurements.
- **metal‑insulator‑metal waveguide** — Waveguide geometry supporting confined gap plasmons.
- **metal‑insulator‑semiconductor diode consisting of two‑dimensional nanomaterials** — Nano Letters 2016 paper by H. Jeong et al. on MIS diodes built from 2D nanomaterials.
- **methanol** — Methanol introduced to adjust FIR cavity pressure.
- **methanol‑d (ch3od)** — Deuterated methanol used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **methyl chloride (ch3cl)** — Molecule used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **methyl iodide (ch3i)** — Molecule used as gain medium for FIR laser lines.
- **michel calame** — Co‑author of the Nature Materials 2023 article on exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling.
- **mickael l. perrin** — Co‑author of the Nature Materials 2023 article on exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling.
- **micrometer stage** — Stage that adjusts the distance between the two parabolic mirrors.
- **micrometer stage distance** — Adjustable distance between the two parabolic mirrors controlled by the micrometer stage.
- **microscopic calculations** — Theoretical modeling of electronic structure and plasmon spectra.
- **microwave absorption** — Field‑dependent microwave spectroscopy used to probe magnetic excitations.
- **mid‑infrared on‑chip graphene plasmon source** — Device that electrically generates mid‑IR graphene plasmons on chip.
- **mid‑infrared plasmonic biosensing** — Biosensing approach that exploits graphene plasmons in the mid‑IR spectral range.
- **mid‑infrared plasmonic biosensing with graphene** — Science 2015 paper reporting mid‑IR biosensing using graphene plasmons.
- **mid‑infrared plasmons** — Surface plasmons resonant in the mid‑infrared spectral range.
- **mid‑infrared range** — Spectral range where the efficiency drops to zero as photon energy approaches zero.
- **mikael b. lundeberg** — Physicist, lead author on quantum non‑local effects in graphene plasmonics.
- **millimetre and submillimetre wavelength lasers – a handbook of cw measurements** — Handbook by Nigel Douglas covering known FIR laser lines.
- **mini brillouin zone (mbz)** — Reduced Brillouin zone arising from the moiré superlattice.
- **minimal microscopic model** — Minimal model used to fit the INS data and extract exchange couplings.
- **minimal waist w₀** — Smallest beam radius at the waist position.
- **minimal waist w₀** — Minimum beam radius at the waist of a Gaussian beam.
- **minimum total path** — Minimum overall optical path length of about 2.4 m (1.4 m before + 1 m inside the cSNOM box).
- **minor deviations attributed to finite quasiparticle relaxation** — Small mismatches between reconstructed and tip Fermi lines are caused by quasiparticle lifetime broadening.
- **mirror loss** — Power loss due to reflection on metallic or ITO mirrors.
- **mirror loss factor** — Fractional power loss per mirror.
- **mirror transformation about the xz plane** — Symmetry operation relating opposite twist angles.
- **mirrored i–v curve measurement** — Voltage‑bias sweep that shows symmetric phonon peaks in the graphite‑graphite junction.
- **misaligned device shows no resonant tunnelling peak** — I‑V curve of 9 L h‑BN device lacks NDC peak, indicating loss of elastic channel.
- **misaligned gr/h‑bn/gr device (θ > 5°, 8 l h‑bn barrier)** — Device with a twist angle larger than ~5°, leading to momentum‑nonconserving tunneling.
- **missing row** — Structural defect in the second layer of Au(110) that may be invisible to STM.
- **mitigate charge transfer** — How to reduce unwanted charge transfer between graphene and 1T‑TaS₂ in a dual‑gated device.
- **mlg bands** — Electronic bands of monolayer graphene.
- **mlg tip setup** — Scanning configuration employing a doped monolayer graphene tip.
- **mlg tip setup** — Experimental configuration using a monolayer graphene tip for tunneling measurements.
- **mlg tip – tbg sample tunneling setup** — Experimental setup where a monolayer‑graphene tip tunnels into a twisted‑bilayer‑graphene sample.
- **mlg-mlg momentum-resolved tunneling experiment** — Momentum-resolved tunneling measurements between two monolayer graphene sheets.
- **mlg-mlg tunneling project** — Research effort to study momentum‑resolved tunneling in graphene bilayers.
- **mlg-mlg tunneling setup** — Experimental configuration for tunneling between two monolayer graphene sheets.
- **mlg-tbg junction** — Vertical junction between monolayer graphene and twisted bilayer graphene.
- **mlg/hbn/graphite sample** — Heterostructure used for in-situ twistronics experiments.
- **mlg/trilayer wse2/mlg tunnel junction** — Experimental device consisting of a monolayer graphene / trilayer WSe2 / monolayer graphene stack used for momentum‑resolved tunnelling.
- **mlg‑mlg experiment** — Experiment measuring tunneling between two monolayer graphene sheets separated by hBN and WSe2 barriers.
- **mlg‑mlg k‑resolved tunneling experiment** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling measurement between two monolayer graphene sheets.
- **mlg‑tbg device** — Heterostructure consisting of monolayer graphene, twisted bilayer graphene and a WSe₂ tunneling barrier.
- **mlg‑tbg k‑resolved tunneling** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling between monolayer graphene and twisted bilayer graphene.
- **mlg‑tbg momentum‑resolved tunneling experiment** — Tunneling spectroscopy between a monolayer graphene probe and twisted bilayer graphene.
- **mlg‑tbg sample** — A tunneling junction where a monolayer‑graphene probe tunnels into a twisted‑bilayer‑graphene target.
- **mlg‑tbg tunneling experiment** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy between a monolayer graphene probe and a twisted bilayer graphene target.
- **mlg‑wse₂‑tbg junction** — Experimental setup consisting of a monolayer‑graphene probe, a bilayer WSe₂ barrier and a twisted‑bilayer‑graphene sample.
- **mn++ charge distribution is expanded in the solid relative to the free atom** — Experimental observation reported in the 1961 PRL article.
- **mnf₂** — Insulating altermagnet where magnon splitting was only observed with exceptional resolution.
- **mnf₂** — Manganese difluoride, examined for altermagnetic magnon band splitting.
- **mnsb** — Manganese antimonide, studied together with CrSb.
- **modality reproducibility finding** — Continuous‑contact and Woodpecker modalities give almost identical results and are highly reproducible.
- **mode dispersion relations** — Dispersion of hBN phonons and graphene plasmons that determine the frequency of the excited plasmons.
- **model probe tip** — Spherical potential‑well model used for the STM tip in the theory.
- **modeling parameters θ and γ** — Theoretical parameters used to simulate the spectral efficiency of the tunnel junction.
- **moderate errors in corrugation amplitude have little effect on conclusions** — Even ~20 % errors in calculated or measured corrugation do not significantly alter the derived results.
- **mode‑projected electron‑phonon coupling** — Electron‑phonon coupling resolved for individual phonon modes.
- **mode‑resolved epc** — EPC measured for individual phonon modes.
- **modified pdms‑based transfer technique** — Method to transfer graphite flakes onto the platinum pyramid using a PDMS stamp.
- **moire materials** — Van‑der‑Waals heterostructures with a long‑wavelength moiré pattern arising from a twist between layers.
- **moiré bands in twisted double-layer graphene** — Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 108, 12233 (2011)
- **moiré electron‑phonon interaction** — Coupling between electrons and phonons mediated by the moiré superlattice.
- **moiré graphene sample** — Twisted graphene sample forming a moiré superlattice, used as the target in QTM measurements.
- **moiré graphene sample** — A graphene device exhibiting a moiré superlattice.
- **moiré heterostructure** — Van‑der‑Waals stack forming a moiré superlattice.
- **moiré heterostructure** — Stacked 2D layers with a twist that creates a moiré pattern.
- **moiré materials** — Van‑der‑Waals heterostructures forming moiré superlattices
- **moiré pattern** — Long‑wavelength interference pattern generated by twisting two atomic lattices.
- **moiré pattern** — Long‑wavelength interference pattern with ~13 nm periodicity observed in TBG.
- **moiré patterns** — Interference patterns arising from lattice mismatch or twist between layers.
- **moiré phonons** — Phonon modes folded by the moiré superlattice in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **moiré phonons in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (nano lett 22, 7791‑7797, 2022)** — Experimental observation of moiré phonons at the magic angle.
- **moiré phonons in twisted bilayer graphene (prb 100, 075416, 2019)** — Identification of moiré‑folded phonon modes in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **moiré plasmons** — Collective plasmonic excitations arising from the moiré superlattice in TBG.
- **moiré superlattices** — Periodic potentials arising from the moiré pattern in twisted 2D materials.
- **moiré superstructure** — Long‑wavelength pattern that emerges when two lattices are misaligned by a twist angle.
- **moiré unit cell** — Fundamental periodic cell of the graphene/hBN moiré superlattice.
- **moiré‑pattern fluctuations** — Spatial variations of the moiré superlattice in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **moiré‑pattern fluctuations and electron‑phason coupling in twisted bilayer graphene (prb 100, 155426, 2019)** — Study of how moiré‑pattern disorder couples electrons to phason modes.
- **molecular orbital imaging** — Technique for visualizing electronic orbitals at the atomic scale.
- **molecular pairing** — Pairing mechanism involving molecular‑like bound states.
- **molecular plasmonics** — Field studying plasmonic resonances of molecular systems, highlighted in 2015 Nano Lett.
- **molecular quantum electrodynamics** — Dover Publications, Mineola, New York, 1998
- **molecular sensing with tunable graphene plasmons** — ACS Photonics 2015 paper on using tunable graphene plasmons for molecular detection.
- **molecule adsorption** — Binding of molecules onto the surface of TBG with varying adsorption energies.
- **molecule filter project** — Application of QTSPM to selectively attract electron‑deficient molecules to AA sites.
- **molecule lattice project** — Application of QTSPM to create controllable molecular lattices based on moiré geometry.
- **molecule selection** — Process of choosing molecules for adsorption experiments according to the octet rule.
- **molybdenum diselenide (mose₂)** — Semiconducting transition‑metal dichalcogenide mentioned as compatible with QTM.
- **molybdenum disulfide (mos₂)** — Semiconducting transition‑metal dichalcogenide mentioned as compatible with QTM.
- **momentum k_t (top layer)** — In‑plane crystal momentum of an electronic state in the top graphene layer.
- **momentum matrix element (|p|)** — Matrix element ⟨Ψ_T|p̂_z|Ψ_B⟩ governing the strength of photon‑assisted tunneling.
- **momentum operator (p̂)** — Quantum operator p̂ = –iħ∇ describing electron momentum.
- **momentum qm** — Momentum at the moiré Brillouin‑zone corner where phonon energy is evaluated.
- **momentum resolution limited by twist‑angle control** — Momentum resolution determined by precision of twist angle and graphene size.
- **momentum resolved tunneling experiments** — Experiments using a WSe2 barrier to probe tunneling between MLG or TBG and a probe MLG, with rotation scanning the momentum axis.
- **momentum scale km** — Characteristic momentum scale of the sample.
- **momentum separation k_m** — Separation between Dirac points of the top and bottom layers in TBG.
- **momentum shift δ₁kₙ** — Characteristic momentum shift appearing in tunneling expressions.
- **momentum transfer (q)** — Reciprocal‑space variable swept during the neutron‑scattering measurements.
- **momentum transfer q** — Momentum exchanged between the graphene layers and the quantum‑spin‑liquid barrier.
- **momentum uncertainty (δq)** — Spread in momentum arising from the finite voltage bias across the tunneling junction.
- **momentum-conserving states** — Energy‑momentum matched states that enable tunneling between the Dirac and parabolic bands.
- **momentum-resolved spectral function** — Spectral function resolved in momentum space.
- **momentum‑ and energy‑resolved spectral function measurement** — Technique that directly maps the spectral function A(k,ω) of a two‑dimensional electron system.
- **momentum‑and energy‑resolved spectral function** — Measurement of the full spectral function of a 2D electronic system.
- **momentum‑conserving cone‑to‑cone tunneling** — Tunneling process where Dirac cones of the two graphene layers align in momentum space, enabling resonant emission.
- **momentum‑conserving current vanishes for θ ≳ 5°** — Large twist suppresses momentum‑conserving tunnelling because ℏΔK becomes too large.
- **momentum‑conserving electron transition** — Transition between electronic states with identical momentum and band index, responsible for the resonant emission.
- **momentum‑conserving interlayer tunneling** — Momentum‑conserving interlayer tunneling process in the QTM setup.
- **momentum‑conserving photon‑assisted tunneling (θ < ~3°)** — Tunneling process that preserves in‑plane momentum when the graphene layers are nearly aligned.
- **momentum‑conserving tunneling experiment** — Experiment probing elastic momentum‑conserving tunneling between a monolayer graphene tip and a superconducting MATBG sample.
- **momentum‑conserving tunnelling** — Tunnelling process that preserves in‑plane momentum, enabling spectroscopy of phonons.
- **momentum‑dependent layer polarization** — Variation of wave‑function weight between top and bottom layers as a function of momentum (twist angle).
- **momentum‑energy resolved tunneling** — Spectroscopic technique that resolves tunneling current as a function of momentum and energy.
- **momentum‑energy resolved tunneling with qtm (manuscript)** — The research article from which the excerpt is taken.
- **momentum‑nonconserving photon‑assisted tunneling (θ > 5°)** — Tunneling process that does not conserve in‑plane momentum for sufficiently misaligned layers.
- **momentum‑resolved features** — Distinct signatures in the tunneling spectrum that are resolved in momentum.
- **momentum‑resolved imaging of tbg energy bands** — Research effort aimed at mapping TBG minibands by scanning Vb and θ with a graphene probe.
- **momentum‑resolved inelastic tunnelling** — Experimental method that combines momentum resolution with detection of inelastic phonon‑assisted tunnelling.
- **momentum‑resolved measurement** — Spectroscopic method that accesses the dispersion E(k) by scanning the tip position and bias.
- **momentum‑resolved plasmon dispersion** — How to map the plasmon energy versus momentum using bias voltage and twist angle in QTM measurements.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling experiment** — The set of measurements described, performed with tip E in continuous‑contact and woodpecker modalities.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy** — A tunneling method that conserves in‑plane momentum between tip and sample.
- **momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy of qsls** — Proposal to use the quantum twisting microscope and IETS to distinguish different quantum spin‑liquid states.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling** — Tunnelling process where the measured signal depends on the in‑plane momentum set by θ_QTM.
- **momentum‑resolved tunnelling measurement** — Measurement performed with the QTM when a tunnel barrier is inserted between tip and sample.
- **momentum‑space arc** — Arc traced by the Dirac point of the probe in momentum space during a measurement.
- **momentum‑space splitting (0.15 r.l.u.)** — Observed splitting of magnon intensity along the (H,0,2.85) direction.
- **monochromatic beam pumping** — Pumping scheme employing a single‑frequency CO₂ beam to address a narrow absorption line.
- **monolayer 1t‑tas₂ sample** — Single‑layer flake of 1T‑TaS₂ used as the tunneling barrier.
- **monolayer 1t‑tas₂ sample** — A single‑layer flake of 1T‑TaS₂ placed between the twisted graphene electrodes.
- **monolayer graphene (mlg)** — Single layer of graphene used as the tip.
- **monolayer graphene on flat sample** — Graphene layer deposited on the flat substrate electrode.
- **monolayer graphene on qtm tip** — Graphene layer deposited on the QTM tip electrode.
- **monolayer graphene qtm tip** — Graphene flake used as the scanning tip in the QTM.
- **monolayer graphene tip (mlg)** — Graphene tip employed in the QTM, providing a steep electron dispersion.
- **monolayer limit** — Regime where the investigated material consists of a single atomic layer.
- **monolayer rgo** — Reduced graphene oxide monolayer.
- **mose₂ device on sio₂** — Fabricated vertical stack containing a MoSe₂ monolayer on a SiO₂ substrate.
- **mose₂ tunnelling device** — Device where a MoSe₂ monolayer is placed above graphene within the tunnelling stack.
- **mos₂ tunnelling device** — Device where a MoS₂ monolayer is placed above graphene within the tunnelling stack.
- **motor** — Motor component mounted in the fridge.
- **motorized stacking setup** — Equipment used to align and stack the 2D layers with controlled twist angle.
- **motorized transfer setup** — Automated stage used for stacking graphene and h‑BN flakes.
- **mott states** — Mott insulating states predicted in strongly correlated MATBG.
- **mott transition** — Interaction‑driven insulating transition observed in α‑RuCl₃ near graphite.
- **mott transition** — Interaction‑driven metal‑insulator transition.
- **mottness versus unit‑cell doubling** — Debate whether Mott physics or structural unit‑cell doubling drives the insulating state.
- **mott‑insulating gap** — Correlation‑driven energy gap of ≈400 meV observed in monolayer 1T‑TaS₂.
- **mourigal et al., nat. phys. 9, 435 (2013)** — Fractional spinon excitations in the quantum Heisenberg antiferromagnetic chain.
- **mo‑based tmds** — TMDs containing molybdenum that show higher‑energy indirect‑exciton peaks.
- **multi-degree-of-freedom control** — On-chip methods to independently tune several parameters of 2D material devices.
- **multilayer graphene systems** — Stacks of more than two graphene layers.
- **multi‑step alignment procedure** — Series of alignment steps required to bring tip and sample into the desired twist configuration.
- **muon spin rotation (μsr)** — Magnetic resonance technique using implanted muons to probe local magnetic fields.
- **n. a. spaldin** — Author listed on the Phys. Today 2010 paper.
- **n. banu** —

- **n. kumada** — First author of the 2014 PRL paper on resonant edge magnetoplasmons.

- **n. m. r. peres** — Co‑author on field‑effect tunneling transistors.

- **n. regnault** — Co‑author of the 2021 PRB on exact analytic many‑body excitations.
- **n. regnault** — Researcher and co‑author of the many‑body excitations paper.
- **n. tilak** — Co‑author of the 2022 arXiv and 2021 Nano Lett. papers.
- **n. wei** — Co‑author on Dirac‑point and plasmon spectroscopy theory.
- **n. wei** — Researcher, co‑author of Dirac‑point spectroscopy paper
- **nanolens** — Sub‑diffraction focusing element made from a self‑similar chain of metal nanospheres.

- **nanometer‑scale lateral p–n junctions in graphene/α‑rucl₃ heterostructures** — Demonstration of nanometer‑scale lateral p–n junctions in graphene/α‑RuCl₃ heterostructures.
- **nanoparticle** — Metallic nanoparticle used as a plasmonic element in the ultrasmall light source.
- **nanoplasmonic phenomena at electronic boundaries in graphene** — Localized plasmonic effects that appear at electronic boundaries of graphene, reported in 2017 ACS Photonics.
- **nanoscale chemical analysis** — Chemical identification with spatial resolution below 10 nm using TERS.
- **nanoscale chemical analysis by tip‑enhanced raman spectroscopy** — Chem. Phys. Lett. 2000 paper demonstrating nanoscale chemical analysis with TERS.
- **nanoscope software** — Software controlling the AFM and providing optical imaging and detector visualization.
- **nanosurf easyscan 2 atomic force microscope** — Commercial AFM platform used as the base of the QTM because of its open geometry.
- **nanotechnology 28, 395702 (2017)** — Reference cited in the manuscript.
- **nanowire semiconductors** — Semiconductor nanowires integrated with graphene for plasmonic detection.
- **nano‑thick crystals of 1t‑tas2** — Very thin 1T‑TaS2 crystals.
- **narrow bands** — Narrow electronic bands that may be present in the sample.
- **narrow electron bands** — Electronic bands with small bandwidth.
- **narrow features at high pressure** — Momentum‑resolved features stay narrow even at 0.68 GPa, indicating negligible pressure gradients.
- **narrow‑band superconductor** — A superconductor with a small electronic bandwidth, probed in the tunneling experiments.
- **nathaniel gabor** — Peer reviewer Nathaniel Gabor
- **national key research and development program of china** — Funding program that provided grants 2016YFA020060 and 2021YFB0300600.
- **natural divergence of gaussian beams** — Intrinsic beam spreading due to diffraction of a Gaussian mode.
- **nature 2023 – the quantum twisting microscope** — Nature article (Vol 614, 23 February 2023) reporting the development and experiments of the quantum twisting microscope.
- **nature 303, 316 (1983)** — Reference authored by L. D. Marks and D. J. Smith.
- **nature 641, 8 may 2025, p.349** — Journal article reporting the QTM results.
- **nature materials 2023 (vol 22, pp 1094–1099)** — Article reporting the exciton‑assisted tunnelling and EL results.
- **nature materials 2023 – exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling in van der waals heterostructures** — Peer‑reviewed article reporting the experimental demonstration of exciton‑assisted tunnelling.
- **nature materials paper** — The reported paper in Nature Materials
- **nature of epc in moiré systems** — What is the strength and mode dependence of EPC in twisted bilayer graphene and related moiré materials?
- **nature of the correlated insulator states** — The 2020 PRL elucidates the nature of correlated insulator states in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **nature paper (this work)** — The manuscript describing the QTM experiments, methods and results.
- **nature vol 614 23 february 2023 article** — The research article describing momentum‑resolved tunnelling imaging of twisted‑bilayer‑graphene energy bands.
- **ndc position defined by twist angle** — The voltage (or bias) at which the NDC peak appears is mainly set by the twist angle θ.
- **nearest‑neighbor exchange interactions** — First three nearest‑neighbor couplings that capture the main features of the magnon spectrum.
- **nearest‑neighbor hopping (t = 3.2 ev)** — Primary hopping parameter in the graphene tight‑binding model.
- **nearest‑neighbor hopping model** — Single‑particle tight‑binding model for graphene based on hopping between p_z orbitals, used to describe the nonlinear dispersion.
- **nearest‑neighbor tight‑binding model** — Model used to describe spinon hopping on a triangular lattice.
- **nearly aligned gr/h‑bn/gr device (θ≈0.5°, 8 l h‑bn)** — Device with a small twist angle used to demonstrate the resonant emission peak.
- **near‑field electrical detection** — Detecting plasmons by measuring electrical signals in the near field.
- **near‑field electrical detection of optical plasmons and single‑plasmon sources. nat. phys. 2009, 5, 475‑479** — Demonstrates near‑field electrical detection of optical plasmons and single‑plasmon emission.
- **near‑field microscopy** — Technique previously used to verify mid‑infrared plasmons in similar Gr/h‑BN/Gr heterostructures.
- **near‑field nanoscopy** — Experimental technique that uses sharp tips to enhance coupling between light and graphene plasmons.
- **near‑infrared region** — Spectral range where the efficiency of strongly misaligned devices gradually increases.
- **near‑infrared spectral range** — Wavelength region (≈0.8–1.5 µm) where the resonant emission peak is observed.
- **near‑infrared spectral region** — Wavelength range where the emission peak is located.
- **needle‑shaped crsb crystal** — Needle‑shaped CrSb crystal (~1 cm long) used for transport studies.
- **negative differential conductance (ndc)** — Peak in the I‑V characteristic where the differential conductance becomes negative, observed at low bias.
- **negative differential conductance (ndc) peak** — Peak in the I‑V curve where differential conductance becomes negative.
- **negative thermal expansion** — Unusual contraction upon heating observed in CrSb.
- **negative thermal expansion in crsb** — Observation of uniaxial negative thermal expansion in antiferromagnetic CrSb.
- **neglect electron‑electron interaction** — Assumption B1 in Bardeen's theory.
- **nematic d‑wave superconducting state** — A nematic superconducting order with d‑wave symmetry observed in TBG.
- **nematic nodal gap function** — Gap function that breaks C3 symmetry and hosts nodes.
- **nematic topological semimetal and insulator at charge neutrality** — The 2021 PRR reports a nematic topological semimetal and insulating phase at charge neutrality in magic‑angle bilayer graphene.
- **nematic topological semimetal and insulator in magic‑angle bilayer graphene at charge neutrality (2021)** — Physical Review Research article on nematic semimetal and insulating phases.
- **nemin wei** — Author Nemin Wei
- **nesting i** — First nesting feature observed in d²I/dV² maps at high bias.
- **nesting i** — Nesting condition where tip and sample dispersions cross at each other's Fermi level.
- **nesting ii** — Second nesting feature observed in d²I/dV² maps at high bias.
- **nesting ii** — Nesting condition where tip and sample dispersions cross between the Dirac points with equal local velocities.
- **nesting line** — Line in Vb‑θ space that determines the energy shift φ between bands.
- **nesting line matches experiment** — The nesting line extracted from peak fitting closely follows the dependence observed in the data.
- **nesting line slope gives c_g/c_q ratio** — Relation between the slope of the nesting line and the ratio of geometric to quantum capacitance.
- **nesting lines** — Features in momentum‑energy space where tunneling probability is enhanced due to band nesting.
- **nesting phenomenon** — Alignment of Fermi surfaces leading to enhanced tunneling along curved lines.
- **nesting‑split investigation project** — Research effort combining QTM experiments and theoretical modeling to determine the origin of the nesting split.
- **neutral collective modes** — Collective excitations without net charge, such as plasmons, magnons and spinons.
- **neutral fermi gas** — Reference system used for the spinon Fermi‑surface calculation.
- **neutron diffraction** — Technique used to determine magnetic moment orientation.
- **neutron diffraction measurement** — Measurement type used to determine magnetic ordering and magnon excitations.
- **neutron scattering** — Probe of lattice dynamics using neutrons.
- **neutron scattering intensity** — Measured intensity of neutrons as a function of momentum and energy transfer.
- **new mechanism for superconductivity** — Reference (3) proposing a novel superconductivity mechanism.
- **next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping** — Spinon hopping beyond nearest neighbours.
- **next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping** — Electronic hopping beyond nearest‑neighbor sites in a lattice.
- **next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping (t′ = 0.3 ev)** — Secondary hopping term that adds electron‑hole asymmetry to the band structure.
- **nh₃ molecule** — Ammonia used in adsorption‑energy measurements.
- **nh₃ molecule** — Electron‑sufficient molecule that prefers AB/BA sites on TBG.
- **ni++ charge distribution is more compact in the solid than in the free atom** — Experimental observation reported in the 1961 PRL article.
- **ni++ in knif3 powder** — Powder sample measured by Scatturin, Corliss, Elliott and Hastings.
- **ni++ ion in octahedral array of point charges** — Theoretical model system used in Hartree‑Fock calculations of Ni++ charge distribution.
- **niclas heinsdorf** — Co‑author of the publication.
- **nigel douglas** — Author of the millimetre‑submillimetre lasers handbook.
- **nikon te300 inverted microscope** — Microscope used for optical measurements under ambient conditions.
- **nini pryds** — Author listed on the Ann. Phys. 2022 paper.
- **nio** — Nickel oxide, the single crystal used for form‑factor measurements.
- **niobium diselenide (nbse₂)** — Layered superconductor mentioned as compatible with QTM.
- **nitrogen‑filled tube** — 2.5 m tube filled with nitrogen used to test beam power before and after the tube.
- **nkt photonics superk extreme laser** — Broadband laser used as part of the calibration of the detection line.
- **nnn hopping insufficient to explain split** — Including NNN hopping introduces slight asymmetry but does not reproduce the observed nesting split.
- **nn‑only model matches low‑bias data** — Simulations with only NN hopping reproduce a linear onset and a single nesting line within the experimental bias range.
- **no difference in polarization dependent current due to symmetries** — Observation that symmetry does not change the polarization dependent current.
- **no g band splitting observed** — Raman spectra showed no splitting despite opposite electrode doping.
- **no new inelastic tunneling features from interlayer coupling** — Interlayer electron‑plasmon coupling does not introduce additional inelastic tunneling features at low bias.
- **nodal band‑off‑diagonal superconductivity in twisted graphene superlattices** — 2023 Nature Communications paper by M. Christos, S. Sachdev and M. S. Scheurer.
- **nodal d‑wave superconducting state** — A nodal d‑wave superconducting order observed in TBG.
- **nodal superconductivity** — Superconducting order parameter that possesses nodes (zeroes) on the Fermi surface.
- **nodal superconductor** — Superconducting sample with nodes in its gap function.
- **nodeless superconducting gap function** — Isotropic superconducting gap |Δ_k| = Δ.
- **nodes at intersections of tip fermi circles** — Observation that a superconducting node coincides with the intersection of two tip Fermi circles at different twist angles.
- **noise magnetometry with spin qubits** — Using spin‑qubit sensors to measure magnetic noise and infer magnetic phases.
- **noise worsens after one week of measurement** — Empirical observation that the noise level increases after ~1 week of continuous operation.
- **nonadiabatic effects** — Breakdown of the Born‑Oppenheimer approximation in electron‑phonon interactions.
- **nonadiabaticity** — Breakdown of the Born‑Oppenheimer approximation for electrons coupled to fast phonons.
- **nonequilibrium green functions** — Alternative formalism for Bardeen's tunneling theory.
- **nonequilibrium green functions (negf)** — A theoretical method that can handle electron‑electron and electron‑phonon interactions in tunneling problems.
- **nonlinear excitation of surface plasmon polaritons by four‑wave mixing** — Palomba, S. & Novotny, L., Phys. Rev. Lett. 2008, reporting SPP excitation via four‑wave mixing.
- **nonlinear g band shift for |δef| < ħωg/2** — Deviation from linear dependence of G band energy on Fermi level shift at low doping.
- **nonlinear plasmonics with gold nanoparticle antennas** — Palomba, S. et al., J. Opt. A 2009, on nonlinear response of gold nanoparticle antennas.
- **nonlinearity of onset** — Deviation of the onset line from a straight Dirac prediction.
- **nonlocal effects in graphene plasmonics** — Observed deviation from local electromagnetic response in graphene plasmons.
- **nonlocal moments in the chern bands of twisted bilayer graphene** — arXiv:2408.16761.
- **nonlocality** — Wave‑vector‑dependent electronic response that limits the hybridization‑induced shift.
- **nonlocality limits optical plasmon shift** — Nonlocal electronic response caps the upward shift of the optical plasmon.
- **non‑destructive technique** — Method that does not alter or damage the sample.
- **non‑interacting green’s function** — Diagonal Green’s function in the Bloch basis for a non‑interacting system.
- **non‑linear responses** — Exotic transport phenomena expected in altermagnetic metals.
- **non‑local conductivity** — Spatially dispersive electronic response of TMD monolayers.
- **non‑local surface conductivity** — Momentum‑dependent optical conductivity of the TMD used to visualise excitonic resonances.
- **non‑reciprocal spin splitting of magnon bands (nrss)** — Non‑reciprocal spin splitting of magnon bands observed in CrSb.
- **non‑relativistic spin splitting above and below the fermi level in ag‑wave altermagnet** — arXiv:2411.18761 (2024) reporting spin splitting in an ag‑wave altermagnet.
- **nordheim‑fowler field emission regime** — High‑bias field emission process described by Nordheim‑Fowler theory.
- **nordheim‑fowler term** — Additional term accounting for breakdown of the WSe₂ tunnel barrier.
- **normal metallic tip** — A metallic electrode that serves as the tunneling tip in the junction.
- **normal modes** — Phonon vibrations of hBN crystal lattice.
- **normal modes in hexagonal boron nitride** — Phys. Rev. 1966 paper by R. Geick et al. reporting phonon normal modes of hBN.
- **normal state** — Non-superconducting phase of MA TBG.
- **normal state** — Metallic phase without superconducting order.
- **normal state twisted bilayer graphene** — TBG in its normal (non‑superconducting) state.
- **normalization constant q** — Normalization factor Q = (2π/L)^{1/2} used for wavefunctions.
- **normalized conductance step δg/(β ν_t ν_b a_tip)** — Conductance step divided by experimental prefactors for comparison across devices.
- **normalized inelastic tunneling rate |m|²** — Dimensionless factor governing the strength of inelastic tunneling processes.
- **normal‑metal tip – superconducting sample junction** — The experimental configuration where a normal‑metal tip tunnels into a superconducting sample.
- **numerical aperture (0.9)** — Light‑collection capability of the microscope objective.
- **numerical calculations** — Performing numerical simulations
- **numerical simulation of di/dv** — Computational modeling of differential conductance spectra including plasmon effects.
- **numerical simulations** — Computational modeling used to calculate tunneling currents and peak positions.
- **nv centers** — Nitrogen‑vacancy color centers in diamond used as nanoscale magnetic sensors.
- **nw10 type flange** — Inlet fitting on the FIRL100 head for gas connection.
- **nw25 to 12 mm nozzle adapter** — Adapter required for the exhaust line of the 215 gas handling system.
- **néel temperature** — Temperature (733 K) below which CrSb orders as an Ising altermagnet.
- **o. a. ashour** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 arXiv preprint on non‑relativistic spin splitting.
- **o. gomonay** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 npj Spintronics on altermagnetic textures.
- **o. j. bardeen** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **o. p. sushkov** — Co‑author of the collective‑excitations superconductivity paper.
- **observation of flat and remote bands in tbg via momentum‑resolved tunnelling** — The experiment reproduces the theoretically predicted flat‑band and remote‑band features.
- **observation of flat bands** — Experimental detection of dispersionless bands in TBG
- **observation of logarithmic correction to dirac spectrum** — Measured logarithmic deviation from linear graphene dispersion with α≈0.32±0.01 at room temperature.
- **observed nonlinearity matches velocity renormalization by electron‑electron interactions** — Experimental observation linking band nonlinearity to many‑body velocity renormalization.
- **observed quantum textures in magic‑angle graphene** — Spatial textures of many‑body wavefunctions reported (Nuckolls et al., 2023).
- **observed several unanticipated features in tunneling spectrum** — Discovery of onset, nesting I, nesting II, and hourglass features.
- **observed slope ≈ 6 in van hove measurement** — Measured slope of the Van Hove feature is ~6, larger than the expected value of 1.
- **observed variation of twist angle ≈ 3°** — Even with fragmentation, the fabricated devices show a spread of about 3° in θ.
- **occupation change** — In the thin‑barrier limit, the bias mainly changes the occupation of the bands.
- **occupation probability** — Probability that a given electronic state is occupied.
- **ocean optics hl2000 calibrated light source** — Reference source employed to measure the transmission function of the optical path.
- **octet rule** — Chemical rule used to select molecules based on electron deficiency or sufficiency.
- **offset 100 nm** — Maximum allowed distance between rotation centre and scan centre after alignment.
- **offset energy φ** — Energy offset between the Dirac points of the two graphene layers at θ → 0°.
- **offset minimization** — Reducing the distance between rotation centre and scan centre to <100 nm.
- **off‑axis parabolic mirror alignment technique** — Specific alignment method for off‑axis parabolic mirrors.
- **off‑resonant excitation** — Excitation that bypasses the two‑step scheme, directly transferring population with an intense electric field.
- **ohmic behavior of series resistance** — Linear relation between voltage drop across RS and current.
- **oil‑immersion objective (nikon 100× na 1.4)** — Collects light emitted from the tunnel junctions.
- **on the electrochemical etching of tips for scanning tunneling microscopy** — J. Vac. Sci. Technol. A 1990 paper describing electrochemical etching of STM tips.
- **one-shot gw calculations** — Computational method for quasiparticle energies performed in a single GW step.
- **only a single conductance peak observed at 21.8° (and 38.2°)** — Experimental result showing a conductance peak only at the first commensurate angle and its symmetric partner.
- **onset condition** — Condition marking the start of measurable tunneling current.
- **onset feature at larger bias** — Shift of the tunneling onset to higher bias voltage when the constant‑vF model fails.
- **onset line** — Condition where the Fermi surface of one layer touches the empty band of the other layer, leading to a tunneling resonance.
- **onset relation vb = ± ħ v_f k θ** — Derived linear relation between bias voltage and twist angle for the charge‑neutral case.
- **on‑chip single plasmon detection. nano lett. 2009, 10, 661‑664** — Reports detection of single plasmons on a chip.
- **on‑site coulomb interaction u** — Bare on‑site Coulomb interaction estimated ≈10 meV for the screened sample.
- **open‑loop rotation** — Rotation measurement performed without feedback, moving a fixed number of ticks per step.
- **oppenheimer** — Developed the perturbation theory of field ionization of hydrogen, used as an analogy for Bardeen's theory.
- **oppenheimer** — Contributor to early perturbation‑theory treatments of tunneling.
- **oppenheimers perturbation theory** — Perturbative description of field ionization of hydrogen, used as an analogy for tunneling.
- **optical absorption in twisted bilayer graphene (phys. rev. b 2013)** — Phys. Rev. B 87, 205404 (2013) measuring optical absorption in TBG.
- **optical absorption spectroscopy** — Measurement of light absorption to probe electronic structure.
- **optical alignment yields the smallest misorientation** — Devices aligned optically displayed the lowest relative twist angles among the dataset.
- **optical and plasmonic properties of twisted bilayer graphene: impact of interlayer tunneling asymmetry and ground‑state charge inhomogeneity (2020)** — Investigation of optical and plasmonic response of TBG with emphasis on tunneling asymmetry and charge inhomogeneity.
- **optical antennas** — Nanostructures used to boost the optical density of states in a narrow frequency range.
- **optical axis** — The central line of propagation that must be preserved during alignment.
- **optical density of states ρ(ω)** — Density of photonic states in a homogeneous dielectric environment, proportional to ω².
- **optical epc coupling g_optical** — Effective electron‑phonon coupling strength for optical phonons (≈300–350 meV).
- **optical excitations in electron microscopy** — Review of how optical excitations are accessed and interpreted in electron microscopy.
- **optical excitations in electron microscopy** — Interaction of fast electrons with optical modes, as reviewed in Ref. 38.
- **optical microscope** — Instrument used to locate and identify suitable exfoliated flakes.
- **optical microscope imaging** — Imaging system used to record light emitted from the device under bias.
- **optical nanoimaging** — Near‑field optical microscopy for gate‑tunable plasmon imaging.
- **optical nano‑imaging of gate‑tunable graphene plasmons** — Nature 2012 paper by Chen, J. et al. on optical nano‑imaging of gate‑tunable graphene plasmons.
- **optical nano‑imaging of gate‑tunable graphene plasmons** — Showed optical near‑field imaging of plasmons whose frequency is tuned by gating.
- **optical phonon branches (lo, to, zo)** — Higher‑energy phonon modes identified in the graphite dispersion.
- **optical plasmon** — Higher‑energy plasmon branch with larger Drude weight, mainly residing in the highly doped layer.
- **optical plasmon detection** — On‑chip detection of surface plasmons using atomically thin semiconductors.
- **optical plasmon higher drude weight in layer 1** — Optical plasmon has larger Drude weight and is weighted toward the highly doped layer.
- **optical plasmon shift** — Upward energy shift of the optical plasmon due to hybridization, limited by nonlocality.
- **optical plasmons** — Higher‑energy plasmons that dominate the tunnelling current in an optimal voltage range.
- **optical probe** — Using light to interrogate electronic noise or plasmonic activity.
- **optical probe of quantum shot-noise reduction at a single-atom contact. phys. rev. lett. 2010, 105, 026601** — Study of quantum shot‑noise reduction using an optical probe of a single‑atom contact.
- **optical pumping** — Method of using external laser radiation to excite a molecular medium.
- **optical response** — Interaction of light with a material.
- **optical sensing** — Use of graphene plasmons to detect chemical or biological analytes.
- **optical spectroscopy** — Measurement of exciton energies and photon emission spectra.
- **optical theorem** — Method used to compute the imaginary part of the self‑energy to second order in electron‑boson coupling.
- **optical to mode** — Transverse optical phonon near the K point.
- **optical‑microscopy identification** — Visual inspection of exfoliated flakes under an optical microscope.
- **optical‑phonon epc dominated by in‑layer coupling** — Observation that the electron‑optical‑phonon coupling strength is mainly due to in‑layer mechanisms.
- **optics‑free integrated plasmonic devices** — Research effort aiming at on‑chip plasmon generation and detection without external optics.
- **optimal pyramid height 1.5–2.0 µm** — Empirical result that this height range yields stable electrical contact and mechanical stability.
- **optimal thz collimation** — What is the optimal collimation configuration for a 2.5 THz beam?
- **optimum voltage yields unity generation yield** — There exists a bias range (≈ 1 eV) where the number of plasmons generated per tunneled electron is of order one.
- **option 1: 2 ups, standard but noisy** — Design option using two UPS units with a standard but noisy configuration.
- **option 2: 2 ups, stronger design** — Design option using two UPS units with a stronger, more robust configuration.
- **option 3: 3‑p ups** — Design option using a three‑phase UPS (needs redrawing).
- **optoelectronic devices** — Devices that combine optical and electronic functionalities.
- **orange lines (theoretical curves)** — Curves that include both in‑layer and interlayer electron‑plasmon interactions.
- **orbital attraction** — Attractive interaction from molecular orbitals that can overcome electrostatic repulsion.
- **orbital magnetic field effects** — Changes in electronic density of states due to orbital coupling to magnetic field.
- **orbital time‑reversal** — Time‑reversal operation acting on orbital (valley) degrees of freedom.
- **order parameter symmetry** — Symmetry of the superconducting order parameter.
- **order parameters** — Quantities characterizing phases.
- **ordered moment 3.2 µb per cr** — Refined magnetic moment exceeds earlier unpolarized diffraction values.
- **order‑parameter symmetry of two‑dimensional superconductors** — What is the symmetry of the superconducting order parameter in 2D superconductors?
- **organic diode** — Organic semiconductor diode employed as a plasmon‑emitting device.
- **organic diodes** — Organic semiconductor devices employed for near‑field electrical plasmon detection.

- **organic plasmon-emitting diode. nat. photonics 2008, 2, 684‑687** — Demonstrates a diode that emits surface plasmons.
- **origin of magic angles in twisted bilayer graphene** — Reference (11) explaining the origin of magic angles.

- **origin of the nesting split** — What physical mechanism produces the experimentally observed splitting of the nesting line?

- **orthogonality of momentum and polarization** — Condition causing vanishing coupling for the LA mode.
- **oscillating quartz force sensor (afm mode)** — Quartz cantilever used to oscillate the STM tip for dynamic STM operation.
- **oscillator module** — Provides a 1.04 µm high‑power beam; kept running between measurements.
- **out‑of‑plane acoustic (za) phonon mode** — Flexural phonon of graphene.
- **out‑of‑plane conduction** — Electronic transport perpendicular to the layers of a van‑der‑Waals crystal.
- **out‑of‑plane optical (zo) phonon mode** — Optical phonon polarized perpendicular to the plane.
- **out‑of‑plane transport** — Vertical electrical conduction through van der Waals stacks.

- **overall efficiency ≈ 0.60 × 10⁻⁶ photons per electron** — Measured total photon‑per‑electron conversion efficiency of nearly aligned devices at room temperature.
- **overlap function gₛₛ′(k,q,n,l)** — Function accounting for overlap of eigenstate wavefunctions in the structure factor.
- **overlap sites angle (13.2°)** — Specific angle where overlap sites occur, potentially obscured by thermal smearing.
- **o₂ and h₂o concentration** — Residual gas levels (<10 ppm) inside the glovebox during assembly.
- **p(r, ef)** — Local density of states at the Fermi energy used in STM image calculations.

- **pair density waves** — Spatial modulation of the superconducting order parameter.
- **pairing** — Formation of Cooper pairs in a superconductor.
- **pairing glue** — Bosonic excitations (e.g., phonons) mediating electron pairing.
- **pairing in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene: role of phonon and plasmon umklapp** — Phys. Rev. B 103, 235401 (2021)
- **pairing in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene: role of phonon and plasmon umklapp (prb 103, 235401, 2021)** — Study of pairing mechanisms involving phonons and plasmon umklapp processes.
- **pairing symmetry of twisted bilayer graphene: a phenomenological synthesis** — 2022 paper by E. Lake, A. S. Patri and T. Senthil on pairing symmetry in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **paper on momentum‑resolved qtm measurements** — Manuscript reporting the momentum‑resolved tunneling experiments described above.
- **parabola intersection** — Parabolic shape of the intersection between probe and system energy surfaces when v_F^p = v_F^s.
- **parabolic band** — Quadratically dispersing electronic band present in the heterostructure.
- **parabolic dispersion** — Assumed quadratic energy‑momentum relation for electrons in the WSe₂ barrier.
- **parabolic mirror** — Parabolic reflector in front of the bolometer that causes VIS divergence.
- **parabolic mirror (first)** — First mirror of the coupled parabolic telescope that refocuses the THz beam.
- **parabolic mirror (second)** — Second mirror of the coupled parabolic telescope that refocuses the THz beam.
- **parallel alignment** — Condition where the cantilever surface is parallel to the sample surface.
- **parallel wave vector k∥** — In‑plane wave vector component k∥ entering the tunneling current expression.
- **parallel wave‑vector (k∥)** — Component of electron momentum parallel to the layers.
- **paral·lel** — Capacitat de connectar fins a tres unitats en paral·lel.
- **park, h.; et al. observation of fractionally quantized anomalous hall effect. nature 2023, 622, 74-79.** — Experimental observation of a fractionally quantized anomalous Hall effect.
- **partial local density of optical states (ρ_p(ω))** — Density of optical modes in the tunneling gap that determines photon‑assisted tunneling rates.
- **particle-hole interaction vertex function** — Vertex describing particle-hole interactions.
- **particle‑hole excitation** — Creation of an electron‑hole pair during inelastic tunnelling.
- **particle‑hole spectral function a_tb(q,ω,n)** — Spectral function of the graphene layers that appears in the tunneling‑current convolution.
- **particle‑hole symmetric peaks** — Two symmetric peaks in dI/dV at biases ±Δ associated with Bogoliubov quasiparticle dispersion.
- **paste layer** — Solder paste applied to PCB pads before reflow.
- **paste layers** — PCB layers containing solder paste, potential source of flux.
- **path inside csnom box** — Optical path length of 1 m inside the cSNOM chamber.
- **patrick a. lee** — Co‑author of the paper "Probing quantum spin liquids with a quantum twisting microscope".
- **patrick irvin** — Co‑author of the QTM construction paper.
- **patrick ledwith** — Researcher acknowledged for insightful discussions.
- **pauli sigma matrices (σ0, σx, σy, σz)** — Matrices representing sublattice degrees of freedom.
- **pc residues** — Polycarbonate contamination left on hBN/graphene after transfer processes.
- **pcb sample** — Sample holder PCB that connects to PCB_Wings_Sample through spring connectors.
- **pcb tip mini** — Tip holder PCB that connects to PCB_Wings_Tip through spring connectors.
- **pcb tip miniaml** — Tip holder PCB that connects to PCB_Wings_Tip through spring connectors.
- **pcb wings sample flipped** — PCB that connects to PCB Sample via spring connectors and hosts 10 pico-coax lines.
- **pcb wings tip flipped** — PCB that connects to PCB Sample via spring connectors and hosts 20 pico-coax lines from two pico-end PCBs.
- **pcb_sample** — Sample holder that connects to PCB_Wings_Sample through spring connectors.
- **pcb_tip_miniaml** — Tip holder that connects to PCB_Wings_Tip through spring connectors.
- **pcb_wings_tip** — Connects to PCB Sample through spring connectors; 20 pico‑coax lines from two different pico‑end PCBs are soldered onto it.
- **pdms transfer reference [9]** — Paper describing the PDMS‑based transfer method used for QTM tip fabrication.
- **peak energy ~1.57 ev** — Energy of the EL peak assigned to the A exciton of MoSe₂.
- **peak fitting** — Fitting of peaks in the d²I/dV² spectrum to extract the nesting line.
- **peak frequency controlled by bias** — The peak frequency of excited plasmons shifts linearly with the applied bias voltage, matching the threshold and dispersion relations.
- **peak in d²i/dv²** — Strong peak in the second derivative of the current that signals the Dirac points coinciding.
- **peak in d²i/dv² at dirac matching** — Peak observed in the second derivative corresponding to the Dirac matching condition.
- **peak position shift** — Shift of the NDC peak position with twist angle, measured as ~0.4 V per degree.
- **peak position shift finding** — The NDC peak position shifts by ~0.4 V per degree of twist angle.
- **peak position shifts to higher bias at lower temperature** — Bias voltage of the main resonance moves from ~2.05 V at 290 K to ~2.07 V at 10 K.
- **peaks follow onset lines** — Second‑derivative peaks in the data align with the theoretically predicted onset lines.
- **performance** — Overall electrical and optical behaviour of the tunnel devices.
- **peri** — Author of the PRB 2024 paper on quantum spin liquids.
- **peri et al., phys. rev. b 109, 035127 (2024)** — Probing quantum spin liquids with a quantum twisting microscope.
- **periscope** — Optical element that redirects the beam path in the THz/ VIS system.
- **persistent spin currents** — Spin currents that remain without decay over long distances.
- **persistent spin currents in superconducting altermagnets** — arXiv:2507.22139 (2025) reporting persistent spin currents in superconducting altermagnets.
- **phaedon avouris** — Physicist, co‑author of a graphene plasmonics review.
- **phase space of energy‑ and momentum‑conserving tunneling transitions** — Available states enabling tunneling at certain bias.
- **phase transitions** — Changes between distinct states of matter or electronic order.
- **phase‑space non‑analytic bias dependence** — The bias‑induced change in available tunneling states that leads to singular conductance features.
- **phason coupling is as important as optical phonons** — Experimental evidence that phason modes contribute strongly to EPC in TBG.
- **phason epc coupling g_phason** — Effective electron‑phonon coupling strength for the layer‑antisymmetric phason mode.
- **phason mode** — Layer‑antisymmetric low‑energy collective excitation of the moiré lattice, analogous to an acoustic mode.
- **phason mode** — Antisymmetric acoustic vibration of the two graphene layers that stretches interlayer bonds.
- **phenomenological expression γ = γ₀ + γ₁|ε‑μ|** — Empirical formula describing the energy dependence of the inverse electron lifetime.
- **phonon decay into electron‑hole pairs** — Decay channel causing phonon line broadening.
- **phonon dispersion and electron‑phason coupling in twisted bilayer graphene** — Open question addressed by the Ilani 2024 arXiv paper.
- **phonon dispersion in graphite** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 92, 075501 (2004) measuring phonon dispersion in graphite.
- **phonon dispersion measured with qtm** — Demonstrated a new technique for mapping phonon bands in vdW materials.
- **phonon dispersion of graphite by inelastic x‑ray scattering** — Phys. Rev. B 76, 035439 (2007) using inelastic x‑ray scattering to map graphite phonons.
- **phonon emission processes** — Inelastic processes involving momentum‑conserving phonon emission.
- **phonon frequency ωp** — Characteristic phonon frequency involved in tunnelling.
- **phonon line broadening** — Increase of Raman G band linewidth.
- **phonon momentum (q_ph)** — Momentum provided by the emitted phonon, q_ph = 2K_D sin(θ/2).
- **phonon momentum (q_ph)** — Momentum transferred by the emitted phonon.
- **phonon spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic technique to measure phonon modes using the quantum twisting microscope.
- **phonon spectrum and epc measurement of tbg** — Cryogenic QTM experiment that records inelastic momentum‑conserving tunnelling spectra of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **phonon vibration** — Lattice vibrations with characteristic timescales.
- **phonons** — Lattice vibrational excitations probed in twisted bilayer graphene via quantum twisting microscopy.
- **phonon‑assisted luminescence** — Radiative recombination where a phonon supplies the missing momentum.
- **phonon‑assisted resonances** — Resonant features near zero bias attributed to phonon emission/absorption during tunnelling.
- **phonon‑assisted resonances observed near zero bias** — Low‑bias features in dI/dV attributed to phonon emission/absorption.
- **phonon‑assisted resonant tunneling** — Resonant electron tunneling enhanced by phonon emission or absorption.
- **phonon‑assisted resonant tunneling of electrons in graphene–boron nitride transistors** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 186603 (2016) reporting phonon‑assisted resonant tunneling in graphene‑BN transistors.
- **phonon‑induced conductance** — Increase of electrical conductance due to phonon‑assisted tunneling.
- **phonon‑induced giant linear‑in‑t resistivity in magic angle twisted bilayer graphene (prb 99, 165112, 2019)** — Theory linking phonons to the observed linear‑in‑temperature resistivity in magic‑angle TBG.
- **phonon‑induced giant linear‑in‑t resistivity in magic angle twisted bilayer graphene: ordinary strangeness and exotic superconductivity** — Phys. Rev. B 99, 165112 (2019)
- **phonon‑limited resistivity** — Resistivity dominated by electron‑phonon scattering.
- **phonon‑limited resistivity of multilayer graphene systems** — Phys. Rev. B 107, 045426 (2023)
- **phonon‑mediated interlayer conductance in twisted graphene bilayers** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 109, 236604 (2012) describing phonon‑mediated interlayer conductance.
- **phonon‑mediated superconductivity** — Superconductivity arising from electron‑phonon coupling in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **photodetection** — Conversion of light into electrical signals, investigated with active optical antennas.
- **photodetection with active optical antennas** — Science 2011 paper by M. W. Knight et al. on photodetection using active optical antennas.
- **photodetectors** — Devices that convert light into electrical signals, often based on graphene.
- **photodetectors based on graphene, other two-dimensional materials and hybrid systems** — Nature Nanotechnology 2014 article describing graphene‑based photodetectors and hybrid platforms.
- **photoluminescence** — Light emission following optical excitation, observed in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **photoluminescence drops at low twist angle due to exciton** — PL intensity reduction at small angles interpreted as excitonic binding energy changes.
- **photoluminescence emission** — Light emission from recombination of electron‑hole pairs in monolayer TMDs.
- **photoluminescence spectroscopy** — Optical spectroscopy used to identify exciton energies in the TMD monolayers.
- **photon count** — Number of photons detected by the EMCCD, normalized by integration time and pixel area.
- **photon count rate** — Detected photon emission rate from the device.
- **photon emission** — Radiative recombination accompanying inelastic tunnelling.
- **photon emission from gr/h‑bn/gr tunnel junction** — Light emitted when electrons tunnel through the graphene–h‑BN–graphene stack.
- **photon emission from mim tunnel junctions** — Photon emission governed by both optical and electronic density of states in metal/insulator/metal junctions.
- **photon emission per tunnelling electron** — Physical process quantified by η_tot, linking electron tunnelling to photon generation.
- **photon emission spectroscopy** — Measurement of the emitted photon spectrum as a function of bias voltage and twist angle.
- **photon energy 2.1 ev** — Energy of the photons used to probe the TMD conductivity in the schematic.
- **photon energy ℏω** — Energy of the emitted photons.
- **photon frequency ω** — Frequency of emitted photon during inelastic tunnelling.
- **photonics laboratory, eth zurich** — Research group at ETH Zurich where most authors are based.
- **photon‑assisted electron tunneling** — Mechanism by which electrons tunnel while emitting photons.
- **photon‑assisted inelastic electron tunneling** — Inelastic tunnelling process that emits a photon of energy ℏω.
- **photon‑assisted inelastic tunneling jph_inel** — Photon‑assisted tunneling without momentum conservation.
- **photon‑assisted transition** — Transition that emits a photon with arbitrary energy up to eV_B, giving rise to the emission plateau.
- **photon‑assisted tunneling** — Tunneling of electrons assisted by absorption or emission of photons.
- **photon‑assisted tunneling current density (j_ph)** — Current density contributed by tunneling events accompanied by photon emission.
- **photon‑assisted tunneling spectroscopy** — Experimental method used to probe the frequency‑dependent tunneling current and spectral efficiency.
- **photon‑emission spectrum** — Spectral distribution of photons emitted during tunnelling.
- **photovoltage near‑field measurement** — Measurement performed after aligning THz to the cryoSNOM tip, without collecting the scattered beam.
- **photovoltaic measurements** — PV measurements performed at the cSNOM tip after alignment.
- **photo‑thermal tumor ablation in mice using near infrared‑absorbing nanoparticles** — O’Neal, D. P. et al., Cancer Lett. 2004, demonstrating photothermal therapy with NIR‑absorbing nanoparticles.

- **phys. rev. 8 25, 2222 (1982)** — Reference authored by R. B. Laughlin.
- **phys. rev. b 109, 035127 (2024)** — Paper describing theoretical IETS signatures of quantum spin liquids.
- **phys. rev. lett. 130, 066701 (2023)** — Reference to a 2023 Physical Review Letters article.
- **phys. rev. lett. 46, 1227 (1981)** — Reference authored by D. R. Hamann.
- **phys. rev. lett. 50, 1145 (1983)** — Reference authored by I. K. Robinson.
