- **phys. rev. lett. 50, 2002 (1983)** — Reference authored by N. Garcia, C. Ocal and F. Flores.
- **phys. rev. lett. 52, 61 (1984)** — Reference authored by P. J. Feibelman and D. R. Hamann.
- **phys. rev. lett. 6, 57 (1961)** — Reference authored by O. J. Bardeen.
- **phys. today 2010, 63, 38** — Perspective article authored by N. A. Spaldin, S.-W. Cheong and R. Ramesh published in Physics Today in 2010.
- **physical review b 110, 205407 (2024)** — Jiewen Xiao et al.
- **physical review b 111, 085128 (2025)** — Paper by Wei, von Oppen, and Glazman presenting the theoretical analysis.
- **physical review b 111, 085128 (2025)** — Paper reporting analysis of interference suppression in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **pichler et al., 2024, probing magnetism in moiré heterostructures with quantum twisting microscopes** — Phys. Rev. B 110 paper on magnetism probing using QTM.
- **pichler et al., phys. rev. b 110, 045116 (2024)** — Probing magnetism in moiré heterostrures with quantum twisting microscopes.
- **pick‑up technique with pdms/pc stamp** — Standard method to assemble van‑der‑Waals heterostructures.
- **pico end pcb** — PCB where lines from sample/PCB are soldered and provides BM24-10DP/2-0.35V53 connectors and 10 pico-coax lines.
- **pico_end** — Lines from sample/PCB are soldered to this PCB, which has BM24-10DP/2-0.35V53 on the other side (connecting to Inter).
- **pico_end pcb** — PCB where lines from sample/PCB are soldered and which has BM24-10DP/2-0.35V53 connectors and 10 pico‑coax lines.
- **pico‑coax line** — Coaxial cable used to connect samples to PCBs; length specified as X cm.
- **piezoelectric driver** — Actuator that controls tip‑sample distance in STM and topografiner.
- **pillar dimensions** — Width ~100 µm and height ~80 µm of Si/SiO2 pillar.
- **pillar height ≈80 µm** — Height of the Si/SiO₂ pillar.
- **pillar width ≈100 µm** — Width of the Si/SiO₂ pillar supporting the flat substrate device.
- **pl5-qs unit** — PL5‑QS unit supplied by Edinburgh Instruments.
- **planar tunneling junction** — A device formed by two graphene layers separated by a candidate quantum spin‑liquid material, used for tunneling spectroscopy with twist‑angle control.
- **planckian dissipation** — Universal bound on scattering rate proportional to temperature.
- **planckian resistivity** — Resistivity that follows the Planckian bound.
- **plane‑wave basis expansion** — Use of plane‑wave functions to represent wavefunctions in the LAPW calculation.
- **plane‑wave tunneling through a square barrier calculation** — Exact solution used to confirm the exponential dependence of tunneling on barrier width.
- **plasmaron bands** — Hybrid excitations of plasmons and electrons.
- **plasmon** — Collective oscillation of charge carriers in graphene.
- **plasmon** — Collective oscillation of charge carriers in a material.
- **plasmon bands** — Two collective excitation branches (optical and acoustic) arising from coupled graphene layers.
- **plasmon boost of tunneling across insulator** — Theoretical prediction that graphene plasmons enhance electron tunneling through an insulating hBN layer.
- **plasmon confinement** — Spatial compression of plasmonic fields to nanometre scales.
- **plasmon decay** — Loss of plasmon energy through radiative and non‑radiative channels.
- **plasmon decay into particle‑hole pairs** — Damping mechanism whereby a plasmon loses energy by creating particle‑hole excitations, especially at low wave vector.
- **plasmon detection via decay into tunneled electrons** — Reverse process where a plasmon decays and the energy is transferred to a tunneled electron.
- **plasmon emission** — Generation of a collective charge oscillation (plasmon) during tunneling.
- **plasmon energy** — Energy of collective charge oscillations in the flat bands of TBG.
- **plasmon energy ω** — Energy of the plasmon peaks, around 40 meV.
- **plasmon energy ≈ 40 mev** — Characteristic energy of the plasmons observed in the spectra.
- **plasmon enhancement of optical fields** — Amplification of local electromagnetic intensity near graphene plasmons.
- **plasmon excitation** — Creation of collective charge oscillations in graphene by inelastic tunnelling electrons.
- **plasmon excitation** — Generation of plasmons during electron tunneling.
- **plasmon excitation by electron tunneling** — General phenomenon of plasmons being excited via electron tunneling, reported in 1969.
- **plasmon excitations** — Collective charge oscillations (plasmons) that can couple to electronic excitations.
- **plasmon generation by tunneling** — Creation of graphene plasmons mediated by inelastic electron tunneling across the spacer.
- **plasmon generation rate (10¹²–10¹⁴ s⁻¹)** — Predicted number of plasmons generated per second for the double‑layer structure.
- **plasmon generation through electron tunneling in graphene** — Peer‑reviewed article presenting theoretical predictions of electrically driven graphene plasmons.
- **plasmon generation through electron tunneling in graphene** — ACS Photonics 2017 theoretical/experimental work on plasmon generation via electron tunnelling in graphene.
- **plasmon hybridization** — Repulsion between optical and acoustic plasmon branches observed in the dispersion diagram.
- **plasmon lifetime boost** — Increase of plasmon lifetime observed when the plasmon momentum lies in the gap region of both graphene layers.
- **plasmon lifetime measurement** — Extracted plasmon decay time from the calculated spectra.
- **plasmon lifetime τ** — Intrinsic plasmon lifetime used in calculations, 66 fs.
- **plasmon modes** — Collective charge excitations in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **plasmon satellite intensity decreases near γ point** — Observation that the intensity of plasmon satellites drops sharply when approaching the γ point.
- **plasmon satellites appear at ±10 mev** — Spectral function shows almost dispersionless plasmon satellites centered at energies ±10 meV.
- **plasmon satellites are much weaker than quasiparticle peaks at low bias** — Experimental observation that plasmon satellite intensity is ~10⁻² of quasiparticle peaks.
- **plasmon satellites in tunneling spectra** — Inelastic tunneling produces satellite peaks above elastic tunneling peaks.
- **plasmon spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic measurement of collective charge oscillations using the quantum twisting microscope.
- **plasmon spectroscopy in matbg project** — Investigation of plasmon features in MATBG using QTM and GW calculations under strong gate screening.
- **plasmon wave vector qθ** — Momentum transferred to the emitted plasmon, Qθ = Kθ – Kθ_TBG.
- **plasmon wavelength λ** — Wavelength of the propagating graphene plasmon mode.
- **plasmon weight fraction in layer 2** — Fraction of the squared plasmon‑induced charge density residing in graphene layer 2.
- **plasmon width (τ⁻¹)** — Spectral width of plasmons related to electron lifetime.
- **plasmon yield** — Average number of plasmons generated per tunneled electron; of order unity.
- **plasmon-driven extinction** — Extinction cross‑section of graphene plasmons driven by resonant excitation (σ_ext).
- **plasmonic heating** — Conversion of plasmon energy into localized thermal energy.
- **plasmonic resonances of graphene** — Collective charge oscillations in graphene that are strong in the THz range.
- **plasmonic sensing** — Detection of environmental changes via shifts in plasmon resonances.
- **plasmonic solar cells** — Catchpole, K. R. & Polman, A., Opt. Express 2008, on plasmonic concepts for solar cells.
- **plasmons** — Collective charge oscillations in graphene.
- **plasmons** — Collective charge density oscillations that can couple to tunnelling.
- **plasmons exhibit spectral weight transfer and renormalized dispersion** — The 2014 PRL shows that plasmons in strongly correlated systems display spectral weight transfer and a renormalized dispersion.
- **plasmons in strongly correlated systems: spectral weight transfer and renormalized dispersion (2014)** — Physical Review Letters paper on plasmons in strongly correlated electron systems.
- **plasmons in tunnel‑coupled graphene layers: backward waves with quantum cascade gain. phys. rev. b 2016, 94, 115301** — Predicts backward‑propagating plasmonic waves with quantum‑cascade gain in tunnel‑coupled graphene.
- **plasmon‑assisted electron tunneling** — Inelastic tunneling process where an electron emits or absorbs a plasmon, modifying the tunneling conductance.
- **plasmon‑assisted inelastic tunneling increases di/dv by ~4×10⁻³ g_incoh** — Quantitative estimate of conductance enhancement due to plasmon‑assisted processes.
- **plasmon‑assisted resonant tunneling** — Resonant electron tunneling enhanced by coupling to plasmons.
- **plasmon‑assisted resonant tunneling in graphene‑based heterostructures. arxiv:1706.05216v1, 2017** — Theoretical work on plasmon‑assisted resonant tunneling in graphene heterostructures.
- **plasmon‑assisted tunneling** — Inelastic tunneling enabled by plasmon generation that bridges momentum mismatch.
- **plasmon‑dominated current** — Regime at higher bias where plasmon emission carries most of the inelastic current.
- **plasmon‑to‑light wavelength ratio** — Ratio λp/λ = 2α(EF/ħω) ≪ 1 indicating strong confinement.
- **plateau size 50 nm–1 µm** — Range of tip‑apex plateau sizes achievable by varying layer thickness.
- **plate‑like crsb crystal** — Plate‑like CrSb crystal suitable for diffraction experiments.
- **platform movable with micrometers** — Translation platform equipped with micrometer screws for precise positioning.
- **pmma 642.04** — PMMA 642.04 resist (200 K) used in spin coating.
- **pmma 672.01** — PMMA 672.01 resist (950 K, thinner) used in spin coating.
- **polarity/doping dependent image** — STM image may be qualitatively different for opposite tunneling polarity or different sample doping.
- **polarization dependent current** — Current that varies with the polarization of the THz excitation, examined under different symmetries.
- **polarized neutron cross‑section measurement** — Determination of spin‑flip and non‑spin‑flip scattering for multiple Bragg reflections.
- **polarized neutron diffraction measurement on crsb** — The neutron experiment that mapped the magnon spectrum and revealed chiral splitting.
- **polarized neutron scattering** — Neutron scattering technique that resolves spin‑flip (SF) and non‑spin‑flip (NSF) channels.
- **polarized neutron scattering** — Technique employed to reveal altermagnetism.
- **polaron spins** — Spin degrees of freedom associated with polarons.
- **polycarbonate (pc) film** — Polycarbonate film used during the transfer step; melts at ≈165 °C.
- **polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons** — Molecular material supporting tunable molecular plasmons.
- **polymethyl methacrylate (pmma)** — Polymer coating used during stack flipping.
- **pommier et al., phys. rev. lett. 123, 027402 (2019)** — STM‑induced excitonic luminescence of a two‑dimensional semiconductor.
- **ponomarenko et al. 2010 – density of states and zero landau level probed through capacitance of graphene** — Physical Review Letters 2010 work on Landau levels using capacitance measurements.
- **ports usb-hid/rs-232/rj-45/hdmi** — Ports de comunicació per a dongle Wi‑Fi i interfícies diverses.
- **position and momentum mapping of vibrations in graphene nanostructures** — Nature 573, 247–250 (2019) demonstrating spatial‑momentum resolved phonon imaging in graphene nanostructures.
- **position‑momentum mapping of vibrations** — Simultaneous spatial and momentum resolution of phonon modes.
- **potential energy u(x)** — Spatially dependent potential defining the barrier.
- **potential gradient** — Spatial variation of the effective potential between tip and surface.
- **potential wells** — Energy minima in AB/BA areas that attract electron‑sufficient molecules.
- **potential‑well model** — Simple model describing confinement of electron wavefunctions perpendicular to the graphene plane.
- **potential‑well model (as technique)** — Use of a simple potential well to model transverse confinement of electron wavefunctions.
- **powder measurement** — Form‑factor measurement performed on a powdered sample of KNiF3.
- **power detector** — Instrument used to measure optical power.
- **power reduction** — Decrease of THz power along the optical path.
- **power restored after methanol re‑introduction** — Power recovered after re‑introducing a small amount of methanol and repumping the FIR cavity.
- **powermeter** — Device mounted on the micrometer platform to measure beam power and infer beam size.
- **predicted generation rates 10¹²–10¹⁴ s⁻¹** — Theoretical estimate of plasmon generation rates for realistic bias and spacing.
- **prediction of high‑bias d²i/dv² peak tracing plasmon dispersion** — Calculated that the onset of inelastic tunneling into dispersive bands produces a bias‑dependent peak whose position follows the plasmon dispersion.
- **prediction of nearly dispersionless plasmon satellite peaks in di/dv at low bias** — Calculated that flat‑band inelastic tunneling yields bias‑independent satellite peaks.
- **present study** — Research effort that performed the neutron‑scattering measurements and refined the spin Hamiltonian.
- **pressure (mbar)** — Gas pressure expressed in millibar.
- **pressure (mbar)** — Pressure measured in millibar.
- **pressure gradient** — Spatial variation of applied pressure across the QTM tunnel junction.
- **pressure induced compression of flatbands in twisted bilayer graphene** — Electron. Struct. 1, 015001 (2018).
- **pressure range** — Input and output pressure limits for the gas regulator.

- **pressure series measurement** — Nine consecutive dI/dV measurements while sweeping twist angle, bias voltage and pressure.
- **pressure sweep experiment** — Series of measurements of d²I/dV² versus bias and twist angle performed at several pressures (0.01, 0.4, 0.68 GPa).
- **pressure tuning** — Using hydrostatic pressure to modify band structure of moiré superlattices.
- **pressure‑induced compression of flatbands** — Measured narrowing of flat‑band width with increasing pressure.

- **princeton instruments acton spectrapro 300i spectrometer** — Spectrometer for spectral analysis of emitted light.
- **probe dirac point** — Dirac point of the probe’s linear band.
- **probe fermi velocity (v_f^p)** — Fermi velocity of the probe material.

- **probe tip** — The scanning probe tip used in STM, assumed locally spherical with radius of curvature A.
- **probing correlated states with plasmons (2023)** — Shows how plasmons can be used to detect electronic correlations.

- **promotion of superconductivity in magic‑angle graphene multilayers** — Science 2022 paper reporting enhanced superconductivity in magic‑angle graphene multilayers.

- **propagating antiferromagnetic magnons** — Traveling spin‑wave excitations in antiferromagnets.
- **propagating graphene plasmons** — Collective charge oscillations that propagate along graphene, imaged in the 2016 Nat. Mater. work.
- **propagating plasmons** — Surface plasmon polaritons that travel along a graphene sheet in a quantum tunnelling transistor.
- **propagating plasmons observed in charge‑neutral quantum tunnelling transistor** — Charge‑neutral transistor supports long‑range plasmon propagation.
- **propagation distance d (~2 m)** — Typical distance the THz beam must travel from the telescope to the cSNOM.
- **propagation distance ~2 m** — Target distance the THz beam must travel after the telescope.
- **proportional gain 3.0** — Proportional feedback gain setting for the AFM controller.
- **proportionality coefficient 38.7 cm⁻¹/ev** — Coefficient linking G band energy shift to Fermi level shift.
- **proportionality coefficient 38.7 cm⁻¹/ev** — Measured coefficient linking G band shift to Fermi level shift.
- **proposed qtspm over twisted bilayer graphene** — Research effort to develop a QTM using TBG as the functional tip.
- **proximate magnetism** — Magnetic ordering induced in a non‑magnetic layer by proximity to a magnetic material.
- **proximitizing altermagnets** — Inducing superconducting correlations in altermagnets via proximity effect.
- **proximitizing altermagnets with conventional superconductors** — arXiv:2509.03774 (2025) describing proximity coupling of altermagnets to conventional superconductors.
- **ps4p power supply** — Power‑supply unit (112 kg) for the FIRL100 laser.
- **pseudomagnetic fields** — Effective magnetic fields arising from strain.
- **pseudo‑heterodyne scheme** — Detection scheme in which a phase‑modulated reference arm interferes with the THz signal.
- **pt pyramid height 1.2–1.6 µm** — Height of the platinum pyramid grown by FIB.
- **ptax instrument** — Direct‑geometry chopper spectrometer at the SNS used for the measurements.
- **publication [23]** — Reference discussing many tip states.
- **publication [24]** — Reference on validity condition of Fermi's Golden Rule.
- **puchert et al., nat. nanotechnol. 12, 637–641 (2017)** — Spectral focusing of broadband silver electroluminescence in nanoscopic FRET‑LEDs.
- **pulse width** — Duration of each laser pulse (e.g., 100 µs, 300 ns).
- **pulsed mode** — Operating mode delivering pulses of infrared radiation.
- **purcell‑like enhancement** — Enhancement of electron‑phonon coupling analogous to the Purcell effect.
- **purcell‑like enhancement of electron–phonon interactions in long‑period superlattices: linear‑temperature resistivity and cooling power** — Nano Lett. 21, 7465 (2021)
- **pyramid base 2 × 2 µm** — Lateral size of the platinum pyramid.
- **pyramidal afm tip coated with mlg** — Atomic‑force‑microscopy tip used as tunneling electrode, coated with multilayer graphene.
- **pyroelectric detector** — Detector type capable of measuring powers below 1 mW.
- **python rotation script** — Python script that rotates the sample by a specified angle and records feature trajectories.
- **pz electrons** — Out‑of‑plane π‑orbitals of graphene that generate the ESP tip.
- **pz‑orbital tight‑binding model** — Model employed to simulate large moiré systems and ESP of TBG.
- **pₙ tight‑binding model** — A tight‑binding theoretical model used to calculate electronic structure of twisted graphene.
- **q. faure et al., altermagnetism revealed by polarized neutrons in mnf2, arxiv:2509.07087 (2025)** — Paper showing altermagnetism in MnF2 revealed by polarized neutron scattering.
- **q. gao** — Co‑author of the 2022 PRB on symmetry origin of lattice vibration modes in twisted multilayer graphene.
- **q. li** — 
- **q. qin** — Co‑author of the 2024 chiral finite‑momentum superconductivity paper.
- **q. song** — Lead author of the 2025 p‑wave magnet paper.
- **qiaochu wan** — Provided access to the transfer stage.
- **qsl barrier** — Tunneling barrier made of a candidate quantum spin liquid material (e.g., 1T‑TaS₂).
- **qsl study with quantum twisting microscope** — Research project aiming to measure QSL excitations using QTM and IETS.
- **qsl‑dominated region in ev‑θ diagram** — Region of bias‑twist space where IETS signal originates solely from QSL contributions.
- **qtm as a promising method for 2d superconductivity** — Momentum‑conserving tunneling spectroscopy with the QTM is identified as a powerful approach to explore superconductivity in 2D van der Waals systems.
- **qtm can probe electronic structure over extended area of brillouin zone** — Beyond Dirac point line, full momentum map achievable.
- **qtm enables investigation of various quantum materials** — QTM opens avenues for studying van der Waals heterostructures, oxide interfaces, and chiral systems.
- **qtm enhances sensitivity to band modifications** — Interferometric tunneling makes QTM uniquely capable of detecting faint electronic‑band changes.
- **qtm experimental setup** — Full experimental rig for QTM, including tip fabrication and measurement electronics.
- **qtm experiments with other layered materials** — Proposed extension of QTM to a broader range of 2D crystals such as TMDs, superconductors, and magnets.
- **qtm flat side substrate** — Flat side substrate used in QTM experiments.
- **qtm functionality validation** — Project to establish the Quantum Twisting Microscope's ability to measure twist‑angle effects.
- **qtm measurement run** — Run of QTM measurements executing the angle+bias experiment.
- **qtm notebook** — Notebook used for QTM data analysis.
- **qtm observes interaction‑driven band structures at room temperature** — Demonstrates that subtle many‑body effects are visible without cryogenic cooling.
- **qtm probes spectral functions of correlated ground states** — Demonstrated capability to access spectral functions and excitations in twisted and untwisted 2D systems.
- **qtm resolves twist‑angle‑dependent transport features** — Demonstrated ability of the QTM to detect transport signatures that vary with twist angle.
- **qtm set‑up** — Scanning configuration that creates the twisted graphene interface and allows in‑situ tip shape modification.
- **qtm tip** — Tip of the Quantum Twisting Microscope used to hold the system of interest during scanning.
- **qtm tip geometry** — Flat side and sloped side of the QTM tip that forms a tent‑shaped vdW stack over a pyramid.
- **qtm tunnel junction** — Tunnel junction between MLG tip and TBG sample within the QTM.
- **qtm tunnel junction** — Quantum Twisting Microscope tunnel junction used for differential conductance measurements.
- **qtm tunneling spectroscopy** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling spectroscopy performed with the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **qtm tunneling spectroscopy can determine nodal nature and locate nodes** — Result that the spectroscopy method can identify nodal superconductivity and map node positions.
- **qtm validates twist‑angle dependence in graphite bilayer** — Reproduced the expected twist‑angle dependence between two graphite layers.
- **qtspm adsorption study on twisted bilayer graphene** — Project using QTSPM to map adsorption energies on TBG at various twist angles.
- **quadratic hamiltonian** — Effective Hamiltonian for spinons after decoupling the quartic term.
- **qualitative differences in tunneling to gaas(110) versus metal surfaces** — Calculated GaAs(110) STM signals differ markedly from those of metallic Au(110).
- **quantitative agreement with au(110) measurements assuming 9 å tip radius** — Theory reproduces experimental corrugation amplitudes for Au(110) when R≈9 Å.
- **quantitative theory of plasmon-assisted tunneling in qtm** — Research project developing theory for plasmon-assisted tunneling.
- **quantized anomalous hall effect** — Hall conductance quantized without external magnetic field.
- **quantized anomalous hall effect (qahe)** — Hall conductance quantized without external magnetic field.
- **quantized landau level spectrum and its density dependence in graphene** — Phys. Rev. B 2011 measurement of Landau‑level spectrum.
- **quantum anomalous hall effect** — Quantized Hall conductance without external magnetic field, reported in MATBG.
- **quantum capacitance comparable to geometric capacitance** — In the QTM junction the quantum capacitance is of the same order as the geometric capacitance due to low carrier density of graphene.
- **quantum capacitance measurements** — Electrical technique to extract density‑of‑states information via capacitance.
- **quantum coherence** — Phase‑preserving superposition of electron wavefunctions, demonstrated at room temperature in the QTM tip.
- **quantum coherence of the qtm junction** — The experiment demonstrates quantum coherence in the QTM junction at room temperature.
- **quantum criticalities** — Critical points separating distinct quantum phases, tunable in moiré systems.
- **quantum electron transport setup** — Experimental configuration that enables detection of individual tunnelling electrons and plasmons.
- **quantum geometry induced nonlinear transport in altermagnets** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 133, 106701 (2024) linking quantum geometry to nonlinear transport.
- **quantum interference** — Coherent electron wave effects at twist boundaries.
- **quantum interference at the twist boundary in graphene** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 101, 056803 (2008).
- **quantum lifetime spectroscopy** — Spectroscopy that extracts the quantum lifetime of carriers from tunnelling measurements.
- **quantum lifetime spectroscopy and magnetotunneling in double bilayer graphene heterostructures (prasad et al., 2021)** — Study of quantum lifetime and magnetotunneling in double bilayer graphene.
- **quantum liquids** — Quantum many‑body liquid states.
- **quantum mechanics: an introduction** — Springer-Verlag, Berlin, 2001
- **quantum spin liquid** — Highly entangled magnetic state without long‑range order, hosting fractionalized excitations.
- **quantum spin liquid** — A state of matter with highly entangled spins and no magnetic order.
- **quantum spin liquid (qsl)** — Highly entangled magnetic state lacking long‑range order.
- **quantum spin liquid behaviour** — Absence of magnetic order down to low temperatures, featuring fractionalized excitations.
- **quantum spin liquid behaviour in single‑layer 1t‑tase₂** — Observation reported in the STM study of 1T‑TaSe₂.
- **quantum spin liquid states** — Rev. Mod. Phys. 89, 025003 (2017) by Y. Zhou, K. Kanoda, T.-K. Ng.
- **quantum spin liquids** — Science 367, eaay0668 (2020) by C. Broholm, R. J. Cava, S. A. Kivelson, D. G. Nocera, M. R. Norman, T. Senthil.
- **quantum spin liquids: a review** — Rep. Prog. Phys. 80, 016502 (2017) by L. Savary, L. Balents.
- **quantum textures of the many-body wavefunctions in magic-angle graphene** — Nature 620, 525 (2023) by Nuckolls et al.
- **quantum textures of the many‑body wave‑functions in magic‑angle graphene (nuckolls et al., 2023)** — Imaging of quantum textures in magic‑angle graphene.
- **quantum tunneling** — Electron tunneling between tip and sample states at the Fermi level.
- **quantum tunnelling transistor** — Device that exploits quantum tunnelling between graphene layers to control current.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm) device** — Instrument that brings a vdW‑stacked tip into tunneling contact with a twisted bilayer sample.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm) experiment** — Experimental configuration that probes momentum‑resolved tunneling in twisted graphene heterostructures.
- **quantum twisting microscope (qtm) technique** — Method that combines in‑situ twist control with tunneling spectroscopy to map band structures.
- **quantum twisting microscope development** — Research effort to build and apply the QTM for angle‑resolved tunnelling spectroscopy.
- **quantum twisting microscope project** — Research effort developing and applying the QTM to study momentum‑resolved tunneling in vdW heterostructures.
- **quantum twisting microscope project** — Research effort developing and applying the quantum twisting microscope.
- **quantum twisting microscope theory** — Theoretical framework for phonon spectroscopy using a twisted‑layer probe.
- **quantum twisting microscopy** — Experimental technique that combines scanning probe microscopy with twist‑angle control to study 2D materials.
- **quantum twisting microscopy of phonons in twisted bilayer graphene** — Research effort to develop cryogenic QTM and apply it to map phonon dispersions and EPC in TBG.
- **quantum twisting scanning probe microscope (qtspm)** — Proposed instrument that exploits the tip‑shaped ESP of TBG for non‑invasive SPM.
- **quantum twisting scanning probe microscope (qtspm)** — Scanning probe setup used for adsorption‑energy measurements on TBG.
- **quantum‑well wave function (φ⊥(z))** — Out‑of‑plane component of the graphene electron wave function approximated by a real quantum‑well state.
- **quarter‑metal** — Material with conduction in only a quarter of the spin/valley space.
- **quartic term** — Interaction term of order four in spinon operators.
- **quasi-fermi energy µ** — Quasi-Fermi energy µ, the energy around which tunneling is evaluated.
- **quasifreestanding graphene** — Graphene weakly coupled to a substrate.
- **quasiparticle broadening** — Finite lifetime effects that smear the quasiparticle spectral features.
- **quasiparticle broadening suppresses singularities** — Increasing the sample broadening γ_S strongly reduces both Dirac‑point and Fermi‑edge singularities.
- **quasiparticle relaxation** — Finite lifetime of quasiparticles that broadens spectral singularities.
- **quasiparticle spectral function** — Spectral function measured by ARPES that reveals electron-electron interaction effects.
- **quasiparticle spectral function in doped graphene: electron-electron interaction effects in arpes** — Phys. Rev. B 77, 081412(R) (2008).
- **quasistatic approximation** — Approximation employed to evaluate Fresnel reflection coefficients in the electrostatic limit.
- **quasistatic limit** — Regime where the plasmon wavelength is much smaller than the free‑space wavelength.
- **quasi‑flat plasmonic bands in twisted bilayer graphene (2016)** — Predicts the existence of nearly flat plasmonic bands in TBG.
- **quasi‑one‑dimensional electronic states** — Electronic states that are confined along a single spatial direction.
- **quasi‑particle energy** — Energy of Bogoliubov excitations in a superconductor.
- **quasi‑particle spectrum** — Energy spectrum of excitations in a superconductor.
- **q‑switch** — Q‑switching device mounted inside the FIRL100 laser head for pulse generation.
- **q‑switched mode** — High‑peak‑power pulsed operation of the FIRL100.

- **random‑phase approximation (rpa)** — Approximation employed to model graphene conductivity with non‑local effects.
- **ratio μ_t/(2πγ_s) for singularity intensities** — The ratio of maximal dI/dV from Fermi-edge singularity to Dirac-point jump equals μ_T/(2πγ_S) when μ_T,S independent of V.
- **rayleigh distance z_r** — Characteristic propagation length where diffraction becomes significant.
- **rayleigh distance z_r** — Distance over which diffraction becomes significant for the Gaussian beam.

- **real‑space tunneling hamiltonian** — The microscopic model describing electron hopping between the two graphene layers across the barrier.

- **reciprocal space** — Momentum space used for calculations of the screened interaction.

- **reduced planck constant (ħ)** — Fundamental constant appearing in dispersion relations.
- **reduced planck constant ħ** — Fundamental constant appearing in the density‑of‑states expression.

- **reference 21** — Notes on notation where ^W excludes first order Coulomb interaction and differs from GW approximation.

- **reference 22** — Notes that Gincoh is the scale for dI/dV features from elastic, momentum‑conserving tunneling.
- **reference 37** — Citation providing previous resolution and direct tunneling analysis.
- **reference 4** — Proposed experiment on spinons in quantum spin liquids.
- **reference 49** — Source that details the derivation of Eq. (2) and the simplifications used for extracting EPC.

- **reference [14]** — Reference cited for tunneling studies.
- **reference [37]** — Original proposal describing the quantum twisting microscope concept.
- **references [16–18]** — Publications describing transfer Hamiltonian formalism.

- **reference 48** — Cited work on electron‑energy‑loss and cathodoluminescence mapping of plasmons.

- **reference [3]** — Procedure originally used to fabricate the initial devices.
- **reference [4]** — Study reporting thermally induced graphene rotation on h‑BN.
- **refractive index n_bn** — Optical refractive index of h‑BN used in the expression for ρ(ω).
- **reittu 1995 – fermi’s golden rule and bardeen’s tunnelling theory** — American Journal of Physics 1995 pedagogical article linking Fermi’s rule to tunnelling theory.
- **relative permittivity (εr)** — Effective dielectric constant of the environment.
- **relaxation rate (η)** — Finite relaxation rate of Bogoliubov quasiparticles in the sample.
- **relaxation time** — Timescale for energy relaxation in the system.
- **release of photoactivatable drugs from plasmonic nanoparticles for targeted cancer therapy** — Luo, Y. L. et al., ACS Nano 2011, on drug release from plasmonic nanoparticles.
- **remote conduction bands c₁, c₂** — Two lowest conduction bands of TBG that are degenerate at the γ point.
- **removing chopper** — Removing the chopper to restore normal beam shape.
- **renaissance motor** — Motor used for capacitive readout, providing six lines to SMP_PCB.
- **renishaw raman spectrometer** — Raman spectrometer equipped with green and IR lasers, used for vibrational spectroscopy.
- **renishawwire (pypi)** — Python library for handling Renishaw WIRE file format.
- **renormalization factor z(k, ω)** — A slowly varying factor that enters the Green’s function and gap function.
- **renormalization of the graphene dispersion velocity determined from scanning tunneling spectroscopy** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 2012 study using scanning tunneling spectroscopy.
- **renormalization‑group approach** — Method to study scale‑dependent behavior of interacting systems.
- **reproducibility** — Ability to reproduce the same output parameters across multiple runs.
- **rescaling rotation angle to potential** — Procedure that converts the twist angle θ to an electrostatic potential φ using φ = ħ v_F K_D θ.
- **research center for functional materials, nims** — Institute in Tsukuba, Japan where h‑BN crystals were grown.
- **residual resistivity ratio (rrr)** — RRR = 6 for CrSb, indicating metallic quality.
- **resistivity of rotated graphite–graphene contacts** — Nano Letters 16, 4477–4482 (2016) measuring resistivity across rotated graphite‑graphene interfaces.
- **resolution and sensitivity of stm** — Performance metrics of the microscope determined by tip radius and p(r,EF).
- **resolving intervalley gaps and many‑body resonances in moiré superconductor** — arXiv 2025 preprint.
- **resonance** — Pronounced spectral feature appearing for small twist angles.
- **resonance becomes sharper and stronger at lower temperature** — Observed narrowing and increase of the tunnelling resonance as temperature is reduced.
- **resonance peak at ~2.05 v indicating exciton‑assisted tunnelling** — Observed sudden increase of current matching the WS₂ exciton energy.
- **resonance peak in photon emission spectrum** — Near‑infrared peak centered at ℏω_R = Φ observed for nearly aligned devices.
- **resonant behavior evolves with twist angle** — Systematic change of resonant features observed across devices with θ ≈ 0.5°, 1.1°, 2.9°, and >5°.
- **resonant edge magnetoplasmons and their decay in graphene** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 2014 paper by Kumada, N. et al. on resonant edge magnetoplasmons.
- **resonant emission spectrum for small twist angles** — Observation of a pronounced resonance when the twist angle is small.
- **resonant light emission** — Narrow spectral photon emission observed for near‑aligned graphene layers, tunable in the near‑infrared.
- **resonant light emission from graphene/hexagonal boron nitride/graphene tunnel junctions (nano lett. 2021)** — 2021 Nano Letters article reporting twist‑controlled resonant light emission from Gr/h‑BN/Gr tunnel junctions.
- **resonant light emission from graphene/h‑bn/graphene tunnel junctions** — The paper (and its supporting information) describing resonant light emission in graphene/h‑BN/graphene tunnel devices.
- **resonant peak** — Sharp emission feature that appears for near‑aligned graphene layers.
- **resonant peak splitting** — Division of the tunnelling spectral peak into two sub‑peaks as the twist angle increases.
- **resonant peak vanishes for θ ≳ 3°** — The resonant emission peak broadens and disappears when the twist angle exceeds about 3 degrees.
- **resonant peak vanishing** — Suppression of the resonant spectral peak at large twist angles (≈ 3°).
- **resonant photon energy ħω_r** — Photon energy equal to the barrier work function Φ.
- **resonant spectral peak** — Spectral emission peak that can contain up to ~40 % of the total emitted power for θ≈0°, broadening and disappearing as θ increases.
- **resonant tunneling oscillations** — Oscillations used experimentally to estimate tip‑sample distance.
- **resonant tunnelling and negative differential conductance in graphene transistors** — Nature Communications 2013 paper by L. Britnell et al. reporting resonant tunnelling and NDC in graphene transistors.
- **resonant tunnelling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic method to study energy‑resolved tunnelling in graphene/hBN heterostructures.
- **retarded green’s function** — Green’s function used to compute the spectral function of the superconductor.
- **retarded green’s function in nambu space** — Matrix Green’s function describing electron and hole propagation in a superconductor.
- **revealing electron‑electron interactions in graphene at room temperature with a quantum twisting microscope** — Research effort investigating electron‑electron interactions in graphene using a quantum twisting microscope.
- **review article: tunneling‑based graphene electronics: methods and examples** — J. Vac. Sci. Technol. B 2017 review by V. L. Katkov & V. A. Osipov on tunnelling methods in graphene electronics.
- **review of scanning probe microscopies** — Article that evaluates the reliability of the Tersoff‑Hamann model for various imaging conditions.
- **re‑aligning parabola** — Adjusting the telescope parabola to correct instability.
- **re‑alignment** — Procedure using a helium‑neon laser to realign optical components.
- **rhombohedral graphene** — A stacking variant of graphene studied as the host material for superconductivity.
- **rhombohedral graphene sample** — Graphene with rhombohedral stacking used to study displacement‑field‑dependent superconductivity.
- **rhombohedral multilayer graphene (rmlg)** — Graphene with more than three ABC‑stacked layers.
- **rhombohedral trilayer graphene** — ABC‑stacked trilayer graphene.
- **rhombohedral trilayer graphene (rtg)** — Three‑layer graphene with ABC stacking.
- **rigid compressed twisted bilayer graphene** — Compressed TBG kept rigid (no relaxation).
- **rizzo et al., charge-transfer plasmon polaritons at graphene/α-rucl3 interfaces, nano lett. 20, 8438 (2020)** — Paper reporting charge‑transfer plasmon polaritons at graphene/α‑RuCl₃ interfaces.
- **rkky interaction** — Indirect exchange interaction between localized spins mediated by conduction electrons, assumed weak.
- **robust superconductivity in magic‑angle multilayer graphene family** — Nature Materials 2022 paper on robust superconductivity across a multilayer graphene family.
- **rocking scan** — Angular scan of the crystal to monitor Bragg peak intensity versus temperature.
- **roland hillenbrand** — Researcher, co‑author of the 2016 Nat. Mater. paper on thermoelectric detection of graphene plasmons.
- **rolled edges and cracks** — Structural defects created when a SLG flake is torn during the fragmentation process.
- **room temperature** — Ambient temperature at which QTM measurements were demonstrated.
- **room temperature smearing** — Thermal broadening that can obscure fine features such as overlap‑site signatures.
- **room-temperature qtm measurement setup** — Setup comprising rotating stage and commercial AFM for QTM measurements.
- **room‑temperature quantum coherence demonstrated at the tip** — Observation that the QTM tip maintains coherent electron wavefunctions at ambient temperature.
- **roothaan‑hartree‑fock atomic wavefunctions** — Atomic Data Nucl. Data Tables 1974 paper by E. Clementi & C. Roetti.
- **roothaan‑hartree‑fock method** — Quantum‑chemical approach for atomic wavefunctions.
- **rossi et al., direct visualization of the charge transfer in graphene/α-rucl3 heterostructure, nano lett. 23, 8000 (2023)** — Paper showing direct visualization of charge transfer in a graphene/α‑RuCl₃ heterostructure.
- **rotated brillouin zones** — Brillouin zones of tip and sample rotated relative to each other by the twist angle.
- **rotation alignment** — Technique that combines rotation stage motion with visual feedback to find the centre of rotation.
- **rotation angle (θ)** — Twist angle between the two graphene layers.
- **rotation angles 0°,45°,90°,135°** — Discrete rotation positions used during topography scans.
- **rotation axis alignment** — Ensuring that the tip, sample area and rotation centre are collinear.
- **rotation matrix o(θ)** — Matrix describing rotation by the twist angle θ.
- **rotation used to scan momentum axis** — Rotating the sample to vary the momentum alignment during tunneling measurements.
- **rotational symmetry** — Symmetry that stabilises the antiferromagnetic order in altermagnets.
- **rpa density-density response function** — The random phase approximation density-density response function expressed as a 2×2 matrix in layer space.
- **run at v_bg = +10 v** — Measurement run performed with back‑gate voltage set to +10 V.
- **run at v_bg = 0 v** — Measurement run performed with back‑gate voltage set to 0 V.
- **run at v_bg = –10 v** — Measurement run performed with back‑gate voltage set to –10 V.
- **run at vbg = 2 v** — Conductance linecut taken at a back‑gate voltage of 2 V.
- **run at vbg = 8 v** — Conductance linecut taken at a back‑gate voltage of 8 V.

- **s. a. kivelson** — Co‑author of the 2020 Science review on quantum spin liquids.
- **s. adam** — Co‑author of several superconductivity papers.
- **s. biswas** — Co‑author of the 2025 arXiv preprint on Andreev vs tunneling spectroscopy.
- **s. chatterjee** — Co‑author of the 2015 spin‑current injection study.

- **s. de v.** — Co‑author acknowledged for financial support through the FPU program.
- **s. de vega** — First author of the 2017 ACS Photonics paper on plasmon generation via electron tunnelling in graphene.
- **s. dröscher** — Lead author on quantum capacitance of graphene.
- **s. d’souza** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nature paper on altermagnetic lifting of Kramers spin degeneracy.

- **s. e. nagler** — Co‑author of the 2020 Nano Lett. paper.

- **s. g. sharapov** — Researcher co‑author on next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping effects in graphene.

- **s. h. oh** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.

- **s. jeon** — Co‑author of the Nature 2020 paper on cascade of electronic transitions.

- **s. karube** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nat. Commun. on handedness manipulation.

- **s. l. tomarken** — Co‑author of the 2018 Nature papers on magic‑angle graphene.
- **s. lee** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **s. lee** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 PRL on broken Kramers degeneracy in MnTe.
- **s. lee** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 PRL on broken Kramers degeneracy in MnTe.

- **s. m. griffin** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 arXiv preprint on non‑relativistic spin splitting.
- **s. nadj‑perge** — Researcher, co‑author of the 2019 Nat. Phys. paper.
- **s. namgung** — First author of the 2018 ACS Nano article on an ultrasmall plasmonic light source driven by a graphene tunnel junction.
- **s. radelaar** — Researcher
- **s. reimers** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nat. Commun. on altermagnetic band splitting.

- **s. stavrić** — Co‑author of the 2025 p‑wave magnet paper.
- **s. stemmer** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **s. suman** — Co‑author of the 2025 arXiv preprint on Andreev vs tunneling spectroscopy.

- **s. yang** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2025 Nat. Commun. on 3D mapping of spin splitting.
- **s. yuan** — 
- **s. yun** — Author listed on the APL Mater. 2024 paper.
- **s.-d. li** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **s.-h. hung** — Co‑author of the 2022 arXiv paper.
- **s.-j. han** — Researcher, co‑author of the 2016 ACS Photonics paper on ultrasensitive plasmonic detection with graphene.
- **s.-l. yu** — Co‑author of the 2019 experimental identification of quantum spin liquids.
- **s.-y. choi** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **sample a** — Device measured repeatedly for nesting‑line statistics.
- **sample atom** — Surface atom of the specimen that participates in tunneling.
- **sample heating negligible** — Sample heating is negligible under nanoamp current conditions.
- **sample stage** — The platform that holds the sample and can be moved by the micro‑positioner.
- **sample states** — Quantum states of the sample involved in tunneling.
- **sample wings** — Sample assembly referred to as "wings".
- **satellite bias ev* ≈ 105 mev** — Bias at which plasmon‑assisted inelastic tunneling peaks appear.
- **satellite peaks at ±50 mev are nearly twist‑angle independent (except near γ)** — Observation that plasmon‑satellite features appear at a fixed bias regardless of the twist angle, revealing weak angle dependence.
- **scale factor k** — Experimental factor relating measured form factor to the free‑atom value.
- **scan angle (set to zero)** — Rotation angle of the scan grid, initially zeroed.
- **scan area (µm²)** — Lateral size of the region imaged or cleaned (e.g., 3 µm × 3 µm or 10 µm × 10 µm).
- **scanning beam shape with slit** — Manual scanning of beam shape and diameter using an adjustable slit.
- **scanning in continuous mode** — AFM imaging mode that continuously saves each scan line, used for cleaning monitoring.
- **scanning path ab → aa → ba** — Trajectory used to position molecules and record adsorption curves.
- **scanning probe microscopy** — Family of techniques that locally probe electronic properties; the QTM is a new member of this family.
- **scanning probe microscopy (spm)** — Family of techniques (AFM, STM) for nanoscale imaging.
- **scanning tunneling microscope (stm)** — Instrument that images surfaces via quantum tunnelling.
- **scanning tunneling microscopy (stm) imaging** — Experimental technique that obtains surface topography via quantum tunneling.
- **scanning tunnelling microscope (stm)** — Established technique that images electrons in real space; used as a conceptual comparison in the article.
- **scanning tunnelling microscopy** — Surface‑sensitive microscopy used in earlier graphene experiments that observed step‑like conductance features.
- **scanning tunnelling spectroscopy (sts)** — Local measurement of electronic density of states via tunnelling.
- **scattering continua** — Continuum of scattering observed in spin dynamics.
- **scattering continuum** — Broad continuum of scattering intensity indicative of fractionalized excitations.
- **scattering rate** — Rate at which electrons scatter between tip and sample states.
- **schottky barriers** — Metal‑semiconductor junctions used to convert plasmon energy into electrical signals.
- **schottky‑type electrical detector** — Nanoscale Schottky diode used to detect surface plasmons.
- **schrödinger equation** — Fundamental equation solved for the many‑particle states in the barrier problem.
- **screened coulomb potential (w)** — Effective interaction mediating electron‑electron coupling in the tunneling calculation.
- **screened interaction** — Effective interaction between two electrons in the sample after accounting for screening.
- **screened interaction** — Effective interaction obtained by combining material responses.
- **second derivative analysis (d²i/dvb²)** — Numerical extraction of peaks from conductance data to highlight phonon features.
- **second derivative d²i/dv²** — Second derivative of the I‑V characteristic used to reveal phonon peaks.
- **second derivative d²i/dv²** — Second derivative of the tunneling current with respect to bias voltage, used to highlight spectral features.
- **second derivative d²i/dv²** — Numerical second derivative of current with respect to bias.
- **second derivative d²i/dv² spectroscopy** — Derivative of the I‑V curve used to reveal sharp peaks that mark phonon turn‑on energies.
- **second derivative measurement (d²i/dv²)** — Derivative of the I‑V curve that reveals sharp peaks corresponding to phonon turn‑on energies.
- **second derivative of current with respect to voltage (d²i/dv²)** — Spectroscopic observable used to map electronic band features.
- **second derivative of tunneling current (d²i/dv²)** — Quantity whose bias dependence directly yields the dynamical spin structure factor of the QSL barrier.
- **second parabola of the telescope** — Movable parabola component of the telescope that showed instability during alignment.
- **second-order perturbation theory** — Perturbative calculation up to second order in the interaction.
- **second‑derivative analysis** — Numerical derivation of d²I/dV² to highlight phonon features.
- **second‑derivative measurement** — Extraction of d²I/dV² from conductance data to identify phonon modes.
- **see‑1d‑qmatter project** — ERC‑Cog grant project supporting the research.
- **self‑consistent calculation** — Computational technique solving charge density self‑consistently.
- **self‑consistent electrostatic solution** — Iterative solution of carrier density, chemical potentials and electrostatic potential φ.
- **self‑consistent electrostatics** — Electrostatic modeling of the graphene/hBN/graphene junction that determines the areal capacitance.
- **self‑energy correction** — Interaction‑induced modification of the quasiparticle energy.
- **self‑similar chain of metal nanospheres as an efficient nanolens** — Li, K. R. et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 2003, describing a nanolens made of metal nanospheres.
- **semiconductor imaging** — STM‑based imaging of semiconductor surfaces that typically requires a large bias.
- **semiconductor surface** — Typical sample type imaged by STM.
- **sequoia chopper spectrometer** — A newly operating chopper spectrometer at the SNS.
- **sequoia instrument** — Fine‑resolution chopper spectrometer at the SNS used for the measurements.
- **sequoia time‑of‑flight spectrometer (ornl)** — High‑flux neutron spectrometer employed for INS measurements on CrSb.
- **sergio** — Researcher involved in THz collimation experiments.
- **sergio salvía** — Presenter and author of the THz cSNOM alignment guide.
- **series resistance** — Additional resistance in the device circuit that influences I‑V characteristics.
- **series resistance identification** — Method to determine parasitic resistances in the electrical circuit.
- **service engineer** — Engineer from Edinburgh Instruments who installs and commissions the FIRL100 laser.
- **service panel** — Service panel located near the edge of the bench for easy access.
- **set of wave vectors s(k_f , θ)** — The collection of k‑vectors where the tip and sample Fermi lines intersect.
- **setpoint force (20–50 nn)** — Force applied to establish stable electrical contact without damaging graphite layers.
- **setpoint voltage** — Control voltage applied to the AFM feedback loop to define the desired cantilever deflection.
- **set‑point force 10‑20 nn** — Force range used during tip approach to avoid damage.
- **sev2015-0522** — Spanish MINECO grant supporting the work.
- **sfpx polarization channel** — Polarization analysis channel used to isolate magnetic scattering.
- **shahal ilani** — Corresponding author of the Nature article.
- **sharp momentum‑resolved map aligns with theory** — Observation that the measured momentum‑resolved map matches theoretical predictions.
- **sharp peak from tip plasmon** — A sharp peak appears in the conductance due to interaction mediated by the tip’s plasmon.
- **sharp tip** — Metallic tip used in near‑field nanoscopy to enhance light‑plasmon coupling.
- **sheet resistance** — Resistance per square of a thin film, here graphene.
- **shelf** — Shelf with height 2.5 cm used to hold other instruments
- **shift of ndc peak with twist angle** — Increasing θ moves the NDC peak to higher bias voltage.
- **shipping damage** — Physical damage that may occur to the laser during transport.
- **short magnon lifetimes in metals** — Experimental difficulty in observing magnon splitting in metallic altermagnets.
- **short-ranged coulomb interaction** — Coulomb interaction becomes short‑ranged due to gate screening.
- **shot‑noise limited performance** — The laser output exhibits noise at the fundamental quantum limit.
- **should we always use 4 layers?** — Question about the necessity of using four-layer PCBs.
- **should we use paste layers? can the flux in them create batteries?** — Question regarding paste layers and flux‑induced battery risk.
- **si(111) surface** — Silicon (111) surface examined with STM under various bias and doping conditions.
- **si(111)-7×7 surface** — Complex silicon reconstruction first imaged with atomic resolution by STM.
- **si/sio2 pillar with gold contacts** — Pillar (~100 µm wide, ~80 µm tall) supporting devices.
- **si/sio₂ pillar (≈100 µm wide, ≈80 µm tall)** — Micropillar on which the flat‑substrate heterostructure is placed.
- **si/sio₂ wafer (90 nm sio₂)** — Substrate on which the polymer membrane is initially spin‑coated.
- **signatures of chiral superconductivity in rhombohedral graphene** — Nature 2025 paper on chiral superconductivity signatures.
- **signatures of goldstone modes in tunneling spectra** — Investigate signatures of Goldstone modes in tunneling spectra of symmetry‑broken phases in MATBG.
- **signatures of phonon and defect‑assisted tunneling in planar metal–hexagonal boron nitride–graphene junctions** — Nano Letters 16, 7982–7987 (2016) identifying phonon and defect‑assisted tunneling signatures.
- **signatures of plasmons in matbg tunneling spectrum** — What are the observable signatures of flat‑band plasmons in the QTM tunneling spectrum of MATBG?
- **silicon (si)** — Alternative THz lens material mentioned for the QTM.
- **silicon chip** — Silicon substrate onto which the exfoliated graphite is transferred.
- **silicon detection limit (~1.3 ev)** — Minimum photon energy detectable by silicon photodiode.
- **silicon detection window** — Spectral range of the detector; resonant peaks shifted outside this window become invisible.
- **silicon technology** — Conventional semiconductor platform that can be enhanced with 2D materials.
- **silicon wafer** — Standard silicon wafer that holds the chip carrier.
- **silicon‑based surface plasmon polariton source setup** — Silicon device that electrically launches SPPs.
- **silver gallium sulfide (aggas2)** — A mid‑IR nonlinear crystal.
- **silver periscope** — Silver‑coated periscope employed in the beam path.
- **silver‑coated afm ters tip** — AFM tip coated with silver for enhanced TERS performance.
- **silver‑epoxy contacting** — Use of conductive silver epoxy to attach copper pads to electrodes.
- **similar features for different twist angles** — Both 2.7° and 3.4° TBG samples show comparable flat‑band signatures.
- **simulated di/dv spectra for nodeless superconducting state** — Numerical simulation of dI/dV(kF,θ) for a 1.05° TBG in a nodeless superconducting state.
- **simulation of tunneling conductance** — Numerical calculation of dI/dV spectra based on band structure and tunneling matrix elements.
- **simultaneous transport and tunneling spectroscopy** — Combined measurement of transport and tunneling on the same device.
- **simultaneous transport and tunneling spectroscopy of moiré graphene** — arXiv 2025 preprint reporting the superconducting gap and nodal signatures.
- **single crystal of nio** — The NiO crystal supplied by E. Uchida and Y. Nakazumi for form‑factor measurements.
- **single-electron scattering rate** — Rate at which an electron initially in a sample state scatters into tip states (or vice versa).
- **single-layer graphene (slg)** — Single-layer graphene used as electrodes in the tunnel devices.
- **single‑atom contact optical probe setup** — Configuration for probing quantum shot‑noise at a single‑atom junction.
- **single‑crystal x‑ray diffraction** — Single‑crystal X‑ray diffraction for structural refinement of CrSb.
- **single‑ion anisotropy (d)** — On‑site anisotropy term in the CrSb spin Hamiltonian.
- **single‑plasmon sources** — Emitters that generate individual plasmons.
- **single‑q incommensurate magnetic structures** — Magnetic structures described by a single incommensurate propagation vector.
- **single‑step tunnelling mechanism (iind‑ex)** — Tunnelling process that creates an indirect TMD exciton in one step.
- **singularities** — Sharp features in d²I/dV² arising from band edges or Dirac points.
- **singularities in di/dv** — Bias‑dependent singularities in zero‑temperature differential conductance that reveal momentum‑resolved information about Bogoliubov spectra.
- **singularity in di/dv** — Sharp feature in the differential conductance indicating the onset of tunneling into a superconducting gap.
- **sinθ/λ** — Scattering variable used to express the range of the form‑factor measurement.
- **sio2 beamsplitter** — Beamsplitter opaque to VIS light, used for THz routing.
- **site preparation for laser installation** — Activities required to ready a laboratory space before installing the FIRL100 laser.
- **six-fold distribution of spectral weight** — Signature of g‑wave altermagnets observed in the spin‑excitation spectra of CrSb.
- **six‑fold spectral weight distribution as signature of g‑wave altermagnets** — The six‑fold pattern in the spin‑excitation spectra confirms g‑wave altermagnetism.
- **six‑fold spin‑wave splitting** — Experimental observation of six‑fold splitting of magnon branches along symmetry‑equivalent directions.
- **skin burns** — Potential injury caused by exposure to the FIRL100 beam.
- **slave-particle mean-field hamiltonian** — Theoretical approach used to model spin dynamics of quantum spin liquids.
- **slit** — Narrow aperture formed by the two blades that enables higher‑resolution power measurements.
- **slit scanning** — Technique of moving a slit across the beam to map its shape.
- **slope flips on carrier polarity change** — The sign of the linear dependence reverses when the dominant carrier type switches.
- **slope of approach curve (v/nm)** — Linear relationship between deflection error voltage and tip displacement (≈ 1 V/80 nm).
- **slot intel·ligent per snmp/modbus** — Slot intel·ligent per a gestió remota via SNMP o Modbus.
- **small fermi wavevector (kf)** — Assumption of a small Fermi momentum used to simplify tunneling calculations.
- **small twist angles can probe significant portion of qsl brillouin zone** — Result that a twist angle of ≈11.7° suffices to reach the BZ corners of 1T‑TaS₂.
- **small velocity renormalisation (~10 % per decade)** — Observed weak renormalisation due to dielectric encapsulation and double‑layer screening.
- **smp 1211‑40113 male** — Male SMP 1211‑40113 connector.
- **smp connector** — Connector used on SMP PCB for motor lines.
- **smp connector** — Standard SMP connector used on SMP_PCB.
- **smp pcb** — PCB that connects 6 lines with SMP connectors from Renaissance motors to fridge lines.
- **smp_pcb** — Connects 6 lines with SMP connectors from the 6 Renaissance motors (used for capacitive readout) to SMP connectors on the PCB, and routes them to fridge lines.
- **soft modes** — Low‑energy collective excitations that soften near phase transitions.
- **soft modes** — Low‑energy collective excitations that soften near a transition.
- **soft modes in magic angle twisted bilayer graphene (arxiv 2020)** — arXiv:2009.14827 discussing soft collective modes in MATBG.
- **soft modes in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene** — The 2020 arXiv identifies soft collective modes in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **sotirios papadopoulos** — Co‑author of the Nature Materials 2023 article on exciton‑assisted electron tunnelling.
- **so₃ molecule** — Sulfur trioxide used in adsorption‑energy measurements.
- **so₃ molecule** — Electron‑sufficient molecule that prefers AB/BA sites on TBG.
- **space charge** — Accumulation of charge that could affect tunneling, considered negligible.
- **space charge negligible** — Space charge effects are negligible under nanoamp current conditions.
- **spallation neutron source** — DOE user facility providing the PTAX and SEQUOIA spectrometers.
- **spanish ministry cex2019-000910-s** — Spanish Ministry of Science and Innovation grant CEX2019-000910-S
- **spanish ministry pid2020-112625gb-i00** — Spanish Ministry of Science and Innovation grant PID2020-112625GB-I00
- **spatial current** — Current measured locally on the sample surface.
- **spatial inhomogeneities (local strain, crystallographic orientation locking)** — Device‑level variations that can split the resonant peak but are not captured by the simple model.
- **spatial resolution (~100 nm)** — Lateral resolution of the QTM scanning capability.
- **spatial scan imaging** — Scanning the tip over atomic defects to map the tip shape in situ.
- **specific contact resistivity** — Resistance associated with the metal‑graphene contact interface.
- **spectral broadening** — Widening of the emission peak with increasing twist angle and with finite temperature.
- **spectral characteristic model** — Model used to fit the emission spectra.
- **spectral characteristics measurement** — Optical spectroscopy used to analyse the emitted light from the tunnel devices.
- **spectral efficiency** — Distribution of emitted photons per tunneling electron as a function of photon energy.
- **spectral efficiency (η_ph)** — Ratio of emitted photons to tunneling electrons as a function of photon energy.
- **spectral efficiency of misaligned device is ~10× smaller than aligned device** — Experimental observation that η_ph_inel is an order of magnitude lower than η_ph_el.
- **spectral efficiency η_ph** — Number of photons emitted per electron traversing the tunnel barrier, defined as γ_ph / J_total.
- **spectral efficiency η_ph^el** — Photon‑per‑electron efficiency as a function of photon energy, derived from the emitted spectrum.
- **spectral efficiency η_ph_el grows linearly with photon frequency** — Model predicts a linear increase of the resonant peak intensity with ω for aligned devices.
- **spectral function a_s** — Spectral function obtained from the GW Green’s function (A_S = −Im G/π).
- **spectral function a_s(k,ε)** — The electronic spectral function derived from the imaginary part of the Green’s function.
- **spectral function a_tb** — A_tb(q,ω,n) describing particle‑hole excitations in graphene.
- **spectral function of tip** — Energy-dependent density of states of the STM tip.
- **spectral functions** — Energy‑ and momentum‑dependent electronic Green’s function describing quasiparticle excitations.
- **spectral functions of strongly correlated quantum states** — Momentum‑resolved density of states of phases such as superconductors or Chern insulators.
- **spectral peak decreases with twist angle** — The resonant spectral peak broadens and eventually vanishes as the twist angle increases beyond a few degrees.
- **spectral tuning (~0.2 ev)** — Ability to shift the resonant photon‑emission peak by more than 0.2 eV via twist angle.
- **spectroscopy of single atoms in the scanning tunneling microscope** — Physical Review B 34 (8), 5947-5950 (1986)
- **spectrum analyser** — CO2 spectrum analyser covering the 9‑11 µm wavelength range.
- **specular‑reflection model** — Model for the non‑local response of the gold surface.
- **spill‑out of gold electrons** — Enhanced tunnelling probability when gold electrons extend into the hBN barrier near the Γ point.
- **spin configuration** — Alignment of electron spins (e.g., all parallel in Mn++).
- **spin excitations** — Collective magnetic excitations probed by neutron scattering.
- **spin hamiltonian** — Microscopic spin Hamiltonian describing the exchange couplings in CrSb.
- **spin index (s)** — Index labeling electron spin up or down.
- **spin liquid** — A magnetic state lacking conventional order, often exhibiting fractionalized excitations.
- **spin polarization** — Difference in radial dependence of wave functions for opposite electron spins.
- **spin structure factor** — Correlation function describing spin correlations, derived in the appendix.
- **spin structure factor s(q,ω)** — Dynamical quantity describing spin correlations in the barrier.
- **spin susceptibility** — Static magnetic response measured in bulk samples.
- **spin susceptibility χ^{(2)}(iωₙ)** — Linear response function governing the spin‑dependent tunneling contribution.
- **spin symmetric density of states** — Assumed property of graphene where the density of states is spin‑symmetric.
- **spin wave velocity (in‑plane)** — Velocity of 61 km s⁻¹ for magnons propagating within the CrSb plane.
- **spin wave velocity (out‑of‑plane)** — Velocity of 58 km s⁻¹ for magnons propagating perpendicular to the CrSb plane.
- **spin waves** — Collective excitations of spins in magnetic materials.
- **spin-flip tunneling** — Tunneling contribution involving spin flips mediated by QSL fluctuations.
- **spin-valley flavors** — Fourfold degeneracy (spin and valley) of electronic states.
- **spinon fermi momentum (k_{s,f})** — Momentum defining the spinon Fermi surface.
- **spinon fermi surface qsl** — A QSL with a spinon Fermi surface and parabolic band.
- **spinon fermi surface u(1) spin liquid in the spin-orbit-coupled triangular-lattice mott insulator ybmggao4** — 2017 paper reporting a U(1) spin liquid in YbMgGaO4.
- **spinon fermi velocity (v_s)** — Velocity parameter of Dirac spinons.
- **spinon gap (δ1s)** — Energy gap at k = 0 for chiral spinons.
- **spinon hopping** — Amplitude for spinon tunneling between neighboring sites.
- **spinon hopping on a triangular lattice** — Toy model describing spinon motion on a triangular lattice, including next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping.
- **spinon kondo effect** — Kondo‑like screening of spinons observed on cobalt adatoms.
- **spinon kondo effect in cobalt atoms on 1t‑tase₂** — Observation reported in the spinon Kondo effect paper.
- **spinon particle‑hole excitations** — Excitations consisting of a spinon particle and a spinon hole.
- **spinon tight-binding model** — Tight-binding calculation of spinon bands on a triangular lattice.
- **spinon velocity parameter (v_s)** — Defined as ħ k_{s,F}/m_s, the characteristic spinon velocity.
- **spinons** — Fractionalized quasiparticles in quantum spin liquids that carry spin‑½ but no electric charge.
- **spinons** — Fractionalized spin excitations that can couple to tunnelling.
- **spinw** — Software package used to fit neutron‑scattering data to spin‑wave models.
- **spin‑coating** — Spin‑coating of polymer films (PVA, PPC) on Si/SiO₂.
- **spin‑current generation** — Predicted effect of NRSS in metallic altermagnets.
- **spin‑current injection** — Method of generating spin currents to probe excitations in insulating materials.
- **spin‑density‑wave antiferromagnetism** — Spin‑density‑wave order in chromium.
- **spin‑dependent part** — Component of the tunneling operator that involves spin‑flip processes.
- **spin‑dependent tunneling current** — Current component that depends on the spin structure factor of the barrier.
- **spin‑dependent tunneling current** — Tunneling current that depends on electron spin, derived from the spin susceptibility χ^{(2)}.
- **spin‑independent part** — Component of the tunneling operator that does not involve spin flips.
- **spin‑neutral currents** — Charge‑neutral flow of spin that does not carry net charge.
- **spin‑neutral currents for spintronics** — Nat. Commun. 12, 7061 (2021) introducing spin‑neutral currents.
- **spin‑orbit‑coupled triangular‑lattice mott insulator** — Class of Mott insulators with strong spin‑orbit coupling.
- **spin‑polarized scanning tunneling microscopy** — STM variant sensitive to the spin of tunneling electrons.
- **spin‑polarized superconductivity** — Superconductivity with spin polarization.
- **spin‑split magnons** — Magnon bands that are split in energy due to NRSS.
- **spin‑valley locking** — Coupling between spin and valley degrees of freedom.
- **spin‑wave excitations** — Coherent magnon excitations observed in CrSb.
- **spin‑wave transport** — Propagation of collective spin excitations (magnons) through a material.
- **spin–orbit‑coupled bilayer graphene** — Bilayer graphene with proximitized spin–orbit coupling.
- **split onset lines** — Four distinct onset lines that appear for finite back‑gate voltage due to different Fermi‑surface sizes.
- **splitting of nesting lines** — Observed separation of nesting features caused by electron‑electron interactions.
- **splitting of nesting lines** — Observed separation of the upper and lower nesting branches at low bias.
- **splitting of nesting lines at high bias** — Observation that the nesting line bifurcates into two branches (Nesting I and II) for |V_b| > 1 V, reproducible across tips, samples and locations.
- **spm tip** — Scanning probe microscopy tip that participates in surface‑plasmon generation.
- **spontaneous breaking of c₃z symmetry** — Emergent loss of three‑fold rotational symmetry in the tunneling spectrum.
- **spontaneous breaking of c₃z symmetry can triple singularities in tunneling spectrum** — Theoretical result that loss of three‑fold symmetry multiplies spectral singularities.
- **spontaneous vortex‑antivortex lattice and majorana fermions in rhombohedral graphene** — 2025 arXiv preprint by F. Gaggioli, D. Guerci and L. Fu.
- **spot mode** — Spot scanning mode for beam control.
- **spring connector** — Connector used to join PCBs via spring contacts.
- **spring connector** — Spring‑type connector used to join PCB modules.
- **spring constant 48 n/m** — High stiffness of the tipless cantilever needed for stable twist operation.
- **square well potential** — Potential model with depth V₀ and width a used to fit the out‑of‑plane graphene electron wave function.
- **stabilisation system** — System (14.5 kg) that stabilises the FIRL100 on an optical table.
- **stable photon emission** — Observation that the light output does not degrade over time.
- **stacking‑driven gap formation** — Band‑gap opening caused by the stacking order of layered crystals.
- **stage modification** — Adjustments to the AFM stage to accommodate the QTM geometry.
- **start exposure** — Initiating exposure in lithography mode.
- **statistical clustering** — Data‑analysis method applied to multiple spectra from the same sample.
- **step increase in current** — Sharp increase of I at the onset bias.
- **steplike behavior** — Step‑like increase of the spin structure factor at a threshold frequency.
- **steps amplitude scales linearly with vbg** — The amplitude of conductance steps grows proportionally to the backgate voltage.
- **steps in conductance vs bias** — Discrete increases in G associated with inelastic phonon emission.
- **stimulated emission** — Process where an excited molecule emits a photon coherently with the stimulating field.
- **stm corrugation** — Variation in tunneling current that reflects surface topography.
- **stm corrugation amplitude** — Measured height variation of the surface in STM scans: 0.45 Å for 2×1 and 1.4 Å for 3×1.
- **stm hamiltonian (1)** — Full Hamiltonian describing tip, sample, and tunneling coupling.
- **stm images of si(111) similar for heavily doped low‑voltage and low‑doped high‑voltage** — Comparable STM images were obtained for Si(111) with different doping levels when the bias voltage was adjusted.
- **stm imaging** — Constant‑current mode imaging of surface electronic density using an STM tip.
- **stm imaging of si(111)-7×7** — Atomic‑resolution measurement that confirmed the capability of STM.
- **stm insensitive to second layer at distances >8 å** — Removal of the second and deeper layers has negligible effect on the STM signal beyond ~8 Å.
- **stm insensitivity to surface‑layer position** — STM images change little when the topmost surface layer is shifted relative to underlying layers.
- **stm measurement on au(110) surfaces** — STM experiment measuring corrugation amplitudes on Au(110) 3×1 and 2×1 reconstructions.
- **stm measurement on si(111) surface** — STM experiment investigating bias‑voltage and doping effects on Si(111) images.
- **stm tip setup** — Scanning tunnelling microscope tip used to create a tunnelling junction with TBG.
- **stm tip with 9 å radius** — STM tip characterized by a radius of 9 Å used in the calculations.
- **stm tunneling spectroscopy measurement** — Measurement of the I‑V characteristic at a fixed tip position.
- **stochastic semiclassical approach** — Computational technique used to study spin dynamics.
- **strain** — Mechanical deformation applied to a material.
- **strain < 0.1 %** — Determination that the strain in both graphene layers is smaller than 0.1 % based on the momentum‑resolved map.
- **strain‑driven mechanism unlikely** — The nesting split is reproducible across devices, indicating that biaxial or uniaxial strain does not cause it.
- **strange metallicity** — Non‑Fermi‑liquid metallic behavior.
- **strange metallicity of moiré twisted bilayer graphene** — Phys. Rev. Res. 4, 033061 (2022)
- **strange‑metal behaviour** — Unconventional metallic transport regime often linked to strong EPC in moiré systems.
- **strange‑metal behaviour phenomenon** — Anomalous metallic transport observed in moiré systems.
- **stray‑field‑free waveguides** — Property of altermagnetic magnon waveguides that eliminates magnetic stray fields.
- **strength of inelastic features increases with coupling to pairing glue** — Potential probe of pairing mechanism.
- **strong correlation** — Regime where electron‑electron interactions dominate the physics.
- **strong coupling phases of partially filled twisted bilayer graphene narrow bands** — Reference (7) theoretical study of strong‑coupling phases in tBG.
- **strong electron‑phonon coupling, electron‑hole asymmetry, and nonadiabaticity in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Phys. Rev. B 98, 241412(R) (2018)
- **strong electron‑phonon coupling, electron‑hole asymmetry, and nonadiabaticity in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (prb 98, 241412, 2018)** — Study reporting unusually strong electron‑phonon coupling and nonadiabatic effects in magic‑angle TBG.
- **strong inter-valley electron-phonon coupling in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Nat (London) 636, 342 (2024)
- **strong magnetic field** — Magnetic field accompanying the localized electric field when the TBG surface is polarized.
- **stronger landau damping on acoustic plasmon** — Interband Landau damping attenuates the acoustic plasmon more than the optical one.
- **strongly correlated fermi systems** — Fermi systems with strong electron correlations.
- **strongly correlated many-body systems** — Systems where electron‑electron interactions dominate the physical behavior.
- **strongly coupled electronic systems** — Electronic systems with strong coupling.
- **strong‑coupling superconductivity. i** — 1966 Phys. Rev. paper by D. J. Scalapino, J. R. Schrieffer and J. W. Wilkins.
- **strong‑coupling superconductor** — Superconductor where electron‑phonon coupling is large, giving frequency‑dependent gap and finite lifetimes.
- **structural characterisation** — Assessment of crystal structure and defects via Raman peaks.
- **structure and control of charge density waves** — Understanding and manipulating CDW order.
- **structure and control of charge density waves in two-dimensional 1t‑tas2** — 2015 study of CDW structure and control in 2D 1T‑TaS2.
- **structure of the superconducting order parameter** — What is the symmetry and momentum dependence of the order parameter in graphene‑based superconductors?
- **structure sensitivity of stm image** — Degree to which the STM image reflects atomic positions beyond the first surface layer.
- **structure, control, and dynamics of altermagnetic textures** — npj Spintronics 2, 35 (2024) discussing altermagnetic texture properties.
- **study of au(110) surface by tersoff and hamann** — Paper describing self‑consistent LAPW calculations and comparison with STM measurements.
- **stuttgart instruments alpha ultrastable tunable laser** — An ultrafast, wavelength‑tunable frequency conversion system based on a fiber‑feedback optical parametric oscillator (FF‑OPO).
- **su(2) gauge invariance** — Invariance under SU(2) gauge transformations.
- **su(2) gauge invariance and order parameters in strongly coupled electronic systems** — 1988 paper on SU(2) gauge invariance and order parameters.
- **su(2) gauge symmetry** — SU(2) symmetry in gauge theories.
- **su(2) gauge symmetry of the large-u limit of the hubbard model** — 1988 paper discussing SU(2) gauge symmetry in the Hubbard model.
- **sub-moire potential landscape** — Potential landscape arising from sub‑moire patterns in layered materials.
- **sublattice position τ_a** — In‑plane position of sublattice A within the graphene unit cell.
- **sublattice vectors (δ_a, δ_b)** — Displacements of the two sublattices within the graphene unit cell.
- **substrate‑tuned phase transitions in 1t‑tas₂** — Demonstration that the substrate can tune phase transitions in 1T‑TaS₂.
- **sub‑nano‑newton force** — Constant force maintained during rotation with sub‑nano‑Newton precision.
- **superconducting altermagnet** — Altermagnetic material that also exhibits superconductivity.
- **superconducting gap** — Energy scale of the superconducting order parameter.
- **superconducting gap δ** — Energy gap magnitude of the superconducting state.
- **superconducting ma tbg sample** — MA TBG sample in the superconducting state with electron doping μ_S = 2.5 meV.
- **superconducting matbg sample** — Superconducting magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene sample used as the tunneling target.
- **superconducting node** — Momentum point in the moiré Brillouin zone where the superconducting gap vanishes.
- **superconducting node location project** — Research effort aimed at mapping superconducting nodes in the moiré Brillouin zone.
- **superconducting pairing** — Cooper pairing possibly mediated by phason and optical phonons.
- **superconducting state** — Phase where MA TBG exhibits superconductivity.
- **superconducting tbg** — Research project studying superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **superconducting twisted bilayer graphene (tbg)** — Twisted bilayer graphene exhibiting superconductivity.
- **superconductivity and strong interactions in a tunable moiré quasicrystal** — Nature 2023 paper on superconductivity in a moiré quasicrystal.
- **superconductivity from collective excitations in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene (2020)** — Proposes that collective modes can mediate superconductivity in TBG.
- **superconductivity in 5.0° twisted bilayer wse₂** — 2025 Nature paper by Y. Guo et al.
- **superconductivity in twisted bilayer wse₂** — 2025 Nature paper by Y. Xia, Z. Han, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, J. Shan and K. F. Mak.
- **superconductivity in twisted double bilayer graphene stabilized by wse₂** — Nature Materials 2023 paper on superconductivity in twisted double bilayer graphene with WSe₂.
- **superconductivity in two‑dimensional van der waals materials** — Emergence of superconducting order in atomically thin layered crystals.
- **superconductivity: in two volumes** — Comprehensive textbook on superconductivity.
- **superconductor sample** — Superconducting material used in the tunneling calculation.
- **superconductors** — Superconducting contacts used in plasmon detection schemes.
- **superfluid stiffness of magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene** — Nature 2025 paper.
- **superfluid stiffness of twisted multi-layer graphene superconductors** — arXiv:2406.13742
- **superfluid stiffness of twisted trilayer graphene superconductors** — Nature 2025 paper.
- **superlattice minibands** — Subbands formed by the moiré superlattice.
- **superlattice superconductivity** — Superconductivity modulated by a superlattice potential.
