- **supplementary information for the quantum twisting microscope** — Supplementary document containing methods and additional data for the QTM study.
- **supporting information (si)** — Supplementary document containing detailed methods, device analysis, and theory.
- **suppressed tunneling matrix elements near κ′** — Tunneling matrix elements are strongly suppressed near κ′ due to layer polarization.
- **suppression of tip‑induced broadening** — Strong suppression of tip‑induced broadening when the tip lies on the sample’s Fermi line.
- **suppression of tunneling matrix elements** — Suppression of tunneling matrix elements due to interference effects.
- **suprunenko, y. f.; gorbar, e. v.; loktev, v. m.; sharapov, s. g. effect of next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping on the electronic properties of graphene. low temp. phys. 2008, 34, 812-817.** — Analysis of how next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping modifies graphene’s electronic properties.
- **surf. sci. 119, l393 (1982)** — Reference authored by M. Manninen, J. F. Ngrskov and C. Umrigar.
- **surf. sci. 126, 236 (1983)** — Reference authored by H. Rohrer.
- **surf. sci. 131, l379 (1983)** — Reference authored by G. Binnig, H. Rohrer, Ch. Gerber and E. Weibel.
- **surf. sci. 88, l29 (1979)** — Reference authored by W. Moritz and D. Wolf.
- **surface bloch wave vector** — Wave‑vector k describing the periodic part of a surface electronic state.
- **surface brillouin zone** — Reciprocal‑space region of the surface; states near its centre dominate the long‑range STM signal.
- **surface lattice constant** — Lattice spacing of a surface reconstruction influencing STM resolution.
- **surface plasmon** — Collective electron oscillation induced between the substrate and the SPM tip.
- **surface plasmon coupling to nanoscale schottky‑type electrical detectors. appl. phys. lett. 2010, 97, 161110** — Shows coupling of surface plasmons to nanoscale Schottky detectors.
- **surface plasmon dispersion** — Relation between frequency and wavevector of surface plasmons on oxidized Al.
- **surface plasmon excitation by tunneling electrons** — Excitation of surface plasmons when electrons tunnel through a junction, observed in GaAs‑Pb tunnel junctions (1969).
- **surface plasmon polariton detection** — Electrical measurement of SPPs.
- **surface plasmon resonance** — Resonant excitation of collective electron oscillations at a metal‑dielectric interface, used for sensing.
- **surface plasmon resonance for gas detection and biosensing** — Liedberg, B. et al., Sens. Actuators 1983, reporting SPR as a sensing technique.
- **surface topography imaging** — STM imaging that directly reflects the geometric surface corrugation.
- **surface waves** — Evanescent electromagnetic modes bound to the graphene surface.
- **surface‑enhanced optical microscopy** — Microscopy technique that exploits surface‑enhanced optical effects for improved contrast.
- **surface‑enhanced optical microscopy** — JOSA B 1985 paper introducing surface‑enhanced optical microscopy.
- **surface‑enhanced raman intensity (i_sers)** — Measured Raman signal intensity after enhancement.
- **surface‑enhanced raman spectroscopy (sers)** — Raman spectroscopy enhanced by metallic nanostructures.
- **surface‑phonon‑plasmon polariton modes** — Hybrid modes arising from coupling between graphene plasmons and h‑BN phonons.
- **susceptibility χ** — Density response function χ_S or χ_T.
- **suspended graphene** — Graphene sheet suspended over a substrate, minimizing substrate effects.
- **swiss national science foundation eccellenza fellowship pcefp2_203663** — Swiss National Science Foundation Eccellenza Professorial Fellowship no. PCEFP2_203663
- **swiss national science foundation sinergia grant 189924 (hydronics)** — Swiss National Science Foundation Sinergia grant no. 189924 (Hydronics)
- **swiss national science fund grant 200020_192362** — Funding source supporting the current study.
- **swiss state secretariat contract mb22.00076** — Swiss State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation contract no. MB22.00076
- **symmetric charge‑neutral case** — Theoretical situation of two identical MLG layers at overall charge neutrality.
- **symmetrization of di/dv data** — Procedure of averaging positive and negative bias spectra.
- **symmetry origin of lattice vibration modes in twisted multilayer graphene: phasons versus moiré phonons (prb 106, 075420, 2022)** — Theoretical analysis distinguishing phason and moiré phonon symmetries.
- **symmetry restored by flipping bias** — Flipping the bias axis and the sign of the current makes the –10 V and +10 V back‑gate measurements nearly identical.
- **system dirac point** — Dirac point of the system’s band.
- **system fermi velocity (v_f^s)** — Fermi velocity of the system material.
- **t (temperature)** — Temperature of the system.
- **t matrix** — Matrix describing the amplitude for inelastic tunneling processes.
- **t. bauer** — Co‑author of the Majorana‑zero‑mode STM study.
- **t. bauer et al., scanning tunneling spectroscopy of majorana zero modes in a kitaev spin liquid, phys. rev. b 107, 054432 (2023)** — Paper reporting STM spectroscopy of Majorana zero modes in a Kitaev spin liquid.
- **t. bein** — Researcher
- **t. benschop** — 
- **t. e. hartman** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **t. f. heinz** — Researcher, co‑author of several graphene‑plasmon sensing papers (2014, 2016).
- **t. hsu** — Co‑author of the SU(2) gauge symmetry paper.
- **t. low** — Co‑author of the 2014 Phys. Rev. Lett. paper on plasmons in black phosphorus.
- **t. micklitz** — Co‑author of the 2009 spinon‑Fermi‑surface measurement paper.
- **t. moriyama** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nat. Commun. on handedness manipulation.
- **t. ono** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nat. Commun. on handedness manipulation.
- **t. r. paudel** — Author listed on the Science 2015 paper.
- **t. schmid** — Co‑author of the TERS status and outlook paper.
- **t. senthil** — Co‑author of the 2020 Science review on quantum spin liquids.
- **t. t.** — Supplier of hBN crystals.
- **t. yu** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2025 arXiv preprint on d‑wave Fermi surface.
- **t.-h. han** — Lead author of the 2012 kagome‑lattice spin‑liquid paper.
- **ta phonon mode (in-plane transverse acoustic)** — In-plane transverse acoustic phonon mode.
- **tangency condition** — Geometric condition where the tip Fermi line is tangent to a contour line of the quasiparticle dispersion, giving parallel group velocities.
- **tangency wave vector (p*)** — Specific k where the tip Fermi line touches the Bogoliubov band, producing the singularity.
- **taniguchi & watanabe 2007, j. cryst. growth** — Reference describing high‑purity h‑BN crystal growth.
- **taniguchi, t.** — Co‑author of the 2007 h‑BN crystal growth paper.
- **tap water** — Clean mains water used as an alternative coolant for the FIRL100.
- **tbg bands** — Electronic bands of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **tbg sample** — The experimental sample consisting of twisted bilayer graphene.
- **tbg sample (θ ≈ 2.7°)** — Twisted bilayer graphene device with a nominal twist angle of 2.7 degrees used in the pressure experiments.
- **tbg sample with θ = 16.8°** — Twisted bilayer graphene device measured at a twist angle of 16.8 degrees.
- **tbg sample with θ = 6°** — Twisted bilayer graphene device measured at a twist angle of 6 degrees.
- **tbg θ = 16.8°** — Twisted bilayer graphene with a twist angle of 16.8°.
- **tbg θ = 22.7°** — Twisted bilayer graphene with a twist angle of 22.7°.
- **tbg θ = 9.4°** — Twisted bilayer graphene with a twist angle of 9.4°.
- **teagues electron tunneling experiments** — 1978 vacuum tunneling experiments between two gold electrodes that demonstrated the feasibility of STM.
- **tearing produces rolled edges and cracks** — Fragmentation can lead to structural defects that further affect alignment.
- **technical guide to bardeens tunneling theory in stm** — The present tutorial article by Gottlieb and Wesoloski.
- **telescope** — Telescope assembly used for THz beam collimation.
- **temperature (°c)** — Ambient temperature in degrees Celsius.
- **temperature 50 °c** — Temperature applied to the cantilever during heating.
- **temperature and gate voltage dependent raman spectra of single‑layer graphene** — ACS Nano 2011 paper reporting Raman spectra of graphene as a function of temperature and gate voltage.
- **temperature broadening** — Smearing of spectral features due to finite thermal energy, limiting resolution.
- **temperature broadening** — Reduction of feature intensity due to finite temperature.
- **temperature broadening (kbt = 25 mev)** — Thermal broadening at room temperature included in the theoretical fit.
- **temperature control stability** — Stability of the coolant temperature, ±0.1 °C for the thermocirculator.
- **temperature smearing** — Smoothing of dI/dV singularities due to finite temperature.
- **temperature-dependent electron-electron interaction** — Variation of electron‑electron interaction strength with temperature.
- **temperature-dependent electron-electron interaction in graphene on srtio3** — Nano Lett. 2017 study of temperature dependence of e‑e interactions in graphene on SrTiO3.
- **temperature‑dependent raman study of the spin‑liquid pyrochlore tb₂ti₂o₇** — Phys. Rev. B 78, 134420 (2008) by M. M. ˛aczka et al.
- **temperature‑dependent rocking scans at q=(111)** — Rocking scans performed while varying temperature to determine the magnetic transition temperature.
- **temps de recàrrega** — Temps de recàrrega de 3 h al 90 %.
- **ten pico‑coax lines** — Number of pico‑coaxial lines (10) used on pico_end and PCB_Wings_Sample.
- **tensió dentrada** — Tensió d'entrada nominal 230 V (rànquing 100 ÷ 750 V).
- **terahertz circular dichroism** — Differential absorption of left- and right-circularly polarized THz light in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **terahertz circular dichroism in commensurate twisted bilayer graphene** — Phys. Rev. B 108, 085421 (2023).
- **terahertz continuum** — Broad spectral response in the terahertz frequency range.
- **terahertz wave generation** — Emission of THz radiation from double‑graphene heterostructures.
- **terahertz wave generation and detection in double‑graphene layered van der waals heterostructures. 2d mater. 2016, 3, 045009** — Experimental demonstration of THz generation/detection in double‑graphene van‑der‑Waals stacks.
- **tersoff & hamann (1978) – atom superposition method** — Paper that introduced the atom superposition approximation for STM image calculations.
- **tersoff and hamann 1983** — First application of Bardeen's theory to STM.
- **tersoff and hamann 1983** — 1983 publication by Tersoff and Hamann introducing the STM tunneling formula.
- **tersoff‑hamann approximation** — Simplified model that relates STM tunneling current to the local density of states.
- **tersoff‑hamann formula** — Formula for STM tunneling current derived using first‑order perturbation theory.
- **tersoff‑hamann model reliable for feature size >1‑2 å and tip‑sample distance >5‑6 å** — Conclusion drawn from the review of scanning probe microscopies that the model works when structures are larger than ~1‑2 Å and the tip is kept >5‑6 Å away.
- **tersoff‑hamann theory** — Model that relates STM image intensity to the local density of states of the sample, valid for low bias and large tip‑sample distances.
- **tetralayer graphene** — Four‑layer graphene.
- **th4 thermocirculator** — Specific thermocirculator model (62 kg) listed among the FIRL100 auxiliary equipment.
- **the band theory of graphite** — Phys. Rev. 1947 paper by P. R. Wallace presenting the band theory of graphite.
- **the cryogenic quantum twisting microscope, part i** — 2023 publication describing the cryogenic quantum twisting microscope (QTM).
- **the cryogenic quantum twisting microscope, part ii** — 2023 continuation of the description of the cryogenic quantum twisting microscope.
- **the electronic properties of graphene** — Rev. Mod. Phys. 2009 review by A. H. Castro Neto et al. covering graphene electronic properties.
- **the gw plus cumulant method and plasmonic polarons: application to the homogeneous electron gas (2016)** — Study introducing the GW‑plus‑cumulant approach and plasmonic polarons in the homogeneous electron gas.
- **the marvels of moiré materials** — Nature Reviews Materials 6, 201 (2021) reviewing properties of moiré heterostructures.
- **the quantum twisting microscope** — Nature 2023 paper introducing the Quantum Twisting Microscope.
- **the quantum twisting microscope** — Nature 614, 682 (2023) by Inbar et al.
- **the quantum twisting microscope** — Nature 2023 paper introducing the quantum twisting microscope (QTM).
- **the quantum twisting microscope (nature 2023)** — Seminal paper introducing the quantum twisting microscope.
- **the quantum twisting microscope. nature 614, 682–687 (2023)** — Reference describing the QTM instrument and its capabilities.
- **the role of electron-electron interactions in two-dimensional dirac fermions** — Science 2018 paper on e‑e interactions in 2D Dirac fermions.
- **the shear mode of multilayer graphene** — Nature Materials 11, 294–300 (2012) reporting the shear vibrational mode in multilayer graphene.
- **the sheet resistance of graphene under contact and its effect on the derived specific contact resistivity** — Carbon 2015 paper studying sheet resistance of graphene under metal contacts.
- **the siesta method for ab initio order‑n materials simulation** — J. Phys.: Condens. Matter 2002 paper describing the SIESTA method.
- **theoretical crossing‑condition calculation** — Parameter‑free model that predicts where Dirac cones of one layer intersect empty bands of the other.
- **theoretical determination of the effect of a screening gate on plasmon‑induced superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene (2024)** — Predicts how a screening gate modifies plasmon‑mediated pairing.
- **theoretical framework for tunneling spectra between 2d normal metals and superconductors** — Develop analytical model for QTM tunneling conductance.
- **theoretical reconstruction (fitting θ, γ, j_el, j_inel)** — Model used to fit the measured current‑voltage characteristics and photon‑emission spectra.
- **theory for inelastic momentum‑conserving tunneling spectroscopy with qtm** — Theoretical effort developing the QTM‑based spectroscopy and predicting plasmon signatures.
- **theory of intrinsic acoustic plasmons in twisted bilayer graphene (2024)** — Theoretical description of acoustic plasmons in TBG.
- **theory of phonon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 2024 theoretical work on phonon spectroscopy using QTM.
- **theory of phonon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 110, 205407 (2024) presenting the theoretical basis for phonon spectroscopy using the QTM.
- **theory of phonon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope (2024)** — Theoretical analysis of phonon spectroscopy performed with the QTM.
- **theory of phonon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope (xiao et al., 2024)** — Theoretical framework for phonon spectroscopy using the QTM.
- **theory of phonon‑mediated superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 257001 (2018)
- **theory of phonon‑mediated superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene (prl 121, 257001, 2018)** — Seminal theoretical work proposing phonon‑mediated pairing in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **theory of plasmon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope** — Paper proposing the use of QTM for plasmon spectroscopy in twisted graphene systems.
- **theory of quantum liquids** — 1999 book on quantum liquids.
- **theory of spin‑polarized scanning tunneling microscopy applied to local spins** — Phys. Rev. B 81, 115454 (2010) describing spin‑polarized scanning tunneling microscopy.
- **theory of the scanning tunneling microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 1985 paper presenting the theoretical foundation of STM.
- **theory of underdoped cuprates** — 1996 theoretical work on underdoped cuprate materials.
- **thermal broadening** — Temperature‑induced widening of electronic and excitonic distributions.
- **thermal conductivity** — Measure of heat transport in a material.
- **thermal energy k_b t** — Thermal energy compared with the magnetic‑correlation scale J.
- **thermal energy kbt = 25 mev** — Thermal energy value used in the second toy‑model calculations.
- **thermal graphene rotation cannot be avoided** — Heating above the PC film melting point inevitably induces rotation of graphene on h‑BN.
- **thermal imaging** — Technique to visualise the beam profile via the thermal response of imaging plates.
- **thermal imaging plates** — Plates used to capture the beam profile via thermal response.
- **thermal infrared emission from biased graphene** — Nature Nanotechnology 2010 observation of thermal infrared emission from electrically biased graphene.
- **thermal smearing** — Broadening of electronic features caused by the finite temperature (room temperature ≈ 25 meV).
- **thermal spin dynamics of kitaev magnets: scattering continua and magnetic field induced phases within a stochastic semiclassical approach** — 2022 paper studying thermal spin dynamics in Kitaev magnets.
- **thermal transport measurements** — Experimental method to measure thermal conductivity.
- **thermally activated current** — Background current that increases with bias due to activation over the WSe₂ barrier.
- **thermally induced graphene rotation on h‑bn** — Phenomenon reported by Wang et al. 2016 where graphene rotates relative to h‑BN upon heating.
- **thermal‑transport measurements in a quantum spin‑liquid state of the frustrated triangular magnet κ‑(bedt‑ttf)₂cu₂(cn)₃** — Nat. Phys. 5, 44 (2009) by M. Yamashita et al.
- **thermocirculator cooling capacity** — Cooling capacity of the thermocirculator: 1300 W at 20 °C.
- **thermocirculator operating range** — Allowed operating temperature range of the thermocirculator: 0 °C to 60 °C.
- **thermoelectric detection and imaging of propagating graphene plasmons** — Nat. Mater. 2016 paper by Hone, Hillenbrand and Koppens on thermoelectric imaging of graphene plasmons.
- **thickness** — Layer thicknesses such as 20–50 nm top h‑BN, 7–12 layers h‑BN barrier, 8 L barrier.
- **thickness dependence of current density** — Each additional h-BN layer reduces current density roughly sixfold.
- **thin flakes of 1t‑tas2** — Few‑layer exfoliated 1T‑TaS2.
- **thin insulating barrier** — Typical dielectric layer inserted between two 2D crystals to keep the junction in the tunnelling regime.
- **thin oxide layer** — Insulating barrier separating two metals in the tunnelling experiments.
- **thinner hbn gives stronger tunneling and higher dark current** — Reduced hBN thickness leads to larger tunneling currents and more pronounced dark current.
- **thomas s. jespersen** — Author listed on the Ann. Phys. 2022 paper and on the APL Mater. 2024 paper.
- **thorlabs beam block pbg4560a** — Beam block (model PBG4560A) from Thorlabs mounted on the bench.
- **thorlabs beam block pbg60120a** — Beam block (model PBG60120A) from Thorlabs mounted on the bench.
- **three bragg scattering processes** — Threefold Bragg scattering terms in the interlayer tunneling Hamiltonian that couple the tip Fermi circle to three wave vectors in the mini‑Brillouin zone.
- **three notes on the quantum theory of aperiodic effects** — Physical Review 13, 66-81 (1928)
- **three‑band tight‑binding model for monolayers of group‑vib transition metal dichalcogenides** — Phys. Rev. B 2013 paper by G.-B. Liu et al. presenting a tight‑binding model for TMDC monolayers.
- **three‑dimensional mapping** — Spatially resolved measurement in three dimensions.
- **three‑fold rotational ambiguity from c₃z symmetry** — Identification that lack of C₃z symmetry leads to up to three equivalent node positions.
- **three‑layer hexagonal boron nitride tunneling barrier** — A tunneling barrier consisting of three layers of hBN with thickness 1.2 nm.
- **three‑monolayer‑thick wse₂ layer** — Transition‑metal‑dichalcogenide flake used as a marker for tip‑scanning imaging.
- **three‑step process dominates \(\hat t_1\)** — The three‑step tunneling pathway provides the primary contribution to the matrix element \(\hat T_1\).
- **three‑step tunneling process** — Dominant tunneling pathway contributing primarily to the matrix element \(\hat T_1\).
- **threshold at ω** — Threshold behavior of the spin structure factor at ω > ħ v_s q².
- **threshold behavior at bias ±δ** — Onset of dI/dV when tip Fermi line intersects the edge of Bogoliubov quasiparticle dispersions.
- **threshold voltage** — Minimum bias required to generate acoustic or optical plasmons, dependent on the Fermi energies.
- **thz** — Terahertz frequency range employed in the experiment.
- **thz alignment to detector** — Procedure to align the THz beam to the detector using back‑reflection and a pseudo‑heterodyne scheme.
- **thz collimation project** — Project focused on THz beam collimation and power delivery to the cSNOM.
- **thz csnom alignment guide** — The overall effort to document and optimise the THz‑cSNOM alignment.
- **thz frequency (2.52 thz)** — Operating frequency of the THz gas laser mentioned in the alignment notes.
- **thz gas laser** — Source of the THz radiation used in the cSNOM system.
- **thz gas laser (fir‑edinburgh)** — THz gas laser source used as the illumination beam for the project.
- **thz laser** — Source of terahertz radiation that will be delivered to the sample via the hollow tube.
- **thz laser absorption by air humidity** — Loss of THz power caused by water vapor in the beam path.
- **thz powermeter** — Device used to locate the THz beam centre and to maximise transmitted power.
- **thz qtm + altermagnetism** — Combined THz‑driven quantum twisting microscope setup used to study altermagnetic p‑wave magnets.
- **thz qtm documentation** — Internal documentation of the THz Quantum Twisting Microscope (QTM) prepared in May 2025.
- **thz scattering** — THz measurement mode that requires the bolometer to be aligned.
- **thz‑qtm zoom meeting series** — Bi‑weekly virtual meetings to coordinate THz‑QTM development between ICFO and Weizmann.
- **ti/au electrodes** — Gold electrodes patterned by electron‑beam evaporation for contacting the tunnel devices.
- **tight‑binding band structure** — Tight‑binding model for graphene that can include nearest‑neighbor (NN) and next‑nearest‑neighbor (NNN) hopping terms.
- **tight‑binding description of interlayer tunneling** — Microscopic model based on hopping between p_z orbitals on the two graphene layers.
- **tight‑binding electron wave functions** — Method to correct electron energies and matrix elements when adapting the formalism to other materials.
- **tight‑binding model** — Theoretical framework to calculate electronic bands using hopping parameters.
- **tight‑binding modeling** — Computational implementation of the tight‑binding approach.
- **tilt compensation** — Adjustment of the wedge angle and AFM leg heights to make the cantilever surface parallel to the sample.
- **tilting sample using goniometer** — Tilting the sample by ~11° to reduce tip-sample inclination to ~1°.
- **tilt‑compensating wedge** — Mechanical element used to keep the tip normal to the sample during rotation.
- **time‑dependent solution** — Construction of a time‑dependent wavefunction as a linear combination of many‑particle states.
- **time‑reversal symmetry** — Symmetry relating the +K and –K valleys in graphene.
- **tip a** — QTM tip with a plateau composed of two regions with slightly different tilt angles.
- **tip alignment** — Procedure to align the AFM tip with the sample region of interest.
- **tip apex atom** — The single atom at the very end of the STM tip that dominates tunneling.
- **tip b** — QTM tip showing a clear flat plateau.
- **tip c** — QTM tip where junk accumulated in sloped regions of the van‑der‑Waals tent.
- **tip contact area** — Measured lateral area of the tip that contacts the sample.
- **tip contact area a_tip** — Effective area of the STM tip in contact with the sample.
- **tip d** — QTM tip used in measurements of the 3.4° TBG sample.
- **tip diameter (~150 nm)** — The physical size of the tip, comparable to the extracted scattering length and likely limiting momentum resolution.
- **tip displacement (nm)** — Vertical movement of the AFM tip, inferred from Z‑piezo voltage changes.
- **tip doping** — Level of carrier concentration introduced in the graphene tip.
- **tip doping level** — Carrier density (Fermi level) of the graphene tip.
- **tip electrode** — The electrode formed by the normal metallic tip.
- **tip electrons** — Electrons residing in the scanning tip (monolayer graphene).
- **tip fermi velocity (v_f)** — Fermi velocity of the graphene tip.
- **tip fermi wave vector (k_f)** — Fermi wave vector of the graphene tip.
- **tip fermi wave vector (kf)** — Fermi momentum of the graphene tip, tunable by gating.
- **tip follows contour of constant p(r,ef) at constant current** — During constant‑current imaging the tip height traces a contour of constant surface LDOS.
- **tip hamiltonian** — Hamiltonian governing the tip alone.
- **tip holder** — Concept of a mechanical holder for the tip PCB.
- **tip inclination angle** — Angle between AFM tip axis and sample surface (~1° in QTM, ~12° in conventional AFM).
- **tip pyramid size** — Size of QTM tip pyramid (~1–1.5 µm) versus conventional AFM tip (~20 µm).
- **tip radius** — Effective lateral size of the ESP tip, linked to AA or AB stacking domains.
- **tip radius (r)** — Radius of the STM tip; used as a fitting parameter (e.g., 4 Å, 9 Å).
- **tip radius increases with decreasing twist angle** — Observed systematic increase of tip radius as the twist angle is reduced.
- **tip screening** — Screening effect arising from the graphene tip placed near the sample.
- **tip screening reduces plasmon energy** — Tip screening lowers the plasmon energy in the sample and weakens plasmon‑mediated interaction.
- **tip shape** — Geometrical shape of the AFM tip influencing tunneling area.
- **tip shape stable after detachment** — Repeated tip retraction and approach does not modify tip geometry.
- **tip stability** — Requirement that the TERS tip maintains its geometry and position during measurement.
- **tip states** — Quantum states of the tip involved in tunneling.
- **tip wings** — Tip assembly referred to as "wings".
- **tip-enhanced raman spectroscopy (ters)** — A Raman spectroscopy technique that uses a metallic tip to enhance the local electromagnetic field and boost Raman signal.
- **tip-sample distance (d_t)** — Separation between tip and sample, assumed much smaller than q^{-1}.
- **tip-sample tunneling setup** — Experimental configuration where a tip is positioned at a distance dT from the sample to measure tunneling current.
- **tip/sample** — Physical tip or sample that is electrically connected via the Inter PCB.
- **tipless afm cantilever (commercial, k=40 n m⁻¹, l=232 µm, w=37 µm, t=7 µm)** — AFM cantilever used as the mechanical platform for vdW device‑on‑tip.
- **tipless cantilever (nanosensors)** — Silicon‑nitride cantilever without a tip, 220 µm long, 40 µm wide, spring constant 48 N/m.
- **tip‑enhanced raman spectroscopy (anal. chem.)** — Anal. Chem. 2016 paper on TERS methodology.
- **tip‑enhanced raman spectroscopy (ters)** — Raman technique that uses metal‑coated tips, mentioned as a comparison.
- **tip‑enhanced raman spectroscopy (ters)** — Raman enhancement technique that uses surface plasmons between substrate and tip.
- **tip‑enhanced raman spectroscopy – its status, challenges and future directions** — Chem. Phys. Lett. 2009 paper discussing the state of TERS.
- **tip‑enhanced raman spectroscopy: technique and recent advances** — Chem. Rev. 2017 review of TERS methodology and progress.
- **tip‑induced broadening** — Broadening of tunneling spectra caused by the electronic states of the tip.
- **tip‑sample disengagement** — Separation of the tip from the sample during rotation.
- **tip‑sample distance >5‑6 å** — Distance regime where the Tersoff‑Hamann model is considered reliable.
- **tip‑sample distance control** — Feedback‑controlled positioning of the tip via piezoelectric actuation.
- **tip‑sample distance ≈4.1 å** — Distance at which the tip may suddenly jump into contact with a metal surface.
- **tip‑sample distance ≈4.6‑4.7 å** — Distance range where significant chemical interaction still occurs.
- **tip‑sample tunneling measurement** — Experimental configuration where a metallic tip is brought close to the TBG sample to measure tunneling current.
- **tip‑scanning afm head** — AFM head with open access and adjustable legs that allow the cantilever‑sample tilt angle to be changed.
- **tip‑shaped electrostatic potential** — Localized ESP of TBG that mimics a scanning probe tip.
- **tmd‑hbn capped sample** — Air‑sensitive TMD crystal encapsulated with a thin hBN layer for transfer.
- **tombros, n.** — Co‑author of the 2014 fast pick‑up technique paper.
- **tongay et al., nano lett. 12, 5576–5580 (2012)** — Study of thermally driven indirect‑to‑direct bandgap crossover in MoSe₂ and MoS₂.
- **tonndorf et al., opt. express 21, 4908–4916 (2013)** — Investigation of photoluminescence emission and Raman response of monolayer MoS₂, MoSe₂ and WSe₂.
- **top gating** — Application of a voltage to the built‑in graphite layer of the tip to tune the probe’s carrier density.
- **top graphene layer** — Upper graphene sheet of the device.
- **top graphene layer** — Upper graphene electrode in the heterostructure.
- **top hbn protection layer** — Thin hBN layer covering the top of the device to protect the active region.
- **top layer carrier density (nt)** — Electron density in the top graphene layer.
- **top mirror of the periscope** — Upper mirror inside the periscope used to steer the VIS beam.
- **top vdw layer (tip side)** — Active van‑der‑Waals layer on the tip side of the junction.
- **topografiner** — Early surface‑scanning device using a piezoelectric driver, precursor to the STM.
- **topography scan** — AFM scan of surface topography acquired at different rotation angles.
- **topological flat bands** — Flat electronic bands with nontrivial topology.
- **topological flat bands, valley polarization, and interband superconductivity in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene with proximitized spin‑orbit couplings** — 2024 Phys. Rev. B paper by Y.-Z. Chou, Y. Tan, F. Wu and S. Das Sarma.
- **topological incommensurate fflo superconductor** — Fulde‑Ferrell‑Larkin‑Ovchinnikov state with topological features.
- **topological incommensurate fflo superconductor and bogoliubov fermi surface in rhombohedral tetra‑layer graphene** — 2024 arXiv preprint by H. Yang and Y.-H. Zhang.
- **top‑down lithography** — Patterning method that becomes challenging for sub‑10 nm graphene features.
- **total current density jtotal** — Sum of electronic and photon‑assisted tunneling currents.
- **total density of states (ν_t)** — Density of electronic states at the tip Fermi level.
- **total path before csnom box** — Optical path length of approximately 1.4 m before the cSNOM box.
- **total path length before snom box** — Approximate 1.4 m optical path length preceding the SNOM box.
- **toy model** — Simplified theoretical model containing heavy‑ and light‑electron sectors and density–density repulsion.
- **trace layer up** — Top layer used for routing traces.
- **traditional approach to bardeens theory** — Interpretation due to C. B. Duke, linking Bardeen's theory to Oppenheimer's perturbation theory.
- **transfer hamiltonian** — Small time‑harmonic perturbation ˆH′ that enables tunneling between electrodes.
- **transfer matrix element (|t|)** — Overlap integral ⟨Ψ_T|Ĥ′|Ψ_B⟩ that quantifies tunneling strength.
- **transfer matrix element (|t|)** — Quantum mechanical matrix element governing tunneling between the two electrodes.
- **transfer of large‑area graphene films for high‑performance transparent conductive electrodes** — Nano Letters 2009 paper on a transfer process for large‑area graphene used as transparent electrodes.
- **transfer‑hamiltonian formalism** — Theoretical framework used to calculate inelastic current density.
- **transformation to band basis** — Step that rewrites spinon operators in terms of eigenvectors of the mean‑field Hamiltonian.
- **transition at tₙ with β≈0.33 consistent with 3d‑ising universality** — Temperature‑dependent rocking scans reveal a critical exponent β=0.33(5), matching 3D‑Ising behavior.
- **transition probability** — Probability for an electron to transfer across the barrier, proportional to |M|²·ρ_f.
- **transition temperature (tₙ)** — Temperature at which CrSb undergoes magnetic ordering.
- **transition‑metal dichalcogenide compounds** — Layered semiconductors such as MoS₂, WS₂, etc., mentioned as future QTM targets.
- **translational invariance** — The measured physics does not change when the experiment is repeated at different locations on the sample.
- **transmission ribbon** — Ribbon that transmits plasmons and is exposed to the analyte in plasmon‑based sensors.
- **transmission t(ε)** — Energy‑dependent transmission coefficient governing tunneling probability.
- **transparent conductive electrodes** — Electrodes that are both optically transparent and electrically conductive, made from graphene films.
- **transport between twisted graphene layers** — The 2010 PRB studies electron transport across twisted graphene layers.
- **transport between twisted graphene layers** — Phys. Rev. B 81, 245412 (2010) theoretical study of interlayer transport in twisted graphene.
- **transverse acoustic (ta) phonon mode** — Low‑energy acoustic phonon observed as conductance steps.
- **triangular lattice** — Geometric arrangement of AA‑stacked regions in a moiré pattern.
- **triangular lattice symmetry** — Geometric symmetry of the moiré lattice that influences antitip behavior.
- **trigonal warping** — Three‑fold anisotropy of the electronic dispersion near the Fermi level.
- **trigonal warping** — Triangular distortion of the Fermi surface that appears near the M points at higher energies (≈1.5 eV).
- **tunability of tip fermi wave vector limited by electrostatic constraints of gating** — ΔD≈0.4 V/nm needed for ΔkF≈0.22 nm⁻¹.
- **tunable band gap** — Adjustable electronic gap in twisted bilayer graphene via twist angle or strain.
- **tunable band gap in twisted bilayer graphene** — Phys. Rev. B 105, 115106 (2022).
- **tunable band gap in twisted bilayer graphene** — Demonstration that the band gap can be tuned by twist angle or strain.
- **tunable band structure** — Ability to modify electronic bands by changing twist angle, interlayer distance, or doping.
- **tunable bandgap** — Adjustable electronic bandgap in bilayer graphene controlled by external fields.
- **tunable graphene plasmons** — Graphene plasmons whose frequency can be electrically or chemically tuned, enabling molecular sensing.
- **tunable moiré quasicrystal** — A quasicrystalline structure formed by moiré patterns.
- **tunable molecular plasmons** — Molecular plasmon resonances whose frequency can be tuned, demonstrated in polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
- **tunable strongly coupled superconductivity in magic‑angle twisted trilayer graphene** — Nature paper on superconductivity in twisted trilayer graphene.
- **tungsten disulfide (ws₂)** — Semiconducting transition‑metal dichalcogenide mentioned as compatible with QTM.
- **tuning superconductivity** — Control of superconducting properties via external parameters.
- **tuning superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — 2019 Science paper by M. Yankowitz et al.
- **tuning superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — Science 363, 1059–1064 (2019).
- **tuning superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene** — Showed that carrier density and displacement field control the superconducting dome.
- **tuning the graphene work function by electric field effect** — Nano Lett. 2009 paper by Y.‑J. Yu et al. on electric‑field control of graphene work function.
- **tunnel hbn layer** — ≈3.3 nm thick hBN layer that acts as the tunnelling barrier between graphene and Au.
- **tunnel junction resistance rt** — Resistance of the h-BN barrier, exponentially dependent on thickness.
- **tunneling barrier** — Potential barrier separating tip and sample through which electrons tunnel.
- **tunneling conductance dependent on lateral shift at commensurate angles** — At commensurate twist angles, interference makes conductance vary with lateral shift.
- **tunneling conductance di/dv** — First derivative of the tunneling current, showing satellite peaks.
- **tunneling conductance independent of lateral shift at incommensurate angles** — In the weak‑tunneling regime, conductance does not depend on lateral shift for incommensurate twist angles.
- **tunneling conductance measurement** — Measurement of tunneling current through WSe2 barrier.
- **tunneling conductance singularity** — A sharp feature in dI/dV arising from the Fermi‑edge singularity and band‑alignment geometry.
- **tunneling current i vs. rotation angle θ measurement** — Measurement of the tunneling current as a function of the relative twist angle.
- **tunneling current independent of lifetime** — Calculated tunneling current integrated over plasmon widths shows little dependence on the chosen electron lifetime τ.
- **tunneling current measurement** — Detection of the nanoampere‑scale current flowing across the tunneling barrier.
- **tunneling event** — Electron tunneling between top and bottom graphene layers that satisfies Pauli exclusion, energy, and momentum conservation.
- **tunneling heterostructure** — Stack consisting of a top graphene layer, a monolayer quantum‑spin‑liquid barrier, and a bottom graphene layer.
- **tunneling heterostructure** — Stack of graphene layers and a quantum‑spin‑liquid barrier used for inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy.
- **tunneling in solids** — Concept of electron tunneling phenomena in solid-state materials.
- **tunneling into higher electron bands traces momentum dependence of plasmons** — Result that higher‑band tunneling maps the momentum‑dependent part of the plasmon dispersion.
- **tunneling junction** — Junction formed between graphene tip and graphene sample across hBN.
- **tunneling magnetoresistance** — Resistance change arising from spin‑dependent tunneling.
- **tunneling matrix element** — Amplitude governing electron tunneling between tip and sample states.
- **tunneling matrix element calculation** — Evaluation of the overlap integral M that determines the tunneling current.
- **tunneling matrix elements contain interlayer information** — Finding that tunneling matrix elements encode the interlayer tunneling parameters of MA TBG.
- **tunneling matrix elements in three dimensional space: the derivative rule and the sum rule** — Physical Review B 42 (14), 8841-8857 (1990)
- **tunneling measurement** — Spectroscopic technique that probes electron‑plasmon interactions via tunneling currents.
- **tunneling peak** — The peak in tunneling conductance as a function of rotation angle, whose width reflects momentum resolution.
- **tunneling polarity** — Direction of electron flow (bias polarity) that can affect STM image contrast.
- **tunneling regime** — Transport condition where current flows only when momentum and energy of states in the two layers match.
- **tunneling spectroscopic signatures of charge doping and associated mott transition in α‑rucl₃ near graphite** — Identification of charge‑doping signatures and a Mott transition in α‑RuCl₃ when proximate to graphite.
- **tunneling spectroscopy** — Spectroscopic measurement of tunneling current versus bias.
- **tunneling spectroscopy** — Differential conductance measurement used to map electronic dispersion.
- **tunneling spectroscopy as a probe of fractionalization in two‑dimensional magnetic heterostructures (2020)** — Demonstrates that tunneling spectroscopy can reveal fractionalized excitations.
- **tunneling spectroscopy experiment in qtm** — Measurement of tunneling current through a graphene/quantum‑spin‑liquid junction using the quantum twisting microscope.
- **tunneling spectroscopy measurement** — Momentum‑resolved tunneling measurement of a monolayer‑graphene / twisted‑bilayer‑graphene device as a function of twist angle and bias.
- **tunneling spectroscopy of two-dimensional superconductors with the quantum twisting microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 113, 064502 (2026) study of tunneling spectroscopy on 2D superconductors performed with the quantum twisting microscope.
- **tunneling spectroscopy study and modeling of electron transport in small conjugated azomethine molecules** — Physical Review B 62 (15), 10459-10466 (2000)
- **tunneling spectrum** — Energy‑resolved conductance measured across the MLG–TBG junction.
- **tunneling theory** — The framework describing electron transfer between a tip and a sample across a barrier.
- **tunneling to semiconductor surfaces differs from metal surfaces** — Qualitative differences observed in calculations for GaAs(110) compared with metal surfaces.
- **tunneling‑based graphene electronics** — Approaches that exploit quantum tunnelling in graphene for electronic functionality.
- **tunnelling** — Quantum mechanical electron transfer across the hBN spacer.
- **tunnelling current** — Current flowing between two metals separated by a thin oxide barrier.
- **tunnelling current** — Electrical current flowing through a barrier via quantum tunnelling.
- **tunnelling current and its derivatives** — Experimental observable plotted versus twist angle and bias voltage.
- **tunnelling current density** — Current per unit area, evaluated over a region of size equal to a squared plasmon wavelength.
- **tunnelling current i** — Current measured through the tip as a function of position and bias.
- **tunnelling experiment** — Measurement of current across a thin oxide barrier between two metals.
- **tunnelling from a many‑particle point of view** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 1961 seminal paper introducing Bardeen’s tunnelling theory.
- **tunnel‑coupled graphene layers** — Stacking graphene layers to enable interlayer tunneling.
- **twenty pico‑coax lines** — Number of pico‑coaxial lines (20) used on PCB_Wings_Tip.
- **twist angle** — Angle of relative rotation between stacked layers.
- **twist angle (θ)** — Relative rotation angle between graphene layers.
- **twist angle (θ)** — Angle between the two graphite layers, used as a control knob.
- **twist angle 1.07°** — Moiré twist angle of the studied MATBG sample.
- **twist angle influences only the overall magnitude of di/dv via tunneling matrix elements, not the line shape** — Observation that in the off‑resonant bias regime the twist angle scales the amplitude of the differential conductance.
- **twist angle provides momentum resolution** — Controlling the twist angle tunes the momentum separation of the Dirac cones, enabling momentum‑resolved tunneling.
- **twist angle resolution** — Precision of twist angle control (δθ ≈ 0.001°).
- **twist boundary** — Region where the lattice orientation changes, acting as a scattering center.
- **twist-angle dependence** — Dependence of physical quantities on the relative rotation angle between graphene layers.
- **twist_measurement script** — Python script that performs bias‑angle and bias‑angle‑FTIR measurements.
- **twist_measurement script** — Python routine that performs angle‑bias (classical QTM) measurements by stepping the rotator and acquiring data.
- **twist_module.py** — Module providing the function Twist_openloop_ftir_save().
- **twist_openloop_ftir_save function** — Function implementing open‑loop FTIR measurement with twist angle.
- **twisted bilayer graphene** — Two graphene layers stacked with a relative twist angle.
- **twisted bilayer graphene (2.7°)** — Graphene bilayer rotated by 2.7° forming a moiré superlattice.
- **twisted bilayer graphene as topological heavy fermion: ii. analytical approximations of the model parameters (2023)** — Low Temperature Physics article presenting analytical approximations for a topological heavy‑fermion model of TBG.
- **twisted bilayer graphene sample** — The TBG device whose plasmons are studied.
- **twisted bilayer graphene sample (θ > 6°)** — TBG device with twist angles larger than 6 degrees used for the phonon and EPC measurements.
- **twisted bilayer graphene with 2.88° twist angle** — TBG compressed at a 2.88° twist angle.
- **twisted bilayer graphene with 7.34° twist angle** — TBG compressed at a 7.34° twist angle.
- **twisted bilayer graphene. iii. interacting hamiltonian and exact symmetries (2021)** — Physical Review B paper on interacting Hamiltonian and symmetries.
- **twisted bilayer graphene: a phonon‑driven superconductor** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 122, 257002 (2019)
- **twisted bilayer graphene: a phonon‑driven superconductor (prl 122, 257002, 2019)** — Proposal that phonons alone can drive superconductivity in twisted bilayer graphene.
- **twisted bilayer wse₂** — Two layers of tungsten diselenide with a twist.
- **twisted graphene** — Graphene bilayers with a relative twist angle.
- **twisted graphene bilayer** — Two graphene layers rotated relative to each other by a twist angle.
- **twisted graphene bilayer (θ ≈ 38.2°)** — Two graphene layers twisted clockwise by a commensurate angle of about 38.2°.
- **twisted graphene/hbn heterostructure sample** — A graphene flake on hBN with a controlled twist angle, measured in the QTM.
- **twisted josephson interferometry** — Interferometric method to probe order‑parameter symmetry in two‑dimensional superconductors.
- **twisted mote₂** — A twisted transition‑metal dichalcogenide heterostructure investigated for finite‑momentum superconductivity.
- **twisted multi-layer graphene** — Graphene stacks with multiple layers and a twist.
- **twisted oxide heterostructure** — Heterostructure formed by twisting two complex‑oxide membranes.
- **twisting path (k θ)** — Trajectory in momentum space defined by the product of wavevector and twist angle.
- **twistronics** — Control of electronic properties by twisting two‑dimensional layers.
- **twist‑angle disorder** — Spatial variation of the twist angle across a TBG sample.
- **twist‑angle evolution of twisted bilayer graphene observed** — Measurement of how the electronic structure of TBG changes with the continuously scanned twist angle.
- **twist‑angle measurement** — Measurement of electronic/optical response as a function of the relative twist angle between tip and sample.
- **twist‑angle‑dependent transport** — Electronic transport that varies with the relative rotation of stacked layers.
- **twist‑angle‑dependent transport feature** — Transport signatures that vary with the twist angle between layers.
- **twist‑controlled gr/h‑bn/gr light‑emitting devices** — Research effort to develop on‑chip optoelectronic sources based on twist‑controlled graphene tunnel junctions.
- **twist‑controlled resonant tunnelling in graphene/boron nitride/graphene heterostructures** — Nature Nanotechnology 2014 paper reporting resonant tunnelling controlled by twist angle.
- **twist‑controlled resonant tunnelling in graphene/boron nitride/graphene heterostructures** — Nature Nanotechnology 2014 demonstration of twist‑angle control of resonant tunnelling in graphene‑hBN‑graphene stacks.
- **twist‑graph project** — Joint ANR‑DFG project TWIST‑GRAPH supporting research at Freie Universität Berlin.
- **twist‑programmable superconductivity** — Superconductivity controlled by twist angle.
- **twist‑programmable superconductivity in spin–orbit‑coupled bilayer graphene** — 2025 Nature paper by Y. Zhang et al.
- **two blades (slit)** — Pair of blades placed in front of the powermeter to create a narrow slit and improve spatial resolution.
- **two mlg layers** — Sample consisting of two monolayer graphene sheets separated by a thin tunneling barrier.
- **two tunnelling channels identified** — Discovery of (1) one‑step indirect‑exciton‑assisted tunnelling and (2) two‑step phonon‑plus‑direct‑exciton tunnelling.
- **two-dimensional metal** — Hypothetical 2D metal with a small Fermi pocket at valley K.
- **two‑dimensional magnetic heterostructures** — Layered magnetic systems built from atomically thin crystals.
- **two‑probe measurement** — Electrical measurement configuration using two contacts, requiring correction for Rc.
- **two‑step tunnelling mechanism (iph+ex)** — Tunnelling process where a phonon supplies momentum and a direct exciton supplies energy.
- **t² resistivity** — Resistivity proportional to the square of temperature.
- **t‑matrix calculation** — T‑matrix calculation for tunneling amplitudes.
- **t‑matrix elements |m|²** — Normalized rate of inelastic tunneling between tip and sample.
- **u(1) chiral spin liquid** — Chiral variant of a U(1) quantum spin liquid.
- **u(1) spin liquid** — Spin liquid with emergent U(1) gauge field.
- **u. zondiner** — Acknowledged for useful discussions.
- **ultrafast collinear scattering and carrier multiplication in graphene** — Nature Communications 2013 study of collinear scattering and carrier multiplication in graphene using ultrafast spectroscopy.
- **ultrafast optical switching of infrared plasmon polaritons in high‑mobility graphene** — Nature Photonics 2016 paper by Ni, G. X. et al. on ultrafast optical switching.
- **ultrafast spectroscopy** — Time‑resolved optical measurement technique used to probe carrier dynamics in graphene.
- **ultrahigh carrier density** — Charge carrier concentration far exceeding typical gating limits.
- **ultrahigh mobility phenomenon** — Very high carrier mobility in monolayer graphene arising from weak EPC.
- **ultrasensitive plasmonic detection of molecules with graphene** — ACS Photonics 2016 paper on highly sensitive molecular detection via graphene plasmons.
- **ultrasmall plasmonic light source** — Nanoscale light emitter driven by a graphene tunnel junction.
- **ultrasmall plasmonic light source driven by graphene tunnel junction demonstrated** — A single nanoparticle coupled to a graphene tunnel junction acts as a nanoscale light emitter.
- **ultrasmall plasmonic single nanoparticle light source driven by a graphene tunnel junction** — ACS Nano 2018 demonstration of a nanoscale plasmonic light source powered by a graphene tunnel junction.
- **ultra‑violet lamp** — Broad‑band UV lamp that illuminates the thermal imaging plates.
- **umklapp** — Momentum‑nonconserving scattering process folded back into the Brillouin zone.
- **umklapp scattering** — Scattering process that couples Dirac points of one layer to wavevectors of the other layer.
- **umklapp scattering onset in di/dv** — Enhancement of dI/dV in the top‑right corner due to intersection with neighboring mini Brillouin zones.
- **unconventional superconductivity** — Superconductivity that does not fit the standard BCS paradigm.
- **unconventional superconductivity in magic‑angle graphene superlattices** — Nature 556, 43 (2018) reporting superconductivity in magic‑angle twisted bilayer graphene.
- **unconventional superconductivity in magic‑angle graphene superlattices** — Reference (1) reporting superconductivity in magic‑angle graphene.
- **undamped plasmon** — Plasmon mode with negligible decay, appearing as poles of the screened interaction.
- **undamped plasmon modes** — Collective charge oscillations without decay.
- **undamped plasmons near the moiré brillouin‑zone boundary despite metallic gating** — RPA‑GW calculations show well‑defined plasmons outside the particle‑hole continuum even with strong screening.
- **underdoped cuprates** — Cuprate superconductors with low hole doping.
- **understand pairing mechanisms via inelastic features** — Use inelastic tunneling signatures to infer the nature of the pairing glue.
- **uniform twist angle across sample** — The twist angle remains constant within ~0.1° over several microns, indicating low disorder.
- **unity‑order plasmon yield** — Calculated plasmon‑per‑electron yield close to one.
- **unperturbed green’s function g0(k, ε)** — Green’s function of the non‑interacting system used as a starting point.
- **unpolarized ins measurement with incident energy 750 mev** — High‑energy INS experiment mapping the full magnon dispersion over the Brillouin zone.
- **unscreeened coulomb potential** — Long‑range electron‑electron interaction giving rise to √q plasmon dispersion at long wavelengths.
- **unstable contact** — Loss of reliable tip‑sample contact caused by vibrations or misalignment.
- **untwisted graphene** — Aligned graphene layers without a twist.
- **upper bound of 0.1 % strain** — From the angular width of tunnelling resonances the experiment infers strain ≤ 0.1 %.
- **upper bound on strain in graphene layers** — Question whether the in‑situ TBG interface exhibits substantial strain and what the quantitative upper limit is.
- **use of step function** — Insertion of a step function to restrict integration to the barrier region.
- **using powermeter as iris for alignment** — Method that treats the centred powermeter position as a virtual iris to guide the VIS beam.
- **v (bias voltage)** — Applied voltage between tip and sample.
- **v. bhardwaj** — 
- **v. deckert** — Co‑author of the tip‑enhanced Raman spectroscopy nanoscale analysis paper.
- **v. i. vasyuchka** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2021 Nature Reviews Materials on coherent magnonics.
- **v. kadirko** — Researcher co‑author of the next‑nearest‑neighbor tight‑binding plasmon paper.
- **v. kravchuk** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 npj Spintronics on altermagnetic textures.
- **v. l. katkov** — First author of the 2017 review on tunnelling‑based graphene electronics.
- **v. m. loktev** — Researcher co‑author on next‑nearest‑neighbor hopping effects in graphene.
- **v. n. strocov** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nat. Commun. on altermagnetic band splitting.
- **v. p. kravchuk et al., chiral magnetic excitations and domain textures of g‑wave altermagnets, phys. rev. b 112, 144421 (2025)** — Paper discussing chiral magnetic excitations and domain textures in g‑wave altermagnets.
- **v. peri** — Co‑author on quantum spin liquid QTM study.
- **v. peri** — Author of probing quantum spin liquids with QTM.
- **v. scatturin** — Researcher co‑author of a paper measuring the form factor of Ni++ in KNiF3.
- **v. strocov** — Researcher and co‑author of a 2024 Nature paper on altermagnetic lifting of Kramers spin degeneracy.
- **v. w. brar** — First author of the 2013 and 2014 Nano Lett. papers on graphene nanoresonators and hybrid modes.
- **v_d (characteristic velocity)** — Velocity parameter appearing in equations.
- **v_s(p*)** — Sample velocity at momentum p*.
- **v_t(p*)** — Tip velocity at momentum p*.
- **vacuum tunneling** — Electron tunneling occurring across a vacuum gap, as in early Teague experiments.
- **vacuum tunneling** — Electron tunneling through vacuum gap between tip and surface.
- **valerio peri** — Co‑author of the paper "Probing quantum spin liquids with a quantum twisting microscope".
- **valley conservation** — Conservation of valley quantum number during tunneling.
- **valley degeneracy factor g_v** — Factor accounting for the two inequivalent K and K′ valleys in graphene.
- **valley jahn‑teller effect** — Symmetry‑breaking distortion driven by valley degrees of freedom.
- **valley jahn‑teller effect in twisted bilayer graphene** — Phys. Rev. X 9, 041010 (2019)
- **valley k** — One of the two inequivalent Dirac valleys in graphene.
- **valley‑apex potential ratio** — Ratio of electrostatic potential between valley and apex of the tip (e.g., 64.25 %).
- **van der waals device** — The device mounted on the rotating stage and brought into contact with the AFM tip.
- **van der waals heterostructures** — Stacks of 2D materials held together by van der Waals forces.
- **van der waals transfer stage** — Standard stage used to bring the PDMS stamp into contact with the cantilever.
- **van hove energy ≈ 6 mev** — Energy of the Van Hove singularity in the central conduction band.
- **van hove singularities** — Density‑of‑states peaks at the Brillouin‑zone boundaries.
- **van hove singularities and excitonic effects in the optical conductivity of twisted bilayer graphene** — Paper discussing Van Hove singularities and excitonic phenomena in TBG optical response.
- **van hove singularity near 3 ev at the m point** — Spectroscopic feature identified around 3 eV, attributed to a Van Hove singularity near the M point.
- **van‑der‑waals crystals** — Layered materials such as hBN, graphene, and black phosphorus that support 2D polaritons.
- **van‑der‑waals materials** — Layered crystals held together by van‑der‑Waals forces.
- **van‑der‑waals quantum tunneling** — Quantum tunnelling across atomically thin van‑der‑Waals gaps.
- **van‑der‑waals quantum tunnelling devices** — Light‑emitting devices based on tunnelling across van‑der‑Waals heterostructures.
- **van wees, b. j.** — Co‑author of the 2014 fast pick‑up technique paper.
- **variable temperature probe station** — Platform for low‑temperature electrical measurements.
- **vdw device on flat substrate** — Van‑der‑Waals heterostructure assembled on a Si/SiO₂ pillar using dry transfer.
- **vdw device on tip** — Heterostructure assembled on a focused‑ion‑beam deposited platinum pyramid, comprising graphene (and optionally WSe₂) layers transferred by polymer‑membrane technique.
- **vector potential (â)** — Operator representing the electromagnetic vector potential in the light‑matter interaction.
- **velocity renormalization by electron‑electron interactions** — Modification of carrier velocity due to many‑body interactions.
- **velocity v_pn** — Velocity associated with the normal‑state dispersion of the TBG electrons.
- **velocity v_sp*** — Group velocity of electrons in the sample at Dirac point p*.
- **velocity v_tp*** — Group velocity of electrons in the tip at Dirac point p*.
- **velocity v_δ** — Velocity associated with the superconducting gap function.
- **versatility and excellent performance predicted for double‑graphene sandwich structure** — Prediction that the double‑graphene sandwich offers high versatility and performance for integrated devices.
- **vertex correction effect** — Vertex correction due to interlayer Coulomb interactions explains differences between orange and blue lines.
- **vertical graphene heterostructure device** — Device architecture stacking graphene and hBN layers for vertical transport.
- **viability of optics‑free ultrathin plasmonic devices** — Conclusion that electrical excitation via tunneling is a feasible route for compact plasmonic components.
- **vibrational properties of h‑bn and h‑bn‑graphene heterostructures probed by inelastic electron tunneling spectroscopy** — Scientific Reports 5, 16642 (2015) measuring vibrational spectra of h‑BN and h‑BN‑graphene via IETS.
- **vibrational stark effect** — Shift of vibrational frequencies under an external electric field, used for field imaging.
- **vibration‑rotation transition** — Transition involving simultaneous change in vibrational and rotational quantum numbers of a molecule.
- **vibronic exciton–phonon states** — Hybrid states arising from strong coupling between excitons and phonons in van‑der‑Waals heterojunctions.
- **virtual tip** — The ESP of TBG acting as a non‑physical tip for electron‑sensitive samples.
- **vis** — Visible‑light frequency range employed in the experiment.
- **vis collimation position** — z = 12.35 mm position used for visible‑light collimation.
- **vis laser** — Visible‑light laser used as a pilot for alignment.
- **vis pilot** — Visible‑light reference beam used to align the THz path.
- **vis pilot** — Visible‑light laser used as a proxy for aligning the THz beam.
- **vis to thz alignment** — Procedure that uses the visible laser to align the invisible THz beam.
- **vis to thz alignment procedure** — The full alignment workflow described in the slides.
- **visible laser alignment** — The behaviour of the visible laser used as a surrogate for THz alignment.
- **visible light plasmonic heating of au‑zno for the catalytic reduction of co₂** — Wang, C. et al., Nanoscale 2013, describing Au‑ZnO plasmonic heating for CO₂ reduction.
- **visual transparency of graphene** — The amount of light transmitted through a graphene sheet.
- **voltage bias vtb** — Electrical bias applied between the top and bottom graphene layers.
- **voltage biasing (dc and ac) of sample** — Applying both DC and AC bias to the sample for simultaneous I and dI/dV measurements.
- **voltage dependence** — Variation of tunneling current with applied voltage
- **voltage drop vb = vexp - rsiexp** — Voltage drop defined as Vexp minus series resistance voltage drop.
- **voltage‑dependent raman spectroscopy** — Raman measurements performed while varying the bias voltage.
- **vortex lattice** — Ordered vortex pattern in the CDW state of 1T‑TaS₂.
- **vortex lattice** — Ordered array of topological defects in a charge‑density‑wave state.
- **vortex‑antivortex lattice** — Ordered array of vortices and antivortices.
- **v‑shaped adsorption energy curve** — Adsorption energy profile for electron‑deficient molecules indicating tip‑like electrostatic potential.
- **w (bandwidth)** — Bandwidth of the flat band.
- **w. chen** — 
- **w. d. oliver** — 
- **w. kadow** — Co‑author on magnetism in moiré QTM study.
- **w. kadow** — Co‑author of probing magnetism in moiré heterostructures with QTM.
- **w. l. mcmillan** — Co‑author of the 1965 paper on lead phonon spectrum.
- **w. lu** — Co‑author of the CDW structure/control paper.
- **w. mahoney** — Researcher
- **w. moritz** — Researcher mentioned in the references.
- **w. shi** — 
- **w. yu** — Co‑author of the 2019 experimental identification of quantum spin liquids.
- **w. zhang** — Co‑author of the TERS status and outlook paper.
- **wafer levelling** — Wafer Z position set to within 0.0010 mm using the laser displacement meter.
- **waist w(z)** — Beam radius at a distance z from the waist.
- **wang, d.** — First author of the 2016 thermally induced graphene rotation paper.
- **wang et al. 2016, phys. rev. lett.** — Reference reporting thermally induced graphene rotation on h‑BN.
- **warping‑induced tangency** — Tangential contact between equal‑energy contours caused by band warping.
- **warranty extension** — Possibility to extend the warranty period up to 4 years.
- **water vapor** — Atmospheric water vapor, quantified by relative humidity.
- **wave vector k′** — Wave vector integrated along the tip Fermi line.
- **wavelength (µm)** — Wavelength expressed in micrometres.
- **wavelength 0.118 mm** — Operating wavelength of the THz laser (≈0.118 mm).
- **wavelength tunability** — Ability of the laser to be tuned continuously across a broad spectral range.
- **wavevector (k)** — Momentum variable along the twisting path.
- **wavevector k** — Magnitude of graphene's Brillouin‑zone corner vector.
- **wavevector mismatch θ** — Angle between graphene Dirac cones, related to momentum mismatch.
- **weak background from tip plasmon** — The plasmon mode of the MLG tip adds only a weak, smooth background to the differential conductance.
- **weak tunneling regime** — Operating condition where the barrier is sufficiently high and wide that perturbative treatment is valid.
- **weak tunneling regime** — Regime where tunneling can be treated perturbatively using Fermi's golden rule.
- **weakly interacting system** — Regime where electron interactions are small.
- **weak‑tunneling approximation** — Assumption that tunneling is sufficiently weak for first‑order treatment.
- **wedge angle 8°** — Inclined wedge incorporated into the stage to reduce effective tilt.
- **wedge‑like region with enhanced di/dv** — Narrow region of increased differential conductance appearing for k_F/k_M ≳ 0.4.
- **wei et al., 2025, dirac‑point spectroscopy of flat‑band systems with the quantum twisting microscope** — Phys. Rev. B 111 paper on Dirac‑point spectroscopy using QTM.
- **wei et al., 2025, theory of plasmon spectroscopy with the quantum twisting microscope** — arXiv preprint presenting plasmon spectroscopy theory with QTM.
- **wei hu** — Corresponding author from Hefei National Research Center for Physical Sciences at the Microscale, University of Science and Technology of China.
- **what causes the imprecision of the alignment?** — Explores the role of thermally induced graphene rotation during transfer.
- **what is the magnetic ground state of crsb?** — Investigation of the ordering pattern and spin orientation in CrSb.
- **what is the optimal layout distance?** — Open question raised on slide 3 about the appropriate distance in the Edinburgh‑suggested layout.
- **what is the quantum coherence of the qtm junction?** — The open question addressed in Section S13 of the supplementary material.
- **what is the role of phason coupling in superconductivity?** — Assessing whether phason modes contribute significantly to pairing in magic‑angle graphene.
- **when are tersoff‑hamann and bardeen formulas accurate in practice and are the assumptions valid?** — Question concerning the practical validity range of STM tunneling formulas under various biases and tip‑sample distances.
- **white card (thorlabs visit card)** — White card placed downstream to visualise the incoming and reflected VIS beams.
- **why does epc increase when momentum decreases?** — Question addressing the unexpected rise of EPC strength at low q_M.
- **why does graphene behave as a weakly interacting system?** — Phys. Rev. Lett. 2014 discussion of weak interaction regime.
- **why does mn++ charge distribution expand in the solid?** — Open question raised by the contrasting behavior of Mn++.
- **why does noise worsen after one week?** — Investigation of the origin of the gradual increase in measurement noise.
- **why is a conductance peak observed only at the first commensurate angle?** — Open question addressing the absence of peaks at higher‑order commensurate angles.
- **why single‑particle models fail to reproduce low‑bias splitting** — Open question addressed by incorporating electron‑electron interactions.
- **why splitting appears at low bias (~1 v)** — Motivation for studying interaction‑driven renormalization.
- **wide angular range data set** — A collection of spectra taken over many twist angles on a single sample.
- **wide‑range t² resistivity and umklapp scattering in moiré graphene** — New J. Phys. 24, 052001 (2022)
- **wire bonding** — Method to connect copper pads to gold electrodes via thin wires.
- **wkb approximation** — Semi‑classical method used to estimate the tunneling amplitude through a barrier.
- **woodpecker modality** — Measurement mode where the tip contacts the sample for each angle, disengages, rotates a small step and repeats.
- **work function** — Energy needed to remove an electron from graphene, used to fit the potential‑well model.
- **work function difference** — Difference between tip and surface work functions.
- **work hours** — Salicru operating hours: weekdays 07:00–15:00, weekends 07:00–14:00.
- **wse2 barrier breakdown** — Dielectric breakdown of the WSe2 barrier at high bias.
- **wse2/mlg/hbn/graphite sample** — Heterostructure used for momentum resolved MLG‑MLG tunneling.
- **wse2/tbg/hbn sample** — Heterostructure used for momentum resolved MLG‑TBG tunneling.
- **wse₂ barrier** — Tungsten diselenide layer that forms the tunneling barrier in the device.
- **wse₂ on bottom graphene** — WSe₂ monolayer placed atop the bottom graphene layer for tip‑area imaging.
- **wse₂ tunnelling device** — Device where a WSe₂ monolayer is placed above graphene within the tunnelling stack.
- **ws₂ device (graphene/hbn/au with ws₂ flake inside graphene area)** — Device used to study exciton‑assisted resonant tunnelling; the WS₂ flake is fully inside the graphene region.
- **x and y piezoelectric nanopositioners (attocube)** — Nanopositioners for lateral translation of the sample.
- **x coordinate** — X coordinate saved in the yaml.txt data file.

- **z. yang** — Co‑author of the 2009 PCCP paper on chemical and electromagnetic mechanisms of TERS.

- **z2 gauge theory** — Gauge theory with Z2 symmetry.
- **z2 gauge theory of electron fractionalization in strongly correlated systems** — 2000 paper on Z2 gauge theory and electron fractionalization.
- **z2 quantum spin liquid** — A QSL characterized by a non‑zero Δ₁ parameter.
- **zero bias** — Zero applied voltage across the tunnelling junction.
- **zero landau level** — Landau level at zero energy characteristic of Dirac fermions in graphene.
- **zero landau level** — Landau level at zero energy in graphene under magnetic field.
- **zero temperature limit (t=0)** — Assumption used to simplify the tunneling‑current expressions.
- **zero‑bias conductance g_{p*}** — Zero‑bias tunneling conductance contribution of a given momentum.
- **zero‑bias di/dv exhibits two peaks when tips fermi line crosses nodes** — Observation that zero‑bias conductance shows two peaks at node intersections.
- **zero‑bias di/dv peak** — Prominent conductance peak at zero bias associated with superconducting nodes.
- **zero‑bias di/dv peaks associated with superconducting nodes** — Peaks in the zero‑bias differential conductance caused by momentum‑conserving tunneling between tip and superconducting nodes.
- **zero‑bias tunneling conductance measurement** — Measurement of dI/dV at zero bias between the TBG sample and an MLG tip.
- **zero‑field quantum hall effect** — Quantum Hall quantization observed without an external magnetic field.
- **zero‑point motion (zpm)** — Quantum ground‑state vibration amplitude of a phonon mode.
- **zero‑point motion amplitude (zpm)** — Amplitude of atomic zero‑point vibrations that modulate hopping.
- **zero‑temperature zero‑bias di/dv spectrum of the normal phase** — Simulated conductance map for the 1.05° TBG sample at ν = –3 in the normal (non‑superconducting) state.
- **zheng et al., tunneling spectroscopic signatures of charge doping and associated mott transition in α-rucl3 in proximity to graphite, phys. rev. b 107, 195107 (2023)** — Paper describing tunneling spectroscopy signatures of charge doping and a Mott transition in α‑RuCl₃ near graphite.
- **zhou et al., evidence for charge transfer and proximate magnetism in graphene–α-rucl3 heterostructures, phys. rev. b 100, 165426 (2019)** — Paper presenting evidence for charge transfer and proximate magnetism in graphene–α‑RuCl₃ heterostructures.
- **znse beamsplitter** — Beamsplitter transparent to VIS light, used in the MIR alignment case.
- **zomer, p. j.** — First author of the 2014 fast pick‑up technique paper.
- **zomer et al. 2014, appl. phys. lett.** — Reference introducing the fast pick‑up technique for graphene/h‑BN heterostructures.
- **z‑stage** — Vertical positioning element of the QTM.
- **z₂ quantum spin liquid (phenomenon)** — Physical realization of a QSL with non‑zero Δ₁.
- **ø. fischer** — Co‑author of the 2007 review on STM of high‑Tc superconductors.
- **α-fe₂o₃** — Insulating altermagnet where spin‑split magnons have been reported.
- **α‑rucl3 nanoflakes** — Nanometer‑scale flakes of α‑RuCl3.
- **β ≈ 3.14** — Parameter relating strain to gauge field.
- **γ** — Hybridization strength γ = –70 meV used in the THF model.
- **γ-centered fermi pocket** — Fermi pocket centered at the γ point in the Brillouin zone.
- **γ_s (spectral broadening)** — Broadening parameter of the sample spectrum.
- **γ_t** — Broadening parameter used in simulated spectra (γ_T = 1 meV).
- **γ‑l direction** — Low‑symmetry reciprocal‑space direction where chiral spin splitting is observed.
- **δ1k** — Momentum difference probed by the tunneling process.
- **δ1k momentum resolution** — Resolution in Δ1K momentum space (δ|Δ1K|≈3×10⁻⁵ Å⁻¹).
- **δg_ta increases with decreasing twist angle (θ)** — The TA‑related conductance step becomes larger for smaller twist angles.
- **δgta** — Amplitude of the conductance step associated with the TA phonon mode.
- **δgta scales linearly with gate voltage** — Measured linear dependence of the TA conductance‑step amplitude on Vbg.
- **δgza** — Amplitude of the conductance step associated with the ZA phonon mode.
- **δ₁** — Parameter that distinguishes Z₂ (Δ₁≠0) from U(1) (Δ₁=0) quantum spin liquids.
- **εz (out‑of‑plane dielectric permittivity)** — Dielectric constant of the spacer perpendicular to the graphene layers.
- **ε⊥/d** — Perpendicular dielectric constant divided by distance, ε⊥/d = 2.4 nm⁻¹.
- **η** — Broadening parameter used in simulated spectra (η = 0.5 meV).
- **θ_max** — Maximum experimentally accessible twist angle (≈30°).
- **κ′ point** — High‑symmetry point in the mini Brillouin zone where the tip Dirac point can be aligned.
- **ν_t (density of states of tip)** — Density of states of the monolayer graphene tip.
- **ξ (xi)** — Parameter that vanishes at the tangency condition.
- **ξ_cm** — Energy of the minimum of the remote conduction band.
- **σ (imaginary part of self‑energy)** — Imaginary component of the electron self‑energy that determines spectral broadening.
- **σx representation of sublattice hilbert space** — Operator that swaps sublattices under mirror transformation.
- **≈ 40 % photons emitted on resonance** — Fraction of emitted photons that belong to the resonant peak for near‑aligned devices.
